ref: Don't you believe that I am one with the Father and that the Father is one with me? What I say isn't said on my own. The Father who lives in me does these things.

text: N yi daa bu ky…õ na n‚ÄĎt…Ē'asi n‚ÄĎti ba, ba daa naa n‚ÄĎku deaai taalli; amaa pampaŇčŇč…Ē ba ku laa n‚ÄĎtoaai n‚ÄĎkyiisi n‚ÄĎyi ba taalli maa ni.

ref: Everyone who hates me also hates my Father.

ref: I have done things no one else has ever done. If they had not seen me do these things, they would not be guilty. But they did see me do these things, and they still hate me and my Father too.

ref: This is why the Scriptures are true when they say, ‚ÄúPeople hated me for no reason.‚ÄĚ
text: Di b…õ la lala ni di ky…õ ka ba zaaligu y…õligu la niŇči y…õlimaŇčni, ni, ‚ÄėM bu niŇči ba seela ka ba yaai ma y…Ēri.‚Äô

ref: Then you will also tell others about me, because you have been with me from the beginning.

ref: I am telling you these things, so that you will not turn away.
text: Ka Yisa daa laa n‚ÄĎy…õli ba, ni, ‚ÄúDinŇč…Ē ka n y…õli ya, ni, yan di ky…õ ka ya y…õdda maa ny…õŇčŇči ya.

ref: You will be chased out of the synagogues. And the time will come when people will kill you and think they are doing God a favor.

text: Ban daa zi ba na sahanseaa la, ba daa ky…õ ka ba zaani la'aŇčŇču maa puuni, ka GyeeŇčŇču Toondaana maa daa boosi ba, ni,

ref: ‚ÄúWe told you plainly not to teach in the name of Jesus. But look what you have done! You have been teaching all over Jerusalem, and you are trying to blame us for his death.‚ÄĚ
text: ‚ÄúTi daa bu zaali ya zaalikpeemni, ni, yan di laa n‚ÄĎwulisiri yuuri wa zugu?‚ÄĚ, ni, ‚ÄúAmaa ya mun daa pun ky…õ ka y…õligu wa y…õligi m‚ÄĎpaali Gy…õrusal…õm tiŇčŇča zaa. Halli ya boori ni ya ky…õ ka doo wa kum taalli tabli ti gba.‚ÄĚ

ref: Peter and the apostles replied: We don't obey people. We obey God.
text: Ka Piita ni u taaba daa labsi n‚ÄĎy…õli, ‚ÄúTi nin sakki la Naawunni noori, ka pa ninsaala noori.

ref: You killed Jesus by nailing him to a cross. But the God our ancestors worshiped raised him to life
text: Naawunni, s…Ē ti badima Ňč…Ēn daa gyeem maa: Ňč…Ēna n daa neaai Yisa kum ni. Yarima muna daa pun zaŇčŇči u n‚ÄĎkpaasi n‚ÄĎyuli doo zugu ka ku u.

ref: and made him our Leader and Savior. Then God gave him a place at his right side, so that the people of Israel would turn back to him and be forgiven.
text: Ňä…Ēn ka Naawunni mun daa duusi n‚ÄĎziili u nudirigu ya'ari ziziiya, hoo nabiiya ni tuligra. U nin ti ky…õ ka Izra…õli dima takki ba halibeoo n‚ÄĎky…õ ala'akyi, ka Naawunni naayi ky…õ ba tumbeeri taalli m‚ÄĎp…õŇčŇči ba.

ref: We are here to tell you about all this, and so is the Holy Spirit, who is God's gift to everyone who obeys God.
text: Tirima n ny…õ ban nya ka wum anŇč…Ē zaa Ňč…Ēn daa niŇči seem la, ka ti y…õtti niriba lala; ka y…õtti zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči Naawunni Vuusim Kasi y…õla, s…Ē Naawunni Ňč…Ēn zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎti seeba ban deaai u y…õligu maa.‚ÄĚ

ref: When the council members heard this, they became so angry they wanted to kill the apostles.
text: Ban daa wum dinŇč…Ē maa, suuri daa ku ba, ka ba daa boori ni ba ku tuumudima maa.

ref: But one of the members was the Pharisee Gamaliel, a highly respected teacher. He ordered the apostles to be taken out of the room for a little while.
text: Amaa Farisis…Ē Ňč…Ēn yuuri daa boonni Gameeliy…õli daa doaai ni u t…Ē'asi la'aŇčŇču maa ni. U daa ny…õ zaaligu la karimbas…Ē daadam maa zaa Ňč…Ēn daa tisiri gyirima n‚ÄĎgaari karimbadima zaa. U daa y…õli, ni, ba yiisim niriba maa beela,

ref: Then he said to the council: Men of Israel, be careful what you do with these men.
text: ka u daa t…Ē'asi n‚ÄĎti ba n‚ÄĎy…õli, ni, ‚ÄúYa Izra…õli dima wa, ya teesim suŇčŇča m‚ÄĎbaŇčŇči yan ya n‚ÄĎzaŇčŇči d…Ēppa wa n‚ÄĎniŇči seem maa.

ref: Later, when the people of our nation were being counted, Judas from Galilee showed up. A lot of people followed him, but he was killed, and all his followers were scattered.
text: Ňä…ĒnŇč…Ē nyaaŇčŇča ka Gyuudasi, Galilii nira la, gba daa yi na, niriba kaaligu la saha, ka daa zaŇčŇči ninvugseeba ka ba daa bu sakki G…Ēmnanti y…õligu. Ba daa ku u gba ka u nyaand…Ēliba zaa daa wurigi.

ref: So I advise you to stay away from these men. Leave them alone. If what they are planning is something of their own doing, it will fail.
text: Dizugu, y…õtt…Ē'aseaa kan z…õya wa ni, man ya n‚ÄĎkpaamni ya, ni, ya ky…õli d…Ēppa wa m‚ÄĎbasi, ka ba kyaŇčŇči. Dama ba y…õligu, ni ba tuma maa, yi ny…õ la ninsaala dinni, a nin lu.

ref: But if God is behind it, you cannot stop it anyway, unless you want to fight against God. The council members agreed with what he said,
text: Amaa a yi yi la Naawunni saani na, ya ku toaai n‚ÄĎgoaai niriba wa. Di ya n‚ÄĎky…õ ka ya zabri ni Naawunni!‚ÄĚ Ka u y…õtt…Ē'a wa daa ky…õ ka ba takki ba teesa.

ref: and they called the apostles back in. They had them beaten with a whip and warned them not to speak in the name of Jesus. Then they let them go.
text: Ka ba daa booli tuumudima maa na n‚ÄĎfeebi ba, ka daa y…õli ba, ni, ba di laa n‚ÄĎt…Ē'asi Yisa yuuri y…õla, ka daa ky…õ ka ba bakki n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči.

ref: The apostles left the council and were happy, because God had considered them worthy to suffer for the sake of Jesus.
text: Ba mun daa yi la'aŇčŇču maa ni ni sufumaasim, dama ba daa sumsi ni ba diisi ba vi Yisa yuuri maa zugu.

ref: Every day they spent time in the temple and in one home after another. They never stopped teaching and telling the good news that Jesus is the Messiah.
text: Beoo kam ba daa b…õ GyeeŇčŇču Yiri maa ni, ni niriba yiya puuni, ka daa bu ky…õ wulisigu; ba daa na kpalim m‚ÄĎmoonni la lahabasuŇčŇču, ni, Yisa n ny…õ Masiiya - Masis…Ē Naawunni Ňč…Ēn tum na ka laa n‚ÄĎdiisi u naam.

ref: A lot of people were now becoming followers of the Lord. But some of the ones who spoke Greek started complaining about the ones who spoke Aramaic. They complained that the Greek-speaking widows were not given their share when the food supplies were handed out each day.
text: Sahanseaa Yisa nyaand…Ēliba maa Ňč…Ēn daa zoaari m‚ÄĎpaasiri la, nangbankpeemni n daa lu ba puuni; di daa ny…õ la seeba ban t…Ē'asiri Griiktiri la ni ban t…Ē'asiri Hibruuli la sunsukkuni. Griiktiri dima maa daa galimni la Hibruuli dima maa, ni, ‚ÄúBan yi t…Ētti diibu n‚ÄĎtisiri dabsa zaa la, ti puk…Ēya bu nyari ba t…Ērli.‚ÄĚ

ref: The twelve apostles called the whole group of followers together and said, ‚ÄúWe should not give up preaching God's message in order to serve at tables.
text: Lala zugu ka tuumudima piiya ni ayi la daa booli nyaand…Ēliba zaa ni ba la'amma taaba, ka y…õli ba, ni, ‚ÄúTirima ky…õli Naawunni y…õligu tuma ka g…Ēsiri diibu y…õla, bee? Ay! - lala ka soori!

ref: My friends, choose seven men who are respected and wise and filled with God's Spirit. We will put them in charge of these things.
text: Dizugu, ti mabiisi, ya yiisim ya niriba ay…Ēp…Ēi, seeba ban b…õ ninvugsuma m‚ÄĎmari Naawunni Sii Kasi yiiko, ni y…õm; ka ti ti ky…õ ka ba su diibu tuma maa.

ref: We can spend our time praying and serving God by preaching.‚ÄĚ
text: Ka tirima mun zaŇčŇči ti sufu zaa n‚ÄĎniŇčni Naawunni puusigu ni lahabasuŇčŇču wulisigu ni.‚ÄĚ

ref: This suggestion pleased everyone, and they began by choosing Stephen. He had great faith and was filled with the Holy Spirit. Then they chose Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and also Nicolaus, who worshiped with the Jewish people in Antioch.
text: Ka ba zaa daa sakki n‚ÄĎti y…õligu wa, ka daa yiisi Sitiivin Ňč…Ēn daa mari y…õdda pam ni Vuusim Kasi yiiko la, ni Filipi ni Pir…Ēky…Ērusi, ni Nikanoori, ni Taimoon, ni Paam…õnaasi, ni Nik…Ēlaasi Ňč…Ēn daa yi Antiy…Ēkku ni na la, ka daa pun yi Griika dima gyeeŇčŇču soori la ni, ni u ti d…Ēli Y…õhudiya dima dinni.

ref: These men were brought to the apostles. Then the apostles prayed and placed their hands on the men to show they had been chosen to do this work.
text: Niriba wa ka ba daa zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎwulisiri tuumudima maa, ka ba daa susi Naawunni n‚ÄĎti ba ka zaŇčŇči ba nuusi m‚ÄĎpa ba zugri n‚ÄĎdiisi ba tuma maa.

ref: God's message spread, and many more people in Jerusalem became followers. Even a large number of priests put their faith in the Lord. Stephen Is Arrested
text: T…Ē; ka di nyaaŇčŇča ka Naawunni wulisigu wumpu maa daa kur y…õligri ni, ka ban daa d…Ēli Yisa soori maa kaaligu daa n…Ēbi Gy…õrusal…õm puuni, halli ka Y…õhudiyadima maalimaanniba pam daa sakki n‚ÄĎdeaai Yisa y…õdda.

ref: God gave Stephen the power to work great miracles and wonders among the people.
text: Sitiivin muna - Naawunni daa zaŇčŇči gyirima ni yiiko n‚ÄĎti u, ka u daa tum alahazibu tunkara ka Y…õhudiyadima niriba daa nya.

ref: But some men from Cyrene and Alexandria were members of a group who called themselves ‚ÄúFree Men.‚ÄĚ They started arguing with Stephen. Some others from Cilicia and Asia also argued with him.
text: Amaa ninvugseeba daa doaai n‚ÄĎŇčm…õ nangbankpeemni ni Sitiivin. Ninvugseeba ban daa la'amni ban daa boonni ‚ÄėSumaŇčŇča La'aŇčŇču Duu‚Äô la ni maa n daa ny…õ ba, ni Sairiini dima ni Al…õkzandiriya dima, ni seeba ban daa yi Silisii na, ni ∆źseaa.

ref: But they were no match for Stephen, who spoke with the great wisdom that the Spirit gave him.
text: Amaa ba daa bu nyaŇčŇči Sitiivin, dama Naawunni Siiya y…õm daa b…õ u y…õtt…Ē'a maa ni.

ref: So they talked some men into saying, ‚ÄúWe heard Stephen say terrible things against Moses and God!‚ÄĚ
text: Dizugu ba daa b…Ē d…Ēseeba ni ba y…õlima, ni, ba wumiya ka Sitiivin t…Ē'asiri n‚ÄĎgalimni Musa y…õla, halli ni Naawunni y…õla gba.

ref: They turned the people and their leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses against Stephen. Then they all grabbed him and dragged him in front of the council.
text: Ban daa t…Ē'asi ba ziri y…õtt…Ē'a lala maa, ba daa ky…õ ka niriba zaa daa doaai n‚ÄĎgbaai Sitiivin, ban ni ba kpamma ni ba karimbadima maa m‚ÄĎpaasi, m‚ÄĎmari u n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči tiŇčŇča maa kpambaya saani ni ba kari u sariya.

ref: Some men agreed to tell lies about Stephen, and they said, ‚ÄúThis man keeps on saying terrible things about this holy temple and the Law of Moses.
text: Ba daa ky…õ ka ninvugseeba kp…õ na n‚ÄĎt…Ē'asi m‚ÄĎma'ai ziri, ni, ‚ÄúDoo wa kur t…Ē'asiri ni n‚ÄĎgalimni Naawunni GyeeŇčŇču Yiri la, ni zaaligu maa.

ref: We have heard him claim that Jesus from Nazareth will destroy this place and change the customs Moses gave us.‚ÄĚ
text: Ti wumiya ka u y…õli Yisa Ňč…Ēn daa ny…õ Nazar…õti nira la y…õla, ni, Ňč…Ēna nin sa'am Naawunni GyeeŇčŇču Yiri wa, ka takki ti kaya seesi Musa Ňč…Ēn daa zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎti ti la.‚ÄĚ

ref: Then all the council members stared at Stephen. They saw that his face looked like the face of an angel.
text: Ka seeba ban daa zi sariya karibu zii la ni zaa nini daa g…Ēsiri la Sitiivin la, ka ba daa ti nya u nindaa maa, ka ka Ňčmanni la hoo malaaka dinni.

ref: The high priest asked Stephen, ‚ÄúAre they telling the truth about you?‚ÄĚ
text: Ka di saha maa ka GyeeŇčŇču Toondaana maa daa boosi Sitiivin, ni, ‚ÄúDi zaa ny…õ la y…õlimaŇčni, bee?‚ÄĚ

ref: Stephen answered: Friends, listen to me. Our glorious God appeared to our ancestor Abraham while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he had moved to Haran.
text: Ka Sitiivin daa gy…õrigi n‚ÄĎy…õli, ‚ÄúM badima, ni m mabiisi, ya wumma! Ti yaaba Ibrahim Ňč…Ēn daa b…õ M…õs…Ēp…Ēteemiya tingbaŇčŇču ni la, ka na bu yisigi n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči Kyaraani tiŇčŇča - di saha ka Naawunni, Gyirima Daana, daa kasigi u maŇčŇča n‚ÄĎwulisi u lala saha maa,

ref: God told him, ‚ÄúLeave your country and your relatives and go to a land that I will show you.‚ÄĚ
text: nti y…õli u, ni, ‚ÄėKy…õl i tingbaŇčŇču wa, ni i d…Ē'am zaa, ka bakki n‚ÄĎky…õ tingbanseaa man ya wulisi i maa na.‚Äô

ref: Then Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After his father died, Abraham came and settled in this land where you now live.
text: Di saha u daa ky…õ u tiŇčŇča maa, Kalidii dima tiŇčŇča la, ka daa kyaŇčŇči nti b…õ Kyaraani tiŇčŇča. U ba Ňč…Ēn daa ti kpi, Naawunni daa ky…õ ka u yi b…õni ka gaari m‚ÄĎpaai la tingbanseaa yan na b…õ ni la.

ref: God didn't give him any part of it, not even a square meter. But God did promise to give it to him and his family forever, even though Abraham didn't have any children.
text: Ka daa bu ti u tingbanseaa zaa ni u b…õ ni n‚ÄĎsu ka, halli beela; amaa ka daa y…õli u, ni, u nin zaŇčŇči tingbaŇčŇču maa n‚ÄĎti u ni u nyaaŇčŇča zaa, ka ba nin ti di ka faari n‚ÄĎsu, halli ni di saha maa ti yaaba Ibrahim daa ka biiya.

ref: God said Abraham's descendants would live for a while in a foreign land. There they would be slaves and would be mistreated
text: Naawunni daa y…õli kanŇč…Ē y…õtt…Ē'a, ni, u ya'ansi nin ti b…õ tinseelli ni, ka tiŇčŇča maa niriba nin zaŇčŇči ba n‚ÄĎky…õ ka ba leebi ba daaba n‚ÄĎniŇči ba wahala yuuma k…Ēbsinaasi.

ref: But he also said, ‚ÄúI will punish the nation that makes them slaves. Then later they will come and worship me in this place.‚ÄĚ
text: Ka laa n‚ÄĎy…õli Ibrahim, ni, ‚ÄėAmaa man nin ti ky…õ ninvugseeba ban su ba maa saani na ka darigi ba tuba p…Ēi, ka naayi ky…õ ka i niriba maa yi na m‚ÄĎb…õ kpa n‚ÄĎgyeemni ma.‚Äô

ref: God said to Abraham, ‚ÄúEvery son in each family must be circumcised to show you have kept your agreement with me.‚ÄĚ So when Isaac was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him. Later, Isaac circumcised his son Jacob, and Jacob circumcised his twelve sons.
text: Ka Naawunni daa zaali d…Ēppa gunibu kaari, ni di ti wulisi Ňč…Ēn ni Ibrahim Ňč…Ēn loori la taaba nangbanyinni seem. T…Ē - ka dizugu ban daa d…Ē'ai Ibrahim bii Yisahaaku sahaseaa la, ba daa guni u daba anii daari maa; Yisahaaku bii mun daa ny…õ Yaakubu, ka Yaakubu mun daa d…Ē'ai u bidibsi pii ni ayi la, tin boonni seeba Izra…õli za'an…Ēya kpamma maa.

ref: These men were our ancestors. Joseph was also one of our famous ancestors. His brothers were jealous of him and sold him as a slave to be taken to Egypt. But God was with him
text: Ka kpamma maa daa ti zabi nyuuri n‚ÄĎniŇči ba tinz…Ē Yisifu, n‚ÄĎdaa ti zaŇčŇči u n‚ÄĎkoosi n‚ÄĎti Igyipti dima. Amaa Naawunni daa zaani u nyaaŇčŇča Igyipti tingbaŇčŇču maa ni,

ref: and rescued him from all his troubles. God made him so wise that the Egyptian king Pharaoh thought highly of him. The king even made Joseph governor over Egypt and put him in charge of everything he owned.
text: n‚ÄĎyiisi u wahala zaa puuni; ka laa n‚ÄĎti u bamsim, ka ky…õ ka u nya gyirima Igyipti naaba NaaFirawuna saani. Ka Naaba daa ky…õ ka u su Ňč…Ēn NaaFirawuna yiri zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎtabli Igyipti tingbaŇčŇču maa zaa.

ref: Everywhere in Egypt and Canaan the grain crops failed. There was terrible suffering, and our ancestors could not find enough to eat.
text: Di saha ka k…Ēm daa ti lu Igyipti ni Ky…õna'an tingbana zaa ni, ka daa ti niŇči ba wahala pam; ka ti yaadima maa daa boori bundirigu ka k…ĒŇčŇči.

ref: But when Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there for the first time.
text: T…Ē, ka di saha maa ka ti yaaba Yaakubu daa ti wum, ni, kyi b…õ Igyipti tingbaŇčŇču ni; ka u daa daŇčŇči n‚ÄĎtum ti yaadima maa, ni ba kyaŇčŇči Igyipti nti da a na.

ref: It was on their second trip that Joseph told his brothers who he was, and the king learned about Joseph's family.
text: Di nyaaŇčŇča ka ba daa ti laa n‚ÄĎdoaai n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči buyi ky…õnni. Di saha maa ka Yisifu daa zaŇčŇči u maŇčŇča n‚ÄĎwulisi u b…õya dima ka ba daa baŇčŇči u, ka NaaFirawuna gba daa baŇčŇči Yisifu b…õya maa Ňč…Ēn ny…õ seeba.

ref: His father went to Egypt and died there, just as our ancestors did.
text: Ka Yaakubu daa yisigi n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči nti zinni Igyipti, halli ka Ňč…Ēn ni ti yaakpamma zaa daa ti ka ni.

ref: Later their bodies were taken back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had bought from the sons of Hamor.
text: Ka ba daa zi ba n‚ÄĎlabi S…õkyeem tiŇčŇča na, nti s…Ē'ai ba ba gbala ziseaa ni, S…õkyeem ziseaa ka ti yaaba Ibrahim Ňč…Ēn daa zaŇčŇči anzulifa n‚ÄĎda Heem…Ēri biisi saani la.

ref: Finally, the time came for God to do what he had promised Abraham. By then the number of our people in Egypt had greatly increased.
text: T…Ē. Ka Naawunni Ňč…Ēn daa pun y…õli Ibrahim, ni, u nin suŇčŇči u seem la, ka di saha daa naayi p…Ētta; di saha maa ka ba zuliya maa daa n…Ēbi n‚ÄĎzoaai pam Igyipti tingbaŇčŇču maa ni,

ref: Another king was ruling Egypt, and he didn't know anything about Joseph.
text: halli ka d…Ēs…Ē Ňč…Ēn daa zi Yisifu y…õla daa ti di Igyipti naam,

ref: He tricked our ancestors and was cruel to them. He even made them leave their babies outside, so they would die.
text: ka daa zaŇčŇči u teesibeeri n‚ÄĎnamsiri ti yaadima ka mugsiri ba. U daa ky…õ ka ba zaŇčŇči ba bileesi m‚ÄĎbasira, ni ba ku laa n‚ÄĎnya biisi ban vu yaasa.

ref: During this time Moses was born. He was a very beautiful child, and for three months his parents took care of him in their home.
text: Di saha ka ba daa d…Ē'ai Musa, ka u daa veeli pam ka Naawunni nini tigi u y…õla; ka u ba ni u ma daa mari u duu ni halli nti paai g…Ēya ata u ba yiri.

ref: Then when they were forced to leave him outside, the king's daughter found him and raised him as her own son.
text: Di nyaaŇčŇča ka sahanseaa daa ti paai ni ba zaŇčŇči bii maa m‚ÄĎbasi, ka NaaFirawuna bipuŇčŇča daa piisi u nti wubsi n‚ÄĎzaŇčŇči u, ni u leebi u mammaŇčŇča biiya.

ref: Moses was given the best education in Egypt. He was a strong man and a powerful speaker.
text: Ka Musa daa bamsi Igyipti dima bamsim zaa nti leebi yaa daana u y…õtt…Ē'a zugu ni u tuma zugu m‚ÄĎpaasi.

ref: One day he saw an Egyptian mistreating one of them. So he rescued the man and killed the Egyptian.
text: Ka u daa kyaŇčŇči nti nya ka Igyipti d…Ēs…Ē Ňč…Ēn mugsiri Ňč…Ēn Musa d…Ē'ariba maa yinni ka di daa bu d…Ēli soori, ka u daa Ňčm…õ Igyipti doo maa n‚ÄĎku u.

ref: Moses thought the rest of his people would realize God was going to use him to set them free. But they didn't understand.
text: U daa teesiya, ni, u mabiisi nin baŇčŇči, ni, Naawunni n tum u na ni u ti fa ba m‚ÄĎbasi, amaa ba daa bu baŇčŇči lala.

ref: The next day Moses saw two of his own people fighting, and he tried to make them stop. He said, ‚ÄúMen, you are both Israelites. Why are you so cruel to each other?‚ÄĚ
text: Ka dizugu beoo Ňč…Ēn daa ti neaai ka Musa laa n‚ÄĎyi nti nya Izra…õli dima maa niriba ayi Ňč…Ēn zabra. Ka u kyaŇčŇči ni u ti goaai ba ka y…õli, ‚ÄėM mabiisi, ya baŇčŇčma, ni, ya ny…õ la mabiitiri yinni dima; ka wula ka ya Ňčmaligi n‚ÄĎzabra?‚Äô

ref: But the man who had started the fight pushed Moses aside and asked, ‚ÄúWho made you our ruler and judge?
text: Amaa Ňč…Ēn daa niŇčni u kpeaa putoo maa daa gy…õ la suuri n‚ÄĎniŇči Musa ka boosi u, ni, ‚ÄėŇä…Ēni n lee n‚ÄĎzaŇčŇči nyini n‚ÄĎleebi ti toondaana, bee, ti sarikatta?‚Äô

ref: Are you going to kill me, just as you killed that Egyptian yesterday?‚ÄĚ
text: ni, ‚ÄėI boori la i ku ma, hoo nyin sa ku Igyipti doo la seem maa, bee?‚Äô

ref: When Moses heard this, he ran away to live in the country of Midian. His two sons were born there.
text: T…Ē; ka Musa Ňč…Ēn daa wum lala maa, ka u daa z…Ē n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči Midiyan tingbaŇčŇču ni. B…õni ka u daa kpalim m‚ÄĎb…õ ni, halli nti d…Ē'ai u bidibsi ayi.

ref: Forty years later, an angel appeared to Moses from a burning bush in the desert near Mount Sinai.
text: Di yuuma pisinaasi nyaaŇčŇča ka malaaka daa pusi n‚ÄĎyi Musa zugu na, yoo kan yuuri boonni Sainayi la ni, n‚ÄĎŇčmanni hoo bugum n‚ÄĎdiri tutuuri puuni.

ref: Moses was surprised by what he saw. He went closer to get a better look, and the Lord said,

text: Ka Gyeemsi daa laa n‚ÄĎy…õli, ni, ‚ÄúMan teesa ny…õ la nŇč…Ēa: ninvugseeba ban pa Y…õhudiyadima la yi ti niŇči tuuba n‚ÄĎd…Ēli Yisa, di bu sumni ni ti daamni ba y…õlimugsa.

text: See ka ti s…Ēbi gbaŇčŇču n‚ÄĎti ba, ni, ba di Ňč…Ēbi numda'aseesi ban k…Ērigi m‚ÄĎmaanni ba'a ni buga la, bee, a seesi an bu k…Ērigi, bee, an na mari ziim; ka ba di tum tumbeeri zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči d…Ēgoora ni p…Ē'agoora y…õla.

ref: We must remember that the Law of Moses has been preached in city after city for many years, and every Sabbath it is read in our synagogues. A Letter to Gentiles Who Had Faith in the Lord
text: Dama ba daa kur t…Ē'asiri Musa y…õla ti yaadima saha halli ni zuna; ba karimni a ni tinsi kam zaa puuni, ka moonni a Vuusim Dabsiri kam, la'aŇčŇču duri puuni.‚ÄĚ

ref: The apostles, the leaders, and all the church members decided to send some men to Antioch along with Paul and Barnabas. They chose Silas and Judas Barsabbas, who were two leaders of the Lord's followers.
text: T…Ē; ka ba zaa daa la'am taaba - tuumudima maa, ni kpamma, zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči ban niŇči Yisa y…õdda maa zaa - ka daa gbaai ba gbaari, ni, ba ya n‚ÄĎyiisi la ba ninvugseeba n‚ÄĎtum ba Antiy…Ēkku, ban ni P…Ēla ni Baanabasi. Ka dizugu ba daa yiisi la Gyuudasi , ni Sailasi; ban mun daa b…õ la Yisa nyaand…Ēliba toondaandima sunsukkuni.

ref: They wrote a letter that said: We apostles and leaders send friendly greetings to all of you Gentiles who are followers of the Lord in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia.
text: Ban ka ba daa t…Ēlim gbaŇčŇču maa, ni#:- ‚ÄúNnya, tirima, ya mabiseeba ban ny…õ Yisa tuumudima ni kpamma la, m puusiri ya: yaseeba ban ny…õ ti mabiisi, halli ka ya pa la ti Y…õhudiyataaba la, ka b…õ la Antiy…Ēkku tiŇčŇča, zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči Siriiya ni Silisii tingbana la zaa.

ref: We have heard that some people from here have terribly upset you by what they said. But we did not send them!
text: Ti wumiya, ni, seeba daa yi ti saani m‚ÄĎpaai ya na - tirima mun daa bu tum ba - ka daa daamni ya n‚ÄĎsa'amni ya sufu ni y…õtt…Ē'aseesi ban y…õtti maa.

ref: So we met together and decided to choose some men and to send them to you along with our good friends Barnabas and Paul.
text: Dizugu ka ti la'am taaba n‚ÄĎsakki n‚ÄĎgbaai, ni, ti yiisi ninvugseeba n‚ÄĎtum ya saani na, m‚ÄĎpaasi P…Ēla ni Baanabasi zugu. Halli ni P…Ēla ni Baanabasi gba ny…õ la ti z…Ēsuma,

ref: These men have risked their lives for our Lord Jesus Christ.
text: ka daa zaŇčŇči ba ny…Ēvuya m‚ÄĎpa talima ti Dugma Yisa yuuri maa zugu.

ref: We are also sending Judas and Silas, who will tell you in person the same things that we are writing.
text: Dizugu ti tumni la Gyuudasi ni Sailasi ni ba zaŇčŇči y…õtt…Ē'a m‚ÄĎpaasi tin s…Ēbi seelli wa, n‚ÄĎwulisiri di gbinni.

ref: The Holy Spirit has shown us that we should not place any extra burden on you.
text: T…Ē: Naawunni Siiya Vuusim Kasi Ňč…Ēn gbaai n‚ÄĎzaali seem nŇč…Ēa - ka ti gba sakki lala: ni, ti di daamni ya m‚ÄĎpaasi y…õtt…Ē'akpana ayi-ata wa#:-

ref: But you should not eat anything offered to idols. You should not eat any meat that still has the blood in it or any meat of any animal that has been strangled. You must also not commit any terrible sexual sins. If you follow these instructions, you will do well. We send our best wishes.
text: ni, ya di Ňč…Ēbi numda'aseesi ban k…Ērigi m‚ÄĎmaanni ba'a ni buga la, bee, seesi an bu k…Ērigi, bee, an na mari ziim; ya di tum tumbeeri zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči d…Ēgoora ni p…Ē'agoora y…õla ni. Ya yi gbaai ya mansi ka ky…õ anŇč…Ē maa, ya nin niŇči suŇčŇča. Naawunni paasim ya goom.‚ÄĚ

ref: The four men left Jerusalem and went to Antioch. Then they called the church members together and gave them the letter.
text: T…Ē; ka d…Ēppa maa daa kyaŇčŇči m‚ÄĎpaai Antiy…Ēkku na, ka daa la'asi y…õdda dima maa zaa nti ti ba gbaŇčŇču maa.

ref: When the letter was read, everyone was pleased and greatly encouraged.
text: Ban daa karim ka maa, di daa niŇči ba sufumaasim ka laa n‚ÄĎkpamsi ba sufu pam.

ref: Judas and Silas were prophets, and they spoke a long time, encouraging and helping the Lord's followers.
text: Gyuudasi ni Sailasi gba daa ny…õ la anabidima, ka ba daa t…Ē'asi y…õtt…Ē'anya'asa pam n‚ÄĎkpamsiri ba mabiisi maa.

ref: The men from Jerusalem stayed on in Antioch for a while. And when they left to return to the ones who had sent them, the followers wished them well.
text: Ban daa niŇči saha beela b…õni, Yisa nyaand…Ēliba maa daa zaali ba soori, ka taaliseaa ka ni. Ba daa labi n‚ÄĎkuli ban daa tum ba maa saani.

ref: But Paul and Barnabas stayed on in Antioch, where they and many others taught and preached about the Lord. Paul and Barnabas Go Their Separate Ways
text: P…Ēla ni Baanabasi mun daa kpalim m‚ÄĎb…õ Antiy…Ēkku timpuuni, ka ban ni ba taaba pam daa wulisiri Naawunni y…õligu ka moonni lahabasuŇčŇču maa.

ref: Sometime later Paul said to Barnabas, ‚ÄúLet's go back and visit the Lord's followers in the cities where we preached his message. Then we will know how they are doing.‚ÄĚ
text: T…Ē. Saha Ňč…Ēn daa ti gaari beela, P…Ēla daa y…õli Baanabasi, ni, ‚ÄúKy…õ ka ti labi n‚ÄĎkaai tinseesi tin daa pun kyaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎwulisi Naawunni y…õtt…Ē'a maa, n‚ÄĎnya ti mabiisi maa Ňč…Ēn b…õ seem.‚ÄĚ

ref: Barnabas wanted to take along John, whose other name was Mark.
text: Ka di saha ka Baanabasi daa boori ni u zaŇčŇči Gy…Ēni, s…Ē Ňč…Ēn boonni Maaki la, ka ba kyaŇčŇči.

ref: But Paul did not want to, because Mark had left them in Pamphylia and had stopped working with them.
text: Amaa P…Ēla daa bu sakki ni ba zaŇčŇči u, Ňč…Ēn daa bakki ka ky…õ ba Pamfilii tingbaŇčŇču ni maa zugu, ka daa bu laa n‚ÄĎd…Ēli ba n‚ÄĎla'amni n‚ÄĎtumna.

ref: Paul and Barnabas argued, then each of them went his own way. Barnabas took Mark and sailed to Cyprus,
text: Ka ba daa Ňčm…õ nangbankpeemni dizugu, halli ka daa w…Ēligi ka ky…õ taaba. Ka dinzugu Baanabasi daa zaŇčŇči Maaki ka ba kp…õ nyariŇčŇču n‚ÄĎdu n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči Saapirusi.

ref: but Paul took Silas and left after the followers had placed them in God's care.
text: P…Ēla mun daa zaŇčŇči la Sailasi m‚ÄĎbakki; nyaand…Ēliba maa daa zaŇčŇči ba n‚ÄĎniŇči Naawunni nuu ni ka kyeoosi ba.

ref: They traveled through Syria and Cilicia, encouraging the churches.
text: Ka ba daa d…Ēli la Siriiya ni Silisiiya n‚ÄĎkpamsiri ban niŇči y…õdda n‚ÄĎla'amni n‚ÄĎgyeemni Yisa maa.

ref: Paul and Silas went back to Derbe and Lystra, where there was a follower named Timothy. His mother was also a follower. She was Jewish, and his father was Greek.
text: Ka P…Ēla daa g…Ē n‚ÄĎg…Ē, halli nti paai D…õribee ni Lisitira tinsi. B…õni ka Yisa nyaand…Ēlis…Ē daa b…õ ni, ka u yuuri boonni Tim…Ēti. U ma gba daa niŇči Yisa y…õdda, ka mun daa ny…õ Y…õhudiyap…Ē'a, amaa u ba daa ny…õ la Griika doowa.

ref: The Lord's followers in Lystra and Iconium said good things about Timothy,
text: Di saha maa ka y…õddaniŇčniba ban b…õ Lisitira ni Aikoonyum la daa y…õtti, ni, Tim…Ēti ny…õ la ninvugsuŇčŇču.

ref: and Paul wanted him to go with them. But Paul first had him circumcised, because all the Jewish people around there knew that Timothy's father was Greek.
text: P…Ēla daa boori ni Tim…Ēti d…Ēli u ka ba kyaŇčŇči. Ka lala zugu u daa guni u. Y…õhudiyadima ban daa b…õ b…õni zugu ka u daa niŇči lala, dama ba zaa baŇčŇči, ni, Tim…Ēti ba daa ny…õ la Griika nira.

ref: As Paul and the others went from city to city, they told the followers what the apostles and leaders in Jerusalem had decided, and they urged them to follow these instructions.
text: T…Ē; ka ba daa d…Ēli tinsi maa, ka daa y…õli Yisa niriba, ni, ba kpaŇčŇčma ba mansi n‚ÄĎsakki zaaliseesi tuumudima ni kpamma ban b…õ Gy…õrusal…õm maa Ňč…Ēn daa zaali n‚ÄĎti ba la, ka d…Ēli a suŇčŇčasuŇčŇča.

ref: The churches became stronger in their faith, and each day more people put their faith in the Lord. Paul's Vision in Troas
text: Dizugu ban daa la'amni n‚ÄĎd…Ēli Yisa maa daa mari yaa ni y…õdda, ka ba daa zoaari m‚ÄĎpaasiri sahakam.

ref: Paul and his friends went through Phrygia and Galatia, but the Holy Spirit would not let them preach in Asia.
text: T…Ē: ka ba daa g…Ē n‚ÄĎd…Ēli Firigyii ni Gal…õseaa tingbana dama Naawunni Sii SuŇčŇču daa bu ti ba soori ni ba t…Ē'asiri lahabaari maa ∆źseaa tingbaŇčŇču maa ni.

ref: After they arrived in Mysia, they tried to go into Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus would not let them.
text: Lala zugu ba daa d…Ēli nti paai Misii tingbaŇčŇču maa t…Ēriga. Ba daa y…õnni, ni, ba nin kp…õ Bitinii tingbaŇčŇču maa ni, amaa Yisa Siiya daa bu sakki lala n‚ÄĎti ba.

ref: So they went on through Mysia until they came to Troas.
text: Lala zugu ba daa l…Ēkki n‚ÄĎgaari Misiiya nti paai Tir…Ē'asi tiŇčŇča.

ref: During the night, Paul had a vision of someone from Macedonia who was standing there and begging him, ‚ÄúCome over to Macedonia and help us!‚ÄĚ
text: Di daari yuŇčŇču maa P…Ēla daa zaasi zaasimni, n‚ÄĎnya Masidoonya nira Ňč…Ēn z…õya m‚ÄĎb…õlimni u, ni, ‚ÄúKy…õm Masidoonya na nti suŇčŇči ti!‚ÄĚ

ref: After Paul had seen the vision, we began looking for a way to go to Macedonia. We were sure that God had called us to preach the good news there. Lydia Becomes a Follower of the Lord
text: Ka P…Ēla Ňč…Ēn daa nya zaasimni kanŇč…Ēa zugu, ti daa s…õ'asi ya'abi ni ti yi b…õni n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči Masidoonya tingbaŇčŇču, dama ti daa teesi, ni, Naawunni n daa booli ti ni ti mooli lahabasuŇčŇču maa n‚ÄĎtisi b…õni niriba maa.

ref: We sailed straight from Troas to Samothrace, and the next day we arrived in Neapolis.
text: Ka dizugu ti daa kp…õ la nyariŇčŇču n‚ÄĎyi Tir…Ē'asi n‚ÄĎdu n‚ÄĎl…ĒŇčŇči m‚ÄĎpaai Sam…Ētreesi, ka beoo Ňč…Ēn daa ti kaai ti daa gaari m‚ÄĎpaai Niyap…Ēlisi tiŇčŇča.

ref: From there we went to Philippi, which is a Roman colony in the first district of Macedonia. We spent several days in Philippi.
text: N‚ÄĎdaa yi b…õni n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči m‚ÄĎpaai Filipaai - kana n ny…õ Masidoonya ya'ari wa tinsi toondaana, ka ny…õ la Room dima suulinsi tiŇčŇča. B…õni ka ti daa yuui beela.

ref: Then on the Sabbath we went outside the city gate to a place by the river, where we thought there would be a Jewish meeting place for prayer. We sat down and talked with the women who came.
text: Ka Vuusim Dabsiri daari maa, ti daa yi tiŇčŇča maa puuni n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči kuliga ya'ari; b…õni ka ti daa teesi, ni, Y…õhudiyadima maa daa la'amni n‚ÄĎgyeemna. Ti daa zinni b…õni n‚ÄĎt…Ē'asiri n‚ÄĎtiri p…Ē'aseeba ban daa la'am b…õni maa.

ref: One of them was Lydia, who was from the city of Thyatira and sold expensive purple cloth. She was a worshiper of the Lord God, and he made her willing to accept what Paul was saying.
text: Ka p…Ē'as…Ē daa b…õ b…õni ka u yuuri boonni Liidiya. U daa yi la Tayatayira tiŇčŇča ni na, ka daa koosiri kyinkyinsuma an ligri daa zoaai. Pa Y…õhudiyap…Ē'a n daa ny…õ u, amaa u daa d…Ēli Y…õhudiyadima soori n‚ÄĎgyeemni Naawunni. Naawunni daa ky…õ ka u nini neaai, ka u daa wum P…Ēla y…õtt…Ē'a maa ka sakki.

ref: Then after she and her family were baptized, she kept on begging us, ‚ÄúIf you think I really do have faith in the Lord, come stay in my home.‚ÄĚ Finally, we accepted her invitation. Paul and Silas Are Put in Jail
text: Ka Ňč…Ēn ni u yiri dima zaa daa deaai Naawunni koom suubu, ka u daa susi ti, ni, ti ky…õm na nti b…õ u yiri. Ni, ‚ÄúYa yi siri baŇčŇči, ni, n ny…õ la y…õdda daana n‚ÄĎti ti Dugma, yan ky…õm na.‚ÄĚ Ka Ňč…Ēn daa b…õlim ti lala zugu daa ky…õ ka ti sakki lala n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči.

ref: One day on our way to the place of prayer, we were met by a slave girl. She had a spirit in her that gave her the power to tell the future. By doing this she made a lot of money for her owners.
text: Dindahinseaa ka ti daa ti wa puusigu ziiya, ka daa ti tuusi p…Ē'asaris…Ē Ňč…Ēn daa ny…õ daabri, ka daa mari arizinni Ňč…Ēn daa ny…õ ba'ak…Ēligu dinni ka daa ky…õ ka p…Ē'a maa bugri m‚ÄĎbaŇčŇčni y…õla. Lala tuma zugu ka baseeba ban daa su p…Ē'a maa daa nyari ligri pam u bugbu maa ni.

ref: The girl followed Paul and the rest of us, and she kept yelling, ‚ÄúThese men are servants of the Most High God! They are telling you how to be saved.‚ÄĚ
text: Ka p…Ē'a wa daa d…Ēli P…Ēla nyaaŇčŇča n‚ÄĎkaasiri n‚ÄĎy…õtta, ‚ÄúNiriba wa ny…õ la Naawunni Ňč…Ēn ny…õ yiiko zaa daana la tuntumniba. Ba y…õtti ya la yan nin niŇči seem n‚ÄĎnya tuliginsim!‚ÄĚ

ref: This went on for several days. Finally, Paul got so upset that he turned and said to the spirit, ‚ÄúIn the name of Jesus Christ, I order you to leave this girl alone!‚ÄĚ At once the evil spirit left her.
text: U daa kpalim n‚ÄĎd…Ēla, ka daa kur t…Ē'asiri lala dabsa pam. U daa ka'ali P…Ēla pam, halli ka u daa Ňčmaligi n‚ÄĎy…õli arizinni maa, ni, ‚ÄúYisa Masiiya yuuri zugu ka n y…õtti i - yim na ka ky…õ u!‚ÄĚ Ka arizinni maa daa siri yi na ya'abi.

ref: When the girl's owners realized they had lost all chances for making more money, they grabbed Paul and Silas and dragged them into court.
text: T…Ē, ka ban daa su p…Ē'asariga maa Ňč…Ēn daa ti baŇčŇči, ni, ba ku laa n‚ÄĎtoaai n‚ÄĎnya u bugbu ligri la, ba daa gbaai P…Ēla ni Sailasi n‚ÄĎdaai ba n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči timpuuni nti niŇči tiŇčŇča zuguraandima nuu ni.

ref: They are telling us to do things we Romans are not allowed to do.‚ÄĚ
text: Ba wulisira s…Ēseesi an bu sumni ni ti wumna, bee, ti d…Ēla; dama Room niriba n ny…õ ti.‚ÄĚ

ref: The crowd joined in the attack on Paul and Silas. Then the officials tore the clothes off the two men and ordered them to be beaten with a whip.
text: Di saha ka daadam pam daa la'am n‚ÄĎgalimni P…Ēla ni Sailasi, ka tiŇčŇča maa sariyakattiba daa zaŇčŇči ba n‚ÄĎtoaai ba kpari n‚ÄĎyeaai ka ky…õ ka ba feebi ba daari.

ref: After they had been badly beaten, they were put in jail, and the jailer was told to guard them carefully.
text: Ka dizugu ba daa bu ba pam, ka zaŇčŇči ba n‚ÄĎniŇči sarika ni; ka y…õli sarikadaana maa, ni, u kparim ba suŇčŇča.

ref: The jailer did as he was told. He put them deep inside the jail and chained their feet to heavy blocks of wood.
text: Ka sarikadaana Ňč…Ēn daa wum lala, u daa deaai ba n‚ÄĎzaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎniŇči sarika dutoonni la ni, ka ba ba n…Ēba ni datibsiri.

ref: About midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing praises to God, while the other prisoners listened.
text: T…Ē; ka yuntasuusi ka P…Ēla ni Sailasi daa puusiri Naawunni ka yuumni yuuma n‚ÄĎtiri u, ka ba dansarikataaba daa wumsira.

ref: Suddenly a strong earthquake shook the jail to its foundations. The doors opened, and the chains fell from all the prisoners.
text: Di saha ka tiŇčŇča maa daa dam, ka dansarika yiri maa daa miimna, halli ni ka nyeeba maa gba. Ka ka duri maa za'an…Ēya maa daa yoosi, ka ban daa ga ba n…Ēba ky…Ērimidima la zaa daa gaasi n‚ÄĎlu.

ref: When the jailer woke up and saw that the doors were open, he thought that the prisoners had escaped. He pulled out his sword and was about to kill himself.
text: Ka dansarikadaana maa Ňč…Ēn daa neaai goom ni n‚ÄĎnya ka duri maa za'an…Ēya la zaa daa yoosi, u daa teesi, ni, dansarika dima maa zaa z…Ēya, ka dizugu u daa foaai u takoobi, ni u ku u maŇčŇča.

ref: But Paul shouted, ‚ÄúDon't harm yourself! No one has escaped.‚ÄĚ
text: Amaa P…Ēla daa tasi pampam n‚ÄĎy…õli, ‚ÄúDi niŇči i maŇčŇča seelli! Ti zaa na b…õ kpa!‚ÄĚ

ref: The jailer asked for a torch and went into the jail. He was shaking all over as he knelt down in front of Paul and Silas.
text: T…Ē, ka dansarikadaana maa daa booli, ni, ba mari bugum na, ka daa kp…õ yooma na. U daa z…Ēri dabeem n‚ÄĎgyigra, ka gbaani tiŇčŇča n‚ÄĎti P…Ēla ni Sailasi.

ref: After he had led them out of the jail, he asked, ‚ÄúWhat must I do to be saved?‚ÄĚ
text: Ka u daa mari ba n‚ÄĎyi na; ka boosi ba, ni, ‚ÄúM Badima, n ya n‚ÄĎniŇči la wula n‚ÄĎnya tuliginsim?‚ÄĚ

ref: They replied, ‚ÄúHave faith in the Lord Jesus and you will be saved! This is also true for everyone who lives in your home.‚ÄĚ
text: Ka ba daa y…õli u, ni, ‚ÄúNiŇčma ti Dugma Yisa y…õdda, ka i nin nya tuliginsim - nyin ni i yiri dima zaa.‚ÄĚ

ref: Then Paul and Silas told him and everyone else in his house about the Lord.
text: Di saha ka ba daa t…Ē'asi Naawunni y…õla maa n‚ÄĎtisi Ňč…Ēn ni u yiri dima maa.

ref: While it was still night, the jailer took them to a place where he could wash their cuts and bruises. Then he and everyone in his home were baptized.
text: Ka dirindaari maa yuŇčŇču ka dansarikadaana maa daa zaŇčŇči ba m‚ÄĎpeaai ba n…Ēra la n‚ÄĎti ba, ka Ňč…Ēn ni u yiri dima zaa daa deaai Naawunni koom suubu.

ref: They were very glad they had put their faith in God. After this, the jailer took Paul and Silas to his home and gave them something to eat.
text: Ka daa mari P…Ēla ni Sailasi n‚ÄĎkp…õ u yiri maa na nti ti ba bundirigu ka ba di: ka Ňč…Ēn ni u yiri dima zaa daa mari sufupeelim pam ba Naawunni y…õdda niŇčbu zugu.

ref: The next morning the officials sent some police with orders for the jailer to let Paul and Silas go.
text: Ka beoo Ňč…Ēn daa ti neaai, tiŇčŇča maa zuguraandima daa tum pirinsi na ka y…õli, ni, ‚ÄúYa yiisim niriba wa m‚ÄĎbasi!‚ÄĚ

ref: The jailer told Paul, ‚ÄúThe officials have ordered me to set you free. Now you can leave in peace.‚ÄĚ
text: Dizugu dansarikadaana maa daa y…õli lala n‚ÄĎti P…Ēla, ni, ‚ÄúZugudaandima maa y…õliya, ni, ti yiisim nyin ni Sailasi m‚ÄĎbasi; dizugu ya ky…õma, ka Naawunni nin paasi ya sufumaasim.‚ÄĚ

ref: But Paul told the police, ‚ÄúWe are Roman citizens, and the Roman officials had us beaten in public without giving us a trial. They threw us into jail. Now do they think they can secretly send us away? No, they cannot! They will have to come here themselves and let us out.‚ÄĚ
text: Amaa P…Ēla daa labsi n‚ÄĎy…õli ba, ni, ‚ÄúHalli ba sa bu t…Ē'asi ti y…õligu gba amaa ba sa bu ti zama maa tooni, tirima mun ny…õ la Room suulinsi niriba, ka sa zaŇčŇči ti n‚ÄĎkpeesi dansarika ni. Ka zuna ba yi boori la ba s…Ē'ai m‚ÄĎbasi ti baalimm, fa! Ti ku sakki, walla! Faasee ka ba mammansi ky…õ na n‚ÄĎyiisi ti m‚ÄĎbasi.‚ÄĚ

ref: When the police told the officials that Paul and Silas were Roman citizens, the officials were afraid.
text: T…Ē; ka pirinsi maa daa labi nti y…õli lala n‚ÄĎti zuguraandima maa. Ka ban daa wum, ni, P…Ēla ni Sailasi ny…õ la Room suulinsi niriba la, dabeem daa gbaai ba.

ref: So they came and apologized. They led them out of the jail and asked them to please leave town.

N yi ti nya dahama n nin booli i na yaasa.‚ÄĚ

text: T…Ē; ka tin daa bu di diibu ka di yuui la, P…Ēla daa z…õ saazugu ba zaa sunsukkuni n‚ÄĎy…õli, ‚ÄúN z…Ēdima, ya yi daa wum n noori la ka daa bu yisigi Kyiriti tin daa b…õ seela maa, di naa n‚ÄĎs…Ēa. Lala yisigri zugu ka ti y…õla sa'am maa.

ref: But now I beg you to cheer up, because you will be safe. Only the ship will be lost.
text: Amaa pampaŇčŇč…Ē n kpamsiri ya, ni, ya ky…õ ka ya sufu d…Ēya; ya s…Ē ku basi y…Ēri, kulla nyariŇčŇču maa k…Ēk…Ēa.

ref: to tell me, ‚ÄúPaul, don't be afraid! You will stand trial before the Emperor. And because of you, God will save the lives of everyone on the ship.‚ÄĚ
text: ka y…õli ma, ni, ‚ÄėP…Ēla, di z…Ēri dabeem! See ka i nin ti z…õ NaaTitaari tooni: ka dizugu Naawunni zaŇčŇči la ninvugseeba ban la'am m‚ÄĎb…õ i saani nyariŇčŇču wa puuni zaa n‚ÄĎti i.‚Äô

ref: Cheer up! I am sure that God will do exactly what he promised.
text: Dizugu, n z…Ēdima, ya ky…õ ka ya sufu d…Ēya. N niŇči Naawunni y…õdda, ni, u nin tum hoo Ňč…Ēn sa y…õli ma seem la.

ref: But we will first be shipwrecked on some island.
text: Ti nin baasi m‚ÄĎpaai tingbanseaa kan b…õ teeku kooma sunsukkuni maa ni.‚ÄĚ

ref: They cut the anchors loose and let them sink into the sea. At the same time they untied the ropes that were holding the rudders. Next, they raised the sail at the front of the ship and let the wind carry the ship toward the beach.
text: Dizugu ba daa l…Ērigi buntibsa maa m‚ÄĎbasi koom maa ni, ka l…Ērigi nyarimpaari la ni ba ti laa m‚ÄĎpiili n‚ÄĎduusiri; ka zaŇčŇči tantibila n‚ÄĎduusi n‚ÄĎtabli nyariŇčŇču tooni, ni ka ti gbaai p…õsiŇčŇču n‚ÄĎdaai ba ka ba kyaŇčŇči.

ref: The sailors wanted to escape from the ship. So they lowered the lifeboat into the water, pretending that they were letting down some anchors from the front of the ship.
text: Di saha ka nyariŇčŇču dima daa y…õtti, ni, ba ya z…Ē ni ka ky…õ ti; dizugu ba daa l…Ērigi nyarimbila maa n‚ÄĎsigsiri ka n‚ÄĎniŇčni teeku koom maa ni; ka ma'ari ziri, ni, ba ya sigsi buntibsa tooni sa m‚ÄĎpaasi an b…õ nyaaŇčŇča zugu.

ref: The sailors were afraid that we might hit some rocks, and they let down four anchors from the back of the ship. Then they prayed for daylight.
text: Di saha ka dabeem daa gbaai ba, ni, di pa seem ba nin Ňčm…õ n‚ÄĎfiri luginseelli din bu veeli puuni, ka dizugu ba daa zaŇčŇči buntibsa anaasi n‚ÄĎl…Ē miisi n‚ÄĎl…Ē nyariŇčŇču nyaaŇčŇča n‚ÄĎsigsi koom maa ni, ni a ti ta'ai nyariŇčŇču ky…õnni; ka naayi kpalim n‚ÄĎsusiri wunsusigu n‚ÄĎguri b…õkyikyeoo saha.

ref: But Paul said to Captain Julius and the soldiers, ‚ÄúIf the sailors don't stay on the ship, you won't have any chance to save your lives.‚ÄĚ
text: Amaa P…Ēla daa y…õli toondaana maa ni u niriba maa, ni, ‚ÄúBana yi bu kpalim m‚ÄĎb…õ nyariŇčŇču maa ni, ya s…Ē ku ti tuligi.‚ÄĚ

ref: The soldiers then cut the ropes that held the lifeboat and let it fall into the sea.
text: Ka langyimadima daa Ňčmaai nyarimbila miisi maa ka ky…õ ka ka lu m‚ÄĎbasi.

ref: I beg you to eat something. Your lives depend on it. Do this and not one of you will be hurt.‚ÄĚ
text: Ya bu ya di diibu pampaŇčŇč…Ēa? Lala nin suŇčŇči ya, ka ya ti yi ya wahala wa puuni ni alaaf…õa. Ya s…Ēs…Ē ku basi y…Ēri - halli ni ya ninvugs…Ē z…Ēbgu yinni gba.‚ÄĚ

ref: After Paul had said this, he took a piece of bread and gave thanks to God. Then in front of everyone, he broke the bread and ate some.
text: Ka Ňč…Ēn daa y…õli lala n‚ÄĎnaai maa, u daa zaŇčŇči diibu m‚ÄĎpuusi Naawunni di zugu ba zaa tooni, ka Ňčmaai n‚ÄĎŇč…Ēbi.

ref: They all felt encouraged, and each of them ate something.
text: Di saha ka ba zaa sufu daa kpaŇčŇči, ka ba gba daa deaai diibu n‚ÄĎdi.

ref: and after everyone had eaten, they threw the cargo of wheat into the sea to make the ship lighter. The Shipwreck
text: Ka ban daa di diibu n‚ÄĎnaai, ba daa zaŇčŇči kyi an daa b…õ nyariŇčŇču maa puuni n‚ÄĎyiisi m‚ÄĎbasi koom maa ni, ni di ky…õ ka nyariŇčŇču maa ku laa n‚ÄĎtibsa pam.

ref: Morning came, and the ship's crew saw a coast they did not recognize. But they did see a cove with a beach. So they decided to try to run the ship aground on the beach.
text: Ka beoo Ňč…Ēn daa ti neaai, ti daa nya ka tingbaŇčŇču b…õ ni, amaa ba daa bu baŇčŇči ka. Amaa ba nya ya'aseaa ban teesi, ni, m…Ē'abisigu m b…õ ni ka tampikara ka ni; ka daa boori ni ba ti kp…õ b…õni n‚ÄĎky…õ ka nyariŇčŇču maa firi tanni maa ni n‚ÄĎzaani.

ref: But it ran aground on a sandbank. The front of the ship stuck firmly in the sand, and the rear was being smashed by the force of the waves.
text: Amaa ba daa paai ya'aseaa ka kooma daa ky…õnni n‚ÄĎtuusiri taaba ka labra, ka ba daa burigi n‚ÄĎfiri, halli ka nyariŇčŇču maa toonni ku sakki n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči bee n‚ÄĎlabi, sahanseaa ka kooma maa daa Ňčmeeri ka nyaaŇčŇča n‚ÄĎkabri ka.

ref: The soldiers decided to kill the prisoners to keep them from swimming away and escaping.
text: Di saha ka langyimadima daa boori ni ba ku dansarika niriba maa, ni, di bu ya'ai ba s…Ē nin ti dugi koom m‚ÄĎpaai tingbaŇčŇču n‚ÄĎz…Ēa.

ref: But Captain Julius wanted to save Paul's life, and he did not let the soldiers do what they had planned. Instead, he ordered everyone who could swim to jump into the water and head for shore.
text: Amaa ba toondaana daa bu sakki lala, Ňč…Ēn daa boori ni u tuligi P…Ēla zugu. U daa y…õli, ni, ban mi koom dugbu la, ni, ba kpeem koom puuni n‚ÄĎdugi m‚ÄĎpaai tingbaŇčŇču maa.

ref: Then he told the others to hold on to planks of wood or parts of the ship. At last, everyone safely reached shore.
text: Ni, ban kpalim muna - ba gbaam taabudaari, bee, nyariŇčŇču daky…õra maa, m‚ÄĎmari n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči: ka lala zugu ba zaa naayi paai teeku duuri maa ni alaaf…õa.

ref: When we came ashore, we learned that the island was called Malta.
text: T…Ē; ka tin daa ti paai tiŇčŇča maa n‚ÄĎtuligi maa, ti daa ti baŇčŇči, ni, tingbaŇčŇču maa yuuri boonni la Malita.

ref: The local people were very friendly, and they welcomed us by building a fire, because it was rainy and cold.
text: TiŇčŇča maa niriba daa suŇčŇči ti ni nirilim pam. Ba daa nyoaai bugum n‚ÄĎky…õ ka ti zaa ky…õ na n‚ÄĎwuusi, saa maa Ňč…Ēn buuri ti zugu, ni waari maa Ňč…Ēn daa niŇči ti seem.

ref: After Paul had gathered some wood and had put it on the fire, the heat caused a snake to crawl out, and it bit him on the hand.
text: Ka P…Ēla daa boori daari na n‚ÄĎniŇčni bugum maa ni, ka lala saha ka waafu daa b…õ daari maa sunsukkuni n‚ÄĎgbisira, ka bugum maa tuulim daa neaai u, ka u yi na nti dum P…Ēla nuu maa.

ref: When the local people saw the snake hanging from Paul's hand, they said to each other, ‚ÄúThis man must be a murderer! He didn't drown in the sea, but the goddess of justice will kill him anyway.‚ÄĚ
text: TiŇčŇča maa biisi Ňč…Ēn daa ti nya ka waafu maa kpalim n‚ÄĎky…õ'ai u nuu maa, ba daa y…õli taaba, ni, ‚ÄúMma! Ninvugkuura n ny…õ u. U tuligi n‚ÄĎyi teeku koom maa ni na, amaa di bu sumni ni u kpalim m‚ÄĎb…õ ni.‚ÄĚ

ref: Paul shook the snake off into the fire and wasn't harmed.
text: P…Ēla mun daa kpaasi waafu maa m‚ÄĎbasi bugum maa ni, ka kur kpalim m‚ÄĎmari alaaf…õa.

ref: The people kept thinking that Paul would either swell up or suddenly drop dead. They watched him for a long time, and when nothing happened to him, they changed their minds and said, ‚ÄúThis man is a god.‚ÄĚ
text: Niriba maa daa teesi, ni, di ku yuui ka u fuusi, bee, ka u lu tiŇčŇča ya'abi ka kpi. Ka ba guusiri lala n‚ÄĎyuui, ka s…õseela bu niŇči u, ka ba naayi takki ba teesa n‚ÄĎy…õli, ni, wuns…Ē n ny…õ u.

ref: The governor of the island was named Publius, and he owned some of the land around there. Publius was very friendly and welcomed us into his home for three days.
text: Ka d…Ēs…Ē daa b…õ ni Ňč…Ēn daa su tingbaŇčŇču fukumsi maa, ka u yuuri daa boonni Publiyusi. Ňä…Ēn daa su puseesi an daa b…õ tin daa b…õ luginseaa la ni. Ka u daa booli ti ni ti kp…õ u yiri m‚ÄĎb…õ b…õni n‚ÄĎniŇči daba ata, ka g…Ēsi ti y…õla suŇčŇčasuŇčŇča.

ref: His father was in bed, sick with fever and stomach trouble, and Paul went to visit him. Paul healed the man by praying and placing his hands on him.
text: Di saha maa ka Publiyusi ba maa daa ka alaaf…õa n‚ÄĎd…Ēya - u puu n daa beera ka u niŇčŇča zaa bii. Ka P…Ēla daa kp…õ u saani n‚ÄĎsusi Naawunni ka zaŇčŇči u nuusi m‚ÄĎpa u zugu ka tibi u.

ref: After this happened, everyone on the island brought their sick people to Paul, and they were all healed.
text: Ka din daa niŇči lala maa, tingbaŇčŇču maa ninvugseeba ban kpalim ka k…ĒŇčŇči alaaf…õ daa ky…õ na, ni ba ti nya tippu.

ref: The people were very respectful to us, and when we sailed, they gave us everything we needed. From Malta to Rome
text: Ba daa ti ti piini pam; ka tin daa nya soori ni ti yi b…õni maa, ba daa ti ti la bunseelli kam kan daa p…Ēsigi ti saani.

ref: Three months later we sailed in a ship that had been docked at Malta for the winter. The ship was from Alexandria in Egypt and was known as ‚ÄúThe Twin Gods.‚ÄĚ
text: T…Ē; ti daa niŇči g…Ēya ata b…õni, ka naayi nya nyarinseaa kan daa pun z…õ tingbaŇčŇču maa lugri n‚ÄĎguri waari saha baasigu. Al…õkzandiriya nyariŇčŇču n daa ny…õ ka, ka ba daa puri ka yuseaa kan ny…õ ba Wungyaasi yuuri.

ref: We arrived in Syracuse and stayed for three days.
text: Ti daa du n‚ÄĎl…ĒŇčŇči m‚ÄĎpaai Sairakuusi tiŇčŇča n‚ÄĎniŇči daba ata b…õni;

ref: From there we sailed to Rhegium. The next day a south wind began to blow, and two days later we arrived in Puteoli.
text: n‚ÄĎyi b…õni n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎgyili halli nti paai R…õgyeoo tiŇčŇča. N‚ÄĎneaai beoo ka p…õsisuŇčŇču daa piili n‚ÄĎzeeri n‚ÄĎyi ti nyaaŇčŇča, ka ti daa gaari yooma n‚ÄĎniŇči daba ayi ky…õnni ka naayi paai Putiyooli tiŇčŇča.

ref: There we found some of the Lord's followers, who begged us to stay with them. A week later we left for the city of Rome.
text: B…õni ka ti daa nya ti nyaand…Ēlitaaba, ka ba daa mukki ti ni ti kpalim m‚ÄĎb…õ ba saani n‚ÄĎniŇči daba ay…Ēp…Ēi. Dizugu ti daa niŇči lala p…Ēi, ka naayi gbaai soori ni ti kyaŇčŇči ti Room maa.

ref: Some of the followers in Rome heard about us and came to meet us at the Market of Appius and at the Three Inns. When Paul saw them, he thanked God and was encouraged. Paul in Rome
text: Ka Yisa nyaand…Ēliseeba ban daa b…õ Room maa daa wum ti ky…õnni na lahabaari, ka daa yi na ni ba ti tuusi ti soori; ka paai ti Apiiya Daa ni na, ka baseeba mun daa paai ti Yirikpeera Ata tiŇčŇča na. Ka P…Ēla Ňč…Ēn daa ti nya ba maa, u daa puusi Naawunni taari, ka u sufu daa maai.

ref: We arrived in Rome, and Paul was allowed to live in a house by himself with a soldier to guard him.
text: T…Ē; ka tin daa ti kp…õ Room timpuuni maa, ba daa ti P…Ēla soori ni u b…Ē yiri seaa u mammaŇčŇča n‚ÄĎkp…õa; see ka ba ky…õ ka langyins…Ē daa kpalim u saani n‚ÄĎguri u.

ref: Three days after we got there, Paul called together some of the Jewish leaders and said: My friends, I have never done anything to hurt our people, and I have never gone against the customs of our ancestors. But in Jerusalem I was handed over as a prisoner to the Romans.
text: Ka P…Ēla daa niŇči daba ata b…õni, ka naayi booli Y…õhudiyadimseeba ban daa b…õ b…õni toondaandima maa, ni ba ky…õ u saani na. Ka ban daa ti paai na maa, u daa y…õli ba, ni, ‚ÄúM mabiisi, man bu tum taaliseaa n‚ÄĎti ti butiri, bee, ti yaadima kaya maa; amaa di zaa y…Ēri, ba daa gbaai ma Gy…õrusal…õm tiŇčŇča maa ni, n‚ÄĎl…Ē ma n‚ÄĎniŇči Room dima nuusi ni.

ref: They looked into the charges against me and wanted to release me. They found that I had not done anything deserving death.
text: Room dima mun daa viisi n y…õla ka daa boori ni ba ky…õ ma m‚ÄĎbasi, dama ba daa bu nya taaliseaa n saani kan sumsi ni kum.

ref: The Jewish leaders disagreed, so I asked to be tried by the Emperor. But I don't have anything to say against my own nation.
text: Amaa Y…õhudiyadima daa bu sakki lala, ka dizugu n daa bu nya soori see ka n ti susi, ni, ba zaŇčŇči n y…õtt…Ē'a maa n‚ÄĎzaali NaaTitaari saani na. Di pa la man mari la y…õtt…Ē'aseaa ni n ti galimni n Y…õhudiyataaba.

ref: I am bound by these chains because of what we people of Israel hope for. This is why I have called you here to talk about this hope of ours.
text: Ka dizugu m boonni ya, ni ya ky…õm na ka ti ti di azama; ka ya nin ti nya ka baŇčŇči din m b…õ seem, ni, Izra…õli dima ban mari tama seaa zugu ka n kpalim n‚ÄĎl…Ē ky…Ērimi wa.‚ÄĚ

ref: The leaders replied, ‚ÄúNo one from Judea has written us a letter about you. And not one of them has come here to report on you or to say anything against you.
text: Ka ba daa labsi n‚ÄĎy…õli u, ‚ÄúTi na bu deaai gbana zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči i y…õla, Gyuudiiya dima Ňč…Ēn s…Ēbi seesi n‚ÄĎti ti na, ka ti mabis…Ē na bu paai ti na ni u ti t…Ē'asi y…õtt…Ē'aseaa n‚ÄĎgalim i.

ref: But we would like to hear what you have to say. We understand that people everywhere are against this new group.‚ÄĚ
text: Amaa ti boori ni ti ti wum din b…õ i saani, dama ti mi, ni, niriba zaa galimni la w…Ēligri soori wa y…õla tinsi zaa puuni.‚ÄĚ

ref: They agreed on a time to meet with Paul, and many of them came to his house. From early morning until late in the afternoon, Paul talked to them about God's kingdom. He used the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets to try to win them over to Jesus.
text: Ka ba daa zaali dabsiri, ka daa ky…õ u yirikpeetiri na ni niriba pampam. Ka P…Ēla daa wulisi ba ni nimmoosi zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči Naawunni naam y…õla. U daa t…Ē'asiri Musa s…Ēppu y…õla, ni u ti ky…õ ka ba baŇčŇči Yisa y…õla a puuni n‚ÄĎniŇči u y…õdda - halli anabidima s…Ēppu gba ka u daa wulisi ba lala - wuntaŇčŇča saha halli nti paai yuŇčŇču.

ref: Some of the leaders agreed with what Paul said, but others did not.
text: T…Ē; ka ba mun seeba daa niŇči u y…õligu y…õdda, baseeba mun daa za'asi y…õdda niŇčbu.

ref: Since they could not agree among themselves, they started leaving. But Paul said, ‚ÄúThe Holy Spirit said the right thing when he sent Isaiah the prophet
text: Ka ban daa ka nangbanyinni ni taaba maa, ba daa doaai ni ba bakki n‚ÄĎkuli; ka P…Ēla daa y…õli ba y…õligu yinni m‚ÄĎpaasi, ni, ‚ÄúY…õlimaŇčni ka Naawunni Sii Kasi daa t…Ē'asi ya y…õla; u daa ky…õ ka anabi Aizaaya y…õli ya yaadima,

ref: to tell our ancestors, ‚ÄėGo to these people and tell them: You will listen and listen, but never understand. You will look and look, but never see.
text: ni, Naawunni y…õliya#:- ‚ÄėKy…õma butiri wa niriba saani n‚ÄĎy…õli ba, ni, ‚ÄúYa nin wum, m‚ÄĎmaali n‚ÄĎwum, Amaa ya ku baŇčŇči di gbinni. Ya nin g…Ēsi m‚ÄĎmaali n‚ÄĎg…Ēsi, Amaa ya ku nya di.‚ÄĚ

ref: All of you have stubborn hearts. Your ears are stopped up, and your eyes are covered. You cannot see or hear or understand. If you could, you would turn to me, and I would heal you.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ
text: Dama n niriba wa muna - Ba sufu ni teesa bu zulima, Ba ligi la ba tuba, Ba mumni ba nini: Dama di yi pa lala Ba nini naa n‚ÄĎnyara, Ba tuba naa n‚ÄĎwumna, Ba sufu ni teesa naa n‚ÄĎneaara, Ka ba ti labi n saani na Nti nya tippu n saani.‚Äô

ref: Paul said, ‚ÄúYou may be sure that God wants to save the Gentiles! And they will listen.‚ÄĚ
text: T…Ē; ya baŇčŇčma! Dizugu ba t…Ēlim tuliginsim wa lahabasuŇčŇču n‚ÄĎti ban pa Y…õhudiyadima maa; ban nin ti wum ka.‚ÄĚ

ref: For two years Paul stayed in a rented house and welcomed everyone who came to see him.
text: Ka P…Ēla daa kpalim m‚ÄĎb…õ lala halli n‚ÄĎniŇči yuuma ayi b…õni. U daa yoori u maŇčtoori ligri n‚ÄĎkuuri u bukaata, ka daa deaari s…Ēkam zaa ban daa ky…õ u saani na.

ref: He bravely preached about God's kingdom and taught about the Lord Jesus Christ, and no one tried to stop him.
text: Ka daa moonni Naawunni naam lahabaari ka wulisiri ti Dugma Yisa Masiiya y…õla ni nimbiri moaa, ka s…Ēs…Ē daa bu za'asi u.

ref: From Paul, a servant of Christ Jesus. God chose me to be an apostle, and he appointed me to preach the good news
text: Man P…Ēla n s…Ēbri gbaŇčŇču wa n‚ÄĎtiri ya. Mani n ny…õ s…Ē Naawunni Ňč…Ēn booli ma ni n ti leebi u tuumu la, ka laa n‚ÄĎgaŇčŇči ma n‚ÄĎzaali u lahabasuŇčŇču tuntumna.

ref: This good news is about his Son, our Lord Jesus Christ! As a human, he was from the family of David. But the Holy Spirit proved that Jesus is the powerful Son of God, because he was raised from death.
text: ZaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči u Naawunni Siiya beebu muna - Naawunni daa zaŇčŇči u yiiko n‚ÄĎky…õ ka u neaai kum ni, ka lala ka u daa gaŇčŇči u ka ti baŇčŇči, ni, u siri ny…õ la Naawunni Biiya. Ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Masiiya nla.

ref: Jesus was kind to me and chose me to be an apostle, so that people of all nations would obey and have faith.
text: Ňä…Ēn zugu n ky…õ ka Naawunni niŇči bunsuma n‚ÄĎniŇči ti ni, ka laa n‚ÄĎky…õ ka tirima leebi u tuumu. Ka u tum ti, ni ti ti ky…õ ka tingbana zaa niriba yi niŇči y…õdda u yuuri zugu ka deaai u noori.

ref: This letter is to all of you in Rome. God loves you and has chosen you to be his very own people. I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! A Prayer of Thanks
text: T…Ē: yarima ka n s…Ēbri gbaŇčŇču wa n‚ÄĎtiri ya, ya zaa ban b…õ Room tiŇčŇča ka Naawunni wuri ya ka booli ya nti ky…õ ka ya leebi u niriba maa. Ti Ba Naawunni, ni ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita, tim ya bunsuma ni alaaf…õ zaa.

ref: First, I thank God in the name of Jesus Christ for all of you. I do this because people everywhere in the world are talking about your faith.
text: T…Ē: din daŇčŇči tooni ny…õ la: ni, n teaari ya y…õla m‚ÄĎpuusiri Naawunni, Yisa Masiiya zugu, dama ya y…õdda niŇčbu y…õla moonni n‚ÄĎgyinni duniya zaa.

ref: God has seen how I never stop praying for you, while I serve him with all my heart and tell the good news about his Son.
text: N kur gyeemni Naawunni ni n sufu zaa, ka tumni u Bii maa lahabasuŇčŇču tuma ni nimbirimoaa. Ka Ňč…Ēn Naawunni nin ky…õ ka ya baŇčŇči man Ňč…Ēn kpalim n‚ÄĎsusiri u n‚ÄĎteaari ya y…õla seem la saha kam.

ref: In all my prayers, I ask God to make it possible for me to visit you.
text: N yi ti b…õ wumpuusigu ni saha kam zaa maa ka n niŇčni lala. N susiri Naawunni, ni, u sakkma, ni, n naayi nya soori ni n ti ky…õ ya saani na.

ref: I want to see you and share with you the same blessings that God's Spirit has given me. Then you will grow stronger in your faith.
text: Dama di kur mugsiri ma, ni n ti nya ya; ni n mari piiniseelli kan yi Naawunni Sii maa saani n‚ÄĎti ya, ka ya nin kpaŇčŇči di zugu.

ref: What I am saying is that we can encourage each other by the faith that is ours.
text: Di ny…õ la: ti kpamsiri taaba sufu, man ni yarima zaa, ti zaa Ňč…Ēn niŇčni y…õdda seem zugu.
