ref: But God treats us much better than we deserve, and because of Christ Jesus, he freely accepts us and sets us free from our sins.
text: Ka Naawunni Ňč…Ēn tisiri ba piini y…Ēri, samni ka ni, la zugu, u yiisi ba taalli ni, ka zaali ba y…õlimaŇčni, Kirisita Yisa Ňč…Ēn fa ba m‚ÄĎbasi zugu.

ref: God sent Christ to be our sacrifice. Christ offered his life's blood, so by faith in him we could come to God. And God did this to show that in the past he was right to be patient and forgive sinners. This also shows that God is right when he accepts people who have faith in Jesus.
text: U boori la u niŇči lala n‚ÄĎwulisi u y…õlimaŇčni Ňč…Ēn b…õ seem saha kanŇč…Ēa; dizugu u mammaŇčŇča ny…õ la y…õlimaŇčni daana, ka laa n‚ÄĎny…õ s…Ē Ňč…Ēn deaari s…Ēkam Ňč…Ēn niŇči Yisa y…õdda la gba, ni, y…õlimaŇčni daana.

ref: What is left for us to brag about? Not a thing! Is it because we obeyed some law? No! It is because of faith.
text: T…Ē, ka ti nin nyu bara dizugu, bee? Ay! Barinyuubu ka ni. Wula zugu? Dama di zaa bu d…Ēli la zaaligu maa d…Ēligu, amaa di d…Ēli la s…Ēseaa - di ny…õ la y…õdda niŇčbu soori.

ref: We see that people are acceptable to God because they have faith, and not because they obey the Law.
text: DinŇč…Ēna ti mari teesa, ni, niriba b…õ la y…õlimaŇčni dima ba y…õdda niŇčbu zugu; di pa la zaaligu la tuma zugu ka ba nya y…õlimaŇčni.

ref: Does God belong only to the Jews? Isn't he also the God of the Gentiles? Yes, he is!
text: Ammi, Naawunni ny…õ la Izra…õli dima k…Ēk…Ē Naawunni? - ka u ba'a ka ban kpalim la ni? Ay! di pa lala - ban pa Izra…õli dima la gba Naawunni n ny…õ u.

ref: Do we destroy the Law by our faith? Not at all! We make it even more powerful.
text: DinŇč…Ē zugu, ti kyiisiri, ni, anfaani ka zaaligu la ni, halli beela? - dama ti boori y…õdda niŇčbu y…õla. Ay! pa lala: ti ya zaŇčŇči zaaligu maa n‚ÄĎzaali la suŇčŇča di zugu.

ref: If he became acceptable to God because of what he did, then he would have something to brag about. But he would never be able to brag about it to God.
text: Ka di yi daa ny…õ la tunsuma zugu ka Naawunni daa y…õli, ni, u ny…õ la y…õlimaŇčni daana, dinŇč…Ēna u da mari seelli ni u kuuli di zugu, amaa pa Naawunni saani ka u daa kuuli.

ref: In the Scriptures David talks about the blessings that come to people who are acceptable to God, even though they don't do anything to deserve these blessings. David says,
text: Halli NaaDauda gba daa pun y…õli lala nira y…õla - di ny…õ la: ninvugs…Ē Naawunni Ňč…Ēn y…õli, ni, u ny…õ la y…õlimaŇčni daana, ka di pa la u tuma zugu. U daa t…Ē'asi di daama zugsuŇčŇču y…õla, ni#:-

ref: ‚ÄúWhat a blessing when God forgives our sins and our evil deeds. What a blessing when the Lord erases our sins from his book.‚ÄĚ
text: ZugsuŇčŇču gba n‚ÄĎti seeba Naawunni sariya Ňč…Ēn bu lusi ba n‚ÄĎy…õli, ni, ba galim n‚ÄĎtum la taalli.‚ÄĚ

ref: Are these blessings meant for circumcised people or for those who are not circumcised? Well, the Scriptures say that God accepted Abraham because Abraham had faith in him.
text: T…Ē: ka NaaDauda Ňč…Ēn daa t…Ē'asi zugsunseaa wa ny…õ la ban guni la k…Ēk…Ē dinni? Bee, ban bu guni su ka gba? Ay! - ban bu guni la gba nin mara, dama Litaafi y…õliya, ni, ‚ÄúU y…õdda zugu ka Naawunni daa y…õli Ibrahim, ni, u ny…õ la y…õlimaŇčni daana.‚ÄĚ

ref: But when did this happen? Was it before or after Abraham was circumcised? Of course, it was before.
text: Ka wula saha ka Naawunni daa y…õli lala? Di saha maa ka Ibrahim guniya, bee, u daa na bu guni? - Ay! di saha ka u na bu guni.

ref: Abraham let himself be circumcised to show he had been accepted because of his faith even before he was circumcised. This makes Abraham the father of all who are acceptable to God because of their faith, even though they are not circumcised.
text: Di nyaaŇčŇča ka ba daa naayi guni u, ka u gunibu wa daa wulisi la, ni, Naawunni pun y…õli, ni, u ny…õ la y…õlimaŇčni daana u y…õdda zugu; y…õdda wa mun ka u daa pun niŇči ka ba daa na bu guni u. Dinzugu Ňč…Ēn Ibrahim ny…õ la y…õdda niŇčniba zaa ba - baseeba ban niŇči Naawunni y…õdda ka na bu guni la - ka di nin ky…õ ka Naawunni y…õtti, ni, ba ny…õ la y…õlimaŇčni dima.

ref: This also makes Abraham the father of everyone who is circumcised and has faith in God, as Abraham did before he was circumcised. The Promise Is for All Who Have Faith
text: Ka u laa n‚ÄĎny…õ la seeba ban guni la zaa ba m‚ÄĎpaasi - baseeba ban pa la ban guni k…Ēk…Ēa, amaa ba y…õdda niŇčbu gba d…Ēli la ti zaa ba, Ibrahim, soori maa, dama Ňč…Ēn daa niŇči Naawunni y…õdda p…Ēi, ka naayi guni.

ref: God promised Abraham and his descendants that he would give them the world. This promise wasn't made because Abraham had obeyed a law, but because his faith in God made him acceptable.
text: Naawunni Ňč…Ēn daa ti u nangbambasiri n‚ÄĎti ‚ÄėIbrahim ni u nyaaŇčŇča‚Äô maa, u daa y…õli, ni, u nin ky…õ ka u di duniya wa hoo u faari; u daa niŇči lala maa, pa la Ibrahim Ňč…Ēn daa d…Ēli zaaligu la zugu, amaa Ňč…Ēn daa niŇči Ňč…Ēn Naawunni y…õdda la zugu.

ref: If Abraham and his descendants were given this promise because they had obeyed a law, then faith would mean nothing, and the promise would be worthless.
text: Ka di yi ny…õ la zaaligu zugu ka Naawunni nangbambasiri paari ninvugseeba ban d…Ēli ka, dinŇč…Ēna y…õdda ny…õ la y…Ēri, ka Naawunni nangbambasiri gba ny…õ y…Ēri.

ref: God becomes angry when his Law is broken. But where there isn't a law, it cannot be broken.
text: Zaaligu la tumni la sufuyisigri zugu, amaa zaaligu yi ka ni, zaaligu galiŇčŇču gba ku b…õ ni.

ref: Everything depends on having faith in God, so that God's promise is assured by his gift of undeserved grace. This promise isn't only for Abraham's descendants who have the Law. It is for all who are Abraham's descendants because they have faith, just as he did. Abraham is the ancestor of us all.
text: Nangbambasiri maa daa ky…õ na la y…õdda zugu, ka ka z…õ la deede, ni ka ti leebi piini n‚ÄĎtiri Ibrahim nyaaŇčŇča zaa, pa la seeba ban d…Ēli zaaligu la k…Ēk…Ēa, amaa ni seeba ban niŇčni y…õdda hoo Ibrahim gba. Ka Ňč…Ēn Ibrahim ny…õ la ti zaa ba.

ref: The Scriptures say that Abraham would become the ancestor of many nations. This promise was made to Abraham because he had faith in God, who raises the dead to life and creates new things.
text: Litaafi y…õliya, ni, ‚ÄúN nin ky…õ ka i leebi bura pam yaaba.‚ÄĚ - Ňč…Ēn daa niŇči Naawunni y…õdda maa zugu nla. U daa niŇči y…õdda, ni, Naawunni tisiri la ny…Ēvuri, ka u y…õligu daa ky…õ ka din daa ka ni la yi ti b…õ ni.

ref: God promised Abraham a lot of descendants. And when it all seemed hopeless, Abraham still had faith in God and became the ancestor of many nations.
text: Ibrahim daa niŇči y…õdda ka mari tama, halli din daa ti paai ni u ti koaai tama maa zaa u daa kpalim m‚ÄĎmari tama, ka di zugu u daa leebi ‚Äėbura pam yaaba‚Äô, dama Litaafi daa pun y…õli lala, ni, ‚ÄúLala ka i nyaaŇčŇča nin ti b…õ ni.‚ÄĚ

ref: But Abraham never doubted or questioned God's promise. His faith made him strong, and he gave all the credit to God.
text: Ii - anŇč…Ē zaa y…Ēri u y…õdda daa bu basi u, ka u daa bu birisi Naawunni nangbambasiri la; u y…õdda wa daa ti u la yiiko, ka u daa p…õ'ai Naawunni.

ref: Abraham was certain that God could do what he had promised.
text: U daa maali n‚ÄĎkpamsi u sufu ni y…õdda, ni, Naawunni Ňč…Ēn basi u nangbambasiri maa seem la, di siri nin niŇči lala.

ref: just as we read in the Scriptures. But these words were not written only for Abraham.
text: Ni, u ‚Äėy…õtti, ni, u ny…õ la y…õlimaŇčni daana‚Äô - ba daa bu s…Ēbi lala Ňč…Ēn Ibrahim zugu k…Ēk…Ēa.

ref: They were written for us, since we will also be accepted because of our faith in God, who raised our Lord Jesus to life.
text: Ay! - ba daa s…Ēbi di lala maa n‚ÄĎti la tirima, dama ba nin y…õli ti gba lala, ti seeba ban niŇči y…õdda n‚ÄĎtiri Naawunni, ni, u daa yisigi ti Dugma Yisa kum ni la.

ref: God gave Jesus to die for our sins, and he raised him to life, so that we would be made acceptable to God.
text: Ti tumbeeri zugu ka ba daa zaŇčŇči Ňč…Ēn Yisa n‚ÄĎtisi ni u kpi; ka u mun yisigi kum ni, ni ti ti yi na hoo y…õlimaŇčni dima.

ref: By faith we have been made acceptable to God. And now, thanks to our Lord Jesus Christ, we have peace with God.
text: T…Ē; ka dizugu tin niŇči y…õdda la zugu ky…õ ka Naawunni deaai ti, ni, y…õlimaŇčni dima. Ka lala ky…õ ka nangbanyinni b…õ ni tin ni Naawunni sunsukkuni, ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Masiiya zugu.

ref: Christ has also introduced us to God's gift of undeserved grace on which we now take our stand. So we are happy, as we look forward to sharing in the glory of God.
text: Dama Ňč…Ēn Yisa zugu daa ky…õ ka ti nya y…õdda niŇčbu soori nti kp…õ n‚ÄĎz…õ tin b…õ zaazisunseaa wa ni. Di ny…õ la Naawunni Ňč…Ēn daa tum lala tunsuma n‚ÄĎti ti maa. Dizugu ka ti mari sufupeelli, dama ti mari tama, ni, ti nin nya Naawunni gyirima veelim.

ref: But that's not all! We gladly suffer, because we know that suffering helps us to endure.
text: Din paasi maa, ti mari sufupeelli halli ti wahala diibu puuni gba, dama ti mi, ni, wahala diibu paasiri ti sugru ni sufukpeemni.

ref: And endurance builds character, which gives us a hope
text: Sugru mun paasiri ti beesisuŇčŇču, ka beesisuŇčŇču mun paasiri ti tama.

ref: that will never disappoint us. All of this happens because God has given us the Holy Spirit, who fills our hearts with his love.
text: Ka tama maa paasigu ku ky…õ ka ti di vi, dama Naawunni zaŇčŇči u Siiya n‚ÄĎti ti ka ti baŇčŇči u wurilim Ňč…Ēn zoaai seem di zugu.

ref: Christ died for us at a time when we were helpless and sinful.
text: Kirisita muna - sahanseaa ti daa na ka yaa la, lala saha maa deede ka u daa kpi n‚ÄĎti tirim tumbeeri dima maa.

ref: No one is really willing to die for an honest person, though someone might be willing to die for a truly good person.
text: Ya bu nya? - Di t…Ē pam ni s…Ē sakki n‚ÄĎkpi n‚ÄĎti ninvugs…Ēa - halli n‚ÄĎti y…õlimaŇčni daans…Ē gba. Amaa di bu ya'ai ka nira yi mari sufukpeemni n‚ÄĎsakki n‚ÄĎkpi ninvugsuŇčŇču zugu.

ref: But God showed how much he loved us by having Christ die for us, even though we were sinful.
text: Amaa Naawunni Ňč…Ēna - Ňč…Ēn daa niŇči seem n‚ÄĎwulisi ti, ni, u wuri ti pam nŇč…Ēa#:- tin daa na b…õ tumbeeri ni sahanseaa la, Kirisita daa kpi n‚ÄĎti ti.

ref: But there is more! Now that God has accepted us because Christ sacrificed his life's blood, we will also be kept safe from God's anger.
text: T…Ē, ka di yi b…õ lala maa, ka Naawunni pun deaai ti, ni, y…õlimaŇčni dima, Yisa Masiiya ziim maa zugu, dinŇč…Ēna ti mi halli, ni, Ňč…Ēn Yisa zugu ka ti nin laa n‚ÄĎyi Naawunni sugyeaa ni gba.

ref: Even when we were God's enemies, he made peace with us, because his Son died for us. Yet something even greater than friendship is ours. Now that we are at peace with God, we will be saved by the life of his Son.
text: Dama tin daa ny…õ Naawunni dindaandima sahanseaa la, u Bii Yisa kum maa zugu daa ky…õ ka Naawunni deaai ti taalli sindaani maa. Di zugu ti mi halli, ni, Yisa ny…Ēvuri zugu nin ky…õ ka ti nya tuliginsim.

ref: And in addition to everything else, we are happy because God sent our Lord Jesus Christ to make peace with us. Adam and Christ
text: Amaa pa dinŇč…Ē k…Ēk…Ē gba. See ka ti mari sufupeelli pam Naawunni saani, ka gyeemni u ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita zugu dama Ňč…Ēn Yisa n daa z…Ē sindaani n‚ÄĎti ti ka u Ba deaai.

ref: Sin was in the world before the Law came. But no record of sin was kept, because there was no Law.
text: Tumbeoo siri daa pun b…õ la duniya wa puuni p…Ēi, ka Musa na bu deaai zaaligu maa y…õla Naawunni saani. Amaa di zaa y…Ēri, ba daa bu nyaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎt…Ē'asiri tumbeoo galinsi y…õla, zaaligu la Ňč…Ēn daa na ka ni la zugu.

ref: Yet death still had power over all who lived from the time of Adam to the time of Moses. This happened, though not everyone disobeyed a direct command from God, as Adam did. In some ways Adam is like Christ who came later.
text: Amaa kum daa pun su ba zaa fukumsi n‚ÄĎyi Adam saha halli nti paai Musa. S…Ēkam daa kpi, halli ban daa bu tum tumb…õseelli kan Ňčmanni Adam dinni la nti za'asi Naawunni noori zaaligu. Ňä…Ēn Adam muna - u ny…õ la s…Ē Ňč…Ēn daŇčŇči tooni n‚ÄĎwulisiri s…Ē Ňč…Ēn nin naayi ky…õ na la y…õla Ňč…Ēn ti ya b…õ seem.

ref: But the gift of God's undeserved grace was very different from Adam's sin. That one sin brought death to many others. Yet in an even greater way, Jesus Christ alone brought God's gift of undeserved grace to many people.
text: Amaa Naawunni piini yiiko bu Ňčmanni tumbeoo dinni. Ay! - di gaari lala zugu pam. Ti mi halli, ni, ninvugyinni taalli zugu ka niriba pam daa kpi; amaa Naawunni piini zoaari m‚ÄĎpaasiri n‚ÄĎsuŇčŇčni niriba pam, ka lala gba yiiri la ninvugyinni zugu ni na - di ny…õ la: Yisa Kirisita Masiiya zugu.

ref: There is a lot of difference between Adam's sin and God's gift. That one sin led to punishment. But God's gift made it possible for us to be acceptable to him, even though we have sinned many times.
text: Din laa m‚ÄĎpaasi: Naawunni piini siri bu Ňčmanni la ninvugyinni maa tumbeoo la y…õla: dama tumbeoo yinni k…Ēk…Ē zugu ka Naawunni daa kari s…Ēkam sariya n‚ÄĎlusi ba, amaa Naawunni piini zugu daa ky…õ ka tumbeeri pam y…õla daa kpiisi, ka ba yiisi niriba maa, ni, ba mari y…õlimaŇčni.

ref: Death ruled like a king because Adam had sinned. But that cannot compare with what Jesus Christ has done. God has treated us with undeserved grace, and he has accepted us because of Jesus. And so we will live and rule like kings.
text: Ya bu nya? - Tumbeoo yinni taalli zugu daa ky…õ ka kum su s…Ēkam fukumsi, ka di daa yi lala tumb…õdaana yinni maa saani na. T…Ē, ka dinŇč…Ēna ti mi halli, ni, seeba ban deaai Naawunni y…õlimaŇčni u piinisuŇčŇču pam zugu la nin ti su ny…Ēvuri naam fukumsi, ka di mun ny…õ la tunsuŇčŇču daana yinni zugu, Ňč…Ēn ny…õ Yisa Kirisita.

ref: Everyone was going to be punished because Adam sinned. But because of the good thing that Christ has done, God accepts us and gives us the gift of life.
text: DinŇč…Ē zaa ky…õ ka ti baŇčŇči, ni, nira yinni tumbeoo zugu ky…õ ka niriba zaa y…õtt…Ē'a lu Naawunni saani, ka ba zaa nya taalli u saani. Lala yaasa ka nira yinni maa tunsuŇčŇču zugu ky…õ ka niriba zaa yi taalli ni, ka ba mari y…õlimaŇčni Naawunni saani nti nya ny…Ēvuri.

ref: Adam disobeyed God and caused many others to be sinners. But Jesus obeyed him and will make many people acceptable to God.
text: Ninvugyinni maa daa za'asi Naawunni noori, ka di zugu niriba pam daa nya taalli, ni, ala'akyi dima n ny…õ ba. Lala yaasa ka ninvugyinni Ňč…Ēnla daa sakki Naawunni noori, ka di zugu niriba pam daa yi taalli ni, ni, y…õlimaŇčni dima n ny…õ ba.

ref: The Law came, so that the full power of sin could be seen. Yet where sin was powerful, God's gift of undeserved grace was even more powerful.
text: Zaaligu muna - ba daa mari ka na ni ba ti ky…õ ka taalli tumpu maali m‚ÄĎpaasira. Amaa taalli Ňč…Ēn maali m‚ÄĎpaasiri seem la, Naawunni piini gba daa paasi n‚ÄĎgaari lala zugu.

ref: Sin ruled by means of death. But God's gift of grace now rules, and God has accepted us because of Jesus Christ our Lord. This means that we will have eternal life.
text: Tumbeoo zugu n‚ÄĎdaa la, dama tumbeoo daa mari kum yiiko n‚ÄĎsu niriba fukumsi, amaa lala ka Naawunni piinisuŇčŇču mari y…õlimaŇčni yiiko n‚ÄĎsu ba fukumsi, halli ka ba ti nya ny…Ēvuri kan ka t…Ēriga, Yisa Kirisita Masiiya zugu.

ref: What should we say? Should we keep on sinning, so that God's gift of undeserved grace will show up even better?
text: T…Ē; dinŇč…Ēna ti ya y…õli la wula? Ni, ti kpalimma n‚ÄĎtumni tumbeeri ni ti ti ky…õ ka Naawunni piinisuma zoaari m‚ÄĎpaasira?

ref: No, we should not! If we are dead to sin, how can we go on sinning?
text: Ay! - di pa lala. Ti ny…õ la ninvugseeba ban pun kpi zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči Tumbeoo yiiko y…õla ni. Ka wula zugu ka ti nin laa m‚ÄĎb…õ tumbeeri tumpu ni?

ref: When we were baptized, we died and were buried with Christ. We were baptized, so we would live a new life, as Christ was raised to life by the glory of God the Father.
text: Din laa m‚ÄĎpaasi, ban daa su ti koom ni ti ti paasi Yisa zugu n‚ÄĎkpi maa, ba daa zaŇčŇči ti m‚ÄĎpaasi u zugu n‚ÄĎs…Ē'ai. Ka di gbinni ny…õ la nŇč…Ēa: ni, ti Ba Naawunni gyirima yiiko daa ky…õ ka Yisa doaai kum ni; ka di zugu lala ky…õ ka ti gba doaai nti b…õ beesipaalli.

ref: If we shared in Jesus' death by being baptized, we will be raised to life with him.
text: Dama ti yi Ňčmanni la ti Dugma Yisa n‚ÄĎkpi u kum wa taabu, di kpa, ni, ti nin Ňčmanni hoo Ňč…Ēna u kum doaari ni gba.

ref: We know that the persons we used to be were nailed to the cross with Jesus. This was done, so our sinful bodies would no longer be the slaves of sin.
text: Ka ti mi, ni, ti beesikura la ka ba daa zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎla'am m‚ÄĎpaasi Masiiya zugu n‚ÄĎkpaasi n‚ÄĎtabli d…Ēpulli zugu, ni lala ky…õ ka ti beesikura ningbina nin sa'am m‚ÄĎbasi, Tumbeoo Ňč…Ēn su a y…õla zaa maa zugu. DinŇč…Ēna ti ningbina ku laa n‚ÄĎny…õ seesi Ala'akyi Ňč…Ēn su hoo ban su daaba seem maa.

ref: We know sin doesn't have power over dead people.
text: Dama Ňč…Ēn kpi zaa pun yi ala'akyi ni.

ref: As surely as we died with Christ, we believe we will also live with him.
text: T…Ē, ka tirim Ňč…Ēn pun paasi Kirisita zugu n‚ÄĎkpi maa, dinŇč…Ēna ti mari la y…õdda, ni, ti nin paasi u zugu m‚ÄĎmari ny…Ēvuri gba.

ref: We know that death no longer has any power over Christ. He died and was raised to life, never again to die.
text: Ti mi, ni, Kirisita daa pun yisigi kum ni, ka di zugu u ku laa n‚ÄĎkpi, dama kum laa n‚ÄĎka yiiko u saani.

ref: When Christ died, he died for sin once and for all. But now he is alive, and he lives only for God.
text: Ňä…Ēn daa kpi maa, u daa kpi yummu zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči Tumbeoo yiiko saani: ka Ňč…Ēn mari ny…Ēvuri maa muna, u mari n‚ÄĎti la Naawunni saani.

ref: In the same way, you must think of yourselves as dead to the power of sin. But Christ Jesus has given life to you, and you live for God.
text: T…Ē, ka dizugu ya gba teesima ya mansi y…õla lala. Ni, ya gba pun kpi zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči Tumbeoo yiiko ni, ka mari ny…Ēvuri n‚ÄĎti Naawunni, yan la'am m‚ÄĎb…õ Kirisita Yisa saani zugu.

ref: Don't let sin rule your body. After all, your body is bound to die, so don't obey its desires
text: Ya ningbina ny…õ la seesi an kpiira, ka di zugu yan di ky…õ ka Tumbeoo su a fukumsi nti ky…õ ka a d…Ēli Tumbeoo k…Ēribeeri y…õla.

ref: or let any part of it become a slave of evil. Give yourselves to God, as people who have been raised from death to life. Make every part of your body a slave that pleases God.
text: Ya di laa n‚ÄĎzaŇčŇči ya ningbina luga zaa n‚ÄĎtiri Tumbeoo, ka a ti leebi Tumbeoo tuma neema. Amaa ya zaŇčŇčma ya ningbina luga zaa n‚ÄĎtiri Naawunni ka a ti leebi u y…õlimaŇčni tuma neema, dama ya ny…õ la seeba ban pun neaai kum ni.

ref: Don't let sin keep ruling your lives. You are ruled by God's undeserved grace and not by the Law. Slaves Who Do What Pleases God
text: DinŇč…Ēna Ala'akyi ku laa n‚ÄĎny…õ ya dugma, dama zaaligu maa bu su ya y…õla, amaa ka Naawunni piinisuma tuma n su ya.

ref: What does all this mean? Does it mean we are free to sin, because we are ruled by God's gift of undeserved grace and not by the Law? Certainly not!
text: B…Ē yaasa? Zaaligu bu su ti, na'ala Naawunni piini n su ti: ammi, ti tumma tumbeeri lala zugu? - Ay! pa lala.

ref: You used to be slaves of sin. But I thank God that with all your heart you followed the example set forth in the teaching you received.
text: Amaa ti puusiri Naawunni barika! Dama ni ya daa b…õ Ala'akyi suulinsi ni n‚ÄĎwumni Tumbeoo y…õligu hoo daaba maa zaa, ya daa wum y…õlimaŇčni mooligu la ka doaai n‚ÄĎtakki ya sufu ni teesa ka sakki n‚ÄĎdeaai wulisigu maa.

ref: Now you are set free from sin and are slaves who please God.
text: Ka ya daa laa n‚ÄĎyi Ala'akyi suulinsi ni na, ka leebi Y…õlimaŇčni Ňč…Ēn su seeba.

ref: I am using these everyday examples, because in some ways you are still weak. You used to let the different parts of your body be slaves of your evil thoughts. But now you must make every part of your body serve God, so that you will belong completely to him.
text: T…Ē, n t…Ē'asiri la ninsaala duniya wa puuni beesigu y…õla n‚ÄĎtiri ya, dama a gbinni bamsim bu t…Ēa; ka yan b…õ la duniya wa puuni la zugu n ky…õ ka ya na ka yaa zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči y…õlizulima y…õla. Ya daa ky…õ ka ya ningbina luga zaa leebi hoo daaba n‚ÄĎti da'ari tuma ni zaaligu za'asigu. Amaa pampaŇčŇč…Ē ya zaŇčŇči ya ningbina n‚ÄĎleebi la daaba n‚ÄĎti Naawunni y…õlimaŇčni la, n‚ÄĎtumni tunsuma kasikasi.

ref: When you were slaves of sin, you didn't have to please God.
text: Yan daa b…õ Ala'akyi suulinsi ni hoo daaba sahanseaa la, Y…õlimaŇčni maa daa bu su ya fukumsi.

ref: But what good did you receive from the things you did? All you have to show for them is your shame, and they lead to death.
text: Ka anfaani b…Ē ka ya daa nya di zugu? Ay, zaalim! Lala tumbeeri maa zugu daa kur ky…õ ka ya di vi, ka y…õla wa zaa naari ny…õ la kum.

ref: Now you have been set free from sin, and you are God's slaves. This will make you holy and will lead you to eternal life.
text: Amaa pampaŇčŇč…Ē ba fa ya m‚ÄĎbasi n‚ÄĎyiisi Ala'akyi fukumsi ni na, ni ya leebi Naawunni suulinsi niriba. DinŇč…Ē zugu ka ya nyari anfaani pam ka leebi la Naawunni dinni, din mun naari ny…õ la ny…Ēvuri kan ka t…Ēriga.

ref: That is how it is with you, my friends. You are now part of the body of Christ and are dead to the power of the Law. You are free to belong to Christ, who was raised to life so we could serve God.
text: T…Ē, ka di daa ny…õ la lala n‚ÄĎti ya, m mabiisi. Ba daa ky…õ ka ya paasi Kirisita ningbina zugu ka ku ya, di zugu ya laa n‚ÄĎka ni n‚ÄĎti zaaligu y…õla. Ka dizugu ya nya soori ni ya ti leebi s…Ē dinni, Ňč…Ēn yisigi kum ni - di ny…õ la Yisa; ni di niŇči anfaani n‚ÄĎti Naawunni.

ref: When we thought only of ourselves, the Law made us have sinful desires. It made every part of our bodies into slaves who are doomed to die.
text: Sahanseaa la, ti beesigu daa d…Ēli ti ningbink…Ērisi y…õla. Di saha ka tumbeeri tumpu boorimma daa d…Ēli zaaligu maa n‚ÄĎnya yaa n‚ÄĎtumni ti ningbina luga zaa ni; ka ti daa bu nya anfaani di zugu - ay! kum k…Ēk…Ē ka ti daa nya.

ref: But the Law no longer rules over us. We are like dead people, and it cannot have any power over us. Now we can serve God in a new way by obeying his Spirit, and not in the old way by obeying the written Law. The Battle with Sin
text: Amaa pampaŇčŇč…Ē ti kpiya ni n‚ÄĎti zaaligu maa, ka di zugu zaaligu wa bu laa n‚ÄĎsu ti y…õla. Lala yi ky…õ ka ti bu laa n‚ÄĎd…Ēli s…Ēkurigu kan ny…õ zaaligu s…Ēppu maa dinni la, amaa s…Ēpaalli kan ny…õ Sii maa dinni.

ref: Does this mean that the Law is sinful? Certainly not! But if it had not been for the Law, I would not have known what sin is really like. For example, I would not have known what it means to want something that belongs to someone else, unless the Law had told me not to do this.
text: Dinzugu, ti ya y…õli la wula? - ni, Naawunni zaaligu maa ny…õ la ala'akyi, bee? Ay! pa lala. Amaa di yi da pa la zaaligu la zugu, n naa n‚ÄĎku baŇčŇči ala'akyi Ňč…Ēn ny…õ seelli. Dama zaaligu maa yi da bu y…õli, ni, ‚ÄúDi ky…õ ka i sufu k…Ērisi kp…õ i kpeaa buna zugu.‚ÄĚ, n naa n‚ÄĎku baŇčŇči niriba neema boobu y…õla.

ref: It was sin that used this command as a way of making me have all kinds of desires. But without the Law, sin is dead.
text: Ala'akyi daa d…Ēli zaaligu la n‚ÄĎnya zaazisuŇčŇču p…Ēi, ka naayi ky…õ ka neema boobu teesibeeri balibu pam kp…õ n sufu ni. Amaa zaaligu yi ka ni, Ala'akyi mun gba kpi ni.

ref: Before I knew about the Law, I was alive. But as soon as I heard that command, sin came to life,
text: Sahanseaa la, n daa mari ny…Ēvuri, ka na zi zaaligu y…õla maa. Amaa zaaligu la Ňč…Ēn daa ti paai ma na, ka daa ky…õ ka Ala'akyi neaai ni,

ref: and I died. The very command that was supposed to bring life to me, instead brought death.
text: ka man kpi. Zaaligu la gbinni ny…õ la ny…Ēvuri, amaa man saani ka daa zi la kum na.

ref: Sin used this command to trick me, and because of it I died.
text: Zaaligu maa zugu ka Ala'akyi daa nya soori n‚ÄĎy…Ēmsi ma, n‚ÄĎzaŇčŇči zaaligu maa y…õla n‚ÄĎky…õ ka n kpi.

ref: Am I saying something good caused my death? Certainly not! It was sin that killed me by using something good. Now we can see how terrible and evil sin really is.
text: Ka bunsuŇčŇču dinŇč…Ē n ky…õ ka kum paai ma la, bee wula? - Ay, pa lala! Ala'akyi n ny…õ din ziiri kum na, ka ka daa d…Ēlisi bunsuŇčŇču wa n‚ÄĎtum lala maa; ka di zugu ka ti baŇčŇči ala'akyi beeri Ňč…Ēn ny…õ seem la. Lala zugu ka zaaligu la ky…õ ka tumbeeri zaa yiiri paalu.

ref: We know that the Law is spiritual. But I am merely a human, and I have been sold as a slave to sin.
text: Ti mi halli, ni, zaaligu la ny…õ la seaa kan d…Ēlisi ni siiya; amaa man mun ny…õ duniya wa ni ninsaala, ka ba koosiri ma ni hoo daabri n‚ÄĎti Ala'akyi.

ref: In fact, I don't understand why I act the way I do. I don't do what I know is right. I do the things I hate.
text: Halli n ku toaai m‚ÄĎbaŇčŇči man tumni seelli. Dama m bu tumni man boori tunseesi. Ay! - tunseesi man bu boori la zaa ka n tumna.

ref: Although I don't do what I know is right, I agree that the Law is good.
text: Lala zugu, n yi tum taaliseaa ka y…õli, ni, m bu boori lala, n y…õtt…Ē'a wa wulisiya, ni, zaaligu la veela, ka n sakki lala.

ref: So I am not the one doing these evil things. The sin that lives in me is what does them.
text: Ka dinŇč…Ē pa la mani n tumni taalli maa, amaa Ala'akyi m b…õ n saani n‚ÄĎtumna.

ref: I know that my selfish desires won't let me do anything that is good. Even when I want to do right, I cannot.
text: M mi, ni, bunsunseelli ka m beesigu ni, halli beela - di ny…õ la n duniya wa ni ningbina la ni beesigu ni. M boori tunsuma tumpu, amaa n k…ĒŇčŇči la a tumpu.

ref: Instead of doing what I know is right, I do wrong.
text: M boori la tunsuŇčŇču tumpu n‚ÄĎk…ĒŇčŇči, ka laa n‚ÄĎtumni tumb…õseelli man bu boori maa.

ref: And so, if I don't do what I know is right, I am no longer the one doing these evil things. The sin that lives in me is what does them.
text: N yi tum taaliseaa ka y…õli, ni, m bu boori lala, dinŇč…Ē pa la mani n tumni taalli maa, amaa Ala'akyi m b…õ n saani n‚ÄĎtumna.

ref: The Law has shown me that something in me keeps me from doing what I know is right.
text: N yi baŇčŇči y…õlimaŇčni nŇč…Ēa: ni, n yi kur boori ni n ti tumni din veela, lala saha maa ka tumbeoo tumpu b…õ n saani.

ref: With my whole heart I agree with the Law of God.
text: Dama n sufu ni teesa zaa kur sakkri ni n‚ÄĎtiri Naawunni zaaligu tuma,

ref: But in every part of me I discover something fighting against my mind, and it makes me a prisoner of sin that controls everything I do.
text: amaa m baŇčŇčiya, ni, zaaliseaa n b…õ n ningbiŇčŇču luga ni. Ka lala zaalibeoo tuusiri t…Ēppu n‚ÄĎniŇčni n sufu ni teesa zaalisuŇčŇču la, ka nyaŇčŇči ma n‚ÄĎgbaai ma nti kpari n ningbiŇčŇču luga ni teesibeoo ziiya.

ref: What a miserable person I am. Who will rescue me from this body that is doomed to die?
text: Waay, waay, waay! Man wahala daana wa! Ňä…Ēni n lee n‚ÄĎya fa ma m‚ÄĎbasi, n‚ÄĎyiisi ma duniya wa ni ningbina beesigu wa ni, din taasiri ma kum ni?

ref: Thank God! Jesus Christ will rescue me. So with my mind I serve the Law of God, although my selfish desires make me serve the law of sin.
text: Ay! M p…õ'ari Naawunni ka puusiri u barika, dama Ňč…Ēn pun fa ma m‚ÄĎbasi, Yisa Kirisita Masiiya ti Dugma zugu. Ah, t…Ē! M mammaŇčŇča sufu ni teesa puuni ka n d…Ēli la Naawunni zaaligu s…ĒsuŇčŇču, amaa n duniya wa ni ningbina beesigu puuni ka n na d…Ēli la Ala'akyi s…Ēbeoo.

ref: If you belong to Christ Jesus, you won't be punished.
text: DinŇč…Ēna ba ku laa n‚ÄĎtoaai n‚ÄĎkari Kirisita Yisa niriba sariya n‚ÄĎlusi ba, halli beela.

ref: The Holy Spirit will give you life that comes from Christ Jesus and will set you free from sin and death.
text: Dama zaalipaalli b…õ la Yisa Kirisita saani; Naawunni Sii maa zaaligu n ny…õ ka, ka ka tiri ny…Ēvuri. Zaaligu wa n ky…õ ka i yi zaalikurigu la suulinsi ni na - Ala'akyi Ňč…Ēn su zaaliseaa maa nla, kan tiri kum.

ref: The Law of Moses cannot do this, because our selfish desires make the Law weak. But God set you free when he sent his own Son to be like us sinners and to be a sacrifice for our sin. God used Christ's body to condemn sin.
text: Ban daa s…Ēbi zaaligu seaa n‚ÄĎdooli la muna, di daa k…ĒŇčŇči yiiko, duniya wa ni ninsaalidima ningbina Ňč…Ēn boori tumbeoo maa zugu. Amaa Naawunni mammaŇčŇča daa zaŇčŇči u yiiko n‚ÄĎtumna. U daa ky…õ ka u toori Biiya ky…õ ti saani na nti leebi Ňč…Ēn mari duniya wa ni ningbina, hoo tumbeeri dima Ňč…Ēn mari seem la - halli tumbeeri zugu ka u daa ky…õ na gba. Lala ka Naawunni daa nyaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkari sariya n‚ÄĎlusi Ala'akyi; dama duniya wa ni ningbina maa ni ka tumbeeri b…õ ni n‚ÄĎtumna.

ref: He did this, so that we would do what the Law commands by obeying the Spirit instead of our own desires.
text: Di zaa gbinni ny…õ la nŇč…Ēa: ni, Naawunni zaaligu Ňč…Ēn ya zaali ti y…õlimaŇčni maa, lala y…õlimaŇčni naayi b…õ ti beesigu ni zaa - tiseeba ban bu d…Ēli ningbina k…Ēribeeri s…Ēkura la, amaa sii maa s…ĒsuŇčŇču, bumpaalli, ka ti d…Ēla.

ref: People who are ruled by their desires think only of themselves. Everyone who is ruled by the Holy Spirit thinks about spiritual things.
text: Dama seeba ban b…õ duniya wa ningbink…Ērisi ni la, ba zikki zaa b…õ ningbink…Ērisi y…õla ni; amaa seeba ban b…õ sii maa beesisuŇčŇču ni la, ban mun zikki kur b…õ sisuŇčŇču y…õla ni.

ref: If our minds are ruled by our desires, we will die. But if our minds are ruled by the Spirit, we will have life and peace.
text: Ningbink…Ērisi teesa tiri la kum, amaa sii maa beesigu teesa tisiri la ny…Ēvuri ni sufudoaa.

ref: Our desires fight against God, because they do not and cannot obey God's laws.
text: Duniya wa ni ningbink…Ērisi teesa kur zaŇčŇči la Naawunni dimni; a bu sakkri n‚ÄĎtiri Naawunni zaaligu - ka mun pun ku toaai n‚ÄĎsakki lala.

ref: If we follow our desires, we cannot please God.
text: Lala zugu ka ninvugseeba ban beesigu d…Ēli ningbink…Ērisi soori la ku toaai n‚ÄĎtigi Naawunni nini.

ref: You are no longer ruled by your desires, but by God's Spirit, who lives in you. People who don't have the Spirit of Christ in them don't belong to him.
text: Yarima muna - duniya wa ningbink…Ērisi bu su ya, amaa Sii maa beesisuŇčŇču ni ka ya b…õ ni. Halli s…Ē yi ka Kirisita Siiya, u pa la Yisa dinni gba.

ref: But Christ lives in you. So you are alive because God has accepted you, even though your bodies must die because of your sins.
text: Ya duniya wa ni ningbina muna - kum su a, tumbeeri zugu. Amaa di zaa y…Ēri, Kirisita yi b…õ ya ni, ya siisi mari la ny…Ēvuri, Naawunni Ňč…Ēn deaai ya hoo y…õlimaŇčni dima maa zugu.

ref: If you do, you will die. But you will live, if by the help of God's Spirit you say ‚ÄúNo‚ÄĚ to your desires.
text: dama ya yi ti d…Ēli ningbina beesibeoo soori ya nin kpi. Amaa ya beesigu yi d…Ēli la Sii maa soori, lala yi ky…õ ka ningbink…Ērisi kpi, ka yarima mun nin neaai.

ref: Only those people who are led by God's Spirit are his children.
text: Dama s…Ēkam Ňč…Ēn d…Ēli Sii maa beesigu la ny…õ la Naawunni biiya.

ref: God's Spirit makes us sure that we are his children.
text: Halli Sii maa mammaŇčŇča paasiri la ti toori siisi zugu n‚ÄĎwulisiri, ni, ti siri ny…õ la Naawunni biisi.

ref: His Spirit lets us know that together with Christ we will be given what God has promised. We will also share in the glory of Christ, because we have suffered with him. A Wonderful Future for God's People
text: T…Ē, ka ti yi ny…õ la Naawunni biisi, ti laa n‚ÄĎny…õ la u faari dittiba. Ti nin di faari Naawunni saani, halli tin ni Kirisita nin la'am n‚ÄĎdi ka. Tin ni Ňč…Ēn yi siri ti la'am n‚ÄĎdi u wahala, dinŇč…Ēna ti ya la'amni n‚ÄĎnya u gyirima gba.

ref: I am sure what we are suffering now cannot compare with the glory that will be shown to us.
text: Man Ňč…Ēn nya seem maa nŇč…Ēa#:- pampaŇčŇč…Ē namsigu wa ku toaai m‚ÄĎma'asi ni gyirima veelim seaa ban nin kasigi n‚ÄĎti ti la.

ref: In fact, all creation is eagerly waiting for God to show who his children are.
text: Dama Naawunni daa naam duniya zaa, ka ka na b…õ ni n‚ÄĎguusiri sahanseaa Ňč…Ēn ya yiisi u biisi paalu ni na maa.

ref: Meanwhile, creation is confused, but not because it wants to be confused. God made it this way in the hope
text: Dama duniya wa zaa ka ba daa zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkpeesi y…õtt…Ē'ay…ĒŇčŇču ni, ka di pa la duniya yuubu zugu, amaa Naawunni Ňč…Ēn daa zaali lala maa zugu. Dama u daa mari tama,

ref: that creation would be set free from decay and would share in the glorious freedom of his children.
text: ni, duniya wa zaa nin nya fa-m‚ÄĎbasi ka ku laa m‚ÄĎb…õ sa'aŇčŇču suulinsi ni; di saha ka ka nin paai mansuulinsi ni gyirima ziiya, hoo Naawunni biisi zaa Ňč…Ēn nin ti mari seem la.

ref: We know that all creation is still groaning and is in pain, like a woman about to give birth.
text: Dama ti mi halli, ni, duniya wa zaa nyuusiri ni ka diri la wahala hoo p…Ē'as…Ē Ňč…Ēn b…õ d…Ē'am wahala maa ni Ňč…Ēn nyuusiri seem la. Ka di b…õ la lala halli ni zuna.

ref: The Spirit makes us sure about what we will be in the future. But now we groan silently, while we wait for God to show that we are his children. This means that our bodies will also be set free.
text: Ka di pa la duniya wa k…Ēk…Ēa, amaa ti mammansi gba, tirim ban daŇčŇči n‚ÄĎdeaai Sii maa, ti gba nyuusiri ni ti sufu ni n‚ÄĎguusiri sahanseaa Naawunni Ňč…Ēn nin deaai ti hoo u biisi, ka fa ti ningbina m‚ÄĎbasi la.

ref: And this hope is what saves us. But if we already have what we hope for, there is no need to keep on hoping.
text: Dama ti pun deaai tuliginsim, ka di ny…õ la tin mari lala tama maa ziiya. T…Ē, ka ti nini yi pun nya bunseelli, lala pa la tama marigu; dama s…Ēs…Ē ku laa n‚ÄĎy…õli, ni, u mari tama, ka mun pun nya.

ref: However, we hope for something we have not yet seen, and we patiently wait for it.
text: Amaa ti yi mari tama zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči ti nini Ňč…Ēn bu nyari bunseaa, ti nin guri di la ni sugru.

ref: In certain ways we are weak, but the Spirit is here to help us. For example, when we don't know what to pray for, the Spirit prays for us in ways that cannot be put into words.
text: Lala ka Sii maa suŇčŇčni ti, tin ka yaa maa zugu. Dama ti bu mi din da sum ni ti susi Naawunni seela, amaa Sii maa z…Ēri ti sindaani n‚ÄĎtiri ti ni nimmoaa, halli ka s…Ē ku toaai n‚ÄĎy…õli u nyuusigu gbinni zaa Ňč…Ēn ny…õ seem gba.

ref: All of our thoughts are known to God. He can understand what is in the mind of the Spirit, as the Spirit prays for God's people.
text: Naawunni mun viisiri m‚ÄĎbaŇčŇči din b…õ ninsaalidima sufu ni, ka laa m‚ÄĎbaŇčŇči din b…õ Sii maa teesa ni, dama Sii maa z…Ēri la Naawunni niriba sindaani n‚ÄĎtiri ba, ka Ňč…Ēn niŇčni seem maa d…Ēli la Naawunni yuubu soori.

ref: We know that God is always at work for the good of everyone who loves him. They are the ones God has chosen for his purpose,
text: Ti mi halli, ni, seelli kam zaa ni, Naawunni suŇčŇčni la ninvugseeba ban wuri u maa - di ny…õ la: baseeba Ňč…Ēn Naawunni Ňč…Ēn daa daŇčŇči m‚ÄĎpii maa, u yuubu la zugu.

ref: and he has always known who his chosen ones would be. He had decided to let them become like his own Son, so his Son would be the first of many children.
text: Dama u siri daa daŇčŇči m‚ÄĎmi u niriba, ka laa n‚ÄĎzaali ba, ni ba ti Ňčmanni u Bii la. Ka lala ky…õ ka u Bii maa nin ny…õ Zuuwa u babiisi pam saani.

ref: God then accepted the people he had already decided to choose, and he has shared his glory with them. God's Love
text: Ka Naawunni Ňč…Ēn daa daŇčŇči n‚ÄĎzaali seeba lala maa, ban ka u daa yiisi; ka daa yiisi ba, n‚ÄĎdeaai ba, ni, y…õlimaŇčni dima. Ka Ňč…Ēn daa deaai ba lala maa, u daa laa n‚ÄĎti ba gyirima.

ref: What can we say about all this? If God is on our side, can anyone be against us?
text: T…Ē, ka dinŇč…Ēna wula ka ti ya y…õli di zugu? Naawunni yi b…õ ti saani, Ňč…Ēni n nin toaai n‚ÄĎzabri ti? Ay! s…Ē ka ni.

ref: God did not keep back his own Son, but he gave him for us. If God did this, won't he freely give us everything else?
text: Ňä…Ēna Ňč…Ēn daa bu za'asi, ni, u toori Bii ku kpi, amaa u daa zaŇčŇči u n‚ÄĎtisi ti zaa zugu; ka dinŇč…Ēna, wula zugu ka u ku ti ti bunsuma kam zaa m‚ÄĎpaasi u zugu? - U ku toaai m‚ÄĎm…ĒŇčŇči.

ref: If God says his chosen ones are acceptable to him, can anyone bring charges against them?
text: Ňä…Ēni n lee n‚ÄĎnya galinsi ni u ti t…Ē'asi n‚ÄĎgalim Naawunni ninvuggamsa maa? - ay! s…Ē ka ni. Dama Naawunni pun deaai ba, ni, y…õlimaŇčni dima.

ref: Or can anyone condemn them? No indeed! Christ died and was raised to life, and now he is at God's right side, speaking to him for us.
text: Bee, Ňč…Ēni n nin lee n‚ÄĎkari ba sariya n‚ÄĎlusi ba? Dama di ny…õ la Kirisita Ňč…Ēn daa kpi, ka din gaari lala, u ny…õ la Ňč…Ēn yisigi kum ni. Ka Ňč…Ēn mun ziya Naawunni nudirigu ya'ari la, ka laa n‚ÄĎz…Ēri ti sindaani Naawunni saani.

ref: Can anything separate us from the love of Christ? Can trouble, suffering, and hard times, or hunger and nakedness, or danger and death?
text: Ka Ňč…Ēni n nin toaai n‚ÄĎw…Ēligi tin ni Kirisita wurilim? DaaŇčŇču bee? - bee mugsigu? Ban tumni ti putoo nin niŇči? K…Ēm Ňč…Ēn ti lu, bee, ti ti k…ĒŇčŇči kyinkyina? Dabeem y…õla nin niŇči, bee t…Ēppu?

ref: It is exactly as the Scriptures say, ‚ÄúFor you we face death all day long. We are like sheep on their way to be butchered.‚ÄĚ
text: - Hoo din pun s…Ēbi n‚ÄĎdooli, ni#:- ‚ÄúNyin, Naawunni, zugu ka ba kuuri ti, Beoo kam zaa ka ba kuuri ti. Dizugu ti Ňčmanni la peesi - Peesi ban ya k…Ēri seeba.‚ÄĚ

ref: In everything we have won more than a victory because of Christ who loves us.
text: - Ay! AnŇč…Ē zaa puuni ti nin nya nyaŇčŇčni, Yisa Ňč…Ēn wuri ti maa zugu.

ref: I am a follower of Christ, and the Holy Spirit is a witness to my conscience. So I tell the truth and I am not lying when I say
text: Man Ňč…Ēn ya t…Ē'asiri n‚ÄĎtiri ya wa ny…õ la y…õlimaŇčni, man Ňč…Ēn b…õ Kirisita saani maa zugu; m bu ma'ari ziri, halli Naawunni Sii Kasi kur ky…õ ka n sufu ni teesa wulisiri lala gba.

ref: I would gladly be placed under God's curse and be separated from Christ for the good of my own people.
text: Ni, m mari la sufusa'aŇčŇču pam, ni beerim din bu naara; ka di zaa ny…õ la m mabiisi, Y…õhudiyadima maa, zugu. Dama di yi naa n‚ÄĎsuŇčŇči bana, man naa n‚ÄĎsakki, ni, Naawunni ti za'asi ma, ka kari ma Kirisita saani.

ref: They are the descendants of Israel, and they are also God's chosen people. God showed them his glory. He made agreements with them and gave them his Law. The temple is theirs and so are the promises that God made to them.

text: Dizugu m boosiri ya, ni: ammi, ba tuya ni ba ti lu tiŇčŇča? - Ay! di pa lala. Amaa di ny…õ la ban burigri la zugu ka fa-m‚ÄĎbasi paai ban pa Y…õhudiyadima la saani na, ka lala nin ky…õ ka ban Y…õhudiyadima maa ti zabri nyuuri.

ref: But if the rest of the world's people were helped so much by their sin and loss, they will be helped even more by their full return.
text: Dizugu, ba burigri maa ky…õ ka bunsuma paari la duniya wa dima maa zaa, halli ba p…Ēsigri zugu ka ban pa Y…õhudiyadima leebi hoo bundaandima. T…Ē, ka di yi b…õ lala, Y…õhudiyadima zaa Ňč…Ēn ti la'am m‚ÄĎpaasi maa, di ya niŇči la wula? - di siri nin veela n‚ÄĎgaari zaa!

ref: I am now speaking to you Gentiles, and as long as I am an apostle to you, I will take pride in my work.
text: T…Ē: ka pampaŇčŇč…Ē n ya t…Ē'asiri n‚ÄĎtiri yaseeba ban pa Y…õhudiyadima la. Ka man Ňč…Ēn ny…õ la tuumu n‚ÄĎti yan ban pa Y…õhudiyadima maa, n nin tum n tuma maa zaa ni nimbirimoaa.

ref: I hope in this way to make some of my own people jealous enough to be saved.
text: N teesiri, ni, di pa seem n Y…õhudiyataaba nin ti zabi nyuuri, ka lala nin ky…õ ka ba ninvugseeba ti nya fa-m‚ÄĎbasi.

ref: When Israel rejected God, the rest of the people in the world were able to turn to him. So when God makes friends with Israel, it will be like bringing the dead back to life.
text: Dama sahanseaa ka Naawunni daa za'asi ba la, lala zugu daa ky…õ ka u ti la'asi duniya dima zaa na. Ka di yi b…õ lala, u yi laa n‚ÄĎdeaari ba na, di ya b…õ la wula? - Pa ny…Ēvuri n‚ÄĎyi kum ni na nla?

ref: If part of a batch of dough is made holy by being offered to God, then all of the dough is holy. If the roots of a tree are holy, the rest of the tree is holy too.
text: Ba yi daŇčŇči n‚ÄĎgbeaai maasibeeri beela nti zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎtisi Naawunni, lala zugu yi ky…õ ka maasa maa zaa ny…õ la Naawunni dinni. Ba yi zaŇčŇči tinya'ari n‚ÄĎtisi Naawunni, lala zugu yi ky…õ ka timunni maa ny…õ la u dinni.

ref: You Gentiles are like branches of a wild olive tree made to be part of a cultivated olive tree. You have taken the place of some branches that were cut away from it. And because of this, you enjoy the blessings that come from being part of that cultivated tree.
text: Di Ňčmanni la hoo ban Ňčmaai tiseaa kan wanni wula m‚ÄĎbasi ka zaŇčŇči moo ni tiwulli n‚ÄĎtugi n‚ÄĎtabli. Lala ka ba zaŇčŇči i n‚ÄĎkpeesi n‚ÄĎtabli Naawunni niriba sunsukkuni. Ka tiwulipaalli wa deaari la sum ni bunsuma zaa an yiiri tii maa nya'a ni na.

ref: But don't think you are better than the branches that were cut away. Just remember you are not supporting the roots of this tree. Its roots are supporting you.
text: Ka di yi b…õ lala, nyin bumpaalli ku toaai n‚ÄĎnyu bara n‚ÄĎzeemni bunkura la. I yi boori i nyu bara, kur teaama, ni, di pa la nyini m‚ÄĎmari tii la nya'a maa, amaa nya'a maa n mari i.

ref: Maybe you think those branches were cut away, so you could be put in their place.
text: Di pa seem i ya y…õli, ni, ba daa Ňčmaai wulikura maa m‚ÄĎbasi ni ba ti tugi i n‚ÄĎtabli.

ref: That's true enough. But they were cut away because they did not have faith, and you are where you are because you do have faith. So don't be proud, but be afraid.
text: T…Ē, y…õlimaŇčni nla. Lala wula maa ka ba daa Ňčmaai m‚ÄĎbasi a y…õdda kaalinsi maa zugu; ka nyini ka ba daa tugi n‚ÄĎtabli, i y…õdda niŇčbu zugu. Dizugu nyin di mari karimbaani, see ka i z…Ēri dabeem,

ref: If God cut away those natural branches, couldn't he do the same to you?
text: dama Naawunni daa bu niŇči sugru n‚ÄĎti wulimaŇča la, ka u ku ti di sugru n‚ÄĎti nyini gba.

ref: Now you see both how kind and how hard God can be. He was hard on those who fell, but he was kind to you. And he will keep on being kind to you, if you keep on trusting in his kindness. Otherwise, you will be cut away too.
text: I bu nya Naawunni Ňč…Ēn z…Ēri namboo seem? - ka laa n‚ÄĎdarigri tuba gba. U darigri ban lu la tuba, amaa ka z…Ē nyini namboo, i yi ti kpalim m‚ÄĎb…õ u namb…Ēzoaa maa ni. Di yi pa lala, u nin Ňčmaai i gba m‚ÄĎbasi.

ref: If those other branches will start having faith, they will be made a part of that tree again. God has the power to put them back.
text: Ka banla gba yi ti ky…õ ba y…õdda za'asigu, ba nin laa n‚ÄĎtugi n‚ÄĎkpeesi yaasa, dama Naawunni mari la yiiko ni u ti niŇči lala.

ref: After all, it wasn't natural for branches to be cut from a wild olive tree and to be made part of a cultivated olive tree. So it is much more likely that God will join the natural branches back to the cultivated olive tree. The People of Israel Will Be Brought Back
text: I bu nya? U daa pun zaŇčŇči nyini, s…Ē Ňč…Ēn ny…õ moo ni tii maa wulli, ni u ti takki i n‚ÄĎtugi n‚ÄĎkp…õ tisuŇčŇču ni n‚ÄĎtabli. T…Ē: lala n ny…õ di; ka dinŇč…Ēna labsim ma: ammi, i teesiri, ni, u ku toaai n‚ÄĎzaŇčŇči tisuŇčŇču wulimaŇča la ka laa n‚ÄĎtugi n‚ÄĎtabli? U nin toaai, walla!

ref: My friends, I don't want you Gentiles to be too proud of yourselves. So I will explain the mystery of what has happened to the people of Israel. Some of them have become stubborn, and they will stay like that until the complete number of you Gentiles has come in.
text: M mabiisi, y…õlis…Ē'ara nŇč…Ēa, amaa m bu boori ni ya k…ĒŇčŇči a bamsim, ka naayi zaŇčŇči ya toori y…õm m‚ÄĎboori a gbinni. Kulla Izra…õli dimseeba daa mari zugkpeeŇčŇču ni tuppuwumni, see ka ban pa Izra…õli dima ti kp…õ na ka ba kaalli maa ti paai.

ref: In this way all of Israel will be saved, as the Scriptures say, ‚ÄúFrom Zion someone will come to rescue us. Then Jacob's descendants will stop being evil.
text: Ka di naayi ky…õ ka Izra…õli dima zaa ti nya tuliginsim hoo din daa s…Ēbi n‚ÄĎdooli, ni#:- ‚ÄúTuligra maa nin yi Zay…Ēn na; U nin yiisi din bu tuusi zaa m‚ÄĎbasi, Ka ky…õ ka Yaakubu dima ti leebi kasi zaa.

ref: This is what the Lord has promised to do when he forgives their sins.‚ÄĚ
text: Lala ka n ti ba noori, n‚ÄĎzaali nangbanyinni zaaligu n‚ÄĎti ba: Di saha ka n nin ky…õ ba tumbeeri m‚ÄĎp…õŇčŇči ba.‚ÄĚ

ref: The people of Israel are treated as God's enemies, so the good news can come to you Gentiles. But they are still the chosen ones, and God loves them because of their famous ancestors.
text: Ban saani ka di kur b…õ la lala: zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči lahabasuŇčŇču maa y…õla, ban ni Naawunni mari la dimni, ka di ny…õ la yarima zugu. ZaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči Naawunni yiisigu piini zugu muna, ba mari la wurilim, ka di ny…õ la ba yaadima zugu.

ref: God doesn't take back the gifts he has given or disown the people he has chosen.
text: Dama Naawunni yi ti ya zaŇčŇči u fa-m‚ÄĎbasi piini n‚ÄĎtisi seeba, bee, u yi ti booli seeba u saani na, u bu ya takki u lala teesa maa.

ref: At one time you Gentiles rejected God. But now Israel has rejected God, and you have been shown mercy.
text: Yarima daa bu wum n‚ÄĎti Naawunni, amaa ka yi nya u namboo pampaŇčŇč…Ēa, ka di ny…õ la ban Ňč…Ēn bu sakkri Naawunni wumpu zugu.

ref: All people have disobeyed God, and that's why he treats them as prisoners. But he does this, so that he can have mercy on all of them.
text: Dama Naawunni kpeesi s…Ēkam tuppuwumni zii ni, hoo ban kpatti la tumbeeri dima dansarika duu ni seem la. Ka u niŇči lala ni u ti ky…õ ka s…Ēkam zaa naayi nya u namb…Ēzoaa.

ref: Who can measure the wealth and wisdom and knowledge of God? Who can understand his decisions or explain what he does?
text: Naawunni bunsuma n zulima, yee! U katti la sariya ka di gbinni s…Ē'aya; u s…Ēya zaa mun ka baŇčŇčni! Ni#:-

ref: ‚ÄúHas anyone known the thoughts of the Lord or given him advice?
text: ‚ÄúKa Ňč…Ēni n na mi m‚ÄĎbaŇčŇči Naawunni teesa gbinni? Bee, Ňč…Ēni n na mi n‚ÄĎkpaam u?‚ÄĚ

ref: Has anyone loaned something to the Lord that must be repaid?‚ÄĚ
text: Ni#:- ‚ÄúHalli Ňč…Ēni n‚ÄĎdaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎti Naawunni piini, Ni Naawunni ti labsi n‚ÄĎti u yaasa?‚ÄĚ

ref: Everything comes from the Lord. All things were made because of him and will return to him. Praise the Lord forever! Amen.
text: Dama bunseelikam zaa yi la u saani na, ka Ňč…Ēn zugu ka a b…õ ni, ka a laa m‚ÄĎpaari u saani. Ňä…Ēna n sum ni p…õ'abu saha din ka t…Ēriga. Ammi.

ref: Dear friends, God is good. So I beg you to offer your bodies to him as a living sacrifice, pure and pleasing. That's the most sensible way to serve God.
text: Dizugu n susiri ya ni, m mabiisi, Naawunni namb…Ēzoaa zugu, ni, ya zaŇčŇčma ya mansi m‚ÄĎmaali sara n‚ÄĎti Naawunni ka u nini tigi. Di ny…õ la: yan na n…õ wa, ya nin leebi Naawunni dinni, dama dina n ny…õ gyeeŇčŇču maŇčni n‚ÄĎtiri u.

ref: Don't be like the people of this world, but let God change the way you think. Then you will know how to do everything that is good and pleasing to him.
text: Yan di ky…õ ka duniya wa dima beesigu mugsi ya, ni ya gba b…õ la lala; amaa ya ky…õ ka ya beesigu takki n‚ÄĎd…Ēli ya nini Ňč…Ēn neaai n‚ÄĎnya s…Ēpaalli la. Ya tumma Naawunni yuubu tuma nti baŇčŇči a gbinni, ni, a veela ka laa n‚ÄĎny…õ bunsuma zaa ka Naawunni nini n tigri a.

ref: I realize God has treated me with undeserved grace, and so I tell each of you not to think you are better than you really are. Use good sense and measure yourself by the amount of faith that God has given you.
text: T…Ē; Naawunni Ňč…Ēn pun zaŇčŇči u suŇčŇčsim piini n‚ÄĎti ma la zugu, n kpaamni ya zaa yinniyinni nŇč…Ēa: ni, yan di g…Ēsi ya mammansi y…õla n‚ÄĎteesi, ni, a veela, halli ka ya lala teesa gaari din siri ny…õ y…õlimaŇčni maa. Ay! - Ya ky…õ ka ya teesa d…Ēli la yam maŇčni, hoo Naawunni Ňč…Ēn zaŇčŇči y…õdda n‚ÄĎti ya s…Ēkam yinniyinni seem.

ref: A body is made up of many parts, and each of them has its own use.
text: Ya nyama - ningbiŇčŇču maa ny…õ la bummunni yinni, amaa tuga pam b…õ ka ni, ka a seelikam zaa mari la ka tumni, ka tumni.

ref: That's how it is with us. There are many of us, but we each are part of the body of Christ, as well as part of one another.
text: Di ny…õ la lala n‚ÄĎti ti zaa: ti zoaaya halli, amaa ti ny…õ la hoo ningbimmunni, tin Ňč…Ēn b…õ Yisa Kirisita ni la zugu. Ka ti zaa Ňčmanni la u ningbiŇčŇču tuga n‚ÄĎsuŇčŇčni taaba.

ref: God has also given each of us different gifts to use. If we can prophesy, we should do it according to the amount of faith we have.
text: Ya bu nya? Naawunni pun zaŇčŇči u piini n‚ÄĎti ti zaa, ka a ny…õ la kookooba. S…Ē yi ny…õ anabi, Naawunni piini maa zugu, Ňč…Ēn t…Ē'asima u y…õdda Ňč…Ēn yi ti ti u, ni u ti y…õli seem la.

ref: If we can serve others, we should serve. If we can teach, we should teach.
text: S…Ē mun yi gyeemni la niriba, Naawunni piini zugu, Ňč…Ēn z…õ yummu u lala tuma maa: ka s…Ē mun yi tumni karimba tuma, Ňč…Ēn gba tumma ni nimmoaa.

ref: If we can encourage others, we should encourage them. If we can give, we should be generous. If we are leaders, we should do our best. If we are good to others, we should do it cheerfully. Rules for Christian Living
text: Yaasa, s…Ē yi kpamsiri niriba, di sum ni u kpamsiri ba suŇčŇča; s…Ē mun yi t…Ētti t…Ēra, Ňč…Ēn tumma ni u sufu zaa; s…Ē mun yi su toondaana fukumsi, nimmoosi ka u tumni u tuma; halli s…Ē yi ya suŇčŇči faradima, Ňč…Ēn mari sufupeelli n‚ÄĎsuŇčŇči ba.

ref: Be sincere in your love for others. Hate everything that is evil and hold tight to everything that is good.
text: Ky…õ ka ya siri wuri la niriba y…õlimaŇčni ni, ka di y…Ēmsira. Ya yaama Sintaani y…õla, ka gyi din ny…õ bunsuŇčŇču.

ref: Love each other as brothers and sisters and honor others more than you do yourself.
text: ZaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči mabitirigu wurilim, see ka ya wuri taaba suŇčŇča. ZaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči gyirima mun tisibu, see ka ya gyeemni taaba nimmoosi.

ref: Let your hope make you glad. Be patient in time of trouble and never stop praying.
text: Yan mari tama maa zugu, ya ky…õ ka ya sufu maai; yan mun nya namsigu sahanseaa maa, ya kur mari sugru di puuni; ka yan susiri Naawunni maa, ya susima nimbirimoaa.

ref: Take care of God's needy people and welcome strangers into your home.
text: Ya suŇčŇčma ya nyaand…Ēlitaaba ban b…õ wahala ni la, ka s…õ'asi n‚ÄĎdeaari saamma n‚ÄĎkpeesiri ya yiya ni.

ref: Ask God to bless everyone who mistreats you. Ask him to bless them and not to curse them.
text: Ya susim bunsuma Naawunni saani n‚ÄĎtiri seeba ban mugsiri ya: ii! - ya susim bunsuma, ka pa la bumbeoo n‚ÄĎtiri ba.

ref: When others are happy, be happy with them, and when they are sad, be sad.
text: Baseeba ban mari sufupeelli maa, ya paasima ba zugu m‚ÄĎmari sufupeelli; ban mun sufu sa'am, ya paasima ba zugu sufusa'aŇčŇču ni.

ref: Be friendly with everyone. Don't be proud and feel that you know more than others. Make friends with ordinary people.
text: Ya la'amma m‚ÄĎb…õ ni taaba ni sugru. Yan di duusi ya mansi amaa ka sakki n‚ÄĎtabli tarimma saani. Yan di boori ya mansi, bee, n‚ÄĎy…õtti ni, y…õm daandima n ny…õ ya.

ref: Don't mistreat someone who has mistreated you. But try to earn the respect of others,
text: Yan di tum tumbeeri n‚ÄĎlabsi s…Ēa, Ňč…Ēn tum tumbeoo n‚ÄĎniŇči ya la zugu. Ay! - ya kpaŇčŇčma ya mansi n‚ÄĎtumni tunseesi niriba zaa Ňč…Ēn g…Ēsiri n‚ÄĎy…õtti, ni, a veela.

ref: and do your best to live at peace with everyone.
text: Ya ky…õ ka yan ni s…Ēkam mari la nangbanyinni, halli ya yi ti nyaŇčŇči lala.

ref: Dear friends, don't try to get even. Let God take revenge. In the Scriptures the Lord says, ‚ÄúI am the one to take revenge and pay them back.‚ÄĚ
text: M mabiwutta, yan di y…Ē s…Ē tumbeeri samni, amaa ya ky…õ u yoobu y…õla n‚ÄĎti la Naawunni, dama Ňč…Ēna n su tubdarigri y…õla zaa. Dama di s…Ēbi n‚ÄĎdooli, ni, ti Dugma y…õliya, ni, labsigu n ny…õ u dinni, ni, Ňč…Ēna nin labsi n‚ÄĎy…Ēa.

ref: The Scriptures also say, ‚ÄúIf your enemies are hungry, give them something to eat. And if they are thirsty, give them something to drink. This will be the same as piling burning coals on their heads.‚ÄĚ
text: N‚ÄĎlaa m‚ÄĎpaasi, ni, ‚ÄúK…Ēm yi mari i dindaana, nyin tim u diibu ka u di; k…Ēnyuuri mun yi mari u, nyin tim u koom: i yi niŇči nŇč…Ē di nin Ňčmanni la i moaari la bugunsaala n‚ÄĎziinni u zugu.‚ÄĚ

ref: Don't let evil defeat you, but defeat evil with good.
text: Di ky…õ ka tumbeoo nyaŇčŇči i; see ka i mari tunsuŇčŇču n‚ÄĎnyaŇčŇči tumbeoo.

ref: Obey the rulers who have authority over you. Only God can give authority to anyone, and he puts these rulers in their places of power.
text: Ya ky…õ ka s…Ēkam zaa sakki n‚ÄĎti tinkpamma, ni ban su fukumsi la zaa. Dama kpamni ni fukumsi n ka ni m‚ÄĎbu yi Naawunni saani na. Ňä…Ēn Naawunni pun zaali naam fukumsi dima an b…õ ni zaa.

ref: People who oppose the authorities are opposing what God has done, and they will be punished.
text: Ka Ňč…Ēnkam za'asi fukumsi dima, di daama za'asi la Naawunni Ňč…Ēn zaali seela. Ka seeba ban za'asi ba la muna - ba sumsi la tubdarigri.

ref: Rulers are a threat to evil people, not to good people. There is no need to be afraid of the authorities. Just do right, and they will praise you for it.
text: Y…õlimaŇčni, Ňč…Ēni n z…Ēri fukumsi dima dabeem? - Di pa la seeba ban tumni tunsuma la, amaa ban tumni tumbeeri la. I yi boori ni i b…õ ni ka ku z…Ēri fukumsi dima dabeem, nyin tumma tunsuma ka ba nin p…õ'ai i.

ref: After all, they are God's servants, and it is their duty to help you. If you do something wrong, you ought to be afraid, because these rulers have the right to punish you. They are God's servants who punish criminals to show how angry God is.
text: Ňä…Ēn su naam fukumsi la ny…õ la Naawunni tuntumna, i suŇčŇčsim zugu. Amaa i yi ti tum din bu tuusi, nyin z…Ēma u, dama u bu mari naam yiiko maa y…Ēri; dama u ny…õ la Naawunni Ňč…Ēn zaali s…Ē ni u ti zaŇčŇči Ňč…Ēn Naawunni sufuyisigri n‚ÄĎdarigri tumbeeri dima tuba.

ref: But you should obey the rulers because you know it is the right thing to do, and not just because of God's anger.
text: Dizugu di sum ni ti sakkri n‚ÄĎtiri naam fukumsi dima, pa dabeem zugu k…Ēk…Ēa, amaa ti sufu ni teesa Ňč…Ēn mi, ni, lala veela maa zugu.

ref: You must also pay your taxes. The authorities are God's servants, and it is their duty to take care of these matters.
text: Lala zugu gba ka ya yoori lampoo ni faarigu, dama ban deaari maa tumni n‚ÄĎtiri Naawunni ni nimbirimoaa.

ref: Let love be your only debt! If you love others, you have done all that the Law demands.
text: Yan di di s…Ē zaa samni. Sanseaa bu d…Ēli ya, di yi pa la, ni, ya wuri taaba; dama Ňč…Ēnkam wuri u taraana pun d…Ēli la zaaligu zaa.

ref: In the Law there are many commands, such as, ‚ÄúBe faithful in marriage. Do not murder. Do not steal. Do not want what belongs to others.‚ÄĚ But all of these are summed up in the command that says, ‚ÄúLove others as much as you love yourself.‚ÄĚ
text: Ya mi zaaligudima la y…õla: ni, ‚ÄúDi tum zina.‚ÄĚ, ni, ‚ÄúDi ku ninsaala.‚ÄĚ, ‚ÄúDi zu.‚ÄĚ, ni, ‚ÄúDi ky…õ ka i sufu k…Ērisi kp…õ i kpeaa buna zugu.‚ÄĚ - halli ni an kpalim zaa m‚ÄĎpaasi: t…Ē, a zaa b…õ la y…õlli yinni wa puuni, ni, ‚ÄúWuri i taraana hoo nyin wuri i mammaŇčŇča seem la.‚ÄĚ

ref: No one who loves others will harm them. So love is all that the Law demands. The Day When Christ Returns
text: Wurilim daana ku toaai n‚ÄĎsa'am u taraana y…õla, lala zugu ka wurilim lu n‚ÄĎti zaaligudima la zaa.

ref: You know what sort of times we live in, and so you should live properly. It is time to wake up. You know that the day when we will be saved is nearer now than when we first put our faith in the Lord.
text: T…Ē, ya tumma anŇč…Ēa, dama ya mi saha maa Ňč…Ēn paai seem la. Saha paaya: yan neaam goom ni, dama ti tuliginsim saha maa ku laa n‚ÄĎyuui hoo din daa yuui seem tin daa daŇčŇči n‚ÄĎniŇči Naawunni y…õdda sahanseaa la.

ref: Night is almost over, and day will soon appear. We must stop behaving as people do in the dark and be ready to live in the light.
text: YuŇčŇču ky…õnni n‚ÄĎgatta, ka beoo ya neaai. Dizugu ya ky…õ ka ti za'asi tunseesi an tumni zibsim ni la zaa, ka zaŇčŇči la neesim ni tuma hoo t…Ēbbiisi Ňč…Ēn zaŇčŇči ba neema ni ba ti tuusi t…Ēppu seem la.

ref: So behave properly, as people do in the day. Don't go to wild parties or get drunk or be vulgar or indecent. Don't quarrel or be jealous.
text: WuntaŇčŇča ni ka ti b…õ ni, dizugu ya ky…õ ka ti beebu d…Ēli la soori, ka pa la dimaŇčŇča ni daam nyuubu, bee, p…Ē'agoorilim ni d…Ēgoorilim ni tumbeeri zaa, bee, zaba ni nyuuri zappu. Ay, ya ky…õl anŇč…Ē zaa m‚ÄĎbasi,

ref: Let the Lord Jesus Christ be as near to you as the clothes you wear. Then you won't try to satisfy your selfish desires.
text: ka zaŇčŇči ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Masiiya n‚ÄĎniŇči ya beesigu ni, hoo nira Ňč…Ēn yi y…õ u sutura seem la. Ka yan di d…Ēli ya ningbink…Ērisi yuubu tumbeeri maa, halli beela.

ref: Welcome all the Lord's followers, even those whose faith is weak. Don't criticize them for having beliefs that are different from yours.
text: Ninvugs…Ē yi b…õ ni ka u y…õdda bu kpeem Naawunni d…Ēligu ni, yan deaam u ya saani, amaa yan di Ňčm…õ u nangbankpeemni zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči u kyisigu y…õla.

ref: Some think it is all right to eat anything, while those whose faith is weak will eat only vegetables.
text: Dama seeba mari la y…õdda din ky…õ ka ba diri bunseelikam zaa; seeba mun y…õdda bu kpeem, ka ba diri la z…õvaari k…Ēk…Ē di zugu.

ref: But you should not criticize others for eating or for not eating. After all, God welcomes everyone.
text: Ninvugs…Ē Ňč…Ēn nin toaai n‚ÄĎdi bunseelikam zaa la di galim Ňč…Ēn bu diri la, ka ninvugs…Ē Ňč…Ēn diri z…õvaari k…Ēk…Ē la gba di galim Ňč…Ēnla, dama Naawunni deaai di daama gba.

ref: What right do you have to criticize someone else's servants? Only their Lord can decide if they are doing right, and the Lord will make sure that they do right.
text: I ny…õ la Ňč…Ēni n‚ÄĎkatti s…Ē daabri sariya? Di ti ny…õ la Ňč…Ēn su u maa n nin baŇčŇči u nyaŇčŇčiya, bee, u luya. Halli u nin nyaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎz…õ yummu, dama u dugma nin toaai n‚ÄĎky…õ ka u nyaŇčŇči.

ref: Some of the Lord's followers think one day is more important than another. Others think all days are the same. But each of you should make up your own mind.
text: M‚ÄĎpaasi yaasa, seeba saani dabsiseesi veela n‚ÄĎgaari taaba, ka seeba mun saani ka a zaa ny…õ la yimmu. S…Ēkam zaa zaanma suŇčŇča u toori teesa puuni.

ref: Any followers who count one day more important than another day do it to honor their Lord. And any followers who eat meat give thanks to God, just like the ones who don't eat meat.
text: S…Ē yi gamsiri dabsa, Naawunni yuuri zugu ka u niŇčni lala; s…Ē mun yi diri seelli zaa, Naawunni yuuri zugu ka u dira, dama u puusiri la Naawunni bundirigu zugu; halli s…Ē yi bu sakki n‚ÄĎdiri bundiriseesi, Naawunni yuuri ka Ňč…Ēn gba niŇčni lala, ka Ňč…Ēn gba puusiri la Naawunni u bundirigu zugu.

ref: Whether we live or die, it must be for God, rather than for ourselves.
text: Dama ti s…Ē ka ni m‚ÄĎb…õ u toori zugu, s…Ē mun ka ni n‚ÄĎkpiiri u k…Ēk…Ē zugu.

ref: Whether we live or die, it must be for the Lord. Alive or dead, we still belong to the Lord.
text: Ti yi b…õ ni, ti Dugma zugu ka ti b…õ ni; ti mun yi kpi, ti kpi la ti Dugma zugu. Dinzugu ti yi b…õ ni, bee, ti kpi ni, ti Dugma n su ti y…õla zaa.

ref: This is because Christ died and rose to life, so that he would be the Lord of the dead and of the living.
text: DinŇč…Ē zugu ka Kirisita daa kpi ka laa n‚ÄĎyisigi kum ni: ni, u leebi ban pun kpi, ni ban na b…õ ni maa zaa, Dugma.

ref: Why do you criticize other followers of the Lord? Why do you look down on them? The day is coming when God will judge all of us.
text: I ny…õ la Ňč…Ēni? - nyin Ňč…Ēn galimni i mabiiya n‚ÄĎzeemni u lala? Ti zaa nin ti zaani Naawunni maa tooni ka u kari ti sariya.

ref: And so, each of us must give an account to God for what we do. Don't Cause Problems for Others
text: Dizugu ti s…Ēkam zaa nin t…Ē'asi u toori beebu y…õtt…Ē'a n‚ÄĎti Naawunni.

ref: We must stop judging others. We must also make up our minds not to upset anyone's faith.
text: Dinzugu ti di laa n‚ÄĎgalimni taaba, amaa ya ky…õ ka ti g…Ēsi tin nin niŇči seem ka ti mabiisi di burigi, bee, n‚ÄĎlu tumbeeri ni.

ref: The Lord Jesus has made it clear to me that God considers all foods fit to eat. But if you think some foods are unfit to eat, then for you they are not fit.
text: Man Ňč…Ēn b…õ Kirisita ni maa ky…õ ka man bu nya ka bundiriseaa kyisiri ka toori; amaa s…Ē yi teesi u sufu ni, ni, ka kyisira, dinŇč…Ēna ka siri kyisiri n‚ÄĎti di daama.

ref: If you are hurting others by the foods you eat, you are not guided by love. Don't let your appetite destroy someone Christ died for.
text: I yi daamni i mabiiya nyin diri seelli zugu, dinŇč…Ēna i bu tumni la wurilim zugu. Kirisita daa kpi u zugu, ka nyin di ky…õ ka i bundirigu diibu zugu ti sa'am u y…õla.

ref: Don't let your right to eat bring shame to Christ.
text: Yan di ky…õ ka bunsunseaa yan mari maa ti nya yubeoo.

ref: God's kingdom isn't about eating and drinking. It is about pleasing God, about living in peace, and about true happiness. All this comes from the Holy Spirit.
text: Dama Naawunni naam maa pa la diibu, ni nyuubu; amaa naam maa ny…õ la y…õlimaŇčni, ni sufudoaa, ni nya'asim piiniseaa Naawunni Sii maa Ňč…Ēn tisira.

ref: If you serve Christ in this way, you will please God and be respected by people.
text: Ka s…Ē yi d…Ēli la soori wa n‚ÄĎgyeemni Kirisita, Naawunni nini tigi di daama, ka u yuuri du ninsaalidima saani.

ref: We should try to live at peace and help each other have a strong faith.
text: Lala zugu, ya ky…õ ka ti g…Ēsiri la bunseesi an nin mari sufudoaa ka ky…õ ka ti suŇčŇčni taaba ka ky…õnni tooni.

ref: Don't let your appetite destroy what God has done. All foods are fit to eat, but it is wrong to cause problems for others by what you eat.
text: Yan di ky…õ ka bundirigu zugu ti sa'am Naawunni Ňč…Ēn maali seelli. Bundiriseelli bu kyisira, amaa di bu veeli ni i di seelli ka di nin ky…õ ka i mabii maa lu tumbeoo ni.

ref: It is best not to eat meat or drink wine or do anything else that causes problems for other followers of the Lord.
text: Ti yi Ňč…Ēbi numni, bee, n‚ÄĎnyu daam, bee, anŇč…Ē taabu zaa, ka lala zugu ky…õ ka ti mabii lu tumbeeri ni, dinŇč…Ēna ti di Ňč…Ēbi, bee, n‚ÄĎnyu a, halli fiiŇč. Lala n s…Ēa.

ref: What you believe about these things should be kept between you and God. You are fortunate, if your actions don't make you have doubts.
text: Kur mari i y…õdda wa y…õla suŇčŇča, nyin ni Naawunni saani. ZugsuŇčŇču nin b…õ ni n‚ÄĎti Ňč…Ēn baŇčŇči u sufu ni, ni, Ňč…Ēn niŇčni seem wa ka galinsi.

ref: But if you do have doubts about what you eat, you are going against your beliefs. And you know that is wrong, because anything you do against your beliefs is sin.
text: Amaa Ňč…Ēn birisiri Ňč…Ēn di seelli maa zugu, ka naayi di, Naawunni nin galim di daama di zugu, dama u bu d…Ēli la u y…õdda soori. Ka seelli zaa din bu d…Ēli y…õdda ny…õ la tumbeoo.

ref: If our faith is strong, we should be patient with the Lord's followers whose faith is weak. We should try to please them instead of ourselves.
text: T…Ē; ka dizugu di sumsi ni tirima ban mari yaa ti y…õdda zugu la ti mari sugru n‚ÄĎtiri ban ka yaa la: see ka ti maari ba sufu, ka ti di boori ti maari ti mammansi sufu.

ref: We should think of their good and try to help them by doing what pleases them.
text: S…Ēkam niŇčma din nin ky…õ ka u taraana sufu maai ka u tumni tunsuma, ka ky…õnni tooni Naawunni saani.

ref: Even Christ did not try to please himself. But as the Scriptures say, ‚ÄúThe people who insulted you also insulted me.‚ÄĚ
text: Halli Kirisita gba daa bu maai la u mammaŇčŇča sufu, amaa di daa b…õ la hoo din daa s…Ēbi n‚ÄĎdooli seem, ni, ‚ÄúBan n daa boori la Naawunni tuubu la daa tu la mani.‚ÄĚ

ref: God is the one who makes us patient and cheerful. I pray that he will help you live at peace with each other, as you follow Christ.
text: Dama Naawunni siri su sugru ni kpamsigu y…õla, ka u nin ky…õ ka ya d…Ēli Yisa Kirisita ka kpalim m‚ÄĎmari nangbanyinni ni taaba.

ref: Then all of you together will praise God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ. The Good News Is for Jews and Gentiles
text: Ka ya nin la'am n‚ÄĎzaŇčŇči nangbanyinni m‚ÄĎp…õ'ai Naawunni Ňč…Ēn ny…õ ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Ba la.

ref: Honor God by accepting each other, as Christ has accepted you.
text: Dinzugu ya deaama taaba hoo Kirisita Ňč…Ēn pun deaai ya seem la, Naawunni p…õ'abu zugu.

ref: I tell you that Christ came as a servant of the Jews to show that God has kept the promises he made to their famous ancestors. Christ also came,
text: Dama n y…õtti ya, ni, Kirisita daa leebi la Y…õhudiyadima maa daabri ni u ti wulisi Naawunni y…õlimaŇčni Ňč…Ēn ny…õ seem, ni u ti zaali nangbambasiri maa deede - nangbambasiseesi Ňč…Ēn Naawunni Ňč…Ēn daa pun basi n‚ÄĎti ti yaadima la.

ref: so that the Gentiles would praise God for being kind to them. It is just as the Scriptures say, ‚ÄúI will tell the nations about you, and I will sing praises to your name.‚ÄĚ
text: Ka laa n‚ÄĎky…õ ka ban pa Y…õhudiyadima la mun p…õ'ari Naawunni u namb…Ēzoaa zugu, hoo din s…Ēbi n‚ÄĎdooli seem, ni#:- ‚ÄúDinzugu n nin p…õ'ai nyin Naawunni, N nin p…õ'ai i ban pa Y…õhudiyadima la saani. N nin yuumni n‚ÄĎtiri i yuuri.‚ÄĚ

ref: The Scriptures also say to the Gentiles, ‚ÄúCome and celebrate with God's people.‚ÄĚ
text: Ka di laa n‚ÄĎs…Ēbi yaasa, ni#:- ‚ÄúYa mari sufupeelli, yan pa Y…õhudiyadima wa, Ya paasim u niriba zugu m‚ÄĎmara.‚ÄĚ

ref: Again the Scriptures say, ‚ÄúPraise the Lord, all you Gentiles. All you nations, come and worship him.‚ÄĚ
text: Yaasa, ni#:- ‚ÄúYa p…õ'ama ti Dugma, yan pa Y…õhudiyadima wa, Ka ky…õ ka s…Ēkam zaa tiri u gyirima.‚ÄĚ

ref: Isaiah says, ‚ÄúSomeone from David's family will come to power. He will rule the nations, and they will put their hope in him.‚ÄĚ
text: Ka Aizaaya gba daa y…õli, ni#:- ‚ÄúGy…õsi tuŇčŇču ni d…Ēs…Ē nin ti b…õ ni n‚ÄĎdoaai na n‚ÄĎsu ban pa Y…õhudiyadima la naam; Ňä…Ēna zugu ka ba nin ti nya tama.‚ÄĚ

ref: I pray that God, who gives hope, will bless you with complete happiness and peace because of your faith. And may the power of the Holy Spirit fill you with hope. Paul's Work as a Missionary
text: Naawunni siri su tama y…õla, ka n susiri u, ni, u ky…õ ka sufumaasim ni sufudoaa ti paali ya sufu, yan niŇčni y…õdda maa zugu. Ka Sii Kasi la yiiko nin ky…õ ka ya mari tama pam halli nti yakki.

ref: My friends, I am sure that you are very good and that you have all the knowledge you need to teach each other.
text: M mabiisi, m mi halli, ni, ya mari y…õlisuma zaa, ka bamsim kur paali ya ni, ka ya nyaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkpaamni taaba.

ref: But I have spoken to you plainly and have tried to remind you of some things. God treated me with undeserved grace!
text: Amaa y…õtt…Ē'aseesi ziiya ka n niŇči sufukpeemni n‚ÄĎs…Ēbi n‚ÄĎti ya na beela n‚ÄĎteaari ya. Di yi la n y…õlimaŇčni piini maa ni na, piiniseaa Naawunni Ňč…Ēn zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎti ma la.

ref: He chose me to be a servant of Christ Jesus for the Gentiles and to do the work of a priest in the service of his good news. God did this so that the Holy Spirit could make the Gentiles into a holy offering, pleasing to him.
text: U ti ma, ni, n niŇči Yisa Kirisita tuntumna ban pa Y…õhudiyadima maa saani. N tuma mun ny…õ la Naawunni lahabasuŇčŇču tuma, nti ky…õ ka ban pa Y…õhudiyadima la ti la'asi na ka Naawunni Sii Kasi ky…õ ka Naawunni deaai ba la kasikasi. Ka tuma wa Ňčmanni la Ňč…Ēn maanni sara tuma, hoo ban la'asi la puri ni antaam na, m‚ÄĎmaali n‚ÄĎti Naawunni seem la.

ref: Because of Christ Jesus, I can take pride in my service for God.
text: Dizugu Yisa Kirisita zugu ka m mari soori ni n nyu bara zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči Naawunni y…õla.

ref: In fact, all I will talk about is how Christ let me speak and work, so that the Gentiles would obey him.
text: Dama n ku ma'asi ni n t…Ē'asi seelli di yi pa la Kirisita Ňč…Ēn zaŇčŇči ma n‚ÄĎtum tunseesi la; ka laa n‚ÄĎy…õli y…õtt…Ē'aseesi la, ni di ky…õ ka ban pa Y…õhudiyadima maa ti sakki n‚ÄĎti Naawunni.

ref: Indeed, I will tell how Christ worked miracles and wonders by the power of the Holy Spirit. I have preached the good news about him all the way from Jerusalem to Illyricum.
text: U zaŇčŇči lala tunkara ni alahazibu tuma yiiko n‚ÄĎtum tuma wa n nuu ni, ni Sii Kasi yiiko. Ka di ny…õ la Gy…õrusal…õm zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎgyiligi wa'aligu sa, halli nti paai Illirikum tingbaŇčŇču ni, ka n mooli Naawunni lahabasuŇčŇču maa y…õla zaa.

ref: But I have always tried to preach where people have never heard about Christ. I am like a builder who doesn't build on someone else's foundation.
text: Ya bu nya? - M ba'a kur b…õ la timpaala ni, ni n ti mooli lahabasuŇčŇču maa luginseaa niriba ban na bu wum Kirisita yuuri la ni. Lala ka n ku meeri n‚ÄĎt…Ēmni s…Ē nyeebri zugu.

ref: It is just as the Scriptures say, ‚ÄúAll who haven't been told about him will see him, and those who haven't heard about him will understand.‚ÄĚ Paul's Plan To Visit Rome
text: Amaa di b…õ la hoo din pun s…Ēbi n‚ÄĎdooli seem la, ni#:- ‚ÄúBan na zi n‚ÄĎwum u lahabaari la, Ban nin nya ka baŇčŇči: Ban na zi n‚ÄĎwum u y…õla maa, Ban nin baŇčŇči di gbinni.‚ÄĚ

ref: My work has always kept me from coming to see you.
text: DinŇč…Ē zugu ka di yuui ka m boori soori ni n ti ky…õ ya saani na n‚ÄĎguui.

ref: Now there is nothing left for me to do in this part of the world, and for years I have wanted to visit you.
text: Amaa pampaŇčŇč…Ē n laa n‚ÄĎka tunseelli luga wa puuni, ka n yi boori ni n ky…õ ya saani na yuuma seem nŇč…Ēa,

ref: So I plan to stop off on my way to Spain. Then after a short, but refreshing, visit with you, I hope you will quickly send me on.
text: m mari tama, ni, n yi ya gaari n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči Sipeen tingbaŇčŇču ni, n nin nya ya. Ka n yi ti kaai ya n‚ÄĎniŇči daba ayi p…Ēi, ka ti la'am m‚ÄĎmaai taaba sufu, ya nin naayi toaai n‚ÄĎzaali ma soori ni ka n kyaŇčŇči n ky…õnni la.

ref: I am now on my way to Jerusalem to deliver the money that the Lord's followers in Macedonia and Achaia collected for God's needy people.
text: Dama Masidoonya dima ni Akaaya dima daa gbaai toosigu ni sufupeelli, ni, ba ti toosi n‚ÄĎsuŇčŇči fara dimseeba ban b…õ Naawunni niriba sunsukkuni, Gy…õrusal…õm timpuuni la.

ref: This is something they really wanted to do. But sharing their money with the Jews was also like paying back a debt, because the Jews had already shared their spiritual blessings with the Gentiles.
text: Ba daa gbaai lala ni sufupeelli, halli Gy…õrusal…õm dima samni n d…Ēli ba gba. Dama Gy…õrusal…õm dima daa zaŇčŇči ba Naawunni bunsuma n‚ÄĎt…Ēri n‚ÄĎti ban pa Y…õhudiyadima maa nti suŇčŇči ba siisi, ka lala zugu ky…õ ka di sumsi ni ban ti zaŇčŇči ba duniya wa bunsuma n‚ÄĎt…Ēri n‚ÄĎti Y…õhudiyadima wa n‚ÄĎsuŇčŇči ba ningbina.

ref: After I have safely delivered this money, I will visit you and then go on to Spain.
text: Dizugu n yi tum dinŇč…Ē maa, n‚ÄĎzaŇčŇči ban b…Ē seelli maa nti ti Gy…õrusal…õm dima maa n‚ÄĎnaai, n nin d…Ēli ya saani n‚ÄĎgaari n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči n Sipeen la.

ref: And when I do arrive in Rome, I know it will be with the full blessings of Christ.
text: M mi halli, ni, n yi ky…õ ya saani na, n ya mari Kirisita bunsuma zaa n‚ÄĎky…õ na.

ref: My friends, by the power of the Lord Jesus Christ and by the love that comes from the Holy Spirit, I beg you to pray sincerely with me and for me.
text: N susiri ya ni, m mabiisi, ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita zugu, ni Sii Kasi wurilim zugu gba, ni, ya suŇčŇči ma ka ti susi Naawunni nimmoosi man y…õla zugu.

ref: Pray that God will protect me from the unbelievers in Judea, and that his people in Jerusalem will be pleased with what I am doing.
text: Ya susima, ni, n nin yi Gyuudii dimseeba ban bu niŇči y…õdda la nuusi ni na, ka Gy…õrusal…õm puuni Naawunni niriba la nini nin tigi n suŇčŇčsim maa.

ref: Ask God to let me come to you and have a pleasant and refreshing visit.
text: Ka lala zugu Naawunni nin ti ma soori ni n ky…õ ya saani na ni sufupeelli, ka man ni yarima nin la'am taaba m‚ÄĎmaai taaba sufu.

ref: I pray that God, who gives peace, will be with all of you. Amen.
text: Naawunni siri su sufudoaa y…õla; Ňč…Ēn Naawunni b…õ ya zaa saani. Ammi.

ref: I have good things to say about Phoebe, who is a leader in the church at Cenchreae.
text: T…Ē, ka m boori ni ya ti deaai ti tunz…Ēp…Ē'a wa Ňč…Ēn yuuri boonni Fiibi. U ny…õ la s…Ē Ňč…Ēn tumni Naawunni tuma n‚ÄĎtiri u niriba ban la'amni Ky…õnkyiriiya tiŇčŇča puuni.

ref: Welcome her in a way that is proper for someone who has faith in the Lord and is one of God's own people. Help her in any way you can. After all, she has proved to be a respected leader for many others, including me.
text: Ya deaam u hoo din sumsi ni ya deaai ya mabii seem la, ti Dugma yuuri zugu. U yi ti boori seelli ya saani, ya suŇčŇčma u yan nin toaai seem zaa, dama Ňč…Ēn gba suŇčŇči niriba pam halli ni man gba.

ref: Give my greetings to Priscilla and Aquila. They have not only served Christ Jesus together with me,
text: Ya puusima Prisila ni Akwila n‚ÄĎti ma, dama ba n ny…õ n tuntuntaaba la, Kirisita Yisa saani.

ref: but they have even risked their lives for me. I am grateful for them and so are all the Gentile churches.
text: Ba daa zaŇčŇči ba ny…Ēvuya m‚ÄĎpa talima man zugu, ka di pa la man k…Ēk…Ē m p…õ'ari ba, see ninvugseeba ban pa Y…õhudiyadima ka la'amni ti Dugma yuuri zugu la gba - ban zaa paasi man zugu m‚ÄĎp…õ'ara.

ref: Greet the church that meets in their home. Greet my dear friend Epaenetus, who was the first person in Asia to have faith in Christ.
text: Ya puusima ban mun la'amni ban Akwila ni Prisila yiri maa puuni n‚ÄĎgyeemni Naawunni maa. Ka puusi n z…ĒsuŇčŇču ∆źpeen…õtusi; Ňč…Ēna n ny…õ ∆źseaa tingbaŇčŇču ni ninvugs…Ē Ňč…Ēn daa daŇčŇči ba zaa n‚ÄĎleebi Yisa nyaand…Ēla la.

ref: Greet Mary, who has worked so hard for you.
text: Ka puusi Mariama, dama u tum pam n‚ÄĎti ya.

ref: Greet my relatives Andronicus and Junia, who were in jail with me. They are highly respected by the apostles and were followers of Christ before I was.
text: Ni Andir…Ēnikusi ni Gyuniyasi: n d…Ē'ariba n ny…õ ba, ka man ni ban n daa la'am m‚ÄĎb…õ dansarika ni. Yisa tuumudima maa tiri ba gyirima, halli ni ba daa daŇčŇči ma n‚ÄĎleebi Yisa nyaand…Ēliba gba.

ref: Greet Ampliatus, my dear friend whose faith is in the Lord.
text: Ka laa m‚ÄĎpuusi Ampiliyatusi n‚ÄĎti ma, dama n wuri u hoo Naawunni biiya:

ref: Greet Urbanus, who serves Christ along with us. Greet my dear friend Stachys.
text: ni Yuribanusi ti tuntunkpeaa Kirisita Masiiya zugu, ni Siteekyusi n z…ĒsuŇčŇču.

ref: Greet Apelles, a faithful servant of Christ. Greet Aristobulus and his family.
text: Ap…õliisi gba n ny…õ gyirima daana Kirisita yuuri zugu, ya puusima u n‚ÄĎti ma; Ňč…Ēn ni Arisit…Ēbulusi yiri dima la zaa.

ref: Greet Herodion, who is a relative of mine. Greet Narcissus and the others in his family, who have faith in the Lord.
text: Ka puusi n d…Ē'ara H…õroodiy…Ēni n‚ÄĎti ma, ni Naasisusi yiri dima, ban ny…õ ti Dugma niriba la, m‚ÄĎpaasi.

ref: Greet Tryphaena and Tryphosa, who work hard for the Lord. Greet my dear friend Persis. She also works hard for the Lord.
text: Tirifeena ni Tirifoosa n ny…õ p…Ē'aseeba ban tum tunkara n‚ÄĎti ti Dugma, ya puusima ba n‚ÄĎti ma; ka puusi P…õrisisi man wuri s…Ē la - Ňč…Ēn gba tum tunkara n‚ÄĎti ti Dugma.

ref: Greet Rufus, that special servant of the Lord, and greet his mother, who has been like a mother to me.
text: Ka puusi Ruufusi dama ti Dugma pun yiisi u: ka laa m‚ÄĎpuusi u ma la, Ňč…Ēn ny…õ man gba ma.

ref: Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, and Hermas, as well as our friends who are with them.
text: Ka puusi Asinkyiratusi, ni Fil…õg…Ēni, ni H…õrimiisi, ni Patir…Ēbaasi, ni H…õrimaasi, ni ti mabiisi ban b…õ ba saani maa zaa.

ref: Greet Philologus, Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all of God's people who are with them.
text: Ka laa m‚ÄĎpuusi Fil…Ēl…Ēgusi, ni Gyuliya, ni Neeriyusi ni u tunz…Ēp…Ē'a la, ni ∆Ülimpaasi ni Yisa nyaand…Ēliba ban b…õ ba saani zaa.

ref: Be sure to give each other a warm greeting. All of Christ's churches greet you.
text: Ya puusima taaba suŇčŇčasuŇčŇča ti wurilim puusigu ni. Kirisita Masiiya nyaand…Ēliba la zaa puusiri ya na.

ref: My friends, I beg you to watch out for anyone who causes trouble and divides the church by refusing to do what all of you were taught. Stay away from them!
text: N kpaamni ya ni, ni, ya baŇčŇčma seeba ban wulisiri wulisiseesi an sa'amni yan pun bamsi wulisigu la; ba kur taasiri la nangbankpeemni na, ni ba ti lusi ya. Ya yim lala niriba maa y…õla ni.

ref: They want to serve themselves and not Christ the Lord. Their flattery and fancy talk fool people who don't know any better.
text: Dama lala niriba bu gyeemni ti Dugma Kirisita, amaa ba maŇčtoori k…Ērisi y…õla ka ba gyeemna. Ka ba zaŇčŇči ba y…õtt…Ē'anya'asa ni nangbammalisim la n‚ÄĎy…Ēmsiri ninvugseeba ban teesa bu zulima.

ref: I am glad that everyone knows how well you obey the Lord. But still, I want you to understand what is good and not have anything to do with evil.
text: Yarim muna - s…Ēkam mi, ni, ya deaari Naawunni noori, ka dizugu n sufu maasi ya y…õla zugu. M boori ni ya teesa ti zulima zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči bunsuma, amaa ka laa m‚ÄĎboori ni a ti k…ĒŇčŇči bumbeeri bamsim, halli fiiŇč.

ref: Then God, who gives peace, will soon crush Satan under your feet. I pray that our Lord Jesus will be kind to you.
text: Naawunni n su sufudoaa y…õla zaa, ka di ku yuui ka u nin ky…õ ka ya n…Ē n‚ÄĎt…Ēm Sintaani zugu n‚ÄĎŇčmirigi u. Ti Dugma Kirisita Masiiya wurilim kpalim m‚ÄĎb…õ ya ni.

ref: Timothy, who works with me, sends his greetings, and so do my relatives, Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater.
text: Tim…Ēti, n tuntunkpeaa la, puusiri ya suŇčŇča, ni n d…Ē'ariba m‚ÄĎpaasi - Luusiyusi, ni Gyees…Ēni ni Soosipata nla.

ref: Gaius welcomes me and the whole church into his home, and he sends his greetings. Erastus, the city treasurer, and our dear friend Quartus send their greetings too. Paul's Closing Prayer
text: Gayusi gba puusiri ya ni; n yirikp…õdaana n ny…õ u, ka laa n‚ÄĎky…õ ka Yisa niriba la'amni u yiri maa ni. ∆źrasitusi gba puusira - Ňč…Ēn su ti tiŇčŇča wa ligri y…õla fukumsi la - ni ti mabii Kuwaatusi m‚ÄĎpaasi.

ref: Praise God! He can make you strong by means of my good news, which is the message about Jesus Christ. For ages and ages this message was kept secret,
text: Ya ky…õ ka ti p…õ'ari Naawunni! Ňä…Ēna nin toaai n‚ÄĎky…õ ka ya nya yaa, ka di yi la man wulisiri lahabasuŇčŇču maa ni na, ni Yisa Kirisita y…õla mooligu ni na. Di ny…õ la din wulisi y…õlis…Ē'ara gbinni n‚ÄĎtiri ya la. Ba daa s…Ē'ai y…õla wa, piiligu ni halli ni zuna,

ref: but now at last it has been told. The eternal God commanded his prophets to write about the good news, so that all nations would obey and have faith.
text: amaa pampaŇčŇč…Ē ba kasigi a gbinni n‚ÄĎwulisi ti. Hoo anabidima Ňč…Ēn daa pun s…Ēbi n‚ÄĎdooli seem la, lala lahabaari maa ka Naawunni, saha din ka naari la Daana, y…õtti, ni, ba yiisima na n‚ÄĎwulisiri ya'ari kam, ni duniya zaa niriba ti deaai u noori n‚ÄĎniŇči Yisa y…õdda.

ref: And now, because of Jesus Christ, we can praise the only wise God forever! Amen.
text: Naawunni siri b…õ la u k…Ēk…Ē m‚ÄĎmari y…õm; gyirima zaa ny…õ la u dinni halli ni saha din ka t…Ēriga. Ti p…õ'ama u, Yisa Kirisita Masiiya zugu! Ammi.

ref: From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Christ Jesus, and from Sosthenes, who is also a follower.
text: Man P…Ēla n‚ÄĎs…Ēbri gbaŇčŇču wa n‚ÄĎti ya, man ni ti mabii S…Ēsit…õniisi. Man ny…õ s…Ē Naawunni Ňč…Ēn booli ma ni n ti leebi u tuumu, dama lala ka u sufu yuubu daa wulisi.

ref: My prayer is that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
