text: Ti Ba Naawunni, ni ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita, zaŇčŇčma u piinisuma ni alaaf…õ zaa n‚ÄĎtisi ya.

ref: I never stop thanking my God for treating you with undeserved grace by giving you Christ Jesus,
text: M puusiri Naawunni barika ya zugu sahakam, Ňč…Ēn daa zaŇčŇči u piinisuma n‚ÄĎti ya la zugu, ka di ny…õ la Kirisita Yisa saani.

ref: Now you are certain that everything we told you about our Lord Christ Jesus is true.
text: Lala zugu ka ya beebu kur wulisiri, ni, lahabasuŇčŇču zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči Kirisita y…õla maa ny…õ la y…õlimaŇčni deede.

ref: You are not missing out on any blessings, as you wait for him to return.
text: Dinzugu ya bu k…ĒŇčŇči Naawunni piinisuma, halli yinni gba, ka kpalim n‚ÄĎguusiri ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Masiiya yiibu na saha.

ref: And until the day Christ does return, he will keep you completely innocent.
text: U siri nin mari ya suŇčŇča halli nti paai di t…Ēriga, ka ya nin z…õ yummu ti Dugma Yisa dabsiri daari maa ni, galinseelli ka ni.

ref: God can be trusted, and he chose you to be partners with his Son, our Lord Jesus Christ. Taking Sides
text: Naawunni ny…õ la y…õdda daana, ka Ňč…Ēna zugu ka ba daa booli ya, ni ya yi na n‚ÄĎla'amni m‚ÄĎb…õ u Bii Yisa Kirisita ti Dugma saani.

ref: My dear friends, as a follower of our Lord Jesus Christ, I beg you to get along with each other. Don't take sides. Always try to agree in what you think.
text: T…Ē: ka n susiri ya, m mabiisi, ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita zugu, ni, ya zaa mari nangbanyinni ni taaba; yan di Ňčmeeri nangbankpeemni n‚ÄĎw…Ēligri ka ky…õri taaba. See ka ya kpamsiri taaba ka mari sufu yinni ni teesiyinni.

ref: Several people from Chloe's family have already reported to me that you keep arguing with each other.
text: M mabiisi wumma! N wumni ya y…õla Kloowi yiri dima maa saani, ni, zaba m b…õ ya saani.

ref: Has Christ been divided up? Was I nailed to a cross for you? Were you baptized in my name?
text: Ammi, ba purigi Kirisita zii bunaasi? Ba daa kpaasi P…Ēla n‚ÄĎtabli d…Ēpulli zugu ni ya ti nya tuliginsim, bee? - Ay! pa lala. Bee, ba daa su ya P…Ēla koom? - Ay, halli fiiŇč gba!

ref: I thank God that I didn't baptize any of you except Crispus and Gaius.
text: Di niŇči ma nya'asim Naawunni zugu, ni, n daa bu su ya ninvugs…Ē koom, na'ala Krispusi ni Gayusi k…Ēk…Ēa.

ref: Not one of you can say that you were baptized in my name.
text: Dama lala zugu ya ku toaai n‚ÄĎy…õli, ni, n daa su ya koom m mammantoori yuuri zugu.

ref: Christ did not send me to baptize. He sent me to tell the good news without using words that sound wise and would make the cross of Christ lose its power. Christ Is God's Power and Wisdom
text: Ya bu nya? - di daa pa la koom suubu y…õla zugu ka Kirisita daa tum ma ya saani na, amaa lahabasuŇčŇču mooligu zugu; ka m bu zaŇčŇči ninsaala y…õm m‚ÄĎmoonna, dama lala ky…õ ka Kirisita d…Ēpulli zugu kum y…õla ti basi y…Ēri.

ref: The message about the cross doesn't make any sense to lost people. But for those of us who are being saved, it is God's power at work.
text: Dama d…Ēpulli maa lahabaari muna - ninvugseeba ban b…õ baasiy…Ēri soori ni maa yi ti wum di, ba y…õtti, ni, gy…õrilim; amaa tirima mun ban b…õ tuliginsim soori maa ni la saani ka di mari la Naawunni yiiko.

ref: As God says in the Scriptures, ‚ÄúI will destroy the wisdom of all who claim to be wise. I will confuse those who think they know so much.‚ÄĚ
text: Dama di s…Ēbi n‚ÄĎdooli, ni#:- ‚ÄúN nin sa'am y…õm dima bamsim maa; N nin gbarigi ba miilinsi.‚ÄĚ

ref: What happened to those wise people? What happened to those experts in the Scriptures? What happened to the ones who think they have all the answers? Didn't God show that the wisdom of this world is foolish?
text: T…Ē, ka y…õm daana la lee? Bamsim daana la lee? Ňä…Ēn viisiri m‚ÄĎbaŇčŇčni pampaŇčŇč…Ē duniya beesigu y…õla la b…õ la yani? Naawunni n zaŇčŇči duniya wa y…õm n‚ÄĎleebi gy…õrilim, bee, m ma'ari ni?

ref: God was wise and decided not to let the people of this world use their wisdom to learn about him. Instead, God chose to save only those who believe the foolish message we preach.
text: Naawunni mammaŇčŇča y…õm zugu daa ky…õ ka duniya wa dima daa zaŇčŇči ba toori y…õm ni ba ti baŇčŇči u y…õla n‚ÄĎk…ĒŇčŇči. Ka u sufu yuubu zugu ka baseeba ban wum lahabaari mooligu din Ňčmani hoo gy…õrilim la n‚ÄĎniŇči y…õdda la muna - ban nyari fa-m‚ÄĎbasi.

ref: Jews ask for miracles, and Greeks want something that sounds wise.
text: T…Ē: Y…õhudiya dima maa boori la alahazibu tuma nyaabu, ka Griika dima maa mun boori y…õm y…õla wumpu;

ref: But we preach that Christ was nailed to a cross. Most Jews have problems with this, and most Gentiles think it is foolish.
text: amaa tirima Ňč…Ēn moonni lahabaseaa maa ny…õ la Yisa Kirisita Masiiya y…õla: ni, ba daa kpaasi u n‚ÄĎtabli d…Ēpulli zugu. Ka Y…õhudiya dima maa y…õtti, ni, din kyisiri nla; ka Griika dima maa mun y…õtti, ni, gy…õrilim n ny…õ di.

ref: Our message is God's power and wisdom for the Jews and the Greeks that he has chosen.
text: Amaa Naawunni Ňč…Ēn booli seeba la Ňč…Ēna, u siri ny…õ la Masiiya n‚ÄĎti ba, ka laa n‚ÄĎny…õ Naawunni y…õm ni u yiiko gba - halli ba yi ny…õ Y…õhudiya dima, ni Griika dima zaa.

ref: Even when God is foolish, he is wiser than everyone else, and even when God is weak, he is stronger than everyone else.
text: Dama Naawunni gy…õrilim mari y…õm n‚ÄĎgaari saalidima y…õm, ka Naawunni bugsim mari yaa n‚ÄĎgaari saalidima yaa.

ref: My dear friends, remember what you were when God chose you. The people of this world didn't think that many of you were wise. Only a few of you were in places of power, and not many of you came from important families.
text: M mabiisi, ya teesima yan daa b…õ seem ka ba daa booli ya. Ya sunsukkuni ya ka niriba pam ban mari duniya wa y…õm; ya ka niriba pam ban mari yiiko, bee, n‚ÄĎyi naam zuliya ni.

ref: But God chose the foolish things of this world to put the wise to shame. He chose the weak things of this world to put the powerful to shame.
text: Amaa Naawunni yiisi la bunseesi an mari gy…õrilim duniya wa dima saani, ka mari a n‚ÄĎdiisiri y…õm dima maa vi: Naawunni yiisi la an ny…õ bumbugsa duniya wa dima saani, ka mari a n‚ÄĎdiisiri yaa dima maa vi.

ref: What the world thinks is worthless, useless, and nothing at all is what God has used to destroy what the world considers important.
text: U yiisi la tarimma, ni niriba Ňč…Ēn zeemni bunseesi, halli bunseesi an ka ni gba, ka mari a n‚ÄĎky…õ ka bunseesi an b…õ ni maa ti basi y…Ēri.

ref: God did all this to keep anyone from bragging to him.
text: Ka lala ky…õ ka s…Ē ku toaai n‚ÄĎnyu bara Ňč…Ēn Naawunni saani.

ref: You are God's children. He sent Christ Jesus to save us and to make us wise, acceptable, and holy.
text: Ňä…Ēn Naawunni zugu ka ya b…õ Yisa Kirisita ni; Ňč…Ēna ka Naawunni ky…õ ka u leebi y…õm n‚ÄĎti ti, ni y…õlimaŇčni, ka u laa n‚ÄĎky…õ ka ti leebi Naawunni mammaŇčŇča niriba kasi kasi, ka laa n‚ÄĎnya fa-m‚ÄĎbasi.

ref: So if you want to brag, do what the Scriptures say and brag about the Lord.
text: Dizugu - hoo din pun s…Ēbi n‚ÄĎdooli - ‚ÄúŇä…Ēn ya n‚ÄĎnyu bara zaa, ky…õ ka u nyuuri a ni Naawunni yuuri zugu k…Ēk…Ēa.‚ÄĚ

ref: Friends, when I came and told you the mystery that God had shared with us, I didn't use big words or try to sound wise.
text: T…Ē: m mabiisi, man daa ky…õ ya saani na maa, ni n ti t…Ē'asiri Naawunni y…õligu n‚ÄĎtiri ya, ni ya ti baŇčŇči Naawunni y…õlis…Ē'ara - lala saha n daa bu ky…õ na ni n ti zaŇčŇči y…õlinya'asa an mari bamsim n‚ÄĎt…Ē'asira.

ref: In fact, while I was with you, I made up my mind to speak only about Jesus Christ, who had been nailed to a cross.
text: Ay! - n daa y…õli, ni, man Ňč…Ēn b…õ ya saani la m bu ya sakki m‚ÄĎbaŇčŇči seelli na'ala Yisa Kirisita Masiiya y…õla k…Ēk…Ē ka n nin baŇčŇči - halli ban daa kpaasi u n‚ÄĎtabli d…Ēpulli zugu gba.

ref: When I talked with you or preached, I didn't try to prove anything by sounding wise. I simply let God's Spirit show his power.
text: N y…õligu maa, ni man daa mooli lahabaari la gba, daa pa la y…õm y…õtt…Ē'anya'asa, amaa a daa wulisiri la Naawunni Sii maa yiiko y…õla,

ref: That way you would have faith because of God's power and not because of human wisdom.
text: ni lala ti ky…õ ka ya y…õdda niŇčbu ku b…õ saalidima y…õm zugu, amaa Naawunni yiiko zugu.

ref: We do use wisdom when speaking to people who are mature in their faith. But it isn't the wisdom of this world or of its rulers, who will soon disappear.
text: Y…õm y…õla ka ti siri yi t…Ē'asiri n‚ÄĎtiri ban gbaai kpamni la saani, amaa di pa la pampaŇčŇč…Ē saha y…õm maa, pa din yi ban su pampaŇčŇč…Ē duniya fukumsi la saani na - ban kur naari y…Ēri.

ref: We speak of God's hidden and mysterious wisdom that God decided to use for our glory long before the world began.
text: Ay! Naawunni y…õm ka ti t…Ē'asira - buns…Ē'arigu din daa bu yi paalu na. Lala y…õm ka Naawunni daa zaali p…Ēi ka naayi naam pampaŇčŇč…Ē duniya wa; ka niŇči lala ni u ti ky…õ ka tirima nin nya gyirima.

ref: The rulers of this world didn't know anything about this wisdom. If they had known about it, they would not have nailed the glorious Lord to a cross.
text: Y…õm wa muna - ban su pampaŇčŇč…Ē duniya fukumsi la s…Ēs…Ē daa bu baŇčŇči di gbinni; halli ba yi daa baŇčŇči, ba naa n‚ÄĎku kpaasi ti Dugma gyirima daana n‚ÄĎtabli d…Ēpulli zugu.

ref: But it is just as the Scriptures say, ‚ÄúWhat God has planned for people who love him is more than eyes have seen or ears have heard. It has never even entered our minds!‚ÄĚ
text: Ba siri daa bu baŇčŇči, hoo din pun s…Ēbi n‚ÄĎdooli, ni#:- ‚ÄúBunseesi s…Ē nimbiri Ňč…Ēn na bu nya, Bunseesi s…Ē tubri Ňč…Ēn na bu wum, Bunseesi ninsaalis…Ē sufu Ňč…Ēn na bu teesi a y…õla - AnŇč…Ē ka Naawunni pun maali siri, U nin zaŇčŇči a n‚ÄĎti ban wuri u maa.‚ÄĚ

ref: God's Spirit has shown you everything. His Spirit finds out everything, even what is deep in the mind of God.
text: Tirim muna - Ňč…Ēn Naawunni zaŇčŇči anŇč…Ē taabu n‚ÄĎwulisi ti; u Sii maa zugu ka u wulisiri a ni, dama Sii maa viisiri la seelikam, halli Naawunni y…õlizulima gba.

ref: You are the only one who knows what is in your own mind, and God's Spirit is the only one who knows what is in God's mind.
text: Ka saalidima saani, Ňč…Ēni m mi u saalitaraana y…õla deede? - Ay, s…Ēs…Ē bu mi, na'ala di daama maŇčtoori sufu ni sii maa m mi u y…õla. Lala ka s…Ē na zi Naawunni y…õla deede, na'ala Naawunni Siiya.

ref: But God has given us his Spirit. This is why we don't think the same way that the people of this world think. This is also why we can recognize the blessings God has given us.
text: Pa duniya wa siiya ka ti daa deaai; amaa Sis…Ē Ňč…Ēn yi Naawunni saani na. Ka lala n ky…õ ka ti baŇčŇči Naawunni Ňč…Ēn ti ti piiniseesi la zaa.

ref: Every word we speak was taught to us by God's Spirit, not by human wisdom. And this same Spirit helps us teach spiritual things to spiritual people.
text: T…Ē: lala ka ti wulisira. Di bu yi la duniya wa y…õm wulisigu ni na, amaa hoo Sii maa Ňč…Ēn wulisiri ti seem; ka ti mari n‚ÄĎsuŇčŇčni Sii maa niriba, ni ba ti baŇčŇči u y…õla.

ref: This is why only someone who has God's Spirit can understand spiritual blessings. Anyone who doesn't have God's Spirit thinks these blessings are foolish.
text: Ninvugseeba ban ka Sii maa bu deaari y…õliseesi an yiiri Naawunni Sii maa ni na, dama a ny…õ la gy…õrilim ba saani. Ba bu baŇčŇči a gbinni, dama s…Ē ku toaai m‚ÄĎbaŇčŇči, di yi pa la u d…Ēli Sii maa soori n‚ÄĎviisiri a.

ref: People who are guided by the Spirit can make all kinds of judgments, but they cannot be judged by others.
text: Nira Ňč…Ēn mari Sii maa viisiri la seelikam, amaa s…Ē mun bu viisiri Ňč…Ēna.

ref: The Scriptures ask, ‚ÄúHas anyone ever known the thoughts of the Lord or given him advice?‚ÄĚ But we understand what Christ is thinking.
text: Ni#:- ‚ÄúDama ti Dugma sufu ni teesa muna - Ňä…Ēni n na mi m‚ÄĎbaŇčŇči a? Ňä…Ēni mun Ňčmeeri u saawara?‚ÄĚ Tirima mun mari la Kirisita Masiiya sufu ni teesa.

ref: My friends, you are acting like the people of this world. That's why I could not speak to you as spiritual people. You are like babies as far as your faith in Christ is concerned.
text: M mabiisi, n ku toaai n‚ÄĎt…Ē'asi n‚ÄĎti ya hoo ban mi Sii maa y…õla la, amaa hoo ban mi ninsaala y…õla la k…Ēk…Ēa, dama ya na ny…õ la bileesi zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči Kirisita y…õla.

ref: So I had to treat you like babies and feed you milk. You could not take solid food, and you still cannot,
text: Dizugu n daa m…Ē'asiri ya la bisim, pa dikpeema; dama ya daa bu sa'ai ditibsa diibu. Halli ni zuna ya na ku toaai n‚ÄĎdeaai ninvugkura diibu,

ref: because you are not yet spiritual. You are jealous and argue with each other. This proves you are not spiritual and you are acting like the people of this world.
text: dama ya na zi Sii maa y…õla. Nyuuri zappu ni suuri yisigri Ňč…Ēn na b…õ ya saani, pa duniya wa saalidima y…õla ka ya d…Ēla?

ref: Some of you say you follow me, and others claim to follow Apollos. Isn't this how ordinary people behave?
text: Ya bu nya? - s…Ē y…õtti, ni, P…Ēla ka u d…Ēla; ka s…Ē mun y…õtti, ni, Ap…Ēloosi ka u boora: pa duniya wa ninsaalidima y…õtt…Ē'a nŇč…Ēa?

ref: Apollos and I are merely servants who helped you to have faith. It was the Lord who made it all happen.
text: Ap…Ēloosi ny…õ la Ňč…Ēni? Bee, P…Ēla ny…õ la Ňč…Ēni? Ti zaa ny…õ la Naawunni tuntumniba, ka di ky…õ ka yarima niŇčni y…õdda dima, amaa bunseelikam yi la Naawunni saani na n‚ÄĎti ti.

ref: I planted the seeds, Apollos watered them, but God made them sprout and grow.
text: ZaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči ya y…õdda y…õla, mani n daa buri bumbura la, ka Ap…Ēloosi mun daa y…Ē'ai bumbula la koom, amaa Naawunni yiiko zugu ka a zoaai na n‚ÄĎniŇčna.

ref: The one who plants is just as important as the one who waters. And each one will be paid for what they do.
text: Ňä…Ēn butti ni Ňč…Ēn y…Ē'ari koom la zaa tumni la tumni yinni, ka ti Dugma nin y…Ē ba zaa hoo ban tumni seem la.

ref: Apollos and I work together for God, and you are God's garden and God's building. Only One Foundation
text: Tirima zaa la'am n‚ÄĎtumni Naawunni tuma: yan mun ny…õ la Naawunni puu ka ti koora, ka laa n‚ÄĎny…õ u yiri ka ti meera.

ref: God treated me with undeserved grace and let me become an expert builder. I laid a foundation on which others have built. But we must each be careful how we build,
text: Mani n ny…õ tammeera ni y…õlimaŇčni, hoo man deaai piiniseesi Naawunni saani la, ka n daa nyeebi nyeebri maa; ka s…Ē mun deaai m‚ÄĎmeeri n‚ÄĎt…Ēmna: t…Ē! see ka s…Ēkam mari u maŇčŇča ka baŇčŇči Ňč…Ēn tumni u meebu tuma seem.

ref: Whatever we build on this foundation will be tested by fire on the day of judgment. Then everyone will find out if we have used gold, silver, and precious stones, or wood, hay, and straw.
text: Amaa sariya karibu daari yi paai na, di nin yiisi s…Ēkam tuma paalu ni na, n‚ÄĎwulisi an b…õ seem, dama bugum n ya zamsi s…Ēkam zaa tuma.

ref: We will be rewarded if our building is left standing.
text: T…Ē; ka bugum yi bu ti nyaŇčŇči s…Ē tuma, di daama nin deaai u laara sanyoaa.

ref: But if it is destroyed by the fire, we will lose everything. Yet we ourselves will be saved, like someone escaping from flames.
text: Amaa bugum yi di s…Ē tuma zaa, di daama nin k…ĒŇčŇči marigu; u mammaŇčŇča ku baasi y…Ēri, amaa u nin Ňčmanni la hoo Ňč…Ēn kpeeri bugum ni n‚ÄĎgatta.

ref: All of you surely know you are God's temple and his Spirit lives in you.
text: Ya bu baŇčŇči, ni, yarima n ny…õ Naawunni GyeeŇčŇču Duu, bee? Halli Naawunni Siiya b…õ ya ni gba.

ref: Together you are God's holy temple, and God will destroy anyone who destroys his temple.
text: Ninvugs…Ē yi sa'am Naawunni gyeeŇčŇču ziiya, Naawunni gba nin sa'am di daama. Naawunni GyeeŇčŇču Duu ny…õ la u dinni, kan ny…õ bunkasikasi: ka yarim n ny…õ u GyeeŇčŇču Duu wa.

ref: Don't fool yourselves! If any of you think you are wise in the things of this world, you will have to become foolish before you can be truly wise.
text: T…Ē: yan di ky…õ ka ya s…Ē y…Ēmsi u maŇčŇča; ninvugs…Ē yi b…õ ya saani ka teesi, ni, u ny…õ la y…õm daana duniya wa saani, ky…õ ka di daama leebi gy…õrigu; ka lala zugu nin ky…õ ka u leebi y…õm daana ni y…õlimaŇčni.

ref: This is because God considers the wisdom of this world to be foolish. It is just as the Scriptures say, ‚ÄúGod catches the wise when they try to outsmart him.‚ÄĚ
text: Dama duniya wa y…õm ny…õ la gy…õrilim Naawunni saani. Lala zugu ka di pun s…Ēbi n‚ÄĎdooli, ni, ‚ÄúY…õm daandima maa muna - Naawunni zaŇčŇči ba toori y…õmg…ĒliŇčŇča m‚ÄĎb…õtti n‚ÄĎgbaari ba.‚ÄĚ

ref: The Scriptures also say, ‚ÄúThe Lord knows that the plans made by wise people are useless.‚ÄĚ
text: Ka di laa n‚ÄĎs…Ēbi, ni, ‚ÄúTi Dugma m mi y…õm dima teesa zaa, ka a ny…õ la y…Ēri.‚ÄĚ

ref: So stop bragging about what anyone has done. Paul and Apollos and Peter all belong to you. In fact, everything is yours, including the world, life, death, the present, and the future. Everything belongs to you,
text: Ii! - P…Ēla ny…õ la ya dinni, ni Ap…Ēloosi, ni Siifasi Piita; yarima n su beesigu ni kum fukumsi; din b…õ ni pampaŇčŇč…Ē ni din nin niŇči zaa - anŇč…Ē zaa ny…õ la ya dinni.

ref: and you belong to Christ, and Christ belongs to God.
text: Ka ya mun ny…õ la Kirisita Masiiya Ňč…Ēn su ninvugseeba, ka Ňč…Ēn Kirisita ny…õ la Naawunni dinni.

ref: Think of us as servants of Christ who have been given the work of explaining God's mysterious ways.
text: T…Ē, ka dinŇč…Ēna see ka ba nyari ti, ni, Kirisita tuntumniba, ni seeba Naawunni Ňč…Ēn zaŇčŇči u y…õlis…Ē'ara fukumsi n‚ÄĎniŇči ba nuu ni.

ref: And since our first duty is to be faithful to the one we work for,
text: Din kpalim ka ba boori tuntumniba saani ny…õ la, ni, ba z…õ yummu n‚ÄĎtumna.

ref: it doesn't matter to me if I am judged by you or even by a court of law. In fact, I don't judge myself.
text: Man mun ba'a ka ni, ni, yarima yi katti n sariya, bee ninsaalidima kootu s…õseelli yi katta; halli m mammaŇčŇča gba - man bu katti n y…õla sariya.

ref: I don't know of anything against me, but this doesn't prove I am right. The Lord is my judge.
text: M bu nya teesibeeri n sufu ni, amaa lala ku toaai n‚ÄĎky…õ ka n niŇči y…õlimaŇčni daana; ti Dugma k…Ēk…Ē nin ti kari ma sariya.

ref: So don't judge anyone until the Lord returns. He will show what is hidden in the dark and what is in everyone's heart. Then God will be the one who praises each of us.
text: Lala zugu yan di daŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkari sariya ka saha maa na bu paai na; halli ni ti Dugma ky…õnni na. Ňä…Ēna nin ky…õ ka din s…Ē'ai zibsim ni zaa nin ti yi paalu na, ka wulisi saalidima sufu ni teesa an b…õ seem. Di saha ka s…Ēkam zaa nin ti nya p…õ'abu din sumsi u, Naawunni saani.

ref: Friends, I have used Apollos and myself as examples to teach you the meaning of the saying, ‚ÄúFollow the rules.‚ÄĚ I want you to stop saying one of us is better than the other.
text: M mabiisi, yarima zugu ka n zaŇčŇči man ni Ap…Ēloosi y…õla wa n‚ÄĎwulisiri ya, ni ya baŇčŇči y…õligu wa gbinni Ňč…Ēn ny…õ seem, ni#:- ‚ÄúSee ka ya ti d…Ēli din s…Ēbi n‚ÄĎdooli seem, ka ku yakki Naawunni Ňč…Ēn zaali seem.‚ÄĚ Ni, yan di gyeemni s…Ē ka laa n‚ÄĎzeemni u taraana.

ref: What is so special about you? What do you have that you were not given? And if it was given to you, how can you brag?
text: B…Ē n wulisi, ni, ya w…Ēligiya ni ka ky…õ ya taaba? B…Ē ka ya mara, di yi pa la ya daa deaai ka la piini? Ka ya yi deaai piini maa, b…Ē n ky…õ ka ya nyuuri bara, hoo yan bu deaai di zaa?

ref: Are you already satisfied? Are you now rich? Have you become kings while we are still nobodies? I wish you were kings. Then we could have a share in your kingdom.
text: Ni, bundaandima n ny…õ ya, fa! Ni, ya tigiya ka bu k…ĒŇčŇči seela. Ni, ya pun di naam ka ky…õ ti. T…Ē: ya yi siri di naam, di veela! - dama ti gba naa n‚ÄĎla'am n‚ÄĎdi naam maa.

ref: It seems to me that God has put us apostles in the worst possible place. We are like prisoners on their way to death. Angels and the people of this world just laugh at us.
text: N teesi, ni, Naawunni zaŇčŇči tirima, u tuumudima maa, ka labsi ti y…õla nyaaŇčŇča. Ti Ňčmanni la hoo ninvugseeba ban kari ti sariya n‚ÄĎlusi ti, ni, ti sum la kum; ka ti diri lala wahala ni s…Ēkam ti g…Ēsira - halli malaakadima ni ninsaalidima zaa gba.

ref: Because of Christ we are thought of as fools, but Christ has made you wise. We are weak and hated, but you are powerful and respected.
text: Tirima siri ny…õ la gy…õra, Kirisita zugu; ka yarima mun wulisiri ninneesim Kirisita saani, fa! Bumbugsa n ny…õ ti, ka ya mun mari kpeeŇčŇču. Ya nyari gyirima, ka ti mun nyari fiiliŇčŇču.

ref: Even today we go hungry and thirsty and don't have anything to wear except rags. We are mistreated and don't have a place to live.
text: Halli ni zuna ti kpiiri la k…Ēm ni k…Ēnyuuri, n‚ÄĎka bunyeera, ka laa n‚ÄĎnyari mugsigu ti gyiliŇčŇču la puuni.

ref: We work hard with our own hands, and when people abuse us, we wish them well. When we suffer, we are patient.
text: Ti namsiri n‚ÄĎtumni tuma ni ti nuusi. Ka niriba tuuri ti, ka ti mun kaari kasuma n‚ÄĎtiri ba; ba namsiri ti ni, ka ti mun bu za'asiri ba yuubu.

ref: When someone curses us, we answer with kind words. Until now we are thought of as nothing more than the trash and garbage of this world.
text: Ba t…Ē'asiri y…õtt…Ē'abeeri n‚ÄĎniŇčni ti, amaa ti kpaŇčŇči ti maŇčŇča n‚ÄĎsakki n‚ÄĎti ba baalimm. Ti Ňčmanni la duniya zaa sa'ari, hoo ninsaalidima maa sa'atampuuri, halli ni zuna.

ref: I am not writing to embarrass you. I want to help you, just as parents help their own dear children.
text: M bu s…Ēbri wa ni n ti diisi ya la vi, amaa n kpaamni ya ni hoo m biwutta.

ref: Ten thousand people may teach you about Christ, but I am your only father. You became my children when I told you about Christ Jesus,
text: Dama halli ya yi mari ya karimbadima tusa tusa zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkpa Kirisita ziiya gba, ya ka badima pam, see yinni k…Ēk…Ēa. Y…õlimaŇčni, mani n ny…õ s…Ē Ňč…Ēn daa d…Ē'ai ya zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkpa lahabasuŇčŇču beesigu ziiya ka di ny…õ la Kirisita Yisa saani.

ref: and I want you to be like me.
text: Lala zugu ka n susiri ya, ni, ya d…Ēli n beesigu soori.

ref: This is why I sent Timothy to you. I love him like a son, and he is a faithful servant of the Lord. Timothy will tell you what I do to follow Christ and how it agrees with what I always teach about Christ in every church.
text: DinŇč…Ē zugu ka n tum Tim…Ēti ya saani na; u ny…õ la m biwuttigu ni y…õlimaŇčni, ka laa n‚ÄĎny…õ s…Ē Ňč…Ēn z…õ yummu ti Dugma y…õla zugu. Ňä…Ēna nin toaai n‚ÄĎteaari ya man Kirisita beesigu s…Ēya y…õla, hoo man Ňč…Ēn wulisi seem lugri kam man la'amni ni Naawunni niriba zaa.

ref: Some of you think I am not coming for a visit, and so you are bragging.
text: Man na bu ky…õ ya saani na zugu ka ya seeba mari karimbaani.

ref: But if the Lord lets me come, I will soon be there. Then I will find out if the ones who are doing all this bragging really have any power.
text: Amaa di siri ku yuui ka n nin kaai ya na, Naawunni yi ti ti ma lala soori. Di saha n nin baŇčŇči karimbaani dima maa gbinni, pa ba y…õtt…Ē'a y…õla k…Ēk…Ēa, amaa ni ba yiiko Ňč…Ēn b…õ seem.

ref: God's kingdom isn't just a lot of words. It is power.
text: Dama Naawunni naam pa la y…õtt…Ē'a k…Ēk…Ēa, amaa yiiko ni gba.

ref: What do you want me to do when I arrive? Do you want me to be hard on you or to be kind and gentle?
text: B…Ē ka ya boora? - N ky…õm na nti darigi ya tuba? - bee, n zaŇčŇči Sii maa wurilim ni nirilim n‚ÄĎky…õ ya saani na?

ref: I have heard terrible things about some of you. In fact, you are behaving worse than Gentiles. A man is even sleeping with his own stepmother.
text: Lahabaari siri yi na, ni, zina m b…õ ya saani - ka ka taabu ka ni, halli ban bu d…Ēli Naawunni soori la saani gba: ni, d…Ēs…Ē n di u ba p…Ē'a.

ref: You are proud, when you ought to feel bad enough to chase away anyone who acts like this.
text: Ka ya mun nyuuri la bara, fa! Di da bu sum ni ya sufu sa'am di zugu? - ka ya yi da zaŇčŇči Ňč…Ēn tumni lala maa n‚ÄĎyiisi ya la'aŇčŇču ni, di da s…Ēa.

ref: I am with you only in my thoughts. But in the name of our Lord Jesus I have already judged this man, as though I were with you in person. So when you meet together and the power of the Lord Jesus is with you, I will be there too.
text: Ya yi ti la'am taaba ti Dugma Yisa yuuri zugu, ka n gba sii b…õ la ya saani, ti Dugma Yisa yiiko kur b…õ la b…õni.

ref: You must then hand that man over to Satan. His body will be destroyed, but his spirit will be saved when the Lord Jesus returns.
text: Di saha ya zaŇčŇčma lala doo maa n‚ÄĎti Sintaani ka u sa'am u ningbina, ka di yi pa seem lala nin ky…õ ka u sii maa nin nya tuliginsim, ti Dugma dabsiri daari.

ref: Stop being proud! Don't you know how a little yeast can spread through the whole batch of dough?
text: Ay! ya bara nyuubu wa bu veela! Ya bu mi paanu dabina y…õla - ni, ba zaŇčŇči a beela n‚ÄĎniŇči boroboro zum puuni, ka a nin nyaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎtum m‚ÄĎpitti paanu n‚ÄĎfuusiri a zaa gba.

ref: Get rid of the old yeast! Then you will be like fresh bread made without yeast, and this is what you are. Our Passover lamb is Christ, who has already been sacrificed.
text: T…Ē, ka dinŇč…Ēna ya yiisim dabinkura la m‚ÄĎbasi, ka ya leebi boroboropaalli, an ka dabina. Halli, ya siri pun b…õ la lala, dama ba pun k…Ērigi Kirisita, hoo ti Yakki-n‚ÄĎGaari p…õbila maaligu la - pa paanu kan ka dabina n ny…õ Yakki-n‚ÄĎGaari dinni?

ref: So don't celebrate the festival by being evil and sinful, which is like serving bread made with yeast. Be pure and truthful and celebrate by using bread made without yeast.
text: Dizugu ya ky…õ ka ti maali Kyuu wa suŇčŇča: ti di mari dabinkura paanu an kyisira, bee, tumbeeri ni putoo buna; amaa bumpaala, an ka dabina, an ny…õ la y…õdda ni y…õlimaŇčni dina.

ref: In my other letter I told you not to have anything to do with immoral people.
text: N daa pun s…Ēbi n‚ÄĎti ya n gbaŇčŇču la ni, ni, yan di la'am m‚ÄĎb…õ ni ni zina dima.

ref: But I wasn't talking about the people of this world. You would have to leave this world to get away from everyone who is immoral or greedy or who cheats or worships idols.
text: T…Ē - pa la duniya wa niriba ban tumni zina la y…õla ka n daa t…Ē'asi, bee, beem dima, bee, ban y…Ēmsiri n‚ÄĎfaari niriba, halli baseeba ban gyeemni buga la gba: di yi da ny…õ la lala, ya naa n‚ÄĎyi la duniya wa ni zaa.

ref: God judges everyone else. The Scriptures say, ‚ÄúChase away any of your own people who are evil.‚ÄĚ
text: Seeba ban pa ti niriba muna - Naawunni nin kari ba sariya. Ni, ‚ÄúYa karima putoo daana n‚ÄĎyiisi ya puuni.‚ÄĚ

ref: When one of you has a complaint against another, do you take your complaint to a court of sinners? Or do you take it to God's people?
text: Ya s…Ē yi mari y…õtt…Ē'a ni u taraana, ka u zaŇčŇči lala y…õtt…Ē'a maa n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči ban bu gyeemni Naawunni la saani ka ky…õ Naawunni niriba maa - lala mari soori? - Ay! lala ka soori.

ref: Don't you know that God's people will judge the world? And if you are going to judge the world, can't you settle small problems?
text: Ammi, ya bu mi, ni, Naawunni niriba maa nin ti kari duniya wa zaa sariya? Ka ya yi ya kari duniya wa sariya, ya ku toaai n‚ÄĎkari sarabisi?

ref: Don't you know we will judge angels? And if this is so, we can surely judge everyday matters.
text: Bee, ya bu mi, ni, ti nin kari halli malaakadima sariya gba? Ka ku toaai n‚ÄĎkari saalidima maa y…õla, fa?

ref: Why do you take everyday complaints to judges who are not respected by the church?
text: Amaa yarima yi mari y…õtt…Ē'a anŇč…Ē taabu, ya ky…õnni seeba saani ni ba kari n‚ÄĎti ya, ka ba pa la ninvugseeba Naawunni niriba Ňč…Ēn tiri gyirima.

ref: I say this to your shame. Aren't any of you wise enough to act as a judge between one follower and another?
text: N y…õtti dinŇč…Ē la, ni n ti diisi ya vi. Ammi, s…Ē ka ya saani Ňč…Ēn mari y…õm n nin toaai m‚ÄĎmaali ya y…õla maa? - halli ninvugu yinni gba?

ref: Why should one of you take another to be tried by unbelievers?
text: Ka ya ziiri taaba n‚ÄĎky…õnni kootu ni, halli seeba ban ka y…õdda saani.

ref: But instead, you cheat and rob other followers.
text: Amaa yarima n tumni taalli ka faari buna - halli ya taaba saani la gba!

ref: Don't you know that evil people won't have a share in the blessings of God's kingdom? Don't fool yourselves! No one who is immoral or worships idols or is unfaithful in marriage or is a pervert or behaves like a homosexual
text: Ammi, ya bu mi, ni, ban tumni taalli la ku nya Naawunni naam maa? Yan di y…Ēmsi ya mansi: see ka ya baŇčŇči, ni, zina dima ku nya Naawunni naam, ka ba'amaanniba gba ku nya, bee gy…õ'amiru dima ni d…Ēppa ban tumni lala, bee ban zaŇčŇčni ba d…Ētaaba n‚ÄĎdoontira;

ref: will share in God's kingdom. Neither will any thief or greedy person or drunkard or anyone who curses and cheats others.
text: ni na'ayigsi, ni beem dima, ni danyuuriba, ni minaafigsi, ni ban y…Ēmsiri niriba - banŇč…Ē s…Ēs…Ē ku nya Naawunni naam; ba nuu ka lala faari maa y…õla ni, halli beela.

ref: Some of you used to be like that. But now the name of our Lord Jesus Christ and the power of God's Spirit have washed you and made you holy and acceptable to God. Honor God with Your Body
text: Ya seeba mun daa b…õ la lala. Amaa ya daa nya koom suubu; ba daa ky…õ ka ya leebi Naawunni niriba kasikasi; ba daa deaai ya, ni, y…õlimaŇčni dima, ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Masiiya yuuri zugu, ni ti Ba Naawunni Vuusim Kasi la saani.

ref: Some of you say, ‚ÄúWe can do anything we want to.‚ÄĚ But I tell you not everything is good for us. So I refuse to let anything have power over me.
text: Ni, ‚ÄėM mari la seelikam yiiko.‚Äô - t…Ē, amaa pa seelikam zaa m paasiri suŇčŇčsim. Ni, ‚ÄėM mari la seelikam yiiko‚Äô - t…Ē, amaa di ky…õ ka seelikam zaa nyaŇčŇči ma.

ref: You also say, ‚ÄúFood is meant for our bodies, and our bodies are meant for food.‚ÄĚ But I tell you that God will destroy them both. We are not supposed to do indecent things with our bodies. We are to use them for the Lord who is in charge of our bodies.
text: Ni, ‚ÄėDiibu ny…õ la puuri dinni, ka puuri mun su diibu y…õla.‚Äô - t…Ē, amaa Naawunni nin ky…õ ka abayi wa zaa ti baasi y…Ēri. Pa zina n su ningbina fukumsi, amaa ti Dugma n su a; ningbina maa Dugma n ny…õ u.

ref: God will raise us from death by the same power he used when he raised our Lord to life.
text: Naawunni yiiko ka u daa zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎyisigi ti Dugma kum ni, ka u nin yisigi ti gba lala.

ref: Don't you know that your bodies are part of the body of Christ? Is it right for me to join part of the body of Christ to a prostitute? No, it isn't!
text: Ya bu mi, ni, ya ningbina ny…õ la Kirisita ningbinluga? Ka n ya niŇči la wula n‚ÄĎzaŇčŇči Kirisita ningbinlugri n‚ÄĎla'am n‚ÄĎleebi gy…õ'amiru ningbiŇčŇču lugri? Ay! - lala ka soori.

ref: Don't you know that a man who does that becomes part of her body? The Scriptures say, ‚ÄúThe two of them will be like one person.‚ÄĚ
text: Bee, ya bu mi, ni, Ňč…Ēn la'am n‚ÄĎtabli ni gy…õ'amiru, ni, babayi maa zaa leebi la yummu? Dama di y…õliya, ni, ‚ÄúBabayi maa zaa nin la'am m‚ÄĎb…õ ni kaman bunni yinni.‚ÄĚ

ref: But anyone who is joined to the Lord is one in spirit with him.
text: Ňä…Ēn mun la'am u maŇčŇča ni ti Dugma, ba leebi la sii yinni.

ref: Don't be immoral in matters of sex. That is a sin against your own body in a way no other sin is.
text: Ya z…Ēma n‚ÄĎyi zina tuma ni; dama tumbeeri zaa pa la nira ningbiŇčŇču puuni y…õtt…Ē'a, na'ala zina tuma ny…õ lala; amaa Ňč…Ēn niŇči zina tuma zaa niŇčni lala tumbeeri u toori ningbiŇčŇču ni.

ref: You surely know that your body is a temple where the Holy Spirit lives. The Spirit is in you and is a gift from God. You are no longer your own.
text: Bee, ya bu mi, ni, ya ningbina maa ny…õ la Sii Kasi gbeebu ziiya? - u GyeeŇčŇču Duu. Dama ya daa deaai Sii Kasi maa Naawunni nuu ni, ka u b…õ la ya saani. Ya bu su ya maŇčtoori y…õla;

ref: God paid a great price for you. So use your body to honor God.
text: ay! - ba daa da ya ni. Dizugu ya zaŇčŇčma ya ningbina n‚ÄĎtiri Naawunni gyirima.

ref: Now I will answer the questions you asked in your letter. You asked, ‚ÄúIs it best for people not to marry?‚ÄĚ
text: T…Ē: ka zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči y…õtt…Ē'aseesi yan daa s…Ēbi a y…õla la: di veela ni doo b…õ ni ka bu di p…Ē'a.

ref: Well, having your own husband or wife should keep you from doing something immoral.
text: Amaa zina k…Ērisi zugu, ya ky…õ ka doo zaa mari u p…Ē'a, ka p…Ē'a zaa mari u sira.

ref: Husbands and wives should be fair with each other about having sex.
text: Ky…õ ka doo maa tiri u p…Ē'a din sumsi u, ka p…Ē'a maa gba tiri u sira, hoo din tuusi seem.

ref: A wife belongs to her husband instead of to herself, and a husband belongs to his wife instead of to himself.
text: Dama p…Ē'a maa ningbiŇčŇču pa la u toori dinni k…Ēk…Ēa, amaa u sira gba dinni; ka lala doo maa ningbiŇčŇču pa la u toori dinni k…Ēk…Ēa, amaa u p…Ē'a gba dinni.

ref: So don't refuse sex to each other, unless you agree not to have sex for a little while, in order to spend time in prayer. Then Satan won't be able to tempt you because of your lack of self-control.
text: Yan di za'asi taaba, ka di yi pa la ya niŇči la nangbanyinni, ni, ya zaŇčŇči lala saha maa n‚ÄĎsusiri Naawunni; ka laa n‚ÄĎla'am taaba yaasa. Ka di ky…õ ka Sintaani zamsi ya n‚ÄĎnyaŇčŇči, yan ku toaai m‚ÄĎmari ya mansi zugu.

ref: In my opinion that is what should be done, though I don't know of anything the Lord said about this matter.
text: Man y…õtti la nŇč…Ēa, di pa la n zaali ya lala zaaligu, amaa ya yi tumni lala, n nin sakki.

ref: I wish all of you were like me, but God has given different gifts to each of us.
text: Man saani, n da boori la s…Ēkam d…Ēli la n toori soori. Amaa s…Ēkam mari la u toori soori Naawunni piini zugu: yinni mari la piini kanŇč…Ēa, ka s…Ē mari la seaa.

ref: My advice for the unmarried and for widows is that it is acceptable for them to remain single, just as I am.
text: T…Ē: ka n y…õtti n‚ÄĎtiri seeba ban na bu di p…Ē'a, bee, n‚ÄĎkuli doowa, ni puk…Ēya gba, ni, ba yi kpalim m‚ÄĎb…õ hoo man b…õ seem wa, di s…Ēa.

ref: But if you don't have enough self-control, then go ahead and get married. After all, it is better to marry than to burn with desire.
text: Amaa ba yi ku toaai n‚ÄĎgbaai ba mansi, ba dim p…Ē'aba, bee, n‚ÄĎkuli d…Ēppa. Ya yi niŇči lala di veela n‚ÄĎgaari ya ky…õ ka p…Ē'aba bee d…Ēppa y…õla ti mugsi ya.

ref: I instruct married couples to stay together, and this is exactly what the Lord himself taught. A wife who leaves her husband
text: Ban mun mari p…Ē'aba, bee, siridima ka n y…õtti wa - amaa pa mani n‚ÄĎy…õtta, amaa ti Dugma zaaligu nŇč…Ēa - ni, p…Ē'a di yi u sira yiri:

ref: should either stay single or go back to her husband. And a husband should not leave his wife.
text: amaa u yi yi, Ňč…Ēn di laa n‚ÄĎkuli d…Ēs…Ēa, bee, Ňč…Ēn labma u sira maa yiri. Ka doo mun di yiisi u p…Ē'a.

ref: I don't know of anything else the Lord said about marriage. All I can do is to give you my own advice. If your wife isn't a follower of the Lord, but is willing to stay with you, don't divorce her.
text: Ban kpalim muna ka n y…õtti wa - mani n‚ÄĎy…õtta, pa ti Dugma zaaligu nŇč…Ēa - ni, Kirisita bis…Ē yi mari u p…Ē'a ka p…Ē'a maa pa la y…õdda nira amaa u sakki ni u kpalim m‚ÄĎb…õ u saani, t…Ē, Ňč…Ēn di yiisi u p…Ē'a maa.

ref: If your husband isn't a follower, but is willing to stay with you, don't divorce him.
text: Ka p…Ē'a mun yi mari u sira ka sira maa pa la y…õdda nira amaa u sakki ni u kpalim m‚ÄĎb…õ u saani, t…Ē, Ňč…Ēn di yi.

ref: Your husband or wife who isn't a follower is made holy by having you as a partner. This also makes your children holy and keeps them from being unclean in God's sight.
text: Dama doo Ňč…Ēn ka y…õdda maa leebi la Naawunni dinni u p…Ē'a la zugu, ka p…Ē'a s…Ē Ňč…Ēn ka y…õdda la gba leebi la Naawunni dinni u sira la zugu: di yi pa lala, ya biisi naa m‚ÄĎpa la Naawunni dinni, amaa ka ba siri ny…õ la u dinni.

ref: If your husband or wife isn't a follower of the Lord and decides to divorce you, then you should agree to it. You are no longer bound to that person. After all, God chose you and wants you to live at peace.
text: Amaa Ňč…Ēn ka y…õdda la yi ya bakki, ky…õ ka u bakki. Y…õdda daana la ka taalli dinŇč…Ē ni: Naawunni m booli ti ni ti b…õ sufudoaa ni.

ref: And besides, how do you know if you will be able to save your husband or wife who isn't a follower? Obeying the Lord at All Times
text: I bu nya, p…Ē'a? - di bu ta'ai ni i fa i sira m‚ÄĎbasi. Bee nyini bu nya, doowa? - di bu ta'ai ni i fa i p…Ē'a m‚ÄĎbasi.

ref: In every church I tell the people to stay as they were when the Lord Jesus chose them and God called them to be his own. Now I say the same thing to you.
text: Di zaa y…Ēri, ya ky…õ ka s…Ēkam kpalim m‚ÄĎb…õ beesiseaa ti Dugma Ňč…Ēn daa zaali u, sahanseaa Naawunni Ňč…Ēn daa booli u maa. N zaaligu n‚ÄĎkur la, man yi b…õ Naawunni niriba la'aŇčŇču kam zaa ni.

ref: Being circumcised or uncircumcised isn't really what matters. The important thing is to obey God's commands.
text: Gunibu bee bu guni zaa ny…õ la y…Ēri: din kpa talasi ny…õ la Naawunni zaaligu zaa d…Ēligu.

ref: So don't try to change what you were when God chose you.
text: Ky…õ ka s…Ēkam kpalim m‚ÄĎb…õ Ňč…Ēn daa b…õ seem sahanseaa Naawunni Ňč…Ēn daa booli u.

ref: Are you a slave? Don't let that bother you. But if you can win your freedom, you should.
text: I yi daa ny…õ la daabri i booligu saha maa, nyin di ky…õ ka di mugsi i - amaa i yi ti nya soori ni i ti yi daabtiri maa na, kur d…Ēla ka n‚ÄĎyi.

ref: When the Lord chooses slaves, they become his free people. And when he chooses free people, they become slaves of Christ.
text: Y…õlimaŇčni, Ňč…Ēn daa ny…õ daabri u booligu maa saha zaa mari la y…Ēlisim ti Dugma saani, ka Ňč…Ēn mun daa su u maŇčŇča u booligu maa saha leebi la daabri n‚ÄĎti Kirisita.

ref: God paid a great price for you. So don't become slaves of anyone else.
text: Ba daa da ya ni n‚ÄĎy…Ēa; dizugu yan di leebi daaba n‚ÄĎti saalidima.

ref: Stay what you were when God chose you. Unmarried People
text: Dizugu s…Ēkam Ňč…Ēn daa b…õ seem zaa ka Naawunni daa booli u, u kpalimma m‚ÄĎb…õ lala, Naawunni zugu.

ref: I don't know of anything the Lord said about people who have never been married. But I will tell you what I think. And you can trust me, because the Lord has treated me with kindness.
text: T…Ē; ka ban na bu di p…Ē'aba muna, ni p…Ē'aseeba ban na bu kuli d…Ēppa gba - n ka ti Dugma zaaligu zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči ba y…õla; amaa man Ňč…Ēn ny…õ s…Ē Naawunni Ňč…Ēn z…Ē namboo n‚ÄĎti ma la, ka u ky…õ ka n niŇči y…õdda daana di zugu, m mari la saawara n‚ÄĎti ba.

ref: We are now going through hard times, and I think it is best for you to stay as you are.
text: Ni, n teesiya, ni, zuna mugsigu Ňč…Ēn b…õ seem zugu, ba yi kpalim m‚ÄĎb…õ lala, di s…Ēa.

ref: If you are married, stay married. If you are not married, don't try to get married.
text: Ni, i pun di p…Ē'a? - t…Ē, di yiisi u. I na ka p…Ē'a - t…Ē, di b…Ē p…Ē'a.

ref: It isn't wrong to marry, even if you have never been married before. But those who marry will have a lot of trouble, and I want to protect you from this.
text: Amaa i yi di p…Ē'a, i bu tum taalli; ka p…Ē'asariga yi kuli doowa, u gba bu tum taalli. Amaa ban mari p…Ē'aba, bee siridima, nin di wahala duniya wa puuni, ka m boori ni n ky…õ ka ya ku di lala wahala.

ref: My friends, what I mean is that the Lord will soon come, and it won't matter if you are married or not.
text: Man wulisiri seelli maa nŇč…Ēa, m mabiisi: saha maa ku yuui. PampaŇčŇč…Ē zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎwa, see ka ban mari p…Ē'aba gba b…õ hoo ban ka.

ref: It will be all the same if you are crying or laughing, or if you are buying or are completely broke.
text: Lala yaasa, ky…õ ka ban kumni la b…õ hoo ban bu kumna, ka ban mari sufumaasim b…õ hoo ban mari sufusa'aŇčŇču, halli ban daari neema gba b…õ hoo ban bu su marigu.

ref: It won't make any difference how much good you are getting from this world or how much you like it. This world as we know it is now passing away.
text: M‚ÄĎpaasi lala, ban mari duniya buna n‚ÄĎtumna, ba di ky…õ ka ba ba'a zaa b…õ a ni. Dama duniya kanŇč…Ē gatti ni maa.

ref: I want all of you to be free from worry. An unmarried man worries about how to please the Lord.
text: M bu boori ni seelli ti mugsi ya. Dakoori ba'a zaa b…õ la ti Dugma y…õla ni; ni u baŇčŇči Ňč…Ēn nin niŇči seem ka ti Dugma sufu maai.

ref: But a married man has more worries. He must worry about the things of this world, because he wants to please his wife.
text: P…Ē'adaana mun ba'a b…õ la duniya y…õla ni; ni u baŇčŇči Ňč…Ēn nin niŇči seem ka u p…Ē'a sufu maai;

ref: So he is pulled in two directions. Unmarried women and women who have never been married worry only about pleasing the Lord, and they keep their bodies and minds pure. But a married woman worries about the things of this world, because she wants to please her husband.
text: ka u ba'a puri buyi. Lala ka pukoori, bee, p…Ē'asariga ba'a zaa b…õ la ti Dugma y…õla ni; ni u baŇčŇči Ňč…Ēn nin niŇči seem ka b…õ kasi n‚ÄĎti ti Dugma, u ningbiŇčŇču ni, ni u sii ni gba. P…Ē'a s…Ē Ňč…Ēn mari u sira muna - u ba'a b…õ la duniya y…õla ni; ni u baŇčŇči Ňč…Ēn nin niŇči seem ka u sira sufu maai.

ref: What I am saying is for your own good‚ÄĒit isn't to limit your freedom. I want to help you to live right and to love the Lord above all else.
text: N y…õtti dinŇč…Ē la ya suŇčŇčsim zugu; pa la man ya ta'ari ya y…õla. N ya wulisiya ya ya nin niŇči seem ka ya teesa ku puri, amaa ka ya tumni din tuusi, ka kur gyeemni ti Dugma la suŇčŇčasuŇčŇča.

ref: But suppose you are engaged to someone old enough to be married, and you want her so much that all you can think about is getting married. Then go ahead and marry. There is nothing wrong with that.
text: T…Ē: ka zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči d…Ēs…Ē Ňč…Ēn su p…Ē'asariga y…õla maa: p…Ē'a maa yuuma yi paai doo kulibu ka d…Ēs…Ē Ňč…Ēn su u y…õla maa teesi, ni, u bu tumni din tu seem n‚ÄĎtiri u, ky…õ ka u niŇči u sufu Ňč…Ēn boori seem: ba yi ti niŇči amriya di ka taalli.

ref: It is perfectly all right to marry, but it is better not to get married at all.
text: DinŇč…Ēna p…Ē'a maa yi ti kuli doowa, lala d…Ēli la soori amaa u yi bu kuli doowa, di s…Ēa.

ref: A wife should stay married to her husband until he dies. Then she is free to marry again, but only to a man who is a follower of the Lord.
text: P…Ē'a s…Ē yi kuli doowa, di sum ni u b…õ la u sira maa saani, halli ni doo maa ny…Ēvuri t…Ēriga. Amaa u sira maa yi ti kpi, u mari soori ni u kuli d…Ēs…Ē Ňč…Ēn boora; amaa see ka doo maa ny…õ la ti Dugma nira.

ref: However, I think I am obeying God's Spirit when I say she would be happier to stay single.
text: Di zaa y…Ēri, u yi kpalim la pukoori u nin mari sufumaasim n‚ÄĎgaari lala - man teesi seem nŇč…Ēa, ka m mi, ni, n gba mari Naawunni Siiya.

ref: In your letter you asked me about food offered to idols. All of us know something about this subject. But knowledge makes us proud of ourselves, while love makes us helpful to others.
text: T…Ē; ka zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči bunseesi ban zaŇčŇči m‚ÄĎmaali ba'a la y…õla: ni, ‚ÄėTi zaa mari la bamsim.‚Äô - t…Ē, ti siri mi lala halli, amaa bamsim lala taabu zugu ka niriba kur duusiri ba mansi; wurilim mun ny…õ la din paasiri niriba beesisuŇčŇču.

ref: In fact, people who think they know so much don't know anything at all.
text: Ka nira Ňč…Ēn teesi, ni, Ňč…Ēn mi la seelli la - di daama na ka miilinsi din d…Ēli soori.

ref: But God has no doubts about who loves him.
text: Amaa nira Ňč…Ēn wuri Naawunni, Naawunni mi u y…õla.

ref: Even though food is offered to idols, we know that none of the idols in this world are alive. After all, there is only one God.
text: T…Ē: zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkpa ba'a bundira diibu ziiya, ti mi, ni, ba'ari pa la bunseelli kam duniya wa ni, ka wunni ka ni di yi pa la Naawunni k…Ēk…Ēa.

ref: Many things in heaven and on earth are called gods and lords, but none of them really are gods or lords.
text: Ba yi t…Ē'asi ba'a ni wuna y…õla duniya wa puuni, saazugu bee tingbaŇčŇču ni - halli ka ba siri t…Ē'asiri la wuna ni dugmadima pam y…õla -

ref: We have only one God, and he is the Father. He created everything, and we live for him. Jesus Christ is our only Lord. Everything was made by him, and by him life was given to us.
text: di zaa y…Ēri, dama tirima saani ka Naawunni yinni b…õ ni. Ti Ba Naawunni n ny…õ u, ka bunseelli kam yi la u saani na, ka ti gba b…õ ni n‚ÄĎtiri u. Ka ti Dugma yinni gba b…õ ni: Yisa Kirisita n ny…õ u, ka bunseelli kam b…õ ni Ňč…Ēn Yisa zugu, halli Ňč…Ēn zugu ka ti gba b…õ ni.

ref: Not everyone knows these things. In fact, many people have grown up with the belief that idols have life in them. So when they eat meat offered to idols, they are bothered by a weak conscience.
text: Y…õlimaŇčni nla: amaa pa s…Ēkam zaa m mari bamsim wa. Seeba na kpalim m‚ÄĎmiili la ba'a y…õla, halli ka ba yi ti di lala diibu ba teesiri, ni, ba diri a ni n‚ÄĎtiri ba'ari maa. Lala ka ban bu mi taalli ni din tu la y…õla galimiya ni.

ref: But food doesn't bring us any closer to God. We are no worse off if we don't eat, and we are no better off if we do.
text: Pa diibu zugu ka ti p…Ētti Naawunni saani na: ti yi bu di, ti ku p…Ēsigi seelli; ti mun yi di, lala ku paasiri ti seelli.

ref: Don't cause problems for someone with a weak conscience, just because you have the right to eat anything.
text: Amaa ya baŇčŇčma ka ya yuubu niŇčbu di ky…õ ka ban y…õdda bu z…õ deede la ti burigi n‚ÄĎlu.

ref: You know all this, and so it doesn't bother you to eat in the temple of an idol. But suppose a person with a weak conscience sees you and decides to eat food that has been offered to idols.
text: Ya bu nya? - Ňč…Ēn bu mi taalli ni din tu la yi ti nya ka nyini bamsim daana wa b…õ la ba'a duri ni n‚ÄĎdiri diibu, ammi, lala ku ky…õ ka u di ba'a diibu? - Ii, u nin di.

ref: When you sin by hurting a follower with a weak conscience, you sin against Christ.
text: Ya yi tum taalli wa taabu n‚ÄĎniŇči ya mabiisi, ka ky…õ ka ba burigi ban na zi soori deede zugu, ya tumni la taalli n‚ÄĎniŇči Kirisita.

ref: So if I hurt one of the Lord's followers by what I eat, I will never eat meat as long as I live.
text: Dizugu n numni wa Ňč…Ēppu yi nin ky…õ ka n taraana burigi, dinŇč…Ēna n ku laa n‚ÄĎŇč…Ēbi numni yaasa, dama di nin ky…õ ka n taraana burigi n‚ÄĎlu.

ref: I am free. I am an apostle. I have seen the Lord Jesus and have led you to have faith in him.
text: Man bu su m mammaŇčŇča? Pa tuumu n ny…õ ma? Bee, m pun bu nya ti Dugma Yisa? Ay! - n daa nya u ka leebi u tuumu, ka Ňč…Ēna n ti ma y…Ēlisimma: halli n tuma zugu ka yarim gba leebi Naawunni niriba.

ref: Others may think that I am not an apostle, but you are proof that I am an apostle to you.
text: Dizugu n yi pa la tuumu n‚ÄĎti seeba, n ny…õ la tuumu n‚ÄĎti yarima - man zugu ka ya ny…õ la Naawunni niriba, ka lala zugu ky…õ ka ba baŇčŇči, ni, tuumu n ny…õ ma.

ref: When people question me, I tell them
text: N labsigu nŇč…Ēa, n‚ÄĎtiri ban ya boosi ma boosa.

ref: that Barnabas and I have the right to our food and drink.
text: Ammi, tirima ka soori ni ti nya ti bundira ni bunnyuura?

ref: We each have the right to marry one of the Lord's followers and to take her along with us, just as the other apostles and the Lord's brothers and Peter do.
text: Halli tirima k…Ēk…Ē n ka soori ni ti di p…Ē'a Ňč…Ēn ny…õ y…õddaniŇčna, ka ky…õ ka u d…Ēli ti ti gyiliŇčŇču ni, bee? - hoo tuumuseeba la Ňč…Ēn tumni seem, ni ti Dugma tunz…Ēppa la, ni Siifasi gba.

ref: Are we the only ones who have to support ourselves by working at another job?
text: Bee, kulla man ni Baanabasi k…Ēk…Ē n ka soori ni ti ku ti bukaata, see ka ti tumni ti toori nuusi tuma?

ref: I am not saying this on my own authority. The Law of Moses tells us not to muzzle an ox when it is grinding grain. But was God concerned only about an ox?
text: Dama di s…Ēbi n‚ÄĎdooli Musa zaaligu s…Ēppu ni, ni, ‚ÄúNigi yi tumni kyi buubu tuma, yan di l…Ē ba n…Ēya.‚ÄĚ - T…Ē, nigi y…õla m mugsi Naawunni, bee?

ref: No, he wasn't! He was talking about us. This was written in the Scriptures so that all who plow and all who grind the grain will look forward to sharing in the harvest.
text: Ay! - U y…õtti dinŇč…Ē zaa n‚ÄĎti la tirima, bee wula? Ii! - tirima zugu ka ba daa s…Ēbi a zaa, ni lala ky…õ ka Ňč…Ēn koora ni Ňč…Ēn buuri kyi zaa nin la'am m‚ÄĎmari tama, ni, ba nin deaai ba t…Ēra.

ref: When we told the message to you, it was like planting spiritual seed. So we have the right to accept material things as our harvest from you.
text: T…Ē; ka tin daa tum ya saani seem la Ňčmanni la hoo kyi koobu tuma ka di b…õ la ya siisi ziiya; ka di yi b…õ lala, ammi, ti ka soori ni ti nya anfaani ya saani duniya wa ziiya?

ref: If others have the right to do this, we have an even greater right. But we haven't used this right of ours. We are willing to put up with anything to keep from causing trouble for the message about Christ.
text: Soori mun yi b…õ ni ka baseeba nyari lala anfaani ya saani ka di bu sum ni tirima gba nya? Amaa di zaa y…Ēri, ti daa bu zaŇčŇči lala soori maa. Ti daa y…Ē ti toori samni zaa, dama ti daa bu boori ni Kirisita lahabasuŇčŇču tuma labi nyaaŇčŇča.

ref: Don't you know that people who work in the temple make their living from what is brought to the temple? Don't you know that a person who serves at the altar is given part of what is offered?
text: M‚ÄĎpaasi lala, ya bu mi, ni, ban tumni Naawunni GyeeŇčŇču Yiri ni maa nyari ba diibu la lala tuma zugu? Ii! - ban deaari bunk…Ēbri niriba saani m‚ÄĎmaanni n‚ÄĎtiri ba kur mari ba t…Ēra.

ref: In the same way, the Lord wants everyone who preaches the good news to make a living from preaching this message.
text: Lala taabu ka ti Dugma zaali, ni, ban tumni u lahabasuŇčŇču tuma maa nin nya lala tuma maa yoori.

ref: But I have never used these privileges of mine, and I am not writing this because I want to start now. I would rather die than have someone rob me of the right to take pride in this.
text: Soori b…õ ni, amaa man mun daa bu deaai lala anfaani. M bu s…Ēbri y…õla wa ni ya ti niŇči lala n‚ÄĎti ma. Ay! - n yi da kpi di naa n‚ÄĎs…Ēa! M bu boori ni ba ta'ari m bara nyuubu.

ref: I don't have any reason to brag about preaching the good news. Preaching is something God told me to do, and if I don't do it, I am doomed.
text: Ya bu nya? - n yi mooli lahabasuŇčŇču maa y…õla, lala bu ti ma soori ni n ti nyuuri bara. Y…õlimaŇčni, di ny…õ ma la talasi - halli n yi bu moonna, n nin di wahala.

ref: If I preach because I want to, I will be paid. But even if I don't want to, it is still something God has sent me to do.
text: N yi tumni lala tuma maa m mammaŇčŇča sufu yuubu zugu, n nyari anfaani di ni; amaa di yi pa la n yuubu zugu, n kur kpalim n‚ÄĎtumni tunseesi ban zaali ma la.

ref: What pay am I given? It is the chance to preach the good news free of charge and not to use the privileges that are mine because I am a preacher.
text: Ka n yoori anfaani ny…õ la b…Ēa? DinŇč…Ē k…Ēk…Ēa: ni, n yi moonni lahabasuŇčŇču maa ka ba deaari y…Ēri, man Ňč…Ēn bu boori ligri zugu, anfaani nla n‚ÄĎti ma.

ref: I am not anyone's slave. But I have become a slave to everyone, so I can win as many people as possible.
text: T…Ē: ni man su n toori maa zaa, n sakki, ni, n leebi la daabri n‚ÄĎti s…Ēkam, ni lala ti ky…õ ka n nya niriba pam ka ba ti tuligi.

ref: When I am with the Jews, I live like a Jew to win Jews. They are ruled by the Law of Moses, and I am not. But I live by the Law to win them.
text: Y…õhudiya dima maa saani ka n daa leebi hoo Y…õhudiya doowa, ni lala nin ky…õ ka n nya ba ka ba ti tuligi; n siri pa la zaaligu Ňč…Ēn su s…Ē fukumsi, amaa n leebi la hoo lala nira n‚ÄĎtiri zaaligu Ňč…Ēn su seeba fukumsi la, ka lala ti ky…õ ka zaaligu niriba wa ti nya tuliginsim.

ref: And when I am with people who are not ruled by the Law, I forget about the Law to win them. Of course, I never really forget about the law of God. In fact, I am ruled by the law of Christ.
text: Ban mun ka zaaligu la saani ka n yi leebi ba taraana n‚ÄĎgba k…ĒŇčŇči zaaligu: n siri pa la s…Ē Ňč…Ēn bu d…Ēli Naawunni Ňč…Ēn zaali soori la, dama Kirisita zaaligu su n y…õla zaa, amaa lala ky…õ ka n nya ban ka zaaligu la ka ba ti tuligi.

ref: When I am with people whose faith is weak, I live as they do to win them. I do everything I can to win everyone I possibly can.
text: Halli ban ka yaa la saani n daa sakki n‚ÄĎleebi Ňč…Ēn gba ka yaa; y…õlimaŇčni, saalidima zaa Ňč…Ēn b…õ seem man gba daa sakki, ni, n ti leebi lala; ka lala ti ky…õ ka n nyaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎfa seeba m‚ÄĎbasi.

ref: I do all this for the good news, because I want to share in its blessings. A Race and a Fight
text: AnŇč…Ē zaa ka n niŇčna, Naawunni lahabasuŇčŇču zugu, ni man gba nin toaai n‚ÄĎla'am n‚ÄĎdi ka bunsuma.

ref: You know that many runners enter a race, and only one of them wins the prize. So run to win!
text: M‚ÄĎpaasi, ya bu mi zoaa y…õla? - ni, ban z…Ēri zoaa ni la saani, z…Ēz…Ēttiba maa zaa z…Ēra, amaa yinni k…Ēk…Ē n yi z…Ē n‚ÄĎdi. Dizugu ya kpaŇčŇčma ya mansi n‚ÄĎz…Ē suŇčŇča din nin ky…õ ka ya z…Ē n‚ÄĎdi.

ref: I don't run without a goal. And I don't box by beating my fists in the air.
text: Dizugu man mun bu z…Ēri zoaa la ni hoo Ňč…Ēn z…Ēri y…Ēriy…Ēri; m bu zabri hoo Ňč…Ēn buuri p…õsigu.

ref: I keep my body under control and make it my slave, so I won't lose out after telling the good news to others.
text: Ay! - n mari n toori ningbiŇčŇču ka gbaari ka ni nimmoaa, ni lala zugu ky…õ ka m pa la s…Ē Ňč…Ēn yiisi wa'azu n‚ÄĎti seeba, ka m mammaŇčŇča k…ĒŇčŇči nyaabu.

ref: Friends, I want to remind you that all our ancestors walked under the cloud and went through the sea.
text: M mabiisi, m bu boori ya kpalim ziilinsi ni, see ka ya baŇčŇči, ni, ti badima zaa daa la'am m‚ÄĎb…õ sangbaŇčŇču la l…ĒŇčni, ka ba zaa daa la'am n‚ÄĎkp…õ teeku maa puuni n‚ÄĎl…ĒŇčŇči.

ref: All of them also ate the same spiritual food
text: Ba zaa daa la'am n‚ÄĎdi diseebu din daa pa duniya wa diibu,

ref: But most of them did not please God. So they died, and their bodies were scattered all over the desert.
text: Di zaa y…Ēri, ba pam n daa ny…õ seeba Naawunni sufu Ňč…Ēn daa bu maai ba zugu; ka ba daa kpi n‚ÄĎkpalim n‚ÄĎd…Ēya yoo maa ni.

ref: What happened to them is a warning to keep us from wanting to do the same evil things.
text: T…Ē, ka anŇč…Ē n ny…õ an kpaamni tirima n‚ÄĎwulisiri ti, ni, ti di d…Ēli k…Ēribeeri hoo ban daa d…Ēli seem.

ref: They worshiped idols, just as the Scriptures say, ‚ÄúThe people sat down to eat and drink. Then they got up to dance around.‚ÄĚ So don't worship idols.
text: Halli yan di leebi ban maanni ba'a, hoo ban seeba daa maanni la - hoo din pun s…Ēbi n‚ÄĎdooli seem, ni, ‚ÄúNiriba maa daa zinni ni ba ti di ka nyu, ka naayi doaai na nti deemna.‚ÄĚ

ref: Some of those people did shameful things, and in a single day about
text: Ti di laa n‚ÄĎniŇči zina tuma, hoo ban seeba Ňč…Ēn daa niŇči seem, ka ba niriba tusa pisi ni ata daa kpi dahinyinni la.

ref: Some of you say, ‚ÄúWe can do whatever we want to!‚ÄĚ But I tell you not everything may be good or helpful.
text: Ni, ‚ÄėM mari la seelikam yiiko.‚Äô - t…Ē, amaa pa seelikam zaa m paasiri suŇčŇčsim. Ni, ‚ÄėM mari la seelikam yiiko.‚Äô - t…Ē, amaa pa seelikam zaa m paasiri niriba beesisuŇčŇču.

ref: And don't try to test Christ, as some of them did and were later bitten by poisonous snakes.
text: Ka ti di zamsiri ti Dugma, hoo ban seeba Ňč…Ēn daa zamsira ka wigi daa ku ba.

ref: Don't even grumble, as some of them did and were killed by the destroying angel.
text: Yaasa, ti di nyurimna, hoo ban seeba Ňč…Ēn daa nyurimni maa ka malaaka kum daana daa ky…õ ka ba baasi y…Ēri.

ref: These things happened to them as a warning to us. All this was written in the Scriptures to teach us who live in these last days.
text: T…Ē: anŇč…Ē zaa daa paai ba na, ka a wulisiri ti y…õla, ka ba daa s…Ēbi a n‚ÄĎdooli ni ba ti kpaamni ti a zugu - tirima ban kpalim nti nya duniya naari saha paari na.

ref: Even if you think you can stand up to temptation, be careful not to fall.
text: Dizugu, i yi teesi, ni, i z…õ la deede, nyin baŇčŇčma ka di lu!

ref: You are tempted in the same way that everyone else is tempted. But God can be trusted not to let you be tempted too much, and he will show you how to escape from your temptations.
text: Yan nya zamsigu seem, di bu yakki n‚ÄĎgaari ninsaalidima Ňč…Ēn nyari seaa. Y…õdda daana n ny…õ Naawunni, u ku ky…õ ka zamsigu seaa kan gaari ya paai ya. Halli zamsigu yi ti paai ya na, u nin b…Ē s…Ēseaa kan nin ky…õ ka ya nyaŇčŇči.

ref: My friends, you must keep away from idols.
text: DinŇč…Ēna, m mabiwutta, ya z…Ēm ka yi ba'a ni buga gyeeŇčŇču ni.

ref: I am speaking to you as people who have enough sense to know what I am talking about.
text: Ya nini n neaai, ka di zugu ka n t…Ē'asiri lala n‚ÄĎtiri ya. Ya mammansi kur baŇčŇčma n y…õtt…Ē'a maa gbinni Ňč…Ēn ny…õ seem.

ref: When we drink from the cup we ask God to bless, isn't that sharing in the blood of Christ? When we eat the bread we break, isn't that sharing in the body of Christ?
text: Ti yi mari bunnyuura k…Ēbeaa la m‚ÄĎpuusi Naawunni barika, pa la tin la'amni n‚ÄĎt…Ētti Kirisita ziim la zugu? Ti mun yi zaŇčŇči diibu la n‚ÄĎŇčmaari n‚ÄĎtiri taaba, pa la tin la'amni n‚ÄĎt…Ētti Kirisita ningbina zugu?

ref: By sharing in the same loaf of bread, we become one body, even though there are many of us.
text: T…Ē, ka diibu yinni m b…õ ni, ka tirim ban zoaai la'amni n‚ÄĎŇč…Ēbra, ka ti ti leebi ninvugyinni ningbina, dama ti zaa la'am n‚ÄĎdiri diibu yinni.

ref: Aren't the people of Israel sharing in the worship when they gather around the altar and eat the sacrifices offered there?
text: Ya bu ya teesi Izra…õli dima maa y…õla? Ban ny…õ Izra…õli dima duniya wa soori zugu la, niriba zaa ban la'amni m‚ÄĎmaanni sara la, ba laa n‚ÄĎla'amni n‚ÄĎt…Ētti numni maa n‚ÄĎŇč…Ēbra.

ref: Am I saying that either the idols or the food sacrificed to them is anything at all?
text: B…Ē ka n wulisiri wa? - Ni, ba'ari numni ny…õ seelli? bee, ni, ba'ari ny…õ seelli?

ref: No, I am not! This food is really sacrificed to demons and not to God. I don't want you to have anything to do with demons.
text: Ay! - amaa ba'amaanniba bu maanni Naawunni, arizimbeeri ka ba maanna. Ka m bu boori ni ya la'amni n‚ÄĎdeaari arizimbeeri y…õla t…Ēra.

ref: You cannot drink from the cup of demons and still drink from the Lord's cup. You cannot eat at the table of demons and still eat at the Lord's table.
text: Y…õlimaŇčni, ya ku toaai n‚ÄĎnyu ti Dugma bunnyuura k…Ēbeaa ni, ka laa n‚ÄĎnyu arizimbeeri dinni m‚ÄĎpaasi - lala ka soori. Ya ku toaai n‚ÄĎdi ti Dugma laa ni, ka laa n‚ÄĎdi arizimbeeri dinni ni - lala gba ka soori.

ref: We would make the Lord jealous if we did this. And we are not stronger than the Lord. Always Honor God
text: Bee, ti ya ky…õ ka ti Dugma nyuuri yisigi? Ammi, ti mari yaa n‚ÄĎgaari u? Ay - ti ka, walla!

ref: We should think about others and not about ourselves.
text: Yan di ky…õ ka s…Ēkam b…Ē u mammaŇčŇča y…õla, amaa u taraana y…õla ka u boora.

ref: However, when you buy meat in the market, go ahead and eat it. Keep your conscience clear by not asking where the meat came from.
text: Ya Ňč…Ēbma nakoosidima numkoosa zaa, amaa di boosiri numseesi y…õla ni ya ti nya galinsi a zugu.

ref: The Scriptures say, ‚ÄúThe earth and everything in it belong to the Lord.‚ÄĚ
text: Dama di s…Ēbiya, ni, ‚ÄúDuniya wa, ni din b…õ ka ni maa - a zaa ny…õ la ti Dugma dinni.‚ÄĚ

ref: If an unbeliever invites you to dinner, and you want to go, then go. Eat whatever you are served. Don't cause a problem for someone's conscience by asking where the food came from.
text: T…Ē. S…Ē Ňč…Ēn bu niŇči y…õdda yi ti zuusi ya u bundirigu ka ya boori ky…õnni, yan dima ban zaali seelli na zaa; yan di boosiri a y…õla ni ya ti nya galinsi a zugu.

ref: But if you are told it has been sacrificed to idols, don't cause a problem by eating it. I don't mean a problem for yourself, but for the one who told you. Why should my freedom be limited by someone else's conscience?
text: - T…Ē, lala nira maa sufu ni teesa y…õla ka n t…Ē'asiri maa, pa ya mammaŇčŇča dina. Mani n su m mammaŇčŇča y…õla, ka dizugu n ku ky…õ ka s…Ē zaŇčŇči u sufu ni teesa n‚ÄĎgalim ma ka di mugsi ma.

ref: If I give thanks for what I eat, why should anyone accuse me of doing wrong?
text: N yi puusi Naawunni ka di diibu maa, ba ya niŇči la wula n‚ÄĎgalim ma, diibu wa zugu? - dama m pun puusi Naawunni di zugu.

ref: When you eat or drink or do anything else, always do it to honor God.
text: Dizugu ya yi diri diibu, bee, n‚ÄĎnyuuri bunnyuura, bee, ya yi niŇčni seelli zaa, ya ky…õ ka ya tumni lala n‚ÄĎtiri Naawunni gyirima.

ref: Don't cause problems for Jews or Greeks or anyone else who belongs to God's church.
text: Yan di ky…õ ka s…Ēs…Ē burigi n‚ÄĎlu ya y…õla zugu - pa Izra…õli dima, bee ban pa Izra…õli niriba, halli Naawunni niriba gba.

ref: I always try to please others instead of myself, in the hope that many of them will be saved.
text: Lala ka man moora, ni n ky…õ ka s…Ēkam sufu maai n y…õla zugu. M bu boori m mammaŇčŇča anfaani y…õla, see din mari anfaani n‚ÄĎtiri s…Ēkam - di ny…õ la: ni, ba nya tuliginsim.

ref: You must follow my example, as I follow the example of Christ. Rules for Worship
text: Ya d…Ēli n soori maa, hoo man gba d…Ēli Kirisita Masiiya soori seem la.

ref: I am proud of you, because you always remember me and obey the teachings I gave you.
text: T…Ē: ka m p…õ'ari ya, dama ya teaari n y…õla sahakam ka kpalim n‚ÄĎd…Ēli wulisigu maa zaa, hoo man daa zaali ya seem la.

ref: Now I want you to know that Christ is the head of all men, and a man is the head of a woman. But God is the head of Christ.
text: Amaa m boori ni ya baŇčŇči, ni, d…Ēppa saani s…Ēkam zaa zuguraana n ny…õ Kirisita Masiiya, p…Ē'a mun zuguraana ny…õ la u sira, hoo Kirisita zuguraana Ňč…Ēn ny…õ Naawunni.

ref: This means that any man who prays or prophesies with something on his head brings shame to his head.
text: D…Ēs…Ē Ňč…Ēn pili u zugu ka susiri Naawunni, bee, n‚ÄĎt…Ē'asiri anabitiri y…õtt…Ē'a, di daama diisiri u maŇčŇča vi.

ref: But any woman who prays or prophesies without something on her head brings shame to her head. In fact, she may as well shave her head.
text: Amaa p…Ē'as…Ē Ňč…Ēn mun bu pili u zugu ka susiri Naawunni, bee, n‚ÄĎt…Ē'asiri anabitiri y…õtt…Ē'a, lala p…Ē'a diisiri u maŇčŇča vi. U yi ti niŇči lala, di Ňčmanni la hoo Ňč…Ēn laa m‚ÄĎpuni u zugu ka doaai ni zugkoaa n‚ÄĎt…Ē'asira.

ref: A woman should wear something on her head. It is a disgrace for a woman to shave her head or cut her hair. But if she refuses to wear something on her head, let her cut off her hair.
text: Dama p…Ē'a yi ku sakki, ni, u b…Ēbi u zugu, dinŇč…Ēna Ňč…Ēn kyeema, bee, m‚ÄĎpuni ka doaai n‚ÄĎzaani zugkoaa. Ka lala mun yi ny…õ la vi, dinŇč…Ēna see ka u b…Ēbi u zugu.

ref: Men were created to be like God and to bring honor to God. This means a man should not wear anything on his head. Women were created to bring honor to men.
text: Doo bu sum ni u pili u zugu, dama Naawunni Ňčmaari n ny…õ u, n‚ÄĎtisiri Naawunni gyirima; p…Ē'a mun tisiri doo maa gyirima.

ref: It was the woman who was made from a man, and not the man who was made from a woman.
text: Dama ba daa bu zaŇčŇči p…Ē'a m‚ÄĎmaali doowa, amaa ba daa naam doowa p…Ēi ka naayi yiisi p…Ē'a u saani na.

ref: He wasn't created for her. She was created for him.
text: Dizugu pa p…Ē'a zugu ka doo daa b…õ ni, amaa doo maa zugu ka ba daa maali p…Ē'a maa.

ref: And so, because of this, and also because of the angels, a woman ought to wear something on her head, as a sign of her authority.
text: DinŇč…Ēna see ka p…Ē'a maa b…Ēbi u zugu suŇčŇča, ban su u fukumsi zugu, ni malaakadima la zugu.

ref: As far as the Lord is concerned, men and women need each other.
text: Ni dinŇč…Ē zaa, ti Dugma saani p…Ē'a pa u k…Ēk…Ēa ka ky…õ doowa, ka doo gba pa u k…Ēk…Ēa ka ky…õ p…Ē'a.

ref: It is true that the first woman came from a man, but all other men have been given birth by women. Yet God is the one who created everything.
text: Ba daa naam doo ka yiisi p…Ē'a u saani na, ka p…Ē'a mun yi laa n‚ÄĎd…Ē'ari doowa. Ka seelikam yi la Naawunni saani.

ref: Ask yourselves if it is proper for a woman to pray without something on her head.
text: Ya mammansi teesim din sumsi ni: p…Ē'a yi bu pili u zugu ka susiri Naawunni - di mari soori?

ref: But long hair is a beautiful way for a woman to cover her head.
text: P…Ē'a mun yi mari u z…Ēbw…Ē'ala, di mari la gyirima n‚ÄĎti u. Ba ti u lala z…Ēbri ni a ti leebi b…ĒbsuŇčŇču n‚ÄĎti u.

ref: This is how things are done in all of God's churches, and this is why none of you should argue about what I have said. Rules for the Lord's Supper
text: S…Ē yi ya laa n‚ÄĎŇčm…õ nangbankpeemni dinŇč…Ē zugu, ky…õ ka n y…õli ya, ni, tirima bu d…Ēli lala soori, halli ya'aseelikam Naawunni niriba Ňč…Ēn la'amni n‚ÄĎgyeemna.

ref: I am told you can't get along with each other when you worship, and I am sure that some of what I have heard is true.
text: Dama, daŇčŇčni, n wum ya lahabaari, ni, ya yi la'am taaba ni ya gyeem Naawunni, ya mari la nangbanwala - ka n sakki n sufu ni, ni, di bu ta'ari ni ya b…õ lala.

ref: You are bound to argue with each other, but it is easy to see which of you have God's approval.
text: Dama see ka nangbanwala b…õ ya ni, ka lala nin ky…õ ka Naawunni nini Ňč…Ēn tigi seeba la y…õla ti yi paalu ni.

ref: You even start eating before everyone gets to the meeting, and some of you go hungry, while others get drunk.
text: Dama ya yi diri ni, s…Ēkam diri la u mammaŇčŇča diibu n‚ÄĎdaŇčŇčni u taraana; ka k…Ēm mari seeba, halli ka seeba mun nyuuri nti bugra.

ref: The Lord meant that when you eat this bread and drink from this cup, you tell about his death until he comes.
text: T…Ē, ka sahanseaa kam ya yi ti di bundira wa ka nyu k…Ēbeaa wa bunnyuura, ya moonni la ti Dugma kum y…õla, halli ni u lappu na.

ref: If you fail to understand that you are the body of the Lord, you will condemn yourselves by the way you eat and drink.
text: Dama Ňč…Ēnkam di ka nyu ka bu baŇčŇči di gbinni, ni, ti Dugma ningbina ni u ziim maa nŇč…Ēa, di daama b…Ē la sariya karibu m‚ÄĎbasi u maŇčŇča zugu.

ref: After the meal, Jesus took a cup of wine in his hands and said, ‚ÄúThis is my blood, and with it God makes his new agreement with you. Drink this and remember me.‚ÄĚ
text: Ka ban daa di n‚ÄĎnaai maa, u daa zaŇčŇči k…Ēbeaa gba lala, ni, ‚ÄúNaawunni nangbanyinni zaalipaalli maa nŇč…Ēa; n ziim nin maali lala zaaligu maa n‚ÄĎti ya. Ya yi kur ti nyu kanŇč…Ē sahanseaa zaa, yan teaama n y…õla.‚ÄĚ

ref: I have already told you what the Lord Jesus did on the night he was betrayed. And it came from the Lord himself. He took some bread in his hands.
text: Dama man daa deaai seelli ti Dugma saani la ka n daa zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎwulisi ya: ni, yunseaa ban daa figsi ti Dugma Yisa maa, u daa daŇčŇči n‚ÄĎzaŇčŇči diibu la

ref: Then after he had given thanks, he broke it and said, ‚ÄúThis is my body, which is given for you. Eat this and remember me.‚ÄĚ
text: m‚ÄĎpuusi Naawunni p…õ'abu n‚ÄĎy…õli, ni, ‚ÄúN ningbina nŇč…Ēa n‚ÄĎti yarima; ya niŇčma lala ka teaari n y…õla.‚ÄĚ

ref: But if you eat the bread and drink the wine in a way that isn't worthy of the Lord, you sin against his body and blood.
text: Dizugu Ňč…Ēn mi, ni, u bu d…Ēli soori, ka laa n‚ÄĎdi bundira wa, bee, n‚ÄĎnyu bunnyuura wa, u galimni la ti Dugma ningbina ni u ziim maa.

ref: This is why you must examine the way you eat and drink.
text: See ka nira viisi u maŇčŇča suŇčŇča p…Ēi ka naayi di bundira maa ka nyu k…Ēbeaa maa bunnyuura.

ref: This is why many of you are sick and weak and why a lot of others have died.
text: Lala zugu ky…õ ka ya niriba pam k…ĒŇčŇči yaa ka beera; halli ya seeba mun pun ka ni.

ref: But when the Lord judges and punishes us, he does it to keep us from being condemned with the rest of the world.
text: Amaa ti Dugma yi viisiri ti y…õla ka kari ti sariya, u wubsiri ti n‚ÄĎzaanni ti soori ni, ni lala ti ky…õ ka ba ku la'am ti ni duniya wa dima n‚ÄĎkari ti sariya n‚ÄĎlusi ti.

ref: My dear friends, you should wait until everyone gets there before you start eating.
text: Ka dinŇč…Ēna, m mabiisi, ya yi la'am taaba ni ya ti di, see ka ya guri taaba.

ref: If you really are hungry, you can eat at home. Then you won't condemn yourselves when you meet together. After I arrive, I will instruct you about the other matters.
text: K…Ēm yi mari s…Ēa, Ňč…Ēn dima u yiri ni p…Ēi, ka lala nin ky…õ ka ya yi la'am taaba ya ku nya galinsi di zugu. T…Ē: n yi ti ky…õ ya saani na, n nin laa n‚ÄĎwulisi ya din kpalim la zaa.

ref: I want you to remember that before you became followers of the Lord, you were led in all the wrong ways by idols that cannot even talk.
text: Ya mi halli, ni, yan daa na bu niŇči tuuba la, ba daa y…Ēmsi ya ka ya d…Ēli ba'a an bu nyaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎt…Ē'asira.

ref: Now I want you to know that if you are led by God's Spirit, you will say that Jesus is Lord, and you will never curse Jesus.
text: Dizugu n y…õtti ya, ni, Naawunni Sii Ňč…Ēn b…õ s…Ē saani zaa, di daama ku toaai n‚ÄĎy…õli ‚ÄúYisa baasima y…Ēri!‚ÄĚ Ka di yi pa Sii Kasi maa zugu, s…Ē mun ku toaai n‚ÄĎy…õli, ni, ‚ÄúYisa ny…õ la ti Dugma!‚ÄĚ

ref: There are different kinds of spiritual gifts, but they all come from the same Spirit.
text: T…Ē: ka piini balibubalibu m b…õ ni, amaa Sii yinni maa b…õ ni n‚ÄĎtisira.

ref: There are different ways to serve the same Lord,
text: GyeeŇčŇču s…Ēya balibubalibu m b…õ ni, amaa ti Dugma yinni ka ti gyeemna.

ref: and we can each do different things. Yet the same God works in all of us and helps us in everything we do.
text: Tuma balibubalibu gba b…õ ni, amaa Naawunni yinni maa n tumni a zaa s…Ēkam puuni.

ref: The Spirit has given each of us a special way of serving others.
text: Sii maa wulisiri u maŇčŇča n‚ÄĎtiri s…Ēkam zaa, ni di zaa ti maali ka kyaŇčŇči tooni.

ref: Some of us can speak with wisdom, while others can speak with knowledge, but these gifts come from the same Spirit.
text: Sii maa zugu ka ninvugs…Ē mari y…õm y…õtt…Ē'a; ka s…Ē mun mari bamsim, lala Sii yinni maa zugu.

ref: To others the Spirit has given great faith or the power to heal the sick
text: S…Ē yaasa mari la y…õdda lala Sii maa zugu, ka s…Ē mun b…õ ni ka u deaai baaridima tippu, Sii yinni maa zugu.

ref: or the power to work mighty miracles. Some of us are prophets, and some of us recognize when God's Spirit is present. Others can speak different kinds of languages, and still others can tell what these languages mean.
text: Seeba yaasa deaari la alahazibu tuma yiiko, bee, anabitiri y…õtt…Ē'a, bee, m‚ÄĎbaŇčŇči siisi y…õla viisigu, bee, n‚ÄĎt…Ē'asiri y…õtt…Ē'a balibubalibu, bee, m‚ÄĎbaŇčŇči lala y…õtt…Ē'a maa gbinni n‚ÄĎwulisira.

ref: But it is the Spirit who does all this and decides which gifts to give to each of us. One Body with Many Parts
text: AnŇč…Ē zaa ny…õ la Sii yinni maa tuma, ka u mari n‚ÄĎtisiri u sufu Ňč…Ēn boori seem.

ref: Some of us are Jews, and others are Gentiles. Some of us are slaves, and others are free. But God's Spirit baptized each of us and made us part of the body of Christ. Now we each drink from that same Spirit.
text: Sii yinni maa zugu ka ti zaa daa su Naawunni koom, ka di daa ky…õ ka ti la'am n‚ÄĎniŇči ningbiŇčŇču yinni; halli ni ti seeba daa ny…õ Y…õhudiyadima ka seeba mun daa ny…õ Griikadima gba; ka seeba daa ny…õ daaba, seeba mun daa su ba mansi; ti zaa daa la'am n‚ÄĎnyu Sii yinni maa.

ref: Our bodies don't have just one part. They have many parts.
text: T…Ē, ka ningbiŇčŇču muna - pa lugri yinni k…Ēk…Ē nla, amaa luga pam.

ref: Suppose a foot says, ‚ÄúI'm not a hand, and so I'm not part of the body.‚ÄĚ Wouldn't the foot still belong to the body?
text: Ya bu nya? - S…Ē n…Ēbri yi ti y…õli, ni, ‚ÄúT…Ē, m pa la nuu, dizugu m pa ningbiŇčŇču maa lugri, halli fiiŇč gba!‚ÄĚ, lala maa ku ky…õ ka ka siri pa ningbiŇčŇču maa luginseaa.

ref: Or suppose an ear says, ‚ÄúI'm not an eye, and so I'm not part of the body.‚ÄĚ Wouldn't the ear still belong to the body?
text: Bee, s…Ē tubri yi ti y…õli, ni, ‚ÄúT…Ē, m pa la nimbiri, dizugu m pa ningbiŇčŇču lugri k…Ētaa!‚ÄĚ, lala maa ku ky…õ ka ka siri pa ningbiŇčŇču maa luginseaa.

ref: If our bodies were only an eye, we couldn't hear a thing. And if they were only an ear, we couldn't smell a thing.
text: Ya bu nya? - ningbiŇčŇču zaa yi da ny…õ la nimbiri, ba ya niŇči la wula n‚ÄĎwumna? Bee, ka zaa yi da ny…õ la tubri, ba ya niŇči la wula n‚ÄĎwumni nyuu?

ref: But God has put all parts of our body together in the way that he decided is best.
text: Amaa din ny…õ seem maa, Naawunni daa niŇči luga maa zaa la a lugri a lugri, hoo Ňč…Ēn Naawunni Ňč…Ēn boori seem.

ref: A body isn't really a body, unless there is more than one part.
text: Y…õlimaŇčni, a zaa yi da nya lugri yinni k…Ēk…Ēa, yani ka ningbiŇčŇču da ya b…õ ni?

ref: It takes many parts to make a single body.
text: Din ny…õ seem maa, luga maa zoaai ni, amaa a la'amni n‚ÄĎniŇči ningbimmunni.

ref: That's why the eyes cannot say they don't need the hands. That's also why the head cannot say it doesn't need the feet.
text: Nimbiri ku toaai n‚ÄĎy…õli nuu, ni, ‚ÄúI ka bukaata man saani!‚ÄĚ, bee, zugu y…õli n…Ēba, ni, ‚ÄúYa ka ma bukaata!‚ÄĚ

ref: In fact, we cannot get along without the parts of the body that seem to be the weakest.
text: Ay! - halli luginseesi an ka yaa mari la bukaata gba.

ref: We take special care to dress up some parts of our bodies. We are modest about our personal parts,
text: Ka luginseesi tin y…õtti, ni, an ka gyirima la n ny…õ a, ana ka ti g…Ēsiri suŇčŇča; ka a seesi an bu veela la, ana ka ti mari suŇčŇča vi zugu

ref: but we don't have to be modest about other parts. God put our bodies together in such a way that even the parts that seem the least important are valuable.
text: - halli ka a bunveela la bu boori lala suŇčŇčsim. Amaa Naawunni Ňč…Ēn maali ningbiŇčŇču seem maa, u zaŇčŇči la gyirima n‚ÄĎti luginseesi an y…õla bu mugsi pam la.

ref: He did this to make all parts of the body work together smoothly, with each part caring about the others.
text: Naawunni tum lala ni u ti ky…õ ka nangbankpeemni ni w…Ēligri ka ningbiŇčŇču maa ni, amaa ka luga maa zaa ti suŇčŇčni m‚ÄĎmari taaba.

ref: If one part of our body hurts, we hurt all over. If one part of our body is honored, the whole body will be happy.
text: Lugri yinni yi ti namsi, a zaa yi la'am n‚ÄĎnamsi ni; lugri yinni mun yi nya p…õ'abu, sufu maari n ny…õ di n‚ÄĎtiri a zaa.

ref: Together you are the body of Christ. Each one of you is part of his body.
text: T…Ē: yarima mun n la'am n‚ÄĎny…õ Kirisita ningbiŇčŇču, ka ya zaa yinni yinni n ny…õ u ningbinluga.

ref: First, God chose some people to be apostles and prophets and teachers for the church. But he also chose some to work miracles or heal the sick or help others or be leaders or speak different kinds of languages.
text: Ka Naawunni daa yiisi u niriba ka ba tumni tuma balibubalibu n‚ÄĎtiri taaba: u daa daŇčŇči n‚ÄĎzaali seeba ni ba ti niŇči u tuumudima maa; ka laa n‚ÄĎzaali seeba m‚ÄĎpa anabidima; nta karimbadima, m‚ÄĎpaasi ban tumni alahazibu tuma, ban tibri baaridima, ban suŇčŇčni niriba, ni Naawunni niriba toondaandima, ni ban t…Ē'asiri y…õtt…Ē'a balibubalibu.

ref: Not everyone is an apostle. Not everyone is a prophet. Not everyone is a teacher. Not everyone can work miracles.
text: S…Ēkam zaa ny…õ la tuumu? - Ay! S…Ēkam ny…õ la anabi, bee, karimba? S…Ēkam n toaai n‚ÄĎtumni alahazibu tuma? - Ay! di pa lala.

ref: Not everyone can heal the sick. Not everyone can speak different kinds of languages. Not everyone can tell what these languages mean.
text: M‚ÄĎpaasi yaasa: s…Ēkam tibri la baaridima? S…Ēkam n toaai n‚ÄĎt…Ē'asiri y…õtt…Ē'a balibu? Bee, m‚ÄĎbaŇčŇči lala y…õtt…Ē'a gbinni? - Ay!

ref: I want you to desire the best gifts. So I will show you a much better way.
text: T…Ē: ka dinŇč…Ēna ya booma piiniseesi an tibsa la. Halli ya ky…õ ka n wulisi ya s…Ēseaa kan veeli n‚ÄĎgaari zaa#:-

ref: What if I could speak all languages of humans and even of angels? If I did not love others, I would be nothing more than a noisy gong or a clanging cymbal.
text: N yi nyaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎt…Ē'asiri ninsaalidima y…õtt…Ē'a kam zaa, halli ni malaakadima y…õtt…Ē'a gba, ka mun ka wurilim, dinŇč…Ēna n Ňčmanni la gbiliŇčŇča bee feeŇčŇča n‚ÄĎkumni y…Ēri.

ref: What if I could prophesy and understand all mysteries and all knowledge? And what if I had faith that moved mountains? I would be nothing, unless I loved others.
text: N yi niŇči anabitiri, ka laa m‚ÄĎmari buns…Ē'ara zaa bamsim, m‚ÄĎmari y…õdda halli nti ky…õ ka z…Ēya vuui m‚ÄĎpaai seela, ka mun ka wurilim, dinŇč…Ēna m pa seelli.

ref: What if I gave away all that I owned and let myself be burned alive? I would gain nothing, unless I loved others.
text: Halli n yi zaŇčŇči n ligri ni n neema zaa n‚ÄĎt…Ēri fara dima, ka laa n‚ÄĎzaŇčŇči n ningbiŇčŇču n‚ÄĎky…õ ka ba ny…Ē ka bugum, ka mun ka wurilim, n ka anfaani anŇč…Ē zaa zugu.

ref: Love is patient and kind, never jealous, boastful, proud, or
text: Wurilim mari la sugru ni namb…Ēzoaa; wurilim bu zabri nyuuri; ka mun bu nyuuri bara, bee, m‚ÄĎmari karimbaani.

ref: rude. Love isn't selfish or quick tempered. It doesn't keep a record of wrongs that others do.
text: Wurilim bu tumni gy…õrilim; di bu boori di mammaŇčŇča yuubu, bee, n‚ÄĎgy…õ suuri yooma: wurilim bu teaari taalli n‚ÄĎguri ni di ti boosi.

ref: Love rejoices in the truth, but not in evil.
text: Wurilim sufu bu maara ninvugseeba tumbeeri zugu; amaa ka mari la sufumaasim y…õlimaŇčni zaa zugu.

ref: Love is always supportive, loyal, hopeful, and trusting.
text: Wurilim ta'ari ni, m‚ÄĎmari y…õdda seelikam puuni, ka laa m‚ÄĎmari tama ka diri sugru seelikam puuni.

ref: Love never fails! Everyone who prophesies will stop, and unknown languages will no longer be spoken. All that we know will be forgotten.
text: Wurilim bu naara: amaa anabitiri y…õla yi b…õ ni, a nin paai a t…Ēriga; ka ba yi ti t…Ē'asiri y…õtt…Ē'a balibubalibu, ba nin naa n‚ÄĎsini ba noori; bamsim gba nin paai di naari.

ref: We don't know everything, and our prophecies are not complete.
text: Dama ti bamsim maa pa la bamsimunni, ka ti anabitiri gba pa la bummunni.

ref: But what is perfect will someday appear, and what isn't perfect will then disappear.
text: Ka din ny…õ bummunni deede la yi ti paai na, lala saha ka an pa bummuna la nin gaari n‚ÄĎnaai.

ref: When we were children, we thought and reasoned as children do. But when we grew up, we quit our childish ways.
text: Ya bu nya? man daa ny…õ bibila sahanseaa la, n daa mari la biilim n y…õtt…Ē'a puuni, ni n teesa ni n y…õm y…õla zaa ni. Amaa man daa leebi bunkurigu maa, n daa zaŇčŇči biilim y…õla maa zaa m‚ÄĎbasi.

ref: Now all we can see of God is like a cloudy picture in a mirror. Later we will see him face to face. We don't know everything, but then we will, just as God completely understands us.
text: Dama pampaŇčŇč…Ē ti nyari liisaliisa hoo mans…Ē'asi ni, amaa ka ti nin nya kasi hoo nini Ňč…Ēn kpa taaba, din daari maa. PampaŇčŇč…Ē n na bu mi y…õla zaa suŇčŇča, a seesi k…Ēk…Ē ka m mi; amaa din daari maa n nin ti baŇčŇči di zaa kasi, hoo ban baŇčŇči ma seem la.

ref: For now there are faith, hope, and love. But of these three, the greatest is love.
text: Dizugu an kpalim m‚ÄĎb…õ ni n ny…õ y…õdda, ni tama, ni wurilim, abata wa; amaa din gaari a zaa ny…õ la wurilim.

ref: Love should be your guide. Be eager to have the gifts that come from the Holy Spirit, especially the gift of prophecy.
text: Ka dinŇč…Ēna ya d…Ēli wurilim s…Ēya, ka b…Ē Sii maa piini; ka kan daŇčŇči tooni la ny…õ la anabitiri y…õla piini.

ref: If you speak languages that others don't know, God will understand what you are saying, though no one else will know what you mean. You will be talking about mysteries that only the Spirit understands.
text: Dama Ňč…Ēn t…Ē'asiri y…õtt…Ē'a balibubalibu maa bu t…Ē'asiri n‚ÄĎtiri saalidima, amaa Naawunni ka u t…Ē'asira. Halli s…Ēs…Ē bu baŇčŇčni a gbinni, ka u kpalim n‚ÄĎy…õtti y…õlis…Ē'ara u siiya puuni.

ref: But when you prophesy, you will be understood, and others will be helped. They will be encouraged and made to feel better.
