text: Ňä…Ēn mun t…Ē'asiri anabitiri y…õtt…Ē'a maa yi t…Ē'asiri n‚ÄĎtiri niriba, di paasiri ba beesigu ka kpamsiri ba.

ref: By speaking languages that others don't know, you help only yourself. But by prophesying you help everyone in the church.
text: Ii - Ňč…Ēn t…Ē'asiri y…õtt…Ē'a balibu maa paasiri u maŇčtoori beesigu, Ňč…Ēn mun y…õtti anabitiri y…õla la paasiri Naawunni niriba zaa beesigu.

ref: I am glad for you to speak unknown languages, although I prefer that you would prophesy. In fact, prophesying does much more good than speaking unknown languages, unless someone can help the church by explaining what you mean.
text: T…Ē, n da boori la ni ya zaa toaai n‚ÄĎt…Ē'asiri y…õtt…Ē'a balibubalibu, ka laa m‚ÄĎboori ni ya y…õtti anabitiri y…õla, ka lala yi s…Ēa. Anabi gaari Ňč…Ēn t…Ē'asiri y…õtt…Ē'a balibu, di yi pa la s…Ē b…õ ni n‚ÄĎwulisiri y…õtt…Ē'a balibu maa gbinni ka lala nin paasi Naawunni niriba beesigu.

ref: My friends, what good would it do, if I came and spoke unknown languages to you and didn't explain what I meant? How would I help you, unless I told you what God had shown me or gave you some knowledge or prophecy or teaching?
text: T…Ē, m mabiisi: n yi ky…õ ya saani na n‚ÄĎt…Ē'asiri y…õtt…Ē'a balibubalibu, n suŇčŇčni ya la wula? Ay, zaalim! - ka di yi pa la n laa m‚ÄĎmari y…õlis…Ē'ara kasigri, bee, bamsim y…õla, bee, anabitiri y…õla, bee, wulisigu n‚ÄĎti ya na.

ref: If all musical instruments sounded alike, how would you know the difference between a flute and a harp?
text: Halli bunseesi an ka ny…Ēvuri saani di kur b…õ la lala: ya bu nya? - bunŇčmeera hoo yiiya bee goongyi yi bu Ňčm…õ n‚ÄĎg…Ēligi suŇčŇča, wula ka nira ya baŇčŇči Ňčmeebu maa gbinni?

ref: If a bugle call isn't clear, how would you know to get ready for battle?
text: Ka bunseaa ban Ňčmeeri m‚ÄĎmoonni t…Ēppu y…õla la muna - ka yi bu Ňčm…õ n‚ÄĎg…Ēligi, wula ka ba ya baŇčŇči n‚ÄĎniŇči t…Ēppu siri?

ref: This is how it is when you speak unknown languages. If no one can understand what you are talking about, you will only be talking to the wind.
text: Ya mammansi y…õtt…Ē'a ni gba, ya yi bu ky…õ ka ya y…õla yiiri suŇčŇča, wula ka s…Ē ya niŇči m‚ÄĎbaŇčŇči yan y…õtti seelli maa gbinni? Ay! - lala kur Ňčmanni la yan t…Ē'asiri n‚ÄĎtiri p…õsigu.

ref: But if I don't understand the language that someone is using, we will be like foreigners to each other.
text: Amaa s…Ē yi ti t…Ē'asi n‚ÄĎti ma ka m bu wum u y…õtt…Ē'a maa, u nin g…Ēsi ma, ni, tinzunnira, ka man mun g…Ēsiri u gba lala.

ref: If you really want spiritual gifts, choose the ones that will be most helpful to the church.
text: T…Ē: dizugu ya mansi Ňč…Ēn boori Sii maa piini la, ya kpaŇčŇčma n‚ÄĎnya an paasiri la Naawunni niriba beesigu, ka ya zaa ti kyaŇčŇči tooni.

ref: When we speak languages that others don't know, we should pray for the power to explain what we mean.
text: DinŇč…Ēna, ky…õ ka Ňč…Ēn t…Ē'asiri y…õtt…Ē'a balibu zaa la ti susiri Naawunni ni u ky…õ ka u toaai n‚ÄĎwulisiri di gbinni gba.

ref: For example, if I use an unknown language in my prayers, my spirit prays but my mind is useless.
text: Dama n yi t…Ē'asi y…õtt…Ē'a balibubalibu m‚ÄĎpuusi Naawunni, n sii maa puusiri ni, amaa n y…õm ka anfaani di puuni.

ref: Then what should I do? There are times when I should pray with my spirit, and times when I should pray with my mind. Sometimes I should sing with my spirit, and at other times I should sing with my mind.
text: DinŇč…Ēna - n ya niŇči la wula? N nin zaŇčŇči n sii maa m‚ÄĎpuusi Naawunni, ka laa n‚ÄĎzaŇčŇči n y…õm m‚ÄĎpuusi u; n nin zaŇčŇči n sii maa n‚ÄĎyuum yuuma n‚ÄĎti Naawunni, ka zaŇčŇči n y…õm n‚ÄĎyuumni m‚ÄĎpaasi.

ref: Suppose some strangers are in your worship service, when you are praising God with your spirit. If they don't understand you, how will they know to say, ‚ÄúAmen‚ÄĚ?
text: Bee, i yi puusiri Naawunni i sii puuni, wula ka ninvugs…Ē Ňč…Ēn b…õ b…õni nin niŇči m‚ÄĎbaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎniŇči ‚ÄúAmmi!‚ÄĚ n‚ÄĎti i puusigu maa? - dama Ňč…Ēn bu wumni nyin y…õtti seelli wa.

ref: You may be worshiping God in a wonderful way, but no one else will be helped.
text: Nyin Ňč…Ēn puusiri suŇčŇča maa zaa y…Ēri, di ku toaai m‚ÄĎpaasi i taraana beesigu.

ref: I thank God that I speak unknown languages more than any of you.
text: Halli man t…Ē'asiri y…õtt…Ē'a balibubalibu n‚ÄĎgaari ya zaa, ka m puusi Naawunni di zugu.

ref: But words that make sense can help the church. This is why in church I would rather speak five words that make sense than to speak
text: Ni di zaa y…Ēri, n yi b…õ la Naawunni niriba sunsukkuni, m boori ni n zaŇčŇči n y…õm n‚ÄĎt…Ē'asi beela ni n ti wulisi n taaba, ka di gaari n yi t…Ē'asi pam y…õtt…Ē'azuna ni ka ba bu wumna.

ref: My friends, stop thinking like children. Think like mature people and be as innocent as tiny babies.
text: T…Ē, m mabiisi: yan di teesiri hoo bibisi: y…õlibeeri ziiya ka ya siri nin b…õ ni hoo bileesi, amaa y…õm ziiya see ka ya nin b…õ ni hoo ninvugkura.

ref: In the Scriptures the Lord says, ‚ÄúI will use strangers who speak unknown languages to talk to my people. They will speak to them in foreign languages, but still my people won't listen to me.‚ÄĚ
text: Di s…Ēbiya, Naawunni zaaligu maa ni, ni#:- ‚ÄúN nin zaŇčŇči y…õtt…Ē'azuna Ni yan bu mi seeba maa y…õligu N‚ÄĎt…Ē'asi n‚ÄĎti butiri wa niriba, Ni di zaa ba ku wum n‚ÄĎti ma. - Lala ka ti Dugma y…õli.‚ÄĚ

ref: Languages others don't know may mean something to unbelievers, but not to the Lord's followers. Prophecy, on the other hand, is for followers, not for unbelievers.
text: DinŇč…Ēna y…õtt…Ē'azuna bulisiri y…õla n‚ÄĎtiri ban ka y…õdda la, pa ban mara; anabitiri y…õla mun bulisiri y…õla n‚ÄĎtiri ban mari y…õdda, pa ban ka.

ref: Suppose everyone in your worship service started speaking unknown languages, and some outsiders or some unbelievers come in. Won't they think you are crazy?
text: Ya bu nya? - Naawunni niriba yi la'am n‚ÄĎgyeemna, ka ba zaa t…Ē'asiri y…õtt…Ē'a balibubalibu, ka saamma bee ban ka y…õdda dima kp…õ na, ba ku y…õli, ni, ya kp…õ la yinyaari?

ref: But suppose all of you are prophesying when those unbelievers and outsiders come in. They will realize that they are sinners, and they will want to change their ways because of what you are saying.
text: Amaa ya zaa yi t…Ē'asiri anabitiri y…õla ka lala nira ti kp…õ na, ya zaa zugu ka u nin ti baŇčŇči u taalli Ňč…Ēn b…õ seem, ni, ba kari u sariya ka lusi u.

ref: They will tell what is hidden in their hearts. Then they will kneel down and say to God, ‚ÄúWe are certain that you are with these people.‚ÄĚ Worship Must Be Orderly
text: Di saha ka u nin y…õli u sufu y…õlis…Ē'ara an b…õ seem zaa, ka gbaani tiŇčŇča n‚ÄĎgyeem Naawunni ka y…õli, ni, ‚ÄúNaawunni siri b…õ ya saani.‚ÄĚ

ref: My friends, when you meet to worship, you must do everything for the good of everyone there. That's how it should be when someone sings or teaches or tells what God has said or speaks an unknown language or explains what the language means.
text: B…Ē n yi kpalim, m mabiisi? - Ya yi la'am taaba, s…Ēkam mari u yuumni, bee, u y…õliwulisira, bee, u buns…Ē'ara kasigri y…õla, bee, u t…Ē'asiri y…õtt…Ē'a balibu bee n‚ÄĎwulisiri lala y…õtt…Ē'a gbinni. T…Ē - ya kpaŇčŇčma ya mansi n‚ÄĎky…õ ka anŇč…Ē zaa nin paasi Naawunni niriba beesigu.

ref: No more than two or three of you should speak unknown languages during the meeting. You must take turns, and someone should always be there to explain what you mean.
text: Seeba yi ya t…Ē'asi y…õtt…Ē'a balibu y…õla, ky…õ ka di ny…õ la babayi-ata k…Ēk…Ēa, ka ba t…Ē'asi yinniyinni n‚ÄĎdeaai taaba; ka laa n‚ÄĎky…õ ka s…Ē b…õ ni n‚ÄĎwulisiri a gbinni Ňč…Ēn ny…õ seem.

ref: If no one can explain, you must keep silent in church and speak only to yourself and to God.
text: Amaa s…Ē yi ka ni n‚ÄĎwulisiri a gbinni, ky…õ ka y…õtt…Ē'a balibu daana sini Naawunni niriba la'aŇčŇču ni, ka naayi t…Ē'asi n‚ÄĎtiri u maŇčŇča ni Naawunni.

ref: Two or three persons may prophesy, and everyone else must listen carefully.
text: Anabidima maa muna - ya ky…õ ka ba gba ti t…Ē'asi ba niriba ayi-ata, ka ban kpalim la kpasi ba y…õtt…Ē'a maa.

ref: If someone sitting there receives a message from God, the speaker must stop and let the other person speak.
text: Ka y…õtt…Ē'a yi ky…õ s…Ē Ňč…Ēn ziya saani na, ky…õ ka Ňč…Ēn pun t…Ē'asiri la ti t…Ē'asi n‚ÄĎnaai p…Ēi, ka Ňč…Ēnla naayi deaai y…õtt…Ē'a.

ref: Let only one person speak at a time, then all of you will learn something and be encouraged.
text: Dama ya zaa nin toaai n‚ÄĎt…Ē'asi ya anabitiri y…õla n‚ÄĎdeaari taaba yinniyinni, ka s…Ēkam bamsi ka di kpamsi ba.

ref: A prophet should be willing to stop and let someone else speak.
text: Anabidima maa n su ba mammaŇčŇča anabitiri y…õla fukumsi,

ref: God wants everything to be done peacefully and in order. When God's people meet in church,
text: dama Naawunni pa la Ňč…Ēn burimna, amaa Ňč…Ēn maanna. See ka ya d…Ēli ban la'amni n‚ÄĎgyeemni Naawunni la zaa kaari,

ref: the women must not be allowed to speak. They must keep quiet and listen, as the Law of Moses teaches.
text: ka ky…õ ka p…Ē'aba kpalim m‚ÄĎb…õ surimm ya la'aŇčŇču maa ni. Ba ka soori ni ba t…Ē'asira, see ka ba d…Ēli soori hoo zaaligu maa Ňč…Ēn y…õli seem la.

ref: If there is something they want to know, they can ask their husbands when they get home. It is disgraceful for women to speak in church.
text: P…Ē'a yi boori ni u baŇčŇči y…õtt…Ē'aseaa gbinni, see ka ba naayi kuli yiri ni ka u ti boosi u sira. P…Ē'a yi ti t…Ē'asiri Naawunni niriba la'aŇčŇču ni, di mari la vi.

ref: God's message did not start with you people, and you are not the only ones it has reached.
text: Wula! Naawunni y…õligu daa piili yarima saani, bee? Bee, ya k…Ēk…Ē saani ka di paai?

ref: If you think of yourself as a prophet or a spiritual person, you will know I am writing only what the Lord has commanded.
text: S…Ē yi y…õli, ni, u ny…õ la anabi, bee, Ňč…Ēn mi Sii maa y…õla, di daama nin sakki, ni, man Ňč…Ēn s…Ēbri seelli n‚ÄĎtiri ya wa ny…õ la ti Dugma zaaligu.

ref: So don't pay attention to anyone who ignores what I am writing.
text: S…Ē mun yi bu wumni n‚ÄĎtiri y…õligu wa, yan di wumni n‚ÄĎtiri di daama gba.

ref: My friends, be eager to prophesy and don't stop anyone from speaking languages that others don't know.
text: T…Ē, ka dinŇč…Ēna, m mabiisi, ya boom suŇčŇča anabitiri y…õla, amaa ka di ta'ari y…õtt…Ē'a balibubalibu y…õla.

ref: But do everything properly and in order.
text: See ka bunseelikam niŇči suŇčŇča ka d…Ēli soori deede.

ref: My friends, I want you to remember the message I preached and that you believed and trusted.
text: T…Ē, m mabiisi, n ya teaai ya la man Ňč…Ēn daa mooli lahabasuŇčŇču maa n‚ÄĎti ya seem. Lahabaari wa ka ya daa deaai, ka lahabaari maa zugu ka ya kpalim n‚ÄĎz…õ yummu.

ref: You will be saved by this message, if you hold firmly to it. But if you don't, your faith was all for nothing.
text: Halli lala lahabasuŇčŇču zugu ka ya nya tuliginsim, ya yi kpalim n‚ÄĎd…Ēla wulisiseaa man daa mooli n‚ÄĎti ya la suŇčŇča. Di yi pa lala, ya y…õdda b…õ la y…Ēri.

ref: I told you the most important part of the message exactly as it was told to me. This part is: Christ died for our sins, as the Scriptures say.
text: Lahabaari wa ka n daa deaai seeba saani ka laa n‚ÄĎzaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎti ya, ka di y…õtt…Ē'akpanni nŇč…Ēa: ni, Kirisita Masiiya daa kpi ti tumbeeri zugu, hoo Litaafi maa Ňč…Ēn daa pun wulisi seem.

ref: He was buried, and three days later he was raised to life, as the Scriptures say.
text: Ka di laa n‚ÄĎwulisi, ni, ba daa s…Ē'ai u, ni, dabsa ata daari ka u daa neaai kum ni, hoo Litaafi maa Ňč…Ēn daa pun wulisi seem.

ref: After this, he appeared to more than five hundred other followers. Most of them are still alive, but some have died.
text: Di nyaaŇčŇča ka u daa ky…õ ka ti mabiisi b…Ēbgu daa nya u, ka ba daa zoaai n‚ÄĎgaari ninvugk…Ēbsinu. Niriba wa pam na b…õ ba ny…Ēvuya ni, amaa baseeba laa n‚ÄĎka ni.

ref: He also appeared to James, then to all of the apostles.
text: Ka u daa laa n‚ÄĎky…õ ka Gyeemsi nya u, ka u tuumudima zaa gba daa nya u.

ref: Finally, he appeared to me, even though I am like someone who was born at the wrong time.
text: AnŇč…Ē zaa nyaaŇčŇča, u daa naayi ky…õ ka n gba nya u, hoo ban gbirigi n‚ÄĎd…Ē'ai s…Ēa.

ref: I am the least important of all the apostles. In fact, I caused so much trouble for God's church that I don't even deserve to be called an apostle.
text: Dama mani n kpalim tuumudima maa zaa nyaaŇčŇča, halli m bu sum ni ba booli ma Naawunni tuumu gba, dama n daa katti Naawunni niriba n‚ÄĎnamsiri ba.

ref: But God treated me with undeserved grace! He made me what I am, and his grace wasn't wasted. I worked much harder than any of the other apostles, although it was really God's grace at work and not me.
text: Amaa Naawunni piini zugu ky…õ ka m b…õ la man b…õ seem; ka lala piini maa bu basi y…Ēri, dama n daa tum tuma n‚ÄĎgaari ba zaa - halli di pa la mani n‚ÄĎtumna, amaa Naawunni piini Ňč…Ēn mari ma n‚ÄĎtumna.

ref: But it doesn't matter if I preached or if they preached. All of you believed the message just the same. God's People Will Be Raised to Life

text: Dama ti bu boori ni ya kpalim n‚ÄĎzi ti y…õla, ti mabiisi, tin Ňč…Ēn daa di wahala ∆źseaa tingbaŇčŇču wa ni seem: ti siri daa namsi ni halli nti yakki, ka ti daa k…ĒŇčŇči tama zaa zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči ti beebu gba.

ref: In fact, we felt sure we were going to die. But this made us stop trusting in ourselves and start trusting God, who raises the dead to life.
text: Ti daa teesiya, ni, kum k…Ēk…Ē n kpalim ti. Amaa dinŇč…Ē daa niŇči ti, ni di ti wulisi ti, ni, ti di niŇčni ti mammansi y…õdda, amaa Naawunni Ňč…Ēn toaai n‚ÄĎneaari ban kpi la.

text: U daa fa ti m‚ÄĎbasi y…õlibeeri anŇč…Ē taabu ni, ka u nin laa n‚ÄĎfa ti m‚ÄĎbasi yaasa. Halli ti niŇči tama, ni, u nin laa n‚ÄĎyiisi ti y…õlibeeri ni,

ref: Please help us by praying for us. Then many people will give thanks for the blessings we receive in answer to all these prayers. Paul's Change of Plans
text: yan Ňč…Ēn suŇčŇčni ti ya wunsusigu zugu la. Lala nin ky…õ ka niriba pam ti puusi Naawunni tirima zugu, dama ba nin nya Naawunni Ňč…Ēn wum niriba pam wunsusigu seem la ka suŇčŇči ti di zugu.

ref: We can be proud of our clear conscience. We have always lived honestly and sincerely, especially when we were with you. And we were guided by God's gift of undeserved grace instead of by the wisdom of this world.
text: T…Ē: ka dinŇč…Ēna tin nyuuri bara y…õtt…Ē'aseaa zugu la ny…õ la: ni, ti sufu ni bamsim wulisiri ti, ni, ti b…õ la beesisuŇčŇču duniya wa ni. Ni, di pa la duniya wa yamg…ĒliŇčŇču soori ka ti d…Ēla, amaa s…ĒsuŇčŇču ka ti d…Ēli n‚ÄĎtiri Naawunni kasikasi. Naawunni Ňč…Ēn daa suŇčŇči ti seem la, lala ka ti b…õ ni, halli yarima saani ka ti beesigu siri daa pun b…õ lala.

ref: I am not writing anything you cannot read and understand. I hope you will understand it completely,
text: Ti bu s…Ēbri bunseelli yan ku toaai n‚ÄĎkarim ka baŇčŇči di gbinni. Ka m mari tama, ni, ya nin baŇčŇči di zaa halli ni di t…Ēriga,

text: dama ya pun baŇčŇči ti y…õliseesi gbinni. Ka lala nin ky…õ ka ya nin toaai n‚ÄĎnyu bara tirima zugu, hoo tin ya n‚ÄĎnyu bara yarima zugu seem wa, ti Dugma Yisa dabsiri daari maa.

ref: I was so sure of your pride in us that I had planned to visit you first of all. In this way you would have the blessing of two visits from me‚ÄĒ

text: N daa y…õli, ni, n ya n‚ÄĎd…Ēli ya saani na n‚ÄĎgaari n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči Masidoonya tingbaŇčŇču ni, ka laa n‚ÄĎyi b…õni n‚ÄĎlabi ya saani na yaasa, ka ya zaali ma soori ka n kyaŇčŇči Gyuudiiya.

ref: Do you think I couldn't make up my mind about what to do? Or do I seem like someone who says ‚ÄúYes‚ÄĚ or ‚ÄúNo‚ÄĚ simply to please others?

ref: God can be trusted, and so can I, when I say our answer to you has always been ‚ÄúYes‚ÄĚ and never ‚ÄúNo.‚ÄĚ
text: Ay! - Naawunni Ňč…Ēn ny…õ y…õdda daana zugu ka ti na zi n‚ÄĎy…õli ya, ‚ÄėIi!‚Äô ka labi n‚ÄĎy…õli, ‚ÄėAyi!‚Äô

text: Dama Naawunni Bii Yisa Kirisita y…õla ka ti daa mooli n‚ÄĎti ya - man ni Sailivanisi ni Tim…Ēti - ka Ňč…Ēn mun Yisa pa la ‚ÄėIi!‚Äô ni ‚ÄėAy!‚Äô daana, amaa ka u seelikam kur sakki, ni, ‚ÄėIi!‚Äô

ref: Christ says ‚ÄúYes‚ÄĚ to all God's promises. This is why we have Christ to say ‚ÄúAmen‚ÄĚ for us to the glory of God.
