text: Dama buna wa ka ba daa maali ni a ti Ňčmani arizana ni buna la. Ka di daa tu ni ba mari a m‚ÄĎmaanni a zugu, ni ba ti ky…õ ka a ny…õ kasi. Ka dinŇč…Ēna arizana buna mammansi boori la maaligu kan gaari anŇč…Ēa.

ref: This is why Christ did not go into a tent made by humans and was only a copy of the real one. Instead, he went into heaven and is now there with God to help us.
text: Dama Yisa Ňč…Ēn daa kp…õ gyeeŇčŇču ziiya halli n‚ÄĎti paai gyeeŇčŇču nimbirimoaa zii maa, ka daa pa la ninsaalidima Ňč…Ēn maali ya'aseaa, ka laa m‚ÄĎpa ban g…Ēsi bunseesi an ny…õ la bummaŇča n‚ÄĎzaŇčŇči ba nuusi m‚ÄĎmaali buna an nin ti Ňčmani lala. Ninsaala Ňč…Ēn maali bunseaa la n kur wulisiri bummaŇčni Ňč…Ēn ny…õ seem. Ay! - u daa kp…õ la arizana ni, ni u zaŇčŇči u maŇčŇča n‚ÄĎwulisi Naawunni ti zugu.

ref: Christ did not have to offer himself many times. He wasn't like a high priest who goes into the most holy place each year to offer the blood of an animal.
text: Di mun pa hoo GyeeŇčŇču Toondaana Ňč…Ēn tumni seem la. Dama u kpeeri n‚ÄĎkpeeri Naawunni Beebu Ziiya yuumni kam, ka kur mari ziim bun pa u toori ziim. Ay! Ňč…Ēn Yisa bu kpeeri arizana ni n‚ÄĎzaŇčŇčni u maŇčŇča n‚ÄĎyiisiri sara sahakam.

ref: If he had offered himself every year, he would have suffered many times since the creation of the world. But instead, near the end of time he offered himself once and for all, so he could be a sacrifice that does away with sin.
text: Dama di yi daa ny…õ lala, u daa naa m‚ÄĎb…õ ni n‚ÄĎkpiiri n‚ÄĎkpiira, duniya piiligu ni zaŇčŇči m‚ÄĎpaai zuna. Amaa din ny…õ seem pampaŇčŇč…Ēa, u ky…õ na yimmu, sahanseaa duniya wa naari Ňč…Ēn p…Ētti na la: u ky…õ na ni u zaŇčŇči u maŇčŇča m‚ÄĎmaali maaliseaa kan nin ky…õ ka Naawunni di sugru n‚ÄĎti ti, ti tumbeeri zugu.

ref: We die only once, and then we are judged.
text: Ninsaala Ňč…Ēn kpiiri yummu ka sariya karibu d…Ēlisiri seem la,

ref: So Christ died only once to take away the sins of many people. But when he comes again, it will not be to take away sin. He will come to save everyone who is waiting for him.
text: lala ka Yisa zaŇčŇči u maŇčŇča m‚ÄĎmaali yummu maa. Ka u nin ti labi na yaasa: ka di pa la u kari tumbeeri sariya zugu, amaa ni u tiligi seeba ban ziya n‚ÄĎguusiri u ni nimbirimoaa la.

ref: The Law of Moses is like a shadow of the good things to come. This shadow isn't the good things themselves, because it cannot free people from sin by the sacrifices offered year after year.
text: Izra…õli dima zaaligu maa daa b…õ la hoo Ňčmansigu, ka teaari ti y…õlisuma an ky…õnni na maa. Ka pa la bunsuma la mammansi. Dama yuumni kam ka ba maanni sara m‚ÄĎmaanni sara, amaa ba ya n‚ÄĎniŇči la wula n‚ÄĎsuŇčŇči ninvugseeba ban ky…õnni Naawunni saani na maa? Ay! - lala ku toaai n‚ÄĎky…õ ka ba leebi hoo din sumsi ni ba b…õ seem deede.

ref: If there were worshipers who already have their sins washed away and their consciences made clear, there would not be any need to go on offering sacrifices.
text: Ya bu nya? - di yi pa lala, ba naa n‚ÄĎku laa m‚ÄĎmaanni a, dama ba sufu da naa n‚ÄĎku laa n‚ÄĎgalim ba tumbeeri zugu. Ba ala'akyi taalli zaa yi siri daa kpiisi m‚ÄĎbasi, maaligu balim kam zaa naa n‚ÄĎku b…õ ni.

ref: But the blood of bulls and goats cannot take away sins. It only reminds people of their sins from one year to the next.
text: Dama nigi ni buusi ziim ku toaai n‚ÄĎti ti tumbeeri taalli ky…õ-m‚ÄĎp…õŇčŇči.

ref: When Christ came into the world, he said to God, ‚ÄúSacrifices and offerings are not what you want, but you have given me my body.
text: DinŇč…Ē zugu, Yisa Ňč…Ēn daa ya n‚ÄĎky…õ duniya wa ni na maa, u daa y…õli, ni#:- ‚ÄúNyin, Naawunni, bu boori sara maaligu, I bu laa m‚ÄĎboori koom tisibu: See ninsaala ningbina ka i maali n‚ÄĎti ma:

ref: No, you are not pleased with animal sacrifices and offerings for sin.‚ÄĚ
text: Pa bunk…Ēbri ka ba maali ka bugum di a, Halli sara maaligu ala'akyi zugu gba: - AnŇč…Ē zaasa zugu i nini bu tigra.

ref: Then Christ said, ‚ÄúAnd so, my God, I have come to do what you want, as the Scriptures say.‚ÄĚ
text: Di saha ka n y…õli#:- ‚ÄėMani nŇč…Ēa - ni n niŇčni nyin boori seem: Ii, mani nŇč…Ē, m Ba Naawunni - Hoo n y…õla pun s…Ēbi n‚ÄĎdooli seem la, Litaafi gbaŇčŇču maa puuni ka a s…Ēbi.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ

ref: The Law teaches that offerings and sacrifices must be made because of sin. But why did Christ mention these things and say that God did not want them?
text: T…Ē: u daŇčŇči n‚ÄĎy…õli, ni, ‚Äėpa bunk…Ēbri ka ba maali ka bugum di a‚Äô, ni, ‚Äėhalli sara maaligu ala'akyi zugu gba‚Äô, ni, ‚ÄėanŇč…Ē zaasa zugu‚Äô ka Naawunni ‚Äėnini bu tigra‚Äô. Amaa anŇč…Ē taabu zaa d…Ēli la zaalikurigu soori.

ref: Well, it was to do away with offerings and sacrifices and to replace them. This is what he meant by saying to God, ‚ÄúI have come to do what you want.‚ÄĚ
text: Ka u naayi y…õli, ni, ‚ÄėMani nŇč…Ēa - ni n niŇčni nyin boori seem, m Ba Naawunni.‚Äô Ka di gbinni ny…õ la: ni, u basi an daŇčŇči tooni la ka zaŇčŇči bumpaala an pa nyaaŇčŇča n‚ÄĎzaali a zaaziiya.

ref: The priests do their work each day, and they keep on offering sacrifices that can never take away sins.
text: T…Ē; ka dinŇč…Ēna beoo kam zaa ka maalimaanniba kur z…õ la ba tuma tumpu ni, m‚ÄĎmaanni ba sahakam bunk…Ēbmaaligu, amaa lala maaligu tuma zaa ku toaai n‚ÄĎtaasi tumbeeri ky…õ-m‚ÄĎp…õŇčŇči na.

ref: But Christ offered himself as a sacrifice that is good forever. Now he is sitting at God's right side,
text: Amaa Yisa Kirisita Ňč…Ēna, u daa maali maaligu yinni k…Ēk…Ē tumbeeri zaa taalli zugu: lala maaligu maa mari yiiko saha kam. U daa maali n‚ÄĎnaai p…Ēi, ka naayi labi n‚ÄĎzinni Naawunni nudirigu ziziiya.

ref: and he will stay there until his enemies are put under his power.
text: B…õni ka u kpalim n‚ÄĎziya, halli ni Naawunni ti zaŇčŇči u dindaandima zaa n‚ÄĎdooli tiŇčŇča ka u noora.

ref: By his one sacrifice he has forever set free from sin the people he brings to God.
text: Y…õlimaŇčni, u daa maali maaligu yinni ka tiligi seeba ban b…õ s…ĒsuŇčŇču ni, ni ba ti paai Naawunni saani.

ref: The Holy Spirit also speaks of this by telling us that the Lord said,
text: Naawunni Vuusim Kasi la gba wulisiri ti lala. U daŇčŇči n‚ÄĎy…õli, ni#:-

ref: Then I will forget about their sins and no longer remember their evil deeds.‚ÄĚ
text: Ni#:- ‚ÄúN ku laa n‚ÄĎteaai ba tumbeeri y…õla, Bee, ba ala'akyi taalli yaasa.‚ÄĚ

ref: When sins are forgiven, there is no more need to offer sacrifices. Encouragement and Warning
text: T…Ē! dinŇč…Ēna anŇč…Ē zaa ka Naawunni tam m‚ÄĎbasi, ka dizugu s…Ēs…Ē bu laa n‚ÄĎsum ni ba maali bunk…Ēbmaaligu u tumbeeri taalli zugu.

ref: My friends, the blood of Jesus gives us courage to enter the most holy place
text: DinŇč…Ē zugu, m mabiisi, ti mari soori ni ti kp…õ Dukasi maa, ya'aseela din kyisiri da'ari zaasa; n‚ÄĎkp…õ ka vi ka ti ni, Yisa kum wa zugu.

ref: We have a great high priest who is in charge of God's house.
text: Yaasa m‚ÄĎpaasi, ti mari la GyeeŇčŇču Toondaana, ka u su Naawunni Yiri maa y…õla.

ref: So let's come near God with pure hearts and a confidence that comes from having faith. Let's keep our hearts pure, our consciences free from evil, and our bodies washed with clean water.
text: Dizugu, ya ky…õ ka ti p…Ēri na n‚ÄĎkp…õ Naawunni saani. Ti yi ya kp…õa, see ka ti sufu z…õ yummu, ka ti niŇči y…õdda ni y…õlimaŇčni. Ya ky…õ ka ti sufu ny…õ sufuveela an bu galimni ti, dama ba pun zaŇčŇči k…ĒsuŇčŇču n‚ÄĎsu ti ningbina zaa, ka di Ňčmani la hoo ban zaŇčŇči ziim n‚ÄĎniŇči ti, kaman zaalikurigu da'ari peaari soori Ňč…Ēn daa ny…õ seem la.

ref: We must hold tightly to the hope we say is ours. After all, we can trust the one who made the agreement with us.
text: Ya ky…õ ka ti mari tama wa ni nimbirimoaa, dama ti nin toaai n‚ÄĎniŇči Naawunni ni u nangbamp…Ēri la y…õdda.

ref: We should keep on encouraging each other to be thoughtful and to do helpful things.
text: Ya ky…õ ka ti sufu b…õ taaba y…õla ni, n‚ÄĎsuŇčŇčni taaba ni ti zaa kyaŇčŇči tooni n‚ÄĎwuri niriba ka tumni tunsuma.

ref: Some people have given up the habit of meeting for worship, but we must not do that. We should keep on encouraging each other, especially since you know that the day of the Lord's coming is getting closer.
text: Yan di za'asi taaba la'aŇčŇču, hoo seeba Ňč…Ēn za'asiri seem la. Amaa ya kpamsiri taaba sufu. Ka niŇčni anŇč…Ē zaa ni nimbirimoaa, dama ya nya ka ti Dugma dabsiri ky…õnni na saha bu ya n‚ÄĎyuui.

ref: No sacrifices can be made for people who decide to sin after they find out about the truth.
text: T…Ē, ka dizugu ya bu nya? Ti yi ti mi y…õlimaŇčni seaa ban pun wulisi ti p…Ēi, ka laa n‚ÄĎkpalim n‚ÄĎtumni tumbeeri, dinŇč…Ēna bunk…Ēbmaaligu zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči ala'akyi laa n‚ÄĎka ni n‚ÄĎti ti.

ref: They are God's enemies, and all they can look forward to is a terrible judgment and a furious fire.
text: Ay! - Din kpalim ny…õ la dabeem zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči sariya karibu din ky…õnni na, ni bugum seelli bun nin di baseeba ban kyiisiri Naawunni y…õla.

ref: If two or more witnesses accused someone of breaking the Law of Moses, that person could be put to death.
text: Ninvugs…Ē Ňč…Ēn daa za'asi Musa zaaligu y…õla, ka niriba ayi- ata di s…õhara, ni, ba wum lala - t…Ē, lala nira daa yi kpi ni di zugu, ka ba daa bu z…Ē u namboo halli beela.

ref: But it is much worse to dishonor God's Son and to disgrace the blood of the promise that made us holy. And it is just as bad to insult the Holy Spirit, who shows us mercy.
text: T…Ē, ka ninvugs…Ē yi ti za'asi Naawunni Biiya, b…Ē n ya n‚ÄĎniŇči Ňč…Ēna? U tu Ňč…Ēn daa kpi u zugu, ka y…õnni ni, u Tiligra ziim pa seela - halli ni lala ziim maa ky…õ ka Naawunni basi nangbanyinni nangbambasiri n‚ÄĎti u gba: ka laa n‚ÄĎzeem Naawunni SisuŇčŇču.

ref: We know that God has said he will punish and take revenge. We also know that the Scriptures say the Lord will judge his people.
text: Dama ti mi Naawunni y…õla, ni, u y…õliya#:- ‚ÄúMani n su labsigu, N nin labsi n‚ÄĎy…Ēa.‚ÄĚ Ka y…õli m‚ÄĎpaasi, ni#:- ‚ÄúTi Dugma nin ti kari u niriba sariya.‚ÄĚ

ref: It is a terrible thing to fall into the hands of the living God!
text: Di siri mari dabeem pam ni Naawunni Ny…Ēvuri Daana la ti kari i sariya n‚ÄĎlusi i!

ref: Sometimes you were abused and mistreated in public, and at other times you shared in the sufferings of others.
text: Lala saha maa ka ba daa yi namsiri ya ka zeemni ya niriba tooni; bee, ya daa yi ti sakki n‚ÄĎzaŇčŇči ya mansi n‚ÄĎz…õ la ya taaba nyaaŇčŇča, ban pun di namsi anŇč…Ē taabu sahanseaa la.

ref: You were kind to people in jail. And you gladly let your possessions be taken away, because you knew you had something better, something that would last forever.
text: Ya daa teesi dansarika dima y…õla n‚ÄĎsuŇčŇči ba; halli niriba ban daa fa ya marigu zaa gba, di daa bu niŇči ya sufusa'aŇčŇču; dama ya daa mi, ni, ya mari seelli din gaari lala ka mun ka naari.

ref: Keep on being brave! It will bring you great rewards.
text: Dizugu yan di ky…õ ya sufukpeemni m‚ÄĎbasi, dama sufukpeemni nin ti ti ya yoori pam.

ref: Learn to be patient, so you will please God and be given what he has promised.
text: See ka ya mari sugru. Lala nin ky…õ ka ya nin toaai n‚ÄĎtum Naawunni Ňč…Ēn boori seem ka naayi deaai bunsunseesi Ňä…Ēn daa basi noori, ni, u nin ti ti seem maa.

ref: As the Scriptures say, ‚ÄúGod is coming soon! It won't be very long.
text: Dama di s…Ēbi n‚ÄĎdooli, ni#:- ‚ÄúDi kpalim la beela, Ni Ňč…Ēn ky…õnni na maa ti ky…õ na; U bu ya n‚ÄĎyuui.‚ÄĚ

ref: The people God accepts will live because of their faith. But he isn't pleased with anyone who turns back.‚ÄĚ
text: Ni, ‚ÄúNinvugs…Ē Ňč…Ēn ka galiŇčŇču n saani, U niŇčni ma y…õdda, Ka dizugu u nin nya ny…Ēvuri. Amaa, u yi ti labi nyaaŇčŇča, N sufu ku maai di daama zugu.‚ÄĚ

ref: We are not like those people who turn back and get destroyed. We will keep on having faith until we are saved.
text: Tirima mun pa la ninvugseeba ban labri nyaaŇčŇča nti baasi y…Ēri, amaa ban niŇčni y…õdda nti nya tiliginsim.

ref: It was their faith that made our ancestors pleasing to God.
text: Y…õdda wa taabu zugu ka ti yaadima y…õlisuma yi paalu#:-

ref: Because of our faith, we know that the world was made at God's command. We also know that what can be seen was made out of what cannot be seen.
text: Y…õdda zugu ka ti baŇčŇči, ni, Naawunni y…õligu n daa ky…õ ka duniya naam, ka tin nya seesi wa zaa an daa ti b…õ ni maa, bunseesi an bu nyara saani ka a daa yi na.

ref: Because Abel had faith, he offered God a better sacrifice than Cain did. God was pleased with him and his gift, and even though Abel is now dead, his faith still speaks for him.
text: Y…õdda zugu ka Eebli daa yiisi sara n‚ÄĎti Naawunni, ka ka anfaani gaari u beeri Kyeen sara. Ka lala daa wulisi, ni, u daa ny…õ y…õlimaŇčni daana, dama Naawunni daa deaai u piini. Eebli daa kpiya, amaa y…õdda zugu ka u kpalim n‚ÄĎt…Ē'asiri ni.

ref: Enoch had faith and did not die. He pleased God, and God took him up to heaven. This is why his body was never found.
text: Y…õdda zugu ka kum daa bu paai In…Ēkyi, dama ‚ÄúNaawunni n daa zaŇčŇči u, ka ba daa bu laa n‚ÄĎnya u, duniya wa ni.‚ÄĚ Niriba daa pun daŇčŇči n‚ÄĎt…Ē'asi u y…õlisuma, ni, Ňč…Ēn daa ‚Äúmi Naawunni suŇčŇčasuŇčŇča, ka d…Ēli u yuubu zaa‚ÄĚ, ka Naawunni na bu zaŇčŇči u.

ref: But without faith no one can please God. We must believe that God is real and rewards everyone who searches for him.
text: T…Ē: y…õdda yi ka ni, s…Ē ku toaai m‚ÄĎmi Naawunni n‚ÄĎd…Ēli u yuubu; di yi pa la nira Ňč…Ēn ky…õ Naawunni saani na niŇči y…õdda, ni, Naawunni b…õ ni, ka laa n‚ÄĎniŇči y…õdda, ni, Ňč…Ēn Naawunni wumni n‚ÄĎtiri ban boori u ziiya.

ref: Because Noah had faith, he was warned about something that had not yet happened. He obeyed and built a boat that saved him and his family. In this way the people of the world were judged, and Noah was given the blessings that come to everyone who pleases God.
text: Y…õdda zugu ka Nuhu daa sakki Naawunni kpaaŇčŇču halli u nini daa na bu nya koom maa gba, ka daa kpaasi nyariŇčŇču maa ka ka daa ti tiligi Ňč…Ēn ni u yiri dima maa. Ka di zugu ba daa kari ninvugseeba ban daa bu niŇči y…õdda lala maa sariya y…õtt…Ē'a n‚ÄĎlusi ba, ba y…õdda kaalinsi zugu. Ka Ňč…Ēn Nuhu daa naayi di faari kan ny…õ la y…õlimaŇčni yuuri kan yi y…õdda niŇčbu ni na.

ref: Abraham had faith and obeyed God. He was told to go to the land that God had said would be his, and he left for a country he had never seen.
text: Y…õdda zugu ka Ibrahim daa sakki, sahaseaa Naawunni Ňč…Ēn daa booli u n‚ÄĎwulisi tingbanseaa Ňč…Ēn ya n‚ÄĎzaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎti u, ka ka ti leebi u faari la. U daa sakki, ka daa doaai n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči, ka dinŇč…Ē zaa daa niŇčni maa ka Ibrahim na bu mi Ňč…Ēn wa luginseaa maa.

ref: Because Abraham had faith, he lived as a stranger in the promised land. He lived there in a tent, and so did Isaac and Jacob, who were later given the same promise.
text: Y…õdda zugu yaasa ka u daa g…Ē n‚ÄĎg…Ē u faari tingbaŇčŇču maa puuni, hoo saana bee s…Ēky…õnna: u daa gbeeri la suga ni, ka Yisahaaku ni Yaakubu gba daa goori n‚ÄĎgbeeri lala, halli ni Naawunni daa la'am ba Ibrahim saani m‚ÄĎbasi nangbambasiri, ni, u nin ti ba faari maa.

ref: Abraham did this, because he was waiting for the eternal city God had planned and built.
text: Ňä…Ēn Ibrahim ba'a zaa daa b…õ la tinseaa kan mari nyeebsuma ni, ka Naawunni su ka fukumsi ka laa n‚ÄĎny…õ Ňč…Ēn meeri ka.

ref: Her husband Abraham was almost dead, but he became the ancestor of many people. In fact, there are as many of them as there are stars in the sky or grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Lala zugu ka doo yinni wa, Ňč…Ēn daa y…õtti, ni, u kpiiri ni, ka u d…Ē'am daa zoaai hoo saŇčmariwuusi Ňč…Ēn zoaai saazugu seem maa, bee, hoo teekuduuri m…Ē'abisigu Ňč…Ēn zoaari seem, nti k…ĒŇčŇči kaaligu.

ref: Every one of those people died. But they still had faith, even though they had not received what they had been promised. They were glad just to see these things from far away, and they agreed that they were only strangers and foreigners on this earth.
text: T…Ē: banŇč…Ē zaa daa niŇči la y…õdda, halli ni ba kum. Ba daa bu deaai Naawunni Ňč…Ēn daa basi noori, ni, u nin ti ba seelli la; ba daa kur ny…õ a ni ka a b…õ la wa'aligu, amaa ba sufu daa gyaari a. Ba daa sakkiya, ni, ba na bu su tingbaŇčŇču maa, amaa ba ny…õ la saamma ni s…Ēky…õnniba duniya wa puuni.

ref: When people talk this way, it is clear they are looking for a place to call their own.
text: Niriba ban t…Ē'asiri lala maa wulisiya, ni, ba boori la ba toori tiŇčŇča.

ref: If they had been talking about the land where they had once lived, they could have gone back at any time.
text: Ya bu nya? - ba yi daa kpalim n‚ÄĎt…Ē'asiri ban daa yi tingbanseaa ni na la y…õla, ba daa naa n‚ÄĎnya soori n‚ÄĎlabi b…õni.

ref: But they were looking forward to a better home in heaven. This is why God wasn't ashamed for them to call him their God. He even built a city for them.
text: Ay! - ba daa boori la tinseaa kan s…Ēa: saazugu tiŇčŇča nla. Dizugu ka Naawunni bu nya vi ni ba ti boonni u, ni, ba Naawunni; dama u daa maanni tiŇčŇča siri n‚ÄĎti ba.

ref: Abraham had been promised that Isaac, his only son, would continue his family. But when Abraham was tested, he had faith and was willing to sacrifice Isaac,
text: halli ni Ňč…Ēn Yisahaaku saani ka Naawunni daa p…Ē noori ni, u nyaaŇčŇča ni u butiri zaa nin ti yi na.

ref: because he was sure that God could raise people to life. This was just like getting Isaac back from death.
text: Ibrahim mun daa y…õnni, ni, Naawunni nin toaai n‚ÄĎneaai kpiima kum ni ka ba vu, ka di siri daa Ňčmanni la hoo Yisahaaku daa neaai kum ni n‚ÄĎlabi vuya ni, ka Ibrahim daa deaai u lala.

ref: Later, when Jacob was about to die, he leaned on his walking stick and worshiped. Then because of his faith he blessed each of Joseph's sons.
text: Y…õdda zugu ka Ňč…Ēn Yaakubu gba daa ka n‚ÄĎniŇči Yisifu biisi la, Ňč…Ēn daa ya n‚ÄĎkpi sahanseaa la, ka daa danni u zangbeaa zugu ka gyeemni Naawunni.

ref: And right before Joseph died, he had faith that God would lead the people of Israel out of Egypt. So he told them to take his bones with them.
text: Y…õdda zugu ka Yisifu kum saha Ňč…Ēn daa ti p…Ētta, u daa t…Ē'asi Izra…õli dima maa Ňč…Ēn nin ti yi Igyipti tingbaŇčŇču ni seem, ka daa laa n‚ÄĎwulisi ba ban nin ti zaŇčŇči u k…Ēba n‚ÄĎniŇči a seem.

ref: Because Moses' parents had faith, they kept him hidden until he was three months old. They saw that he was a beautiful child, and they were not afraid to disobey the king's orders.
text: Y…õdda zugu ka Musa ba ni u ma Ňč…Ēn daa d…Ē'ai u maa, ba daa nya ka u veela pam ka daa s…Ē'ai u ka di paai g…Ēya ata, ba mun daa bu z…Ēri Naaba zaaligu maa.

ref: Then after Moses grew up, his faith made him refuse to be called the king's grandson.
text: Y…õdda zugu ka Ňč…Ēn Musa, Ňč…Ēn daa ti sa'ai nira maa, daa bu sakki, ni, ba boonni u, ni, NaaFirawuna bipuŇčŇča biiya.

ref: He chose to be mistreated with God's people instead of having the good time that sin could bring for a little while.
text: U daa sakkiya, ni, u ti la'am n‚ÄĎdi namsi ni Naawunni niriba la, ka za'asi ala'akyi Ňč…Ēn ky…õ ka beebu nya'asa seem la, halli saha beela.

ref: Moses knew that the treasures of Egypt were not as wonderful as what he would receive from suffering for the Messiah, and he looked forward to his reward.
text: Masiiya zugu ka u daa sakki, ni, u yi di vi, di s…Ēa: u daa ky…õ Igyipti bunsuma, dama u daa g…Ēsiri la u dahinseelli yoori.

ref: Because of his faith, Moses left Egypt. Moses had seen the invisible God and wasn't afraid of the king's anger.
text: Y…õdda daa laa n‚ÄĎky…õ ka u daa bu z…Ē NaaFirawuna sufuyisigri ka daa bakki ka ky…õ Igyipti tingbaŇčŇču. Dama u daa zaŇčŇči u nini n‚ÄĎkpa n‚ÄĎg…Ēsi Naawunni, halli ni Naawunni Ňč…Ēn bu nyara gba.

ref: His faith also made him celebrate Passover. He sprinkled the blood of animals on the doorposts, so that the first-born sons of the people of Israel would not be killed by the destroying angel.
text: Y…õdda zugu muna ka u daa ky…õ ka ba di Yakki-n‚ÄĎGaari Kyuu la ka daa miisi ziim n‚ÄĎniŇči maa, ni Ňč…Ēn kuuri Igyipti bikpamma la di siisi Izra…õli dima maa biisi.

ref: Because of their faith, the people walked through the Red Sea on dry land. But when the Egyptians tried to do it, they were drowned.
text: U y…õdda niŇčbu zugu ka TeekuZeoo koom daa nyuusi, ka Izra…õli dima maa daa sigi n‚ÄĎn…Ē tankuumni n‚ÄĎgaari; amaa Igyipti niriba la Ňč…Ēn daa ma'asi n‚ÄĎsigi teeku maa ni, koom daa di ba.

ref: God's people had faith, and when they had walked around the city of Jericho for seven days, its walls fell down.
text: Niriba Ňč…Ēn daa niŇči y…õdda zugu ka Gy…õrikoo gooma zaa daa lu: ba daa kyaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎgyiligi dabsa ay…Ēp…Ēi, ka a daa lu.

ref: Rahab had been a prostitute, but she had faith and welcomed the spies. So she wasn't killed with the people who disobeyed.
text: Halli Reehabu, gy…õ'amiru la, daa niŇči y…õdda; ka di zugu ba daa bu ku u ban daa ka y…õdda la saani, dama ninvugseeba ban daa ky…õ na ni ba ti g…Ēsi tiŇčŇča la daa gb…õ p…Ē'a maa saani ka u daa deaai ba suŇčŇča.

ref: What else can I say? There isn't enough time to tell about Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets.
text: T…Ē: ka n nin laa n‚ÄĎy…õli la wula? N ka saha n nin t…Ē'asi Gyidiy…Ēn y…õla, ni Barakku, ni Sams…Ēn, ni Gy…õfta, ni NaaDauda, ni Samuw…õli, ni anabidima y…õla maa.

ref: Their faith helped them conquer kingdoms, and because they did right, God made promises to them. They closed the jaws of lions
text: Ba zaa daa niŇči la Naawunni y…õdda, n‚ÄĎtuusi t…Ēppu n‚ÄĎnyaŇčŇči nti di naam, ba daa kari sariya ni y…õlimaŇčni, ka deaai Naawunni Ňč…Ēn daa p…Ē noori, ni, u ti tisi seem la; halli n daa ti kpari gbigma n…Ēya,

ref: and put out raging fires and escaped from the swords of their enemies. Although they were weak, they were given the strength and power to chase foreign armies away.
text: n‚ÄĎkpiisi bugum zilima yaa; niriba Ňč…Ēn daa zaŇčŇči takoobi ni ba ti ky…õ ba, ba daa nya fa-m‚ÄĎbasi. Ba daa ny…õ la bumbugsa ka laa n‚ÄĎleebi yaa daandima; ba daa nya kpeeŇčŇču t…Ēppu ni, ka daa ta'ai tinzundima t…Ēbbiisi n‚ÄĎlabsi ba nyaaŇčŇča.

ref: Some women received their loved ones back from death. Many of these people were tortured, but they refused to be released. They were sure they would get a better reward when the dead are raised to life.
text: Ba daa ky…õ ka seeba neaai kum ni na, ka labsi ba n‚ÄĎti ba madima, bee, ba p…Ē'aba. Ba mun daa namsi lala niriba seeba ni ba ti za'asi Naawunni, amaa ka ba daa bu sakki n‚ÄĎza'asi ni ba ti yi namsigu maa ni. Ay! - Ba daa boori ni ba ti yisigi kum ni n‚ÄĎnya bunsuma an gaari lala.

ref: Others were made fun of and beaten with whips, and some were chained in jail.
text: Ba daa diisi baseeba vi, ka feebi ba; ba daa ba baseeba gba bansi n‚ÄĎkpari dansarika duu ni.

ref: Still others were stoned to death or sawed in two or killed with swords. Some had nothing but sheep skins or goat skins to wear. They were poor, mistreated, and tortured.
text: Ba daa l…Ēbi seeba kuga n‚ÄĎku, ka zaŇčŇči sooyadima ni takoobidima n‚ÄĎŇčmaai seeba n‚ÄĎku ba. Lala niriba seeba mun daa y…õ la peesi ni buusi gbana n‚ÄĎgyinna; fara daa mari ba, ba daa ka seela; niriba daa namsiri ba ka ba daa di wahala zaa.

ref: The world did not deserve these good people, who had to wander in deserts and on mountains and had to live in caves and holes in the ground.
text: Halli ni duniya wa daa bu sum ni lala niriba b…õ ka ni. Ba daa ky…õnni n‚ÄĎgyinni yoo ni, ni z…Ēya ziisi ni, n‚ÄĎgbeeri tampifeeri ni, ni v…Ēya an b…õ tiŇčŇča.

ref: All of them pleased God because of their faith! But still they died without being given what had been promised.
text: BanŇč…Ē zaa - y…õdda zugu ka ba t…Ē'asi ba y…õlisuma y…õla, amaa ba s…Ē daa bu nya nangbamp…Ēri maa seela.

ref: This was because God had something better in store for us. And he did not want them to reach the goal of their faith without us.
text: Di gbinni ny…õ la: ni, Naawunni daa pun daŇčŇči n‚ÄĎgbaai gbaari din ny…õ bunsuŇčŇču n‚ÄĎti tirima: ni, ban ku toaai n‚ÄĎnya ba bunsuma m‚ÄĎmaali m‚ÄĎb…õ deede ba k…Ēk…Ēa, see ka tirima gba la'am ba saani n‚ÄĎnya.

ref: Such a large crowd of witnesses is all around us! So we must get rid of everything that slows us down, especially the sin that just won't let go. And we must be determined to run the race that is ahead of us.
text: T…Ē, ka dinŇč…Ēna, s…õharadima wa n zoaai, ka ba z…õya n‚ÄĎgyiligi ti. Ka di zugu ya ky…õ ka ti yiisi y…õlitibsa an vili n‚ÄĎtabli ti la zaasa, ni ala'akyi din kp…õ la yooma n‚ÄĎgoaai ti ky…õnni maa. Ka lala nin ky…õ ka ti nya sufukpeemni n‚ÄĎz…Ēri n‚ÄĎky…õnni tooni n‚ÄĎd…Ēli s…Ēseaa ban zaali n‚ÄĎti ti la.

ref: We must keep our eyes on Jesus, who leads us and makes our faith complete. He endured the shame of being nailed to a cross, because he knew later on he would be glad he did. Now he is seated at the right side of God's throne!
text: Ka di zugu ti wukkma ti zugu n‚ÄĎky…õ ka ti nini kpa la Yisa. Ňä…Ēna n ny…õ ti y…õdda y…õla piiligu, ka laa n‚ÄĎny…õ a naari gba. Ňä…Ēna n daa di d…Ēpulli kum wahala ka bu zaŇčŇči di ka di ny…õ vi, dama u daa g…Ēsiri la sufupeelim seaa din d…Ē lala wahala maa tooni la. Ka di zugu pampaŇčŇč…Ē ka u paai la u Naawunni nudirigu zizii maa n‚ÄĎziya.

ref: So keep your mind on Jesus, who put up with many insults from sinners. Then you won't get discouraged and give up.
text: Ya siri teaama u y…õla, Ňč…Ēn daa m…Ērigi n‚ÄĎdi wahalaseaa ala'akyi dima Ňč…Ēn daa yaai u y…õla seem la. Lala nin ky…õ ka ya y…õdda ku gbarigi, bee, ya sufu n‚ÄĎkyirim.

ref: None of you have yet been hurt in your battle against sin.
text: Yarima Ňč…Ēn moori situusi moobu ni ya ti za'asiri ala'akyi y…õla la, ya na bu m…Ē halli nti paai dansi ni kum nyaabu.

ref: The Lord corrects the people he loves and disciplines those he calls his own.‚ÄĚ
text: Dama Ňč…Ēn wuri ninvugseeba la ka ti Dugma sa'asiri maa, Ka u feebri Ňč…Ēn zaŇčŇči ninvugs…Ē n‚ÄĎleebi u biiya.‚ÄĚ

ref: Be patient when you are being corrected! This is how God treats his children. Don't all parents correct their children?
text: Ya deaama lala sa'asigu maa kaman di yi la ya Ba saani na: lala nin bugsi, ni, ya ny…õ la Naawunni biisi. Ka bii Ňč…Ēni n lee m‚ÄĎb…õ ni ka u ba bu sa'asiri u? Ay! - s…Ē ka ni!

ref: God corrects all his children, and if he doesn't correct you, then you don't really belong to him.
text: Ya yi bu nya Naawunni sa'asigu kaman u biisi zaa Ňč…Ēn nyari seem maa, dinŇč…Ēna di bugsiya, ni, ya pa la u biisi ni y…õlimaŇčni, amaa seegy…õbisi n kur ny…õ ya.

ref: Our earthly fathers correct us, and we still respect them. Isn't it even better to be given true life by letting our spiritual Father correct us?
text: ZaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči ti duniya wa beebu puuni ti badima n sa'asiri ti, ka ti tiri ba gyirima ka gyeemni ba. Amaa din gaari lala ka laa n‚ÄĎkpa talasi ny…õ la: ni, ti deaai ti Ba Naawunni sa'asigu; dama u su ti siisi y…õla fukumsi, ka u nin ky…õ ka ti nya ny…Ēvuri.

ref: Our human fathers correct us for a short time, and they do it as they think best. But God corrects us for our own good, because he wants us to be holy, as he is.
text: Ti duniya ni badima sa'asiri ti saha beela, ka di d…Ēli la ba sufu yuubu Ňč…Ēn wulisiri ba seem. Naawunni mun sa'asiri ti la hoo din sumsi ti, ka di yuui m‚ÄĎpaai saha din ka naari; ni tin ni Ňč…Ēn ti la'am m‚ÄĎb…õ u beebu kasikasi maa ni.

ref: It is never fun to be corrected. In fact, at the time it is always painful. But if we learn to obey by being corrected, we will do right and live at peace.
text: Tubdarigri saha na mari la beerim, ka ba bu nya di anfaani Ňč…Ēn ny…õ seem; amaa ka di nyaaŇčŇča ban deaai lala sa'asigu maa nin nya di nyoori, ka di ny…õ la sufudoaa ni y…õlimaŇčni beesigu.

ref: Now stand up straight! Stop your knees from shaking
text: Dizugu ya kpamsima ya nuusi ni ya duna, dama a ka yaa.

ref: and walk a straight path. Then lame people will be healed, instead of getting worse. Warning against Turning from God
text: Ka ky…õ ka s…Ēg…Ēlima maa yi teaai m‚ÄĎmaali suŇčŇča. Ka lala nin ky…õ ka ban ny…õ w…Ēbsi maa ku burigri soori ni, amaa ka laa n‚ÄĎnya tippu.

ref: Try to live at peace with everyone! Live a clean life. If you don't, you will never see the Lord.
text: T…Ē: ya kpaŇčŇčma ya maŇčŇča ka nangbanyinni b…õ yan ni s…Ēkam zaa saani, ka galinsi ku b…õ ni; amaa ya la'amni m‚ÄĎb…õ beebu kasi n‚ÄĎtiri taaba, dama di yi pa lala s…Ē ka ni n nin nya ti Dugma maa.

ref: Make sure no one misses out on God's wonderful kindness. Don't let anyone become bitter and cause trouble for the rest of you.
text: Ya mari ya mansi ka di ky…õ ka s…Ē k…ĒŇčŇči Naawunni bunsuma, miri ka putoo y…õla kp…õ na ka taasi galinsi na n‚ÄĎsa'amni niriba pam y…õla.

ref: Watch out for immoral and ungodly people like Esau, who sold his future blessing for only one meal.
text: Halli yan di ky…õ ka s…Ē tumni zina, bee, u b…õ kaman Iis…Ē Ňč…Ēn ba'a daa ka Naawunni y…õtt…Ē'a ni, ka daa zaŇčŇči u d…Ē'am kpamni n‚ÄĎtakki sa'aŇčmanni yinni la.

ref: You know how he later wanted it back. But there was nothing he could do to change things, even though he begged his father and cried.
text: Ka ya mi, ni, di nyaaŇčŇča Iis…Ē daa ti boori ni u ba ka kasuma n‚ÄĎti u, ka daa bu nya s…Ēseelli ni u ti d…Ēli n‚ÄĎtakki Ňč…Ēn pun tum seem la, halli u daa yoolim n‚ÄĎkum nintam la y…Ēri, soori daa ka ni.

ref: You have not come to a place like Mount Sinai that can be seen and touched. There is no flaming fire or dark cloud or storm
text: T…Ē; ka yarima bu paai la luginseelli ban nin toaai n‚ÄĎsiisi na, hoo Izra…õli dima Ňč…Ēn daa paai zoori maa seem la, z…Ēseaa bugum Ňč…Ēn daa dira, ka zibsim ni sangbansabla ni sanzeoo zeebu m b…õ ni,

ref: because they could not obey its commands. They were even told to kill any animal that touched the mountain.
text: Ba daa bu nyaŇčŇči ban daa zaali ba zaaligu seaa maa, ni, ‚ÄúHalli bunk…Ēbgu gba yi siisi zoori maa, yan l…Ēbma u kuga n‚ÄĎku.‚ÄĚ

ref: The sight was so frightening that Moses said he shook with fear.
text: Lala g…Ēsibu daa mari dabeem pam, halli ka Musa u mammaŇčŇča daa ti fabli, ni, dabeem mari u halli ka u daa gyigra.

ref: You have now come to Mount Zion and to the heavenly Jerusalem. This is the city of the living God, where thousands and thousands of angels have come to celebrate.
text: Amaa yarima Ňč…Ēn paai z…Ēseelli na ny…õ la Zay…Ēn zoori; ya paai la Naawunni Ňč…Ēn ny…õ Ny…Ēvuri Daana la tiŇčŇča na - di ny…õ la arizana ni Gy…õrusal…õm la; ya paai la malaakadima maa saani ka ba tusatusa la'amni la taaba ni sufupeelim pam;

ref: Here you will find all of God's dearest children, whose names are written in heaven. And you will find God himself, who judges everyone. Here also are the spirits of those good people who have been made perfect.
text: halli ya paai la bikpamma la'aŇčŇču maa na gba, baseeba yuya s…Ēbi m‚ÄĎb…õ arizana ni bukku maa ni. DinŇč…Ēna ya paai la Naawunni saani na, ka u ny…õ la s…Ēkam zaa sarikatta: ka naayi paai Naawunni niriba ziiya, ya'aseaa ba nin ti kpalimni m‚ÄĎb…õ ni, dama ba daa d…Ēli u soori deede n‚ÄĎnaai.

ref: And Jesus is here! He is the one who makes God's new agreement with us, and his sprinkled blood says much better things than the blood of Abel.
text: Ya paai la Yisa saani na gba, Ňč…Ēn ny…õ sindaanz…Ētta zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči nangbanyinni s…Ēpaalli y…õla, ka laa m‚ÄĎpaai u ziim zii na, dama ba zaŇčŇči u ziim m‚ÄĎmiisi; ka di wulisiri y…õliseesi an mari anfaani n‚ÄĎgaari Eebli y…õla maa.

ref: Make sure you obey the one who speaks to you. The people did not escape, when they refused to obey the one who spoke to them at Mount Sinai. Do you think you can possibly escape, if you refuse to obey the one who speaks to you from heaven?
text: T…Ē, ka dizugu yan di za'asi Ňč…Ēn t…Ē'asiri ya y…õtt…Ē'a wa wumpu. Dama ban Izra…õli dima maa daa za'asi ninvugs…Ē Ňč…Ēn b…õ duniya wa puuni la noori, ka daa bu nyaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎtiligi; ka tirima ya niŇči la wula n‚ÄĎtiligi, ti yi ti za'asi Ňč…Ēn b…õ arizana ni maa noori wumpu?

ref: When God spoke the first time, his voice shook only the earth. This time he has promised to shake the earth once again, and heaven too.
text: Lala saha maa ka Naawunni kuk…Ēri daa ky…õ ka tingbaŇčŇču dam, amaa pampaŇčŇč…Ē u basi la noori, ni, ‚ÄúN ku laa n‚ÄĎky…õ ka tingbaŇčŇču k…Ēk…Ē n dam, amaa n nin laa m‚ÄĎpaasi yummu n‚ÄĎdamni saazugu ni sangbana gba.‚ÄĚ

ref: We should be grateful we were given a kingdom that cannot be shaken. And in this kingdom we please God by worshiping him and by showing him great honor and respect.
text: Dizugu ya ky…õ ka ti p…õ'ari Naawunni, dama u zaŇčŇči la naam n‚ÄĎti ti, ka di ku toaai n‚ÄĎdam. Ka ya laa n‚ÄĎky…õ ka ti zaŇčŇči p…õ'abu din mari dabeem ni gyirima n‚ÄĎgyeemni u, ka lala nin ky…õ ka u nini tigi,

ref: Our God is like a destructive fire!
text: dama ti Naawunni siri ny…õ la ‚Äėbugum din dira‚Äô.

ref: Keep being concerned about each other as the Lord's followers should.
text: Ya ky…õ ka ya kpalim n‚ÄĎwuri taaba hoo mabiisi.

ref: Be sure to welcome strangers into your home. By doing this, some people have welcomed angels as guests, without even knowing it.
text: Ya deaama saamma ya yiya ni, dama lala maa taabu zugu ka seeba daa ti deaai malaakadima ka bu mi.

ref: Remember the Lord's people who are in jail and be concerned for them. Don't forget those who are suffering, but imagine you are there with them.
text: Ya teaama ban kpari dansarika duu ni la y…õla, ka ky…õ ka di Ňčmanni hoo yarima ni ban ka ba la'am n‚ÄĎkpari; ka laa n‚ÄĎteaai ban b…õ namsi ni la y…õla dama ya gba ny…õ la ninsaalidima, ka di pa seem ya gba nin di wahala.

ref: Have respect for marriage. Always be faithful to your partner, because God will punish anyone who is immoral or unfaithful in marriage.
text: Ya ky…õ ka doo ni u p…Ē'a beebu mari gyirima, ka ba la'am m‚ÄĎb…õ ni ni y…õdda, ka di veela; di ky…õ ka ba goori hoo d…Ēgoora bee p…Ē'agoora. Naawunni nin kari lala niriba sariya, ni zina zaa tumniba dinni, n‚ÄĎlusi ba.

ref: Don't fall in love with money. Be satisfied with what you have. The Lord has promised that he will not leave us or desert us.
text: Yan di ky…õ ka ya sufu zaa ti b…õ ligri ni, amaa ya ky…õ ka ya nini nani yan mari bunseesi la; dama Naawunni y…õliya, ni, ‚ÄúN ku bakki ka ky…õ ya, n mun ku za'asi ya.‚ÄĚ

ref: This should make you feel like saying, ‚ÄúThe Lord helps me! Why should I be afraid of what people can do to me?‚ÄĚ
text: Dinzugu ti mari sufukpeemni ka y…õtti, ni#:- ‚ÄúTi Dugma n ny…õ n suŇčŇčna - Dabeem ku mari ma: B…Ē ka ninsaala nin toaai n‚ÄĎniŇči ma?‚ÄĚ

ref: Don't forget about your leaders who taught you God's message. Remember what kind of lives they lived and try to have faith like theirs.
text: Ya teaama ya tooni dima y…õla, ban daa t…Ē'asi Naawunni y…õligu n‚ÄĎti ya la. Ya teesima n‚ÄĎnya ba beebu Ňč…Ēn daa naayi niŇči seem; ka ma'asiri ba, ya y…õdda niŇčbu puuni.

ref: Jesus Christ never changes! He is the same yesterday, today, and forever.
text: Yisa Kirisita Masiiya siri kpalim n‚ÄĎny…õ Ňč…Ēn sa ny…õ seem soosila, n‚ÄĎkpalim n‚ÄĎny…õ lala zuna, ka kur nin kpalim n‚ÄĎny…õ lala sahakam.

ref: Don't be fooled by any kind of strange teachings. It is better to receive strength from God's gift of undeserved grace than to depend on certain foods. After all, these foods don't really help the people who eat them.
text: Yan di ky…õ ka wulisipaala balibubalibu y…Ēmsi ya ka ya b…Ērigi. Di tuya ni Naawunni piini kpamsi ti sufu, ka pa la bundira kyisigu y…õla, an ka bukaata n‚ÄĎti ban d…Ēli a la.

ref: But we have an altar where even the priests who serve in the place of worship have no right to eat.
text: Dama ti mari maaligu ni ti deaari, ka ban kpalim n‚ÄĎtumni GyeeŇčŇču Sugri tuma la ka soori ni ba la'am n‚ÄĎdi diibu wa.

ref: After the high priest offers the blood of animals as a sin offering, the bodies of those animals are burned outside the camp.
text: BanŇč…Ē kaari maa ni ka GyeeŇčŇču Toondaana maa n yi mari bunk…Ēbri ziim n‚ÄĎkp…õ Naawunni Beebu Ziiya; ka di wulisi, ni, tumbeeri zugu maaligu nla. Amaa bunk…Ēbri wa muna - ba daa zaŇčŇči a n‚ÄĎyiisi ba gbeebu ziiya kpaŇčŇča sa n‚ÄĎnyoaai bugum n‚ÄĎdi a.

ref: Jesus himself suffered outside the city gate, so his blood would make people holy.
text: Lala zugu ka Yisa gba daa di namsi tiŇčŇča maa za'an…Ēri kpaŇčŇča sa, ni u toori ziim ky…õ ka daadam ti leebi Naawunni niriba.

ref: This is why we should go outside the camp to Jesus and share in his disgrace.
text: DinŇč…Ēna ya ky…õ ka ti doaai tin gb…õ seela, n‚ÄĎyi kpaŇčŇča sa m‚ÄĎpaai u saani, ka tirima ni Ňč…Ēna la'am n‚ÄĎdi vi seesi Ňč…Ēn daa di maa.

ref: On this earth we don't have a city that lasts forever, but we are waiting for such a city.
text: Dama ti ka tiŇčŇča kan z…õ deede n‚ÄĎyuui duniya wa ni, na'ala ti boori tinseaa kan ky…õnni na la.

ref: Our sacrifice is to keep offering praise to God in the name of Jesus.
text: Ya ky…õ ka ti maanni p…õ'abu sara n‚ÄĎtiri Naawunni sahakam, Ňč…Ēn Yisa yuuri zugu - ti nangbana Ňč…Ēn p…õ'ari u yuuri seem nla.

ref: But don't forget to help others and to share your possessions with them. This too is like offering a sacrifice that pleases God.
text: Yan di tam tunsuma tumpu y…õla, ka t…Ētti ya marigu ni taaba, dama lala sara maaligu maari Naawunni sufu.

ref: Obey your leaders and do what they say. They are watching over you, and they must answer to God. So don't make them sad as they do their work. Make them happy. Otherwise, they won't be able to help you at all.
text: Ya sakkma n‚ÄĎtiri ya tooni dima, ka wumni ba noori. Ban g…Ēsiri ya zugu ni nimmoosi hoo ninvugseeba ban gali g…Ēgala, dama ba zuguraandima nin boosi ba boosa zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči ba tuma y…õla. Ya wumma n‚ÄĎtiri ba; ka ba sufu maasa ba tuma ni, ka y…õlimugsa ka ni, dama di yi pa lala di ku suŇčŇči ya.

ref: But first you must realize that in the last days some people won't think about anything except their own selfish desires. They will make fun of you

ref: and say, ‚ÄúDidn't your Lord promise to come back? Yet the first leaders have already died, and the world hasn't changed a bit.‚ÄĚ
text: Ka ba nin boosi, ni, ‚ÄúU daa basi noori, ni, u nin ky…õ na. Ammi, u ky…õ na? Ka u lee? Ti badima pun ka ni, ka seelli kam zaa na kpalim n‚ÄĎny…õ la hoo din daa pun ny…õ seem duniya piiligu ni la.‚ÄĚ

ref: They will say this because they want to forget that long ago the heavens and the earth were made at God's command. The earth came out of water and was made from water.
text: Ba mi halli, ka za'asiri di y…õlimaŇčni maa, ni, sahanseaa sa la Naawunni daa y…õli la u y…õligu ka arizana ni duniya daa naam; koom ka u daa zaŇčŇči m‚ÄĎmaali lala duniya maa, ka ka yi koom ni na.

ref: Later it was destroyed by the waters of a mighty flood.
text: Di mun daa ny…õ la koom ni ka lala duniyakurigu maa daa sa'am n‚ÄĎnaai.

ref: But God has commanded the present heavens and earth to remain until the day of judgment. Then they will be set on fire, and ungodly people will be destroyed.
text: Amaa sangbana ni duniya seesi an b…õ ni wa muna - Naawunni daa y…õli u y…õligu yaasa, ni, u ti mari a n‚ÄĎguri ni a ti ny…Ē bugum. Ba mari a n‚ÄĎguri la ninvugbeeri maa sariya karibu daari, ni ba kari ba sariya ka sa'am ba.

ref: Dear friends, don't forget that for the Lord one day is the same as
text: M mabiisi, yan di tam y…õlli yinni wa, ni, w…Ēligri ka ni Naawunni saani: dabsiri yinni ni yuuma tusiri - Ňä…Ēn saani a zaa ny…õ la yummu.

ref: The Lord isn't slow about keeping his promises, as some people think he is. In fact, God is patient, because he wants everyone to turn from sin and no one to be lost.
text: Ti Dugma Naawunni bu maasi ni u nangbambasiri, hoo seeba Ňč…Ēn teesiri seem maa. Ay! - u kur mari la sugru n‚ÄĎtiri ya, dama u bu boori s…Ē sa'aŇčŇču, amaa u boori la s…Ēkam zaa ti Ňčmaligi ka ky…õ u tumbeeri.

ref: The day of the Lord's return will surprise us like a thief. The heavens will disappear with a loud noise, and the heat will melt the whole universe. Then the earth and everything on it will be seen for what they are.
text: Amaa ti Dugma dabsiri maa siri ya s…Ē'ai ni n‚ÄĎky…õ na hoo na'ayiga Ňč…Ēn yi s…Ē'ari n‚ÄĎtumni seem la. Lala dabsiri maa ka saazugu nin ti tasi n‚ÄĎniŇči vuuri pam ka gaari; saazugu ni tingbaŇčŇču zaa tuga, ni a neema kam zaa, nin ti di bugum ka lakki m‚ÄĎbasi. TiŇčŇča maa, ni buns…Ē'ara zaa an b…õ ka ni la, nin ti yi paalu.

ref: You should look forward to the day when God judges everyone, and you should try to make it come soon. On that day the heavens will be destroyed by fire, and everything else will melt in the heat.
text: Yan guri ti Dugma Naawunni ky…õnni dabsiri na maa, ya kpaŇčŇčma ya mansi ka ka ky…õ na yooma. Di daari maa ka sangbana nin di bugum n‚ÄĎsa'am, ka saazugu ni tingbaŇčŇču tuga ni a neema zaa nin ny…õligi m‚ÄĎbasi.

ref: But God has promised us a new heaven and a new earth, where justice will rule. We are really looking forward to this!
text: Amaa tirima n guri la Naawunni Ňč…Ēn basi noori, ni, u ti tisi bunseelli kam zaa maa: ni, sangbampaala ni duniyapaalli ka u nin ti tisi, b…õni ka y…õlimaŇčni nin ti mari ka yiri n‚ÄĎgbeeri sahakam.

ref: Don't forget that the Lord is patient because he wants people to be saved. This is also what our dear friend Paul said when he wrote you with the wisdom God had given him.
text: Kur teaama, ni, di ny…õ la ti Dugma Ňč…Ēn di sugru zugu ka ninsaalidima nin nya tiliginsim. Lala ka ti z…ĒsuŇčŇču P…Ēla gba daa pun s…Ēbi n‚ÄĎwulisi ya. Ti Dugma zaŇčŇči bamsim n‚ÄĎti u, ka suŇčŇči u ni u ti wulisiri y…õlimaŇčni y…õla.

ref: Paul talks about these same things in all his letters, but part of what he says is hard to understand. Some ignorant and unsteady people even destroy themselves by twisting what he said. They do the same thing with other Scriptures too.
text: U s…Ēbri anŇč…Ē taabu n‚ÄĎniŇči u gbana zaasa puuni. Amaa u gbana maa ni ka ya'aseesi m b…õ ni an bamsim t…Ē ni: ninvugseeba ban ka yam ka ku toaai n‚ÄĎz…õ yummu b…õ ni, ka lala niriba zaŇčŇčni P…Ēla y…õla n‚ÄĎtakkri n‚ÄĎgy…õtta - hoo ban takkri Naawunni y…õliseesi zaa gba - ka dizugu ba sa'amni ba maŇčtoori y…õla m‚ÄĎbasira.

ref: My dear friends, you have been warned ahead of time! So don't let the errors of evil people lead you down the wrong path and make you lose your balance.
text: Yarima muna, m mabiisi - ya pun wum y…õla wa y…õlimaŇčni Ňč…Ēn ny…õ seem. Dizugu ya mari ya mansi ka di ky…õ ka ninvugbeeri wa daamni ya. Ba za'asiri wumpu, yan di ky…õ ka ba y…Ēmsi ya n‚ÄĎlusi ya, ka ya yi ya zaazisuŇčŇču ni.

ref: Let the gift of undeserved grace and the understanding that come from our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ help you keep on growing. Praise Jesus now and forever! Amen.
text: Ya ky…õ ka ya kur ky…õnni tooni, yan b…õ ni n‚ÄĎdeaari piinisuma ti Dugma ni Tiligra Yisa Kirisita saani sahanseaa la, m‚ÄĎpaasiri bamsim zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči u y…õla gba. Ňä…ĒnŇč…Ē nin mari gyirima Zuna, ni saha din ka t…Ēriga. Ammi.

ref: The Word that gives life was from the beginning, and this is the one our message is about. Our ears have heard, our eyes have seen, and our hands have touched this Word.
text: Ti s…Ēbri n‚ÄĎtiri ya la ny…Ēvuri y…õligu maa zugu. Y…õligu maa daa b…õ ni piiligu ni sa. Tin daa wum bunseesi maa ka ti ya n‚ÄĎs…Ēbi zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči u y…õla maa, ni tin daa g…Ēsi seesi, ka ti nini nya, ka ti daa zaŇčŇči ti nuusi n‚ÄĎsiisi.

ref: The one who gives life appeared! We saw it happen, and we are witnesses to what we have seen. Now we are telling you about this eternal life that was with the Father and appeared to us.
text: Ny…Ēvuri maa n daa yi paalu na, ka ti daa nya; di zugu ka ti t…Ē'asiri m‚ÄĎmoonni lahabaari n‚ÄĎtiri ya. Ti lahabaari ny…õ la ny…Ēvuri kan ka naari wa y…õla, ny…Ēvuridaans…Ē Ňč…Ēn daa b…õ Naawunni ti Ba saani la, ka pampaŇčŇč…Ē u yi paalu na ka ti nya.

ref: We are telling you what we have seen and heard, so you may share in this life with us. And we share in it with the Father and with his Son Jesus Christ.
text: Tin nya ka wum seelli maa ka ti moonni n‚ÄĎtiri ya gba, ni ti ti ky…õ ka tin ni yan nin la'am m‚ÄĎb…õ ni. Halli ti beebu nin ti b…õ ti Ba saani, ni u Bii Yisa Kirisita Masiiya saani.

ref: We are writing to tell you these things, because this makes us truly happy. God Is Light
text: Ti s…Ēbi kanŇč…Ē ni ti zaa sufumaasim leebi bummunni zaa.

ref: Jesus told us God is light and doesn't have any darkness in him. Now we are telling you.
text: T…Ē; lahabaseaa tin daa wum Naawunni Bii maa saani ka moonni n‚ÄĎtiri ya nŇč…Ēa#:- Naawunni ny…õ la neesim, ka zibsim halli fiiŇč ka u saani.

ref: If we say we share in life with God and keep on living in the dark, we are lying and are not living by the truth.
text: Dinzugu, ti yi y…õtti, ni, ti b…õ la u beesigu maa taabu amaa ka na kpalim m‚ÄĎb…õ zibsim ni lala saha maa, ti ma'ari la ziri ti y…õtt…Ē'a maa ni, ni ti beesigu maa ni m‚ÄĎpaasi.

ref: But if we live in the light, as God does, we share in life with each other. And the blood of his Son Jesus washes all our sins away.
text: Amaa ti yi b…õ neesim ni - hoo Ňä…Ēn b…õ neesim ni seem maa - dinŇč…Ēna tin ni Ňč…Ēn beesigu maa sakkri n‚ÄĎtiri taaba. Ka Naawunni Bii Yisa Ňč…Ēn daa kpi n‚ÄĎti ti maa zugu, u ziim nin ti peaari ti n‚ÄĎyiisiri ti tumbeeri zaa taalli ni.

ref: If we say we have not sinned, we are fooling ourselves, and the truth isn't in our hearts.
text: Ti yi ti y…õli, ni, ti ka la tumbeeri, ti y…Ēmsiri la ti maŇčŇča ka y…õlimaŇčni ka ti ni.

ref: If we say we have not sinned, we make God a liar, and his message isn't in our hearts.
text: Amaa ti yi y…õtti, ni, ti bu tum tumbeeri, lala gbinni n ny…õ, ni, Naawunni ny…õ la ziri daana; dizugu ti yi y…õli lala di wulisi, ni, u y…õligu ka ti ni.

ref: My children, I am writing this so you won't sin. But if you do sin, Jesus Christ always does the right thing, and he will speak to the Father for us.
text: T…Ē, m biisi, dinŇč…Ē ka n s…Ēbri n‚ÄĎtiri ya na, ni ya di tum tumbeeri. Amaa s…Ē yi ti tum tumbeeri, u mari la s…Ē Ňč…Ēn z…Ēri sindaani u zugu n‚ÄĎtiri Naawunni ti Ba maa - Yisa Kirisita nla, s…Ē Ňč…Ēn tumni y…õlimaŇčni tuma deede.

ref: Christ is the sacrifice that takes away our sins and the sins of all the world's people.
text: Ka Ňč…Ēn Kirisita leebi la maaligu kan ti ti soori n‚ÄĎky…õ ka ti nya ky…õ-m‚ÄĎp…õŇčŇči ti tumbeeri zugu. Ka di pa la tirima k…Ēk…Ē tumbeeri, amaa s…Ēkam zaa dina.

ref: When we obey God, we are sure we know him.
text: Tin d…Ēli Naawunni zaaligu zugu nin wulisi, ni, ti siri mi u.

ref: But if we claim to know him and don't obey him, we are lying and the truth isn't in our hearts.
text: Ninvugs…Ē yi y…õli, ni, ‚ÄúM mi u.‚ÄĚ, ka bu d…Ēli u zaaligu maa, lala nira ny…õ la ziri daana ka y…õlimaŇčni ka u ni.

ref: We truly love God only when we obey him as we should, and then we know we belong to him.
text: Amaa Ňč…Ēn d…Ēli u y…õligu maa ny…õ la s…Ē Naawunni wurilim maa Ňč…Ēn siri paali u zaa. DinŇč…Ē nin ky…õ ka ti niŇči y…õdda, ni, ti la'am m‚ÄĎb…õ Naawunni saani.

ref: If we say we are his, we must follow the example of Christ. The New Commandment
text: Ňä…Ēnkam y…õnni, ni, u la'am m‚ÄĎb…õ la Yisa saani, di sumni ni di daama b…õ la hoo Yisa Ňč…Ēn daa b…õ seem la.

ref: My dear friends, I am not writing to give you a new commandment. It is the same one you were first given, and it is the message you heard.
text: N z…Ēdima: di pa la n s…Ēbri la zaalipaaliseaa n‚ÄĎtiri ya na, amaa n teaari ya la bunkurigu y…õla, seaa yan daa deaai piiligu ni la. Zaalikurigu wa kur ny…õ la lahabaseaa yan daa pun wum la.

ref: But it really is a new commandment, and you know its true meaning, just as Christ does. You can see the darkness fading away and the true light already shining.
text: Halli zaalipaalli n ny…õ ka gba, ka n tiri ya; dama ka ny…õ la y…õlimaŇčni Yisa Masiiya zugu, ni y…õlimaŇčni yarima saani gba. Ka nin kpalim m‚ÄĎb…õ lala n‚ÄĎtiri ya gba; dama zibsim la viiri ni, ka y…õlimaŇčni neesim neaara.

ref: If we claim to be in the light and hate someone, we are still in the dark.
text: Ninvugs…Ē yi ti y…õli, ni, u b…õ la neesim maa ni, ka yaai u mabiiya, u na kpalim m‚ÄĎb…õ zibsim ni.

ref: But if we love others, we are in the light, and we don't cause problems for them.
text: Ňä…Ēn mun wuri u mabiiya kpalim m‚ÄĎb…õ la neesim maa ni, ka seelli ka u ni kan nin toaai n‚ÄĎky…õ ka u taraana ti tuui n‚ÄĎtum taalli.

ref: If we hate others, we are living and walking in the dark. We don't know where we are going, because we can't see in the dark.
text: Amaa Ňč…Ēn mun yaai u mabiiya b…õ la zibsim ni n‚ÄĎky…õnna. U bu mi Ňč…Ēn ky…õnni m‚ÄĎpaari seela, dama zibsim maa ky…õ ka u nini bu nyara.

ref: Children, I am writing you, because your sins have been forgiven in the name of Christ.
text: M biisi, man s…Ēbri n‚ÄĎtiri ya wa, yan pun deaai ya tumbeeri ky…õ-m‚ÄĎp…õŇčŇči zugu - ya deaai di Yisa yuuri zugu.

ref: Parents, I am writing you, because you have known the one who was there from the beginning. Young people, I am writing you, because you have defeated the evil one.
text: M badima, n s…Ēbri n‚ÄĎtiri ya gba, Yan mi S…Ē Ňč…Ēn daa b…õ ni zugu - duniya piiligu saha ka u daa b…õ ni. Na'akyimbisi, n s…Ēbri n‚ÄĎtiri ya gba, yan pun zabi Sintaani n‚ÄĎnyaŇčŇči u la zugu.

ref: Children, I am writing you, because you have known the Father. Parents, I am writing you, because you have known the one who was there from the beginning. Young people, I am writing you, because you are strong. God's message is firm in your hearts, and you have defeated the evil one.
text: M‚ÄĎpaasi yaasa#:- M bibisi, n laa n‚ÄĎs…Ēbi n‚ÄĎti ya, yan mi Naawunni ti Ba la zugu. M badima, n laa n‚ÄĎs…Ēbi n‚ÄĎti ya gba, Yan mi S…Ē Ňä…Ēn Daa B…õ ni la zugu - duniya piiligu saha ka u daa b…õ ni. Na'akyimbisi, n laa n‚ÄĎs…Ēbi n‚ÄĎti ya gba, yan mari yaa zugu; ka Naawunni y…õligu Ňč…Ēn b…õ ya ni zugu, ka yan pun zabi Sintaani n‚ÄĎnyaŇčŇči u la.

ref: Don't love the world or anything that belongs to the world. If you love the world, you cannot love the Father.
text: Ya wumma! Yan di wuri duniya wa, bee, bunseesi an b…õ ka ni la. S…Ē Ňč…Ēn boori duniya wa y…õla, ti Ba Naawunni wurilim ka u ni.

ref: Our foolish pride comes from this world, and so do our selfish desires and our desire to have everything we see. None of this comes from the Father.
text: Dama bunseelli kam kan b…õ duniya wa ni, hoo ningbink…Ērisi ni bunveela zaa ti nini Ňč…Ēn nya ka boora, ni saalidima Ňč…Ēn gyeemni bunseelli zaa - anŇč…Ē zaa bu yi la ti Ba Naawunni saani na, amaa duniya buna n ny…õ a, ka yi la duniya wa ni na.

ref: The world and the desires it causes are disappearing. But if we obey God, we will live forever. The Enemy of Christ
text: Ka duniya wa paari ka naari ni, kan ni ka k…Ērisi y…õla zaa. Amaa ban tumni Naawunni Ňč…Ēn boori ni ba tum seem la - lala niriba nin kpalim m‚ÄĎb…õ ni halli saha kan ka naari.

ref: Children, this is the last hour. You heard that the enemy of Christ would appear at this time, and many of Christ's enemies have already appeared. So we know the last hour is here.
text: M biisi, saha maa nŇč…Ēa. Ba daa pun y…õli ya, ni, Masiiya Dindaana maa nin ti ky…õ na; t…Ē, ka u dindaandima pam pun ky…õ na. DinŇč…Ē nin ky…õ ka ti baŇčŇči, ni, saha maa nŇč…Ēa.

ref: These people came from our own group, yet they were not really part of us. If they had been part of us, they would have stayed with us. But they left, which proves they did not belong to our group.
text: Ba daa yi ti saani m‚ÄĎbakki, amaa ba daa pa la ti niriba ni y…õlimaŇčni; ba yi siri daa ny…õ ti niriba, ba naa n‚ÄĎkpalim ti saani. Ban yi m‚ÄĎbakki seem la zugu ka ba wulisiri, ni, ba zaa pa la ti niriba.

ref: Christ, the Holy One, has blessed you, and now all of you understand.
text: Amaa yarim zaa pun deaai Naawunni kpaam soosigu, lala zugu ya zaa baŇčŇčiya.

ref: I did not need to write you about the truth, since you already know it. You also know that liars do not belong to the truth.
text: Din ky…õ ka n s…Ēbri y…õla wa n‚ÄĎtiri ya maa, di pa la n teesi, ni, yan ka bamsim zugu. Ay! - n s…Ēbri n‚ÄĎtiri ya yan pun mi la zugu, ka ziri ni y…õlimaŇčni ku toaai n‚ÄĎkp…õ laa yinni ni.

ref: And a liar is anyone who says Jesus isn't truly Christ. Anyone who says this is an enemy of Christ and rejects both the Father and the Son.
text: Ňä…Ēni n ny…õ ziri daana? - Pa Ňč…Ēn kyiisiri, ni, Yisa pa la Masiiya maa? Di daama ny…õ la Masiiya Dindaana maa; u ny…õ la ninvugs…Ē Ňč…Ēn za'asiri ti Ba Naawunni ni u Bii Yisa zaa y…õla.

ref: If we reject the Son, we reject the Father. But if we say we accept the Son, we have the Father.
text: Ninvugs…Ē Ňč…Ēn za'asiri Naawunni Bii la, u Ba Naawunni gba ka u za'asira. Ňä…Ēn mun sakki n‚ÄĎti Bii maa, u sakkri n‚ÄĎtiri u Ba maa gba.

ref: Keep thinking about the message you first heard, and you will always be one in your heart with the Son and with the Father.
text: Ya ky…õ ka yan daa daŇčŇči n‚ÄĎwum seem la kpalim m‚ÄĎb…õ ya saani. Yan daa daŇčŇči n‚ÄĎwum seela la yi kpalim m‚ÄĎb…õ ya saani, ya gba nin kpalim m‚ÄĎb…õ Bii maa saani, ni u Ba maa gba saani.

ref: I am writing to warn you about those people who are misleading you.
text: Lala ka n s…Ēbri n‚ÄĎtiri ya, ka teesiri la seeba ban boori ba burigi ya la y…õla.

ref: But Christ has blessed you with the Holy Spirit. Now the Spirit stays in you, and you don't need any teachers. The Spirit is truthful and teaches you everything. So stay one in your heart with Christ, just as the Spirit has taught you to do. Children of God
text: Yarima muna - ya daa pun deaai Naawunni kpaam soosigu. Lala piini maa nin ti kpalim m‚ÄĎb…õ ya saani. Di zugu di pa la ya boori ninsaalis…Ē ni u ti wulisi ya yam y…õla, dama Naawunni Siiya soosigu wulisiri ya y…õla kam zaa bamsim Ňč…Ēn ny…õ seem; ka y…õtti ya y…õlimaŇčni, ka bu ma'ari ziri. Dinzugu ya kpalimma m‚ÄĎb…õ u saani, hoo Ňč…Ēn daa wulisi ya seem maa.

ref: Children, stay one in your hearts with Christ. Then when he returns, we will have confidence and won't have to hide in shame.
text: T…Ē; ka pampaŇčŇč…Ē, m biisi, ya kpalimma m‚ÄĎb…õ u saani; ka u yi ti labi n‚ÄĎyi paalu sahanseaa na, ti nin nya soori m‚ÄĎpaai u saani na, ka ti ku ti di vi.

ref: You know Christ always does right and everyone who does right is a child of God.
text: Ya mi halli, ni, Ňä…Ēna n ny…õ y…õlimaŇčni daana. Lala zugu ya baŇčŇčma, ni, s…Ēkam zaa Ňč…Ēn tumni y…õlimaŇčni muna - Naawunni ny…õ la u Ba.

ref: Think how much the Father loves us. He loves us so much that he lets us be called his children, as we truly are. But since the people of this world did not know who Christ is, they don't know who we are.
text: Ya baŇčŇčma Naawunni Ňč…Ēn zaŇčŇči wurilim n‚ÄĎti ti la, ni, din galisi seem! U ky…õ ka ba boonni ti, ni, Naawunni biisi. Ka u biisi n siri ny…õ ti gba. Din ky…õ ka duniya wa na zi ti la ny…õ la: ka daa bu mi Ňä…Ēn gba.

ref: My dear friends, we are already God's children, though what we will be hasn't yet been seen. But we do know when Christ returns, we will be like him, because we will see him as he truly is.
text: N z…Ēsuma, pampaŇčŇč…Ē ti ny…õ la Naawunni biisi, ka ti na bu baŇčŇči tin nin ny…õ seem. Amaa tin mi bunseelli nŇč…Ēa: ni, u yi ti yi paalu na, ti nin ti nya u hoo Ňč…Ēn ny…õ seem ni y…õlimaŇčni, ka dizugu ti nin Ňčmanni hoo Ňč…Ēn b…õ seem maa.

ref: This hope makes us keep ourselves holy, just as Christ is holy.
text: Nira kam Ňč…Ēn mari lala tama maa maanni la u maŇčŇča kasi hoo Ňč…Ēn Naawunni Ňč…Ēn ny…õ kasi seem la.

ref: Everyone who sins breaks God's law, because sin is the same as breaking God's law.
text: T…Ē: Ňč…Ēnkam zaa tumni tumbeeri tuma, u galimni zaaligu maa, dama tumbeeri tumpu ni zaaligu galiŇčŇču ny…õ la yummu.

ref: You know Christ came to take away our sins. He isn't sinful,
text: Amaa ya mi halli, ni, Kirisita daa yi na nti zaŇčŇči ti tumbeeri taalli m‚ÄĎbasi. Ka u mammaŇčŇča saani ka tumbeeri s…õseelli ka ni.

ref: and people who stay one in their hearts with him won't keep on sinning. If they do keep on sinning, they don't know Christ, and they have never seen him.
text: S…Ē ka ni n b…õ u ni ka kpalim n‚ÄĎtumni taalli tuma; Ňč…Ēn mun kpalim n‚ÄĎtumni taalli tuma zaa na zi n‚ÄĎnya u, bee, m‚ÄĎmi u.

ref: Children, don't be fooled. Anyone who does right is good, just like Christ himself.
text: M biisi, yan di ky…õ ka s…Ē y…Ēmsi ya ka ya burigi. Ňä…Ēn tumni din tu zaa ny…õ la y…õlimaŇčni daana, hoo Ňč…Ēn Naawunni Ňč…Ēn ny…õ seem maa.

ref: Anyone who keeps on sinning belongs to the devil. He has sinned from the beginning, but the Son of God came to destroy all that he has done.
text: Ňä…Ēn mun kpalim n‚ÄĎtumni taalli tuma ny…õ la Sintaani nira, dama Ňč…Ēn Sintaani kur tumni la taalli zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎyi piiligu ni sa na. Lala zugu ka Naawunni Bii maa daa yi na; u daa ky…õ na nti sa'am Ňč…Ēn Sintaani tuma maa.

ref: God's children cannot keep on being sinful. His life-giving power lives in them and makes them his children, so they cannot keep on sinning.
text: S…Ēkam Ňč…Ēn ny…õ Naawunni biiya ku kpalim n‚ÄĎtumni tumbeeri, dama Naawunni ny…Ēvuri b…õ u ni; u ku toaai n‚ÄĎkpalim n‚ÄĎtumni taalli tuma, dama Naawunni soori ni ka ba d…Ē'ai u.

text: Ňä…Ēnkam zaa y…õnni, ni, Yisa ny…õ la Naawunni Biiya, di daama la'am m‚ÄĎb…õ ni ni Naawunni, ka Naawunni nin la'am ni u m‚ÄĎb…õ ni.

Ka n nin s…Ēbi m mammantoori yupaalli m‚ÄĎpa di daama ni m‚ÄĎpaasi.‚ÄĚ

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Laodicea: I am the one called Amen! I am the faithful and true witness and the source of God's creation. Listen to what I say.
text: Ni, ‚ÄúS…Ēbma dinŇč…Ē n‚ÄĎti malaaka s…Ē Ňč…Ēn su fukumsi zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči ban b…õ Leoodisiiya tiŇčŇča n‚ÄĎla'amni n‚ÄĎgyeemni ma#:- ‚ÄėŇä…Ēn yuuri boonni, ni, ‚ÄėAmmi‚Äô y…õtt…Ē'a nŇč…Ēa: Ňč…Ēna n ny…õ y…õdda ni y…õlimaŇčni daana, s…Ē Ňč…Ēn diri s…õhara suŇčŇča. Ňä…Ēna n daa piili Naawunni Ňč…Ēn daa naam bunseelli kam, ka su di zaa fukumsi. Ňä…Ēna n‚ÄĎy…õtti ya, ni#:-

ref: I know everything you have done, and you are not cold or hot. I wish you were either one or the other.
text: ‚ÄúM mi ya tuma Ňč…Ēn b…õ seem: ni, ya bu maasa, ya mun bu tuula - man da boori ka ya ny…õ la a yinni.

ref: But since you are lukewarm and neither cold nor hot, I will spit you out of my mouth.
text: DinŇč…Ēna yan bu maasa ka mun bu tuula zugu, ka kur kpalim n‚ÄĎny…õ bunŇčmamm, n ya n‚ÄĎti ya ni n‚ÄĎyiisi n noori ni m‚ÄĎbasi.

ref: You claim to be rich and successful and to have everything you need. But you don't know how bad off you really are. You are pitiful, poor, blind, and naked.
text: Halli ya y…õtti, ni, ‚ÄėBundaandima n ny…õ ti - ti mari la marigu pam ka bu k…ĒŇčŇči seelli!‚Äô - amaa ya bu baŇčŇči, ni, ya siri ny…õ la faradima, ka wahala mari ya, ka ya k…ĒŇčŇči bunseelli kam; ya laa n‚ÄĎzoom zaa ka ky…õnni beelli, situra ka ni.

ref: Buy your gold from me. It has been refined in a fire, and it will make you rich. Buy white clothes from me. Wear them and you can cover up your shameful nakedness. Buy medicine for your eyes, so you will be able to see.
text: Mani n kpaamni ya, ni, ya ky…õm n saani na n‚ÄĎda salima - ban zaŇčŇči bugum n‚ÄĎkuri seesi ka a ti leebi kasikasi maa - ka ya siri nin leebi bundaandima: ya daama situripeela n‚ÄĎy…õa, ka lala nin ligi ya beelli ky…õnni vi maa: halli ka da nini mirigri gba, ka di nin ky…õ ka ya nini neaara.

ref: I correct and punish everyone I love. So make up your minds to turn away from your sins.
text: Ya bu nya? - Man Ňč…Ēn wuri seeba zaa, ban ka n sa'asiri ka kpamsiri, ni ba ti bamsi yam. Dinzugu ya ky…õ ka ya nini moaai ka takki ya teesibeeri ni ya tumbeeri soori.

ref: Listen! I am standing and knocking at your door. If you hear my voice and open the door, I will come in and we will eat together.
text: Nnya ma! N z…õ la za'an…Ēri maa ni n‚ÄĎkpaasiri m‚ÄĎboonna; ka Ňč…Ēnkam yi wum n kuk…Ēri maa ka yoaai n‚ÄĎti ma, n nin kp…õ na n‚ÄĎdi diibu u saani, halli man ni Ňč…Ēna nin ti la'am n‚ÄĎdi.

ref: Everyone who wins the victory will sit with me on my throne, just as I won the victory and sat with my Father on his throne.
text: S…Ēkam Ňč…Ēn nyaŇčŇči, n nin ti u soori ni u zinni man saani n naam gbana zugu, hoo man gba daa pun nyaŇčŇči ka zinni m Ba naam gbana zugu seem la.‚ÄĚ

ref: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches.
text: Ňä…Ēn mari tuba n‚ÄĎwumna, ky…õ ka u wum Sii Kasi maa Ňč…Ēn y…õtti seela n‚ÄĎtiri ban la'amni tinsi tinsi puuni n‚ÄĎgyeemni Naawunni la.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ

ref: After this, I looked and saw a door that opened into heaven. Then the voice that had spoken to me at first and that sounded like a trumpet said, ‚ÄúCome up here! I will show you what must happen next.‚ÄĚ
text: T…Ē: anŇč…Ē nyaaŇčŇča ka n daa nya, ka arizana ziiya ka ba yoaai hoo ky…õburu: ka n daa wum man Ňč…Ēn daa pun wum kuk…Ēseaa la, ka ka b…õ hoo kikaa kunkuma maa. Kuk…Ēri maa y…õtti nŇč…Ēa#:- ‚ÄúDuuma kpa na, ka n nin wulisi i din ti ya niŇči dinŇč…Ē nyaaŇčŇča.‚ÄĚ

ref: Right then the Spirit took control of me, and there in heaven I saw a throne and someone sitting on it.
text: Ka Naawunni Sii Kasi daa paali ma, ka n nya naam ziziiya arizana puuni ka s…Ē n ziya.

ref: The one who was sitting there sparkled like precious stones of jasper and carnelian. A rainbow that looked like an emerald surrounded the throne.
text: Ňä…Ēn daa ziya maa g…Ēsibu daa veela hoo mans…Ē'asi kugveela ban boonni ‚Äėgyasipa‚Äô ni ‚ÄėSadiisi kugri‚Äô la Ňč…Ēn veela seem. Ka sasuu veelim daa gyiligi naam zizii maa hoo mans…Ē'asi veelim Ňč…Ēn b…õ seem.

ref: Twenty-four other thrones were in a circle around that throne. And on each of these thrones there was an elder dressed in white clothes and wearing a gold crown.
text: Ka ziziisi pisi ni anaasi gba daa b…õ ni n‚ÄĎgyiligi naam zizii maa, ka kpambaya pisi ni anaasi zi a ni: ba daa y…õ bunyeeripeela n‚ÄĎziya, ka pili salima naam zupilisi ba zugri ni.

ref: Flashes of lightning and roars of thunder came out from the throne in the center of the circle. Seven torches, which are the seven spirits of God, were burning in front of the throne.
text: Ka saa daa nya'asiri ka tasiri m‚ÄĎmari vuuri pam naam zizii maa ni na, ka bugum m…Ēkpala ay…Ēp…Ēi neaari zizii maa tooni - Naawunni siiya buy…Ēp…Ēi n kur ny…õ ana.

ref: Also in front of the throne was something that looked like a glass sea, clear as crystal. Around the throne in the center were four living creatures covered front and back with eyes.
text: Ka bunseelli daa dooni naam zizii maa tooni hoo m…Ē'ari, ka ka g…Ēsibu Ňčmanni la hoo mans…Ē'asi. Ka bunvuya anaasi n daa z…õ n‚ÄĎgyiligi naam zizii maa sunsukkuni, ka ba lugri kam mari la nini n‚ÄĎg…Ēsira.

ref: The first creature was like a lion, the second one was like a bull, the third one had the face of a human, and the fourth was like a flying eagle.
text: Ka bunvus…Ē Ňč…Ēn daŇčŇči tooni maa daa Ňčmanni la gbigimni, ka Ňč…Ēn pa nyaaŇčŇča Ňčmanni la na'araa, ka Ňč…Ēn paasi buta mari nindaa hoo ninsaala dinni, ka Ňč…Ēn paasi bunaasi mun Ňčmanni la wubga n‚ÄĎyikkra.

ref: Each of the four living creatures had six wings, and their bodies were covered with eyes. Day and night they never stopped singing, ‚ÄúHoly, holy, holy is the Lord, the all-powerful God, who was and is and is coming!‚ÄĚ
text: Ka babanaasi maa zaa daa mari kpunkpama ayooyoobu, ka ba lugri kam mari la nini n‚ÄĎg…Ēsira. Ba daa kur yuumni yuuma sahakam, wuntaŇčŇča ni yuŇčŇču, ni#:- ‚ÄúKasi! kasi! kasi! Ti Dugma Naawunni Ňä…Ēn Ka Kpeaa Ny…õ la Ňč…Ēn b…õ kasi maa! Ňä…Ēna n ny…õ Ňč…Ēn daa pun b…õ ni, Ka u kpalim m‚ÄĎb…õ ni pampaŇčŇč…Ēa, Halli Ňč…Ēn ya ky…õ na.‚ÄĚ

ref: The living creatures kept praising, honoring, and thanking the one who sits on the throne and who lives forever and ever.
text: Ka bunvuya maa daa yi yuumni lala, n‚ÄĎtibgri Ňä…Ēn zi naam zizii maa ni, ka tiri u gyirima, ni, u nin b…õ saha din ka naari. Ka lala saha maa

ref: ‚ÄúOur Lord and God, you are worthy to receive glory, honor, and power. You created all things, and by your decision they are and were created.‚ÄĚ
text: ‚ÄúNyini n ny…õ ti Dugma, Nyini n ny…õ Naawunni, I sumni tibgri ni gyirima, I sumni yiiko marigu! Dama nyini n daa naam bunseelli kam, I yuubu zugu ka a zaa daa naam, I yuubu zugu ka a zaa daa ti b…õ ni.‚ÄĚ

ref: In the right hand of the one sitting on the throne I saw a scroll that had writing on the inside and on the outside. And it was sealed in seven places.

ref: I saw a mighty angel ask with a loud voice, ‚ÄúWho is worthy to open the scroll and break its seals?‚ÄĚ
text: Ka n daa nya malaaka kpeeŇčŇču daans…Ēa, ka u tasiri m‚ÄĎboonni pampam, ni, ‚ÄúŇä…Ēni n sum ni u ti lakki buntabinna maa n‚ÄĎyoaai bukku wa?‚ÄĚ

text: Amaa s…Ēs…Ē daa ka ni n‚ÄĎnin nyaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎyoaai bukku maa, halli n‚ÄĎg…Ēsi ka gba; saazugu s…Ē ka ni, tingbaŇčŇču gba s…Ē ka ni, halli tingbanl…ĒŇčŇču ni gba s…Ē ka ni.

ref: I cried hard because no one was found worthy to open the scroll or look inside it.
text: Man mun daa kum pam, dama s…Ē daa ka ni n‚ÄĎsumsi ni u yoaai bukku maa, halli n‚ÄĎg…Ēsi ka gba.

ref: Then one of the elders said to me, ‚ÄúStop crying and look! The one who is called both the ‚ÄėLion from the Tribe of Judah‚Äô and ‚ÄėKing David's Great Descendant‚Äô has won the victory. He will open the scroll and its seven seals.‚ÄĚ
text: Amaa kpambaya pisi ni anaasi s…Ē daa y…õli ma, ni, ‚ÄúDi kumna! Dama Gbigimni maa nyaŇčŇčiya, Gyuuda za'an…Ēri Gbigimni la; NaaDauda d…Ē'am tuŇčŇču maa Nya'ari gba n ny…õ u. Ňä…Ēna n nin toaai n‚ÄĎlakki buntabinna ay…Ēp…Ēi n‚ÄĎyoaai bukkubila maa.‚ÄĚ

ref: Then I looked and saw a Lamb standing in the center of the throne surrounded by the four living creatures and the elders. The Lamb looked as if it had once been killed. It had seven horns and seven eyes, which are the seven spirits of God, sent out to all the earth.

ref: You let them become kings and serve God as priests, and they will rule on earth.‚ÄĚ

ref: As I looked, I heard the voices of a large number of angels around the throne and the voices of the living creatures and of the elders. There were millions and millions of them,

ref: and they were saying in a loud voice, ‚ÄúThe Lamb who was killed is worthy to receive power, riches, wisdom, strength, honor, glory, and praise.‚ÄĚ
text: M‚ÄĎboonni pampam n‚ÄĎy…õtta#:- ‚ÄúP…õbila maa sumni seelikam deaari! Ba daa k…Ērigi u, ka u sumni anŇč…Ē zaa#: U sum ni u deaai kpeeŇčŇču ni arizikki, U sum ni u deaai yam ni yaa, U sum ni u deaai p…õ'abu, ni gyirima, ni y…õlisuma!‚ÄĚ

ref: Then I heard all beings in heaven and on the earth and under the earth and in the sea offer praise. Together, all of them were saying, ‚ÄúPraise, honor, glory, and strength forever and ever to the one who sits on the throne and to the Lamb!‚ÄĚ
text: Di saha ka n daa wum, ka ban daa naam bunseelli kam zaa gba daa t…Ē'asira - an b…õ saazugu, ni an b…õ tingbaŇčŇču ni, ni an b…õ tingbanl…ĒŇčŇču ni, halli an b…õ teeku ni zaa - n‚ÄĎy…õtti, ni#:- ‚ÄúP…õ'abu ka ba zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎti u: Ba tisi Ňä…Ēn zi naam zizii maa ni, P…õbila maa gba ka ba tisira - P…õ'abu, ni gyirima, ni fukumsi Halli ni saha din ka naari.‚ÄĚ

text: Ka bunvuya anaasi maa daa sakki, ni, ‚ÄúAmmi!‚ÄĚ; ka kpambaya maa daa gbaani tiŇčŇča n‚ÄĎgyeemna.

ref: Then I saw a white horse. Its rider carried a bow and was given a crown. He had already won some victories, and he went out to win more.

ref: When the Lamb opened the third seal, I heard the third living creature say, ‚ÄúCome out!‚ÄĚ Then I saw a black horse, and its rider had a balance scale in one hand.
text: N daa laa n‚ÄĎnya, ka P…õbila maa daa lakki buntabinni kan paasiri buta la; ka n daa wum bunvus…Ē Ňč…Ēn paasi buta la Ňč…Ēn gba n y…õtti, ‚ÄúKy…õm na!‚ÄĚ Ka n daa g…Ēsi n‚ÄĎnya wurisabilli, ka Ňč…Ēn daa ba u maa daa mari gbarigbinni kan ma'asiri kyi u nuu ni.

ref: I heard what sounded like a voice from somewhere among the four living creatures. It said, ‚ÄúA liter of wheat will cost you a whole day's wages! Three liters of barley will cost you a day's wages too. But don't ruin the olive oil or the wine.‚ÄĚ
text: Ka n daa wum hoo kuk…Ēri n‚ÄĎtasiri pampam bunvuya anaasi la sunsukkuni, ni, ‚ÄúKy…õ ka ba tumni dabsimunni n‚ÄĎkur nyari kasuma gbarigbinni yinni, bee, kabeeri gbarigbina ata - a ligri nla! - amaa di sa'am kpaam maa, bee, daam maa!‚ÄĚ

ref: Then I saw a pale green horse. Its rider was named Death, and Death's Kingdom followed close behind. They were given power over one fourth of the earth, and they could kill its people with swords, famines, diseases, and wild animals.
text: Ka n daa g…Ēsi n‚ÄĎnya wuripirisiri, ka Ňč…Ēn daa ba u maa yuuri boonni la ‚ÄėKum‚Äô, ka u taraana ‚ÄėKpiimma TiŇčŇča‚Äô daa d…Ēla. Ka ba daa zaŇčŇči yiiko n‚ÄĎti ba zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči duniya purigri bunaasi zugu yinni niriba, ni ba zaŇčŇči takoobi fukumsi n‚ÄĎkuuri ba, ni k…Ēm fukumsi, ni dooru ni mooni bungbaara dinni m‚ÄĎpaasi.

ref: They shouted, ‚ÄúMaster, you are holy and faithful! How long will it be before you judge and punish the people of this earth who killed us?‚ÄĚ
text: Ka ban zaa n daa tasiri kpeeŇčŇčakpeeŇčŇča n‚ÄĎy…õtti, ni ‚ÄúTi Dugma, nyin kur ny…õ la Y…õlimaŇčni Daana kasikasi! - ka b…Ē saha ka i nin ti kari ti sariya n‚ÄĎti ti, ka zaali duniya dima samni, ti kum zugu?‚ÄĚ

ref: Then each of those who had been killed was given a white robe and told to rest for a little while. They had to wait until the complete number of the Lord's other servants and followers would be killed.

text: halli saŇčmariwuusi gba daa luri tingbaŇčŇču ni na, hoo sahanseaa sunz…ĒpiliŇčŇča Ňč…Ēn zeeri n‚ÄĎz…õ'asiri kinkaŇčŇča ka ka wala luri tiŇčŇča ni seem maa.

text: Ka sangbaŇčŇču maa daa kpabi hoo s…ĒŇčŇču m‚ÄĎbakki, ka tingbaŇčŇču ni z…Ēya la daa yigsi a beesigu ziiya, ni tingbanseesi an b…õ teeku ni la gba.

text: Ka Y…Ēmsira maa, Yibrisi Sintaani la, s…Ē Ňč…Ēn daa ky…õ ka niriba maa b…Ērigi la, Ňč…Ēna ka ba daa gbaai n‚ÄĎzaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎl…Ēsi farinwuta bugum m…Ē'ari maa ni, ya'aseaa ba daa pun l…Ēsi bunk…Ēbbeoo maa, ni u ziri anabi maa. Ka ba zaa b…õ b…õni n‚ÄĎdiri wahala wuntaŇčŇča ni yuŇčŇču, saha din ka tariga.

ref: I saw a great white throne with someone sitting on it. Earth and heaven tried to run away, but there was no place for them to go.
text: T…Ē: ka dinŇč…Ē zaa nyaaŇčŇča ka n daa laa n‚ÄĎnya naam ziziiya, bumpeelli, ka ka daa galisi pam. Ka Naaba zi b…õni. TingbaŇčŇču ni saazugu daa z…Ē m‚ÄĎbakki u saani, ka a daa bu laa n‚ÄĎnya beebu ziiya yaasa.

ref: I also saw all the dead people standing in front of that throne. Every one of them was there, no matter who they had once been. Several books were opened, and then the book of life was opened. The dead were judged by what those books said they had done.
text: Ka n daa nya kpiimma, birikyindima ni tarimma zaa, ka ba zaa daa b…õ la naam zizii maa tooni n‚ÄĎz…õya. Di saha ka ba daa lakki buksi maa; ka daa laa n‚ÄĎlakki bukseaa - kana n ny…õ ny…Ēvuri bukku la. Niriba tuma y…õla daa s…Ēbi n‚ÄĎdooli buksi maa puuni, kpiimma wa ban daa tum tunseesi zaa la. Ka ba daa kari kpiimma maa sariya zaŇčŇči m‚ÄĎpa ba tuma Ňč…Ēn s…Ēbi seem la.

ref: The sea gave up the dead people who were in it, and death and its kingdom also gave up their dead. Then everyone was judged by what they had done.
text: Teeku ni m…Ē'a maa daa yiisi niriba na - seeba an daa di maa. Ka kum ni kpiimma tiŇčŇča gba daa yiisi niriba na - seeba ban daa kpi m‚ÄĎb…õ a fukumsi ni maa zaa. Ka ba daa kari s…Ēkam sariya n‚ÄĎd…Ēli ban daa tum seem.

ref: Afterwards, death and its kingdom were thrown into the lake of fire. This is the second death.
text: Halli kum ni kpiimma tiŇčŇča gba ka ba daa zaŇčŇči n‚ÄĎl…Ēsi farinwuta bugum m…Ē'ari maa ni. Lala bugum m…Ē'ari maa ny…õ la kum din paasiri buyi maa.

ref: Anyone whose name wasn't written in the book of life was thrown into the lake of fire.
text: Ňä…Ēn kam yuuri daa bu s…Ēbi n‚ÄĎdooli ny…Ēvuri bukku maa ni, ba daa zaŇčŇči di daama n‚ÄĎl…Ēsi lala bugum m…Ē'ari maa ni.

ref: I saw a new heaven and a new earth. The first heaven and the first earth had disappeared, and so had the sea.
text: Ka n daa nya arizampaala, ni tingbampaalli; dama arizana ni tingbaŇčŇču seesi an daa pun b…õ ni la daa gaariya. Ka teeku m…Ē'ari daa laa n‚ÄĎka ni.

ref: I heard a loud voice shout from the throne: God's home is now with his people. He will live with them, and they will be his own. Yes, God will make his home among his people.
text: Ka n daa wum kuk…Ēri arizana ni, ka ka tasi n‚ÄĎy…õli pampam, ni, ‚ÄúPampaŇčŇč…Ē Naawunni beebu zii paai niriba saani na, ka u nin ti b…õ ba saani. Ba nin ti ny…õ u niriba, ka Naawunni mammaŇčŇča nin b…õ ba saani ka u nin ny…õ Naawunni n‚ÄĎtiri ba.

ref: He will wipe all tears from their eyes, and there will be no more death, suffering, crying, or pain. These things of the past are gone forever.
text: U nin nyeesi ba nintam zaa; kum ku laa m‚ÄĎb…õ ni, bee sufusa'aŇčŇču bee kunkuma, bee toom; dama bunseesi kam an daa daŇčŇči m‚ÄĎb…õ ni la gaariya.‚ÄĚ

ref: Then the one sitting on the throne said: I am making everything new. Write down what I have said. My words are true and can be trusted.
text: Di saha ka Ňč…Ēn daa zi naam zizii maa ni la daa y…õli, ni, ‚ÄúG…Ēsima! M maanni la bunseela zaa bumpaala.‚ÄĚ Ka laa n‚ÄĎy…õli, ni, ‚ÄúS…Ēbma dinŇč…Ē n‚ÄĎdooli! Dama y…õtt…Ē'a anŇč…Ē mari y…õdda ka ny…õ y…õlimaŇčni.‚ÄĚ

ref: Everything is finished! I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end. I will freely give water from the life-giving fountain to everyone who is thirsty.
text: Ka u daa laa n‚ÄĎy…õli ma, ni, ‚ÄúDi niŇčiya. Mani n ny…õ piiligu, ka man laa n‚ÄĎny…õ naari. K…Ēnyuuri Ňč…Ēn mari s…Ēkam - mani nin zaŇčŇči k…Ēseebu bun yi ny…Ēvuri k…Ēbuliga la ni n‚ÄĎti u y…Ēri, ka samni ka ni.

ref: All who win the victory will be given these blessings. I will be their God, and they will be my people.
text: S…Ēkam Ňč…Ēn nyaŇčŇči maa - anŇč…Ē zaa ka u nin ti di faari, ka man nin ny…õ Naawunni n‚ÄĎtiri u, ka Ňč…Ēn mun nin ny…õ m biiya.

ref: But I will tell you what will happen to cowards and to everyone who is unfaithful or dirty-minded or who murders or is sexually immoral or uses witchcraft or worships idols or tells lies. They will be thrown into that lake of fire and burning sulfur. This is the second death. The New Jerusalem
text: Amaa dabeem dima, ban ka y…õdda, ni putoo dima, ni ninvugkuuriba ni ban tumni zina, ni ban tumni tiima ka gyeemni buga ni ziri kam zaa dima - ban zaa ziiya ny…õ la farinwuta bugum m…Ē'ari maa ni. Lala bugum m…Ē'ari maa ny…õ la kum din paasiri buyi maa.‚ÄĚ

ref: I saw one of the seven angels who had the bowls filled with the seven last terrible troubles. The angel came to me and said, ‚ÄúCome on! I will show you the one who will be the bride and wife of the Lamb.‚ÄĚ
text: T…Ē; ka malaakas…Ē daa paai ma na, ka u daa ny…õ la babay…Ēp…Ēi ban daa mari laasi maa yinni, laseesi an mari duniya naari wahala buy…Ēp…Ēi maa m‚ÄĎpaali zaa la. U daa ky…õ n saani na nti y…õli ma, ni, ‚ÄúKy…õm na! N nin wulisi i p…Ē'apaalli maa, P…õbila maa p…Ē'a la.‚ÄĚ

ref: Then with the help of the Spirit, he took me to the top of a very high mountain. There he showed me the holy city of Jerusalem coming down from God in heaven.
text: Di saha ka Naawunni Sii daa na mari ma, ka malaaka maa daa zi ma m‚ÄĎbakki n‚ÄĎkyaŇčŇči m‚ÄĎpaai z…Ētitaari kan w…Ē'a n‚ÄĎdu pam zugu; ka n daa nya tinkasi maa, Gy…õrusal…õm, ka ka daa yi la arizana ni, Naawunni saani, n‚ÄĎsigri na.

ref: The glory of God made the city bright. It was dazzling and crystal clear like a precious jasper stone.
text: TiŇčŇča maa daa nya'ari Naawunni gyirima veelim zugu. Ka daa nyalisiri hoo gyasipa - mans…Ē'asi kugveeliseaa kan ligri zoaai halli nti yakki nla.

ref: The city had a high and thick wall with twelve gates, and each one of them was guarded by an angel. On each of the gates was written the name of one of the twelve tribes of Israel.
text: Ka daa mari la goonwa'alli kan daa z…õ n‚ÄĎgyiligi ka zaa, ka goomni maa za'an…Ēya daa paai za'an…Ēya pii ni ayi. N daa nya ka malaakadima pii ni ayi daa z…õ n‚ÄĎguri za'an…Ēya maa zaa yinniyinni. Izra…õli dima d…Ē'am za'an…Ēya pii ni ayi yuya daa s…Ēbi n‚ÄĎniŇči tiŇčŇča maa za'an…Ēya ni.

ref: Three of these gates were on the east, three were on the north, three more were on the south, and the other three were on the west.
text: Za'an…Ēya ata n daa b…õ ka wuntampusiri lugri ni, abata daa b…õ nudirigu lugri ni, abata daa b…õ wuntanluri lugri ni, ka abata anla daa b…õ tiŇčŇča nuzaa lugri ni.

ref: The city was built on twelve foundation stones. On each of the stones was written the name of one of the Lamb's twelve apostles.
text: TiŇčŇča maa goomni maa daa mari la ka nyeeba pii ni ayi. Ka ba daa s…Ēbi P…õbila tuumudima pii ni ayi yuya m‚ÄĎpa a ni.

ref: The angel who spoke to me had a gold measuring stick to measure the city and its gates and its walls.
text: Malaakas…Ē Ňč…Ēn daa t…Ē'asi n‚ÄĎti ma la daa mari la salima kika'ari kan ma'asiri wa'alim. U daa zaŇčŇči ka, ni u ti ma'asi tiŇčŇča maa, ni ka za'an…Ēya ni ka gooma.

ref: The city was shaped like a cube, because it was just as high as it was wide. When the angel measured the city, it was about
text: Ba daa boaai tiŇčŇča maa bumm…Ēkku - ka wa'alim ni ka y…õlisim daa sa'ai taaba. U daa ma'asi n‚ÄĎnya, ka ka wa'alim ni ka y…õlisim zaa daa paai maalsi tusiri ni k…Ēbsinu, ka ka saazugu wa'alim gba daa sa'ai lala.

ref: The wall was built of jasper, and the city was made of pure gold, clear as crystal.
text: Gyasipa mans…Ē'asi kugveela ka ba daa zaŇčŇči m‚ÄĎm…õ goomni la, ka tiŇčŇča maa zaa mun ny…õ la salima an veela hoo mans…Ē'asi taabu.

text: Ba daa zaŇčŇči mans…Ē'asi kugveela an ligri zoaai pam m‚ÄĎmaali gooma nyeeba maa. A zaa daa ny…õ kookooba. Kan piili maa ny…õ la gyasipapeelli, buyi daana ny…õ la lala kugseaa kan ny…õ bulu, buta ny…õ la bumpirisiri, bunaasi ny…õ la b…õŇčkoom,

ref: the fifth was onyx, the sixth was carnelian, the seventh was chrysolite, the eighth was beryl, the ninth was topaz, the tenth was chrysoprase, the eleventh was jacinth, and the twelfth was amethyst.
text: bunu ny…õ la bunz…õpeelli, buyoobu ny…õ la bunzeoo zimizimi, buy…Ēp…Ēi ny…õ la d…Ēzum, bunii ny…õ la bunfaasiri, buwai ny…õ la bumpup…Ēriga, kan pa piiya ny…õ la vamaasa, pii ni yinni ny…õ la z…õkamvoo, ka kan mun pa pii ni ayi ny…õ la bunzeoo wulawula.

ref: I did not see a temple there. The Lord God All-Powerful and the Lamb were its temple.
text: T…Ē; ka n daa bu nya gyeeŇčŇču yiri tiŇčŇča maa puuni, dama Naawunni Ňä…Ēn Gaari Zaa ny…õ la ka gyeeŇčŇču yiri, ni P…õbila maa gba.

ref: And the city did not need the sun or the moon. The glory of God was shining on it, and the Lamb was its light.

text: Beoo kam tiŇčŇča maa za'an…Ēya kpalim n‚ÄĎyoaai suŇčŇča wuntaŇčŇča ni, halli ka yuŇčŇču laa n‚ÄĎka b…õni.

ref: The glorious treasures of nations will be brought into the city.
text: Ka tinsi zaa nya'asim, ni gyirima, ni veelim ka ba nin mari na n‚ÄĎzaali ka puuni.

ref: But nothing unworthy will be allowed to enter. No one who is dirty-minded or who tells lies will be there. Only those whose names are written in the Lamb's book of life will be in the city.
text: Da'ari halli fiiŇč ku kp…õ tiŇčŇča maa puuni, bee Ňč…Ēn tumni vi bee ziri tuma, na'ala ban yuya b…õ P…õbila Ňč…Ēn su ny…Ēvuri bukku maa ni la.

ref: The angel showed me a river that was crystal clear, and its waters gave life. The river came from the throne where God and the Lamb were seated.
text: Ka malaaka maa daa laa n‚ÄĎwulisi ma ny…Ēvuri koom kuliga, ka bu neaai kasikasi m‚ÄĎp…Ēbri n‚ÄĎyiiri naam zizii maa ni na, kan ny…õ Naawunni ni P…õbila maa dinni.

ref: Then it flowed down the middle of the city's main street. On each side of the river are trees that grow a different kind of fruit each month of the year. The fruit gives life, and the leaves are used as medicine to heal the nations.
text: Kuliga koom maa daa p…Ēbri n‚ÄĎd…Ēla tiŇčŇča maa s…Ēy…õliŇčŇču sunsukkuni. Ka ny…Ēvuri tiisi daa z…õ k…Ēsoori maa ya'a ayi zaa, n‚ÄĎwanni wala balim pii ni ayi, n‚ÄĎwanni g…Ēri g…Ēri. Ka tiisi maa vaari daa ny…õ la an tibri tinsi zaa niriba.

ref: God's curse will no longer be on the people of that city. He and the Lamb will be seated there on their thrones, and its people will worship God

ref: and will see him face to face. God's name will be written on the foreheads of the people.
text: Ka ba nin nya u nindaa, ka u mun yuuri nin pa ba gbeeri ni.

text: YuŇčŇču ku laa m‚ÄĎb…õ ni. Ba ku laa m‚ÄĎboori firila, halli wuntaŇčŇča neesim gba ka ba ku laa m‚ÄĎboora; dama ti Dugma Naawunni nin neaari n‚ÄĎtiri ba. Ka ba nin di naam, saha din ka tariga.

ref: Then I was told: These words are true and can be trusted. The Lord God controls the spirits of his prophets, and he is the one who sent his angel to show his servants what must happen very soon.
text: Di saha ka malaaka maa daa y…õli ma, ni, ‚ÄúY…õtt…Ē'a anŇč…Ē mari la y…õdda ka ny…õ y…õlimaŇčni. Ti Dugma Naawunni daa su anabidima siisi maa, ka Ňč…Ēn Naawunni tum u malaaka na, ni u ti wulisi u tuntumniba maa bunseesi an nin niŇči ka bu ya n‚ÄĎyuui la.‚ÄĚ

ref: My name is John, and I am the one who heard and saw these things. Then after I had heard and seen all this, I knelt down and began to worship at the feet of the angel who had shown it to me.
text: T…Ē. Ka man, Gy…Ēni, muna - mani n daa wum ka nya anŇč…Ē zaa. Ka man Ňč…Ēn daa wum ka nya a maa, n daa gbaani tiŇčni, malaaka maa tooni - malaaka s…Ē Ňč…Ēn daa wulisiri y…õla wa maa - ni n ti gyeem u.

ref: But the angel said, Don't do that! I am a servant, just like you. I am the same as a follower or a prophet or anyone else who obeys what is written in this book. God is the one you should worship.

ref: Don't keep the prophecies in this book a secret. These things will happen soon.
text: Ka u laa n‚ÄĎy…õli ma, ‚ÄúDi kpabi bukku wa n‚ÄĎtabli, ni ka anabitiri y…õtt…Ē'a la! Dama a saha maa bu ya yuui.

ref: Evil people will keep on being evil, and everyone who is dirty-minded will still be dirty-minded. But good people will keep on doing right, and God's people will always be holy.
text: Ky…õ ka tumbeeri daana kpalim n‚ÄĎtumni u tumbeeri, ky…õ ka da'ari daana kpalim n‚ÄĎtumni u tunda'arisi, ka ky…õ ka y…õlimaŇčni daana mun kpalim n‚ÄĎtumni din tu ni y…õlimaŇčni, ka Ňč…Ēn ny…õ Naawunni nira kpalim n‚ÄĎny…õ bunkasi n‚ÄĎtiri u.‚ÄĚ

ref: Then I was told: I am coming soon! And when I come, I will reward everyone for what they have done.
text: Ni, ‚ÄúNyama, pampaŇčŇč…Ē ka n ky…õnni na! Man Ňč…Ēn ya y…Ē ba yoori maa muna, m mari ka n‚ÄĎky…õnni na. S…Ēkam nin deaai u tuma Ňč…Ēn sumsi seem.

ref: I am Alpha and Omega, the first and the last, the beginning and the end.
text: Mani n ny…õ piiligu ka mun laa n‚ÄĎny…õ naari, man daŇčŇči tooni ka laa n‚ÄĎd…Ēli nyaaŇčŇča.‚ÄĚ

ref: God will bless all who have washed their robes. They will each have the right to eat fruit from the tree that gives life, and they can enter the gates of the city.

ref: But outside the city will be dogs, witches, immoral people, murderers, idol worshipers, and everyone who loves to tell lies and do wrong.

ref: I am Jesus! And I am the one who sent my angel to tell all of you these things for the churches. I am David's Great Descendant, and I am also the bright morning star.
text: Ti Dugma Yisa y…õliya, ‚ÄúMani, Yisa, tum n malaaka ni u ti ti ya lahabaari wa, ka ban la'amni tinsi tinsi n‚ÄĎgyeemni ma la nin ti wum. Mani n ny…õ NaaDauda d…Ē'am tuŇčŇču maa Nya'ari: u bii n ny…õ ma gba. Mani n ny…õ b…õŇčmariga ka neaari nyain.‚ÄĚ

ref: The Spirit and the bride say, ‚ÄúCome!‚ÄĚ Everyone who hears this should say, ‚ÄúCome!‚ÄĚ If you are thirsty, come! If you want life-giving water, come and take it. It's free!
text: Naawunni Sii maa gba, ni P…õbila p…Ē'apaalli maa m‚ÄĎpaasi n y…õtti, ni, ‚ÄúKy…õm na!‚ÄĚ Ky…õ ka Ňč…Ēn wumni gba y…õli, ni, ‚ÄúKy…õm na!‚ÄĚ K…Ēnyuuri yi mari s…Ē zaa, ky…õ ka u ky…õ na, ka s…Ē zaa Ňč…Ēn boora: ba nin ti nyu ny…Ēvuri koom maa y…Ēri, ka samni ka ni.
